Google Map地址: 110台灣臺北市信義區松壽路9號6樓
評論1:
[Percy Cheung] (5分, 2 months ago): We didn't know what to expect... good places are usually not available at last second which we were able to make a reservation for...and when got there we were the only people there...

BUT BOY WERE WE SURPRISED when the food we ordered made it's way to our table.  The server presented dish after dish and explained with excitement the ingredients and combinations!  The way he introduced the dishes really had us excited to try the dish.  As each dish arrived, we enjoyed and looked forward to the next one coming.  We ordered: Truffle Stuffed Fried Chicken Wings,  Char Siu Pork Ribs with Dark Plums, Szechuan Red Oil and Burrata Salad, Stinky Tofu Truffe "in Memory of HK Shrimp Toast" and the Braised Beef Trio with Tomato Salsa.  Each dish was very well prepared.  The flavour combinations were very tasty and kept us wanting more.

Thanks for the great service and experience.  We look forward to coming and trying other dishes!
評論2: [XO Handsome] (3分, 5 months ago): Gubami Social is a popular restaurant in Taipei founded by award-winning chef Lanshu Chen.  Online reservation is straightforward.  The main dining room is spacious and clean.  Young servers are patient and helpful.  While the westernized Taiwanese cuisine is unique, most of the dishes have different strong flavors, which do not quite harmonize with one another.  For example, the pungent Bread Basket with Fermented Bean Curd (NT 220) clashes with the fishy BBQ Squid with Do Ban Sauce (NT380/medium; NT 580/large).  Highlight of the lunch is the rich and savory Rice Vermicelli “Bouillabaisse” of Golden Pomfret (NT 1,180).  Price is not cheap as a mall restaurant.
評論3:
[Precious Tendani Kharivhe] (4分, 10 months ago): I absolutely loved the food at Gubami Social—explosive flavours that even made the simplest pita bread come alive! Their signature beef noodle soup was a standout, and I’d highly recommend it to anyone visiting.

The atmosphere was great—intimate and stylish, with private rooms available for larger groups, making it perfect for gatherings.

The only downside was the service which was a bit slow, likely because they were busy. My waiter wasn’t as attentive as I’d hoped, but it didn’t ruin the experience.

Overall, I’d definitely come back for the incredible food and vibe—hopefully, they can improve the service next time!
評論4:
[Bryan Shu . The truth] (4分, a year ago): I entered it by chance.
A casual, but nice ambience.
Knowledgeable staff, simple but good and tasty dishes.
A cross between French and Taiwanese cuisine.
More Taiwanese than French
All in all, we ate reasonably well

If you do not have high expectations, you can try it.
評論5: [James Pan] (5分, 3 years ago): A place for group of people the have drinks and share some food. The flavor mainly come from Taiwanese or Chinese food but the presentation comes from the French fine dining. You cannot category the food into a simple type. It is mixed with some there some there. The price is high but the dishes totally worth the price. Cocktails and wine can be choice for the flavor you ordered. The staffs are super friendly and helpful providing good suggestions if you cannot make a decision. Still lot of flavors to try. Definitely come back when I have free time.

Google Map地址: 110台灣臺北市信義區松高路11號5樓
評論1:
[Stephanie Lee] (5分, 7 months ago): Urban Paradise truly lives up to its name. This bistro-style buffet offers an elevated dining experience that blends style, flavor, and atmosphere seamlessly. The space radiates good vibes—modern yet warm, making it the perfect spot for both casual gatherings and special occasions.

The food selection is outstanding, with a creative fusion of flavors that feels both familiar and refreshingly unique. Every dish I tried was delicious, beautifully plated, and thoughtfully crafted. The cocktails deserve a special mention—they are not only visually stunning but also perfectly balanced, easily some of the best I’ve had in Taipei.

Service was attentive and professional, adding to the overall polished experience without ever feeling intrusive. Urban Paradise strikes that rare balance of great food, amazing drinks, and inviting atmosphere. A must-visit if you’re in Taipei and looking for something truly memorable.
評論2: [Christopher Lee] (5分, 5 months ago): The latest buffet spot at Taipei. Lot of quality wines & beers plus cocktails. Fresh seafood and creative food combinations. Friendly & efficient staffs. Will come back again when we visit Taiwan. Just around $60USD per person.
評論3: [Cindy] (5分, 4 months ago): Nice atmosphere. The food was great and the people were amazing. There’s free-flow tap beer, mocktails, juices (they taste like real fruit juice, not cordial), coffee, and fizzy drinks. Huge scallops and oysters. I recommend the Western-style soup with the crushed pastry on top. We really enjoyed it.
評論4: [陳宥宇] (5分, a month ago): The waiters and waitresses at the restaurant are very friendly, but you have to make a reservation a month in advance😆
評論5: [RB] (3分, 2 months ago): The food was decent. The buffet offer international and local cuisines, featuring renowned dishes from all over the world. Some dishes though did not hit the criteria. The grill and teppanyaki section was terrible, their ingredients were not fresh and tasted average, with their meats being extremely dry, as if being left out all day. The alcohol section was extensive, including numerous wine, with a bar offering cocktail and mocktail. The western section was probably the best in my opinion. The corn soup was really good. The dessert section was decent, though some cakes tasted dry. Overall, its a decent experience but I would not be back, instead I would go to sunrise buffet (旭集) for a better experience. They have better quality meats and teppanyaki section, their desserts are much better than this buffet.

餐廳名稱: 老井極上燒肉 台北信義店
Google Map地址: 110台灣臺北市信義區松仁路99號B1
評論1:
[Vince Cheong] (5分, 3 months ago): Ok place to grab supper till past 12 midnight. Not the best of the best, but still quite alright. Not the best value for money, but it’s alright.
The food is fresh and wait staffs were very friendly, albeit very hard to communicate in English.
Our server Yuan (長遠） tried his very best to accommodate us 😅😅😅.
評論2:
[Olga Maevskaia] (4分, 5 months ago): Visited in September 2025.
Taiwan 🇹🇼 Taipei.

Located within a bustling business mall, Gokujo Yakiniku is a trendy, spacious restaurant with built-in tabletop grills for an authentic yakiniku experience. The interior is bright, clean, and airy, creating a welcoming and open atmosphere that enhances the dining vibe.

FOOD:

The menu revolves around yakiniku-style dining, where you grill premium meats and seafood at your table. Options include curated sets or an à la carte selection for personalized picks. Our meal started with appetizers, including scallop nigiri and cod liver. The scallops were fresh and sweet, but the oversized rice portion overwhelmed their delicate flavor. The cod liver, however, was a highlight—juicy, rich, and perfectly complemented by a fresh onion topping and light dressing. For the main course, we ordered Wagyu tenderloin A5 with sea urchin roe and thin-sliced Wagyu ribeye. Unfortunately, only one portion of the tenderloin was available, and the roe was out of stock. After a brief mix-up, the staff resolved the issue by offering the tenderloin gratis. The tenderloin was melt-in-the-mouth tender, skillfully grilled by our experienced server, though I prefer bolder, wilder flavors with the sweet fattiness of Wagyu. The ribeye, thinly sliced, was exceptional—rich, fatty, and sweet, pairing beautifully with egg yolk and practically dissolving on the tongue. The beef tongue was another standout, packed with flavor and perfectly grilled.

SERVICE:

The service was generally strong, despite a minor hiccup with the tenderloin order. The staff spoke some English, which helped with ordering, and the experienced server expertly handled the grilling, elevating the experience. Dishes arrived promptly, and the staff’s quick resolution of the ordering issue left a positive impression.

VERDICT:

Gokujo Yakiniku is a fantastic yakiniku spot with an impressive selection of meats, seafood, and appetizers. For the best experience, focus on the grill offerings and skip the sushi or nigiri. The bright, lively atmosphere and high-quality ingredients make this a standout dining destination in the business mall, perfect for a fun and flavorful meal. Highly recommended for yakiniku enthusiasts!
評論3: [TungChen Wu] (2分, 2 months ago): Sorry, I don’t understand why the ratings of this restaurant are so high, really don’t. Consider the price, the food is average, the atmosphere is quite low, staffs are kind but I don’t feel any “service” at all. The rationale I use present tense is I am sitting inside for the lunch with my wife RIGHT NOW, and I cannot wait to share our confusing immediately 😶😶
評論4: [Katherine Yu] (5分, 2 months ago): Excellent Japanese BBQ place close to Taipei 101.  We didn’t make reservations so we had to wait for about 30 min.  This place has great A5 and Wagyu from different area.  We tried the Wagyu premium 2 ppl set for T¥3290.  It was superb! The set comes with Salad, rice, apps and dessert and had enough meat and seafood to fill up my son and I. The quality of the meat and seafood was great.
評論5: [Isa Charm] (5分, 5 months ago): Excellent food & service, sets comes with complimentary drinks, yoghurt drinks, appetisers, icecream desserts. The sets provide good selection of beef cuts, choice of salad & dressings. The staff may assist to bbq depending in the set selected. The ambience is nice and the restrooms are clean and well kept.

餐廳名稱: 默爾 pasta pizza(台北信義威秀店)
Google Map地址: 110台灣臺北市信義區松壽路20號2樓
評論1:
[Ryan Liu] (4分, 5 months ago): The menu appears to include a variety of items but about half were unavailable when we visited for lunch, including #2 dishes the menu recommended. Disappointing. Our waiter was friendly and attentive, but had to double-check the menu and run to the kitchen twice while taking our order, so it felt like the kitchen wasn’t sure what they could actually make. Note that there’s a 10% service charge as well.

What we ordered: Truffle chips (220 NTD) — these were excellent; Truffle Mushroom Chicken with cream (420 + 50 NTD) — I had this and it was delicious, the upgraded portion was just right for me; Stir-fried Mixed Seafood (470 + 50 NTD); and Paccheri Carbonara (370 NTD).

The food is very good, and worth the premium price. However, the inconsistent availability and what looked like poor kitchen management bring the experience down a notch. Highly recommended if you’re willing to spend a bit more, and hopefully they can improve the kitchen efficiency.
評論2:
[黃柄霖Andrew] (3分, 5 months ago): 7.5/10 Overall

I wanted the order the cabonara pork pasta, but they only have it in penne and not spaghetti. And somethings are out of stock. Unlucky for me.
For the Truffel Chicken Pasta, the taste is so mild that i cant really taste anything. But for the smell, it is amazing. 6/10
For the Bolognese Pasta, the taste is goodddddd, but the meat is too little. it has more sauce than the meat. 7.5/10

For the service though, it is over the top. Especially the male waiter that greet us, his service is amazing!! His salary needs to be raised, it is hard to find this type of person in this industry. 10/10 for his service.
09/28 Night around 8-9pm
評論3: [jodz] (5分, a year ago): Everything here is of high standard. Delicious food, great service and comfortable atmosphere. At first, they served a bun with some spread (if you ordered a pasta or a risotto). Bun was soft on the inside and nicely toasted on the outside. Good first impression. Food came and we were very surprised as we didn’t check the reviews and left satisfied and pleased. Will strongly recommend the pizza and clams. Food is great for sharing and place is great for celebrations. Thank you to the kitchen crew for the good food and thank you to the service team, very professional as well!
評論4:
[Tina Hsu] (5分, a year ago): The quality of food is good.

I highly recommend the shrimp Teppanyaki and salmon  pasta, yum yum.

You can get buy one get one free coupon in your birthday month if you join their Ocard member. However, you can’t choose different items. You have to choose the same item.
評論5:
[Gwen Mann] (5分, 7 months ago): Went to the branch at Songshan Station but couldn’t find a listing for it on google maps.

Excellent all round 👏We stuffed ourselves❤️

Google Map地址: 110台灣臺北市信義區松壽路11號2 樓
評論1: [Sophia Lek] (5分, a month ago): Nice casual dining place. Was looking for vegetarian options nearby the hotel and saw Miacucina. Portions are very generous (good for sharing between 2 smaller eaters). The pesto pasta was delicious! I wished there was more asparagus and mushrooms for the amount of noodles. Delish!
評論2:
[Culturetravel] (4分, 2 months ago): Healthy Italian meal options.
The bowl was the best. The flat bread was soggy. Salad did not look that fresh..

Friendly and attentive service.
評論3: [N Z] (5分, a month ago): One of the good vegetarian restaurants in Taipei. The service was good, the food was delicious, and the dishes were prepared quickly.
評論4: [Yvette Huang] (5分, 7 months ago): We visited the restaurant twice this month, and we all had wonderful time. I know it is inevitable that we should order the food online, which made me pretty mad. I still had to know the details about the cuisine, and the pictures in the menu did not help at all. However, the waiters were quick to help. Maybe they need to set two tracks. I am not illiterate, but I really hate to order food in such a cold way. If I have to dine alone, I will definitely cross this out, and prefer to go to a cozy little cafe instead of such “fancy” restaurant.
評論5:
[Guy] (4分, 9 months ago): Food and atmosphere were top notch. Rare finds for a western vegetarian restaurant. It is located in A11 level 2, entry within the mall. Spent a total of NTD 1500 for a burger, pizza and pasta which was rather worth it. Food were well done, could taste that it is handmade and ingredients were fresh. Highly recommend to try their burger! Non-allium options available.

Service could be improved, just not as welcoming and friendly as it were.

餐廳名稱: 隱世餐酒館(台北永春店)
Google Map地址: 110台灣臺北市信義區忠孝東路五段297號B1F
評論1: [TY He] (5分, 3 months ago): This place is unique and special. The staff and bartender are extremely nice. The drink menu is original and unique. There is live performance every day which is perfect for date night or hanging out with friends. Highly recommend for everyone. Amazing experience for touring Taipei. Only downside is it’s slightly busy and reservation is highly recommended.
評論2:
[Alice Hou] (5分, 7 months ago): Went there with my family for a Taiwanese Hokkien singer performance and some light bites.

I really liked their handmade bean curd rolls, which were filled with chives, coriander, and shrimp.

We also had the sous vide duck breast, turnip cake with salted egg yolk, and braised pork ears.

The duck breast was a bit dry and the texture was too firm. The turnip cake was good, while the pork ears were a bit too oily and salty.

We also ordered some drinks. As for cocktails, I’d say they were mediocre. I’d recommend going for hard liquor or beer instead.

The vibe was great, and the service was excellent.
評論3: [hoven liew] (1分, 3 months ago): Prices are reasonable but the food is just so-so. The waiter(see attached pic) who served us wasn’t friendly, bad service! Overall, not a very good experience. Probably won’t return.
評論4: [G H] (4分, 2 years ago): Came to this popular live music bistro. It actually feels like a nightclub with free pouring shot in your mouth. Food was surprisingly good, except for the sesame oil chicken, terrible. Service was OK, not so friendly, and over the phone was even worse. Great atmosphere and all singers that night were great!  Even though I didn't know many of their songs.
評論5: [Terence Lim] (1分, 6 months ago): Great band but terrible service. We went half an hour past our booking and they gave away our table which is understandable. They had a few empty tables but the male receptionist with shaggy hair claimed that they were all booked. We were willing to wait to see if they would be available but he said they will definitely come even though it was 11pm. Final snide remark that they do not sell standing tickets and so we left.

Google Map地址: 110台灣臺北市信義區忠孝東路五段71巷25號
評論1:
[Phil Mcclellan] (5分, 4 months ago): Was out for a walk, just looking for a drink and some light food. And a place to sit and work a little bit. I was so happy to find this place!  It was early, so it was fairly quiet which was perfect.

I ended up eating more than I had planned because  the food looked great and I couldn't decide!
I especially loved the kale salad, and fillet.  Fillet had roasted garlic  a pepper sauce, and fries on the side which was perfect.
The staff was also very friendly.  I could practice my very limited Chinese a little bit, but the staff speaks better English than I can speak Chinese, so they could always rescue me if I don't have the words!  I will add this to my list if must-gos when I am in town.
評論2: [Elaine Wu] (5分, a year ago): My husband and I decided to check out this restaurant as we were staying at the W Taipei nearby, and it was excellent!  Coming off of a week-long tour of Taiwan filled with delicious traditional food, we were craving something a bit more current.  We're so glad we stumbled upon Range Bistrokaya. As someone with Taiwanese-American heritage, I really wanted to go somewhere exciting, and this fit the bill.  Everything was cooked to perfection with some Taiwanese inspiration, and the service is impeccable.  We loved everything about it and hope others will, too.
評論3: [Kate Shao] (4分, 2 years ago): A variety of cocktails you can choose from. Including selected and tea cocktail as well as floral herb and seasonal edition. I ordered Long Island 2.0 which was full-bodied and great. I love it! Their wine menu was showed online(google album), I thought it was a little bit inconvenient. Food was tasty but small portion. Noisy and crowded environment.
評論4: [Becky Yu] (5分, 3 years ago): First time here and this is one of my favorite restaurants in Taipei! Food quality, ingredients, and service are top-notch. Highly recommended: scallop uni rice, fried oyster, truffle egg pineapple bun, fried abalone!
評論5:
[Alice Lai] (3分, 7 years ago): My cocktail tasted like juice, tho I ordered a light alcohol one.
We order 2 people set, the dishes were good>ok>what>no way! They didn’t came on with what they suppose to be, appetizer was too late, cheese snack was too early. And their main meal was not very good, they cooked the meat too much, can’t be easy to chew. Feel kinda disappointed.
However, their staffs were nice, friendly, and patient. If I have the next visit, I will never order their set meal, and maybe just grab some classical to drink.
But I’m not sure if I will give them another try.

Google Map地址: 110台灣臺北市信義區松仁路58號14樓
評論1:
[Jeny Rose Torema] (5分, a month ago): Need to have reservations to go to this restaurant especially on holiday.  Luckily I go here weekdays and reserve a sit for 1 person on the day and it has vacant. This is not a fine dining experience and expect loud atmosphere especially some costumer already drunk. They have quick activities for fun like hollahoop show. I like also how the girls waitress serve and entertain you as foreigners.  They can speak English and I don't feel discriminated here.

I ate alone with a good food and ambiance.  I reserve 22:00 and I got early in taipei so I move 21:30 in my reservations and have vacant seat for me.
評論2: [Jecel Calonia] (5分, 6 months ago): We had fun, and it’s a birthday blowout from our September babies friends. We love the ambiance since it is famous already as you can take pictures with taipei 101 from the hooters building. The foods I love is the white Pasta with mushroom, the chickens, not too with the baby back ribs since its dry and no sauce. But overall, the food is good, the view is nice, and entertainment is great. We were thankful that the rain falls heavy when we already got there anyways.
評論3: [Ladyz Buatong] (5分, a month ago): Fun American sports-bar vibe with a lively atmosphere right in Xinyi District. The menu has classic Hooters favourites — wings, burgers, pasta, salads and more — and people like coming for the cold drinks, tasty food, and great views of Taipei 101 from the outdoor terrace at night. The service is friendly and it’s a good spot for casual hangouts or group dinners.
評論4: [Kyle Mitski] (5分, a month ago): Ordered pasta and pizza and it was great, was not a big fan of the fruit beverage I ordered. I politely asked if the server could take a photo of us with the Taipei 101, but she said she’ll get back since she was really busy. Hooters Xinyi Branch could really get busy and jampacked when its near dinner time plus it was a weekend. We made an advance reservation for a 4:30 pm timeslot via their website. Eventually she came back and have taken a photo of us, I really appreciate her time and effort to do so. Regarding the reserved tables we booked, it was really chilly outside and other customers are smoking. They accept credit cards. We left even before our time limit with enough memories of Taipei 101 to get by.
評論5:
[E T] (5分, 4 weeks ago): The wings are outstanding. I don’t normally go for wings but I heard about them so had to try and I was not disappointed.

I opted for the boneless and they were so juicy and big! The sauces went well, curry garlic, cheese and bbq. Thinking about it makes me want to go back.

The atmosphere was fun and the punters were friendly and talkative. The staff also tentative and spoke English well. It was an all round good time and the views are pretty nice too, with 101 right there.

餐廳名稱: 渣男 Taiwan Bistro 信義一渣
Google Map地址: 110台灣臺北市信義區信義路五段150巷315弄12號
評論1: [Cassandra Yeung] (5分, 2 months ago): The atmosphere is amazing and the cocktails even better! All the cocktails were fusions of classic cocktails with asian (taiwanese) style flavours, long island ice tea as melon ice tea! it's honestly one of the best in this trip
評論2: [Yi Peng] (5分, 2 months ago): Very nice place. Great atmosphere, great food and great drink. One of places I like most in Taipei! Will visit again.
評論3:
[C] (2分, 2 months ago): We were pretty excited to try a bistro bar in Taiwan but unfortunately this was not a good experience.While the food was alright (average), the service was worse.

We had 7 people in our group but the whole place only had 3 other tables there. It seemed like one server was in charge of food and the other one for drinks. We made our order and waited.... and waited... some of us ordered beer so that came at a reasonable time, but that leaves them to make 3 drinks.

I believe we waited for 45 mins to an hour for our 3 drinks and only a couple of cold dishes came (i.e. pre prepped marinated dishes). It's not a great dining experience when you have to wait so long for some food/drinks and wait again for so long for more food. These were small plates too, so we were trying to eat them slowly so we wouldn't be just sitting there waiting. I think we were there for almost 2+ hours and majority of the time we were just waiting.
評論4:
[Jen Vo] (5分, 2 years ago): It’s a nice place for a catch up talk in a weekend. The foods seem to be Taiwanese style for casual drinking. Their main dishes are noodle and meat snacks. The foods taste are above average and the price is very affordable. It’s also fun to have a seat near the bar section to see how foods and drinks are prepared. The servers and the chef are also very welcoming. We even got some free drinks (the purple ones and they are not in the menu).
Highly recommend the pineapple drink :). Would come back again if there is a chance.
評論5: [Rui Zhong] (5分, a year ago): Amazing spot, chill ambience,a bit pricey, and really flavorful food. Sausage and fungus were the highlight dish. Every order comes in smallish plates. Easy to order either a paper form in English and mandarin that you can pass to a server. Cash only!

餐廳名稱: 心潮飯店｜台北微風信義店 SINCHAO RICE SHOPPE
Google Map地址: 110台灣臺北市信義區忠孝東路五段68號2樓
評論1:
[S T] (5分, 2 weeks ago): Sinchao Rice Shoppe offers a refined spin on Taiwanese comfort food without losing its roots. The pork cutlet was perfectly crisp on the outside and juicy inside, well-seasoned and not overly greasy. The scallop rice with fish roe was a highlight: sweet, tender scallops paired with the savory roe, all sitting on fragrant rice that tied everything together beautifully.

Even the tea cocktails were thoughtfully done, balanced and not overly sweet, which elevated the whole dining experience beyond just a rice shop.

The space feels modern yet warm, making it a great spot for a relaxed but quality meal in Taipei. Definitely worth a visit if you appreciate comfort food done with finesse.
評論2:
[Happy Sunny] (5分, 5 months ago): Yesterday, upon arriving in Taipei, we followed glowing online reviews and made a reservation at Sinchao Rice Shoppe, a gem of innovative Taiwanese cuisine, and it was an absolute delight! Every dish was bursting with unique flavors and creativity, showcasing a fresh take on traditional Taiwanese fare. From the perfectly balanced tastes to the vibrant presentation, each plate was a delight for both the palate and the eyes.
This place is ideal for family gatherings or catching up with friends, as the diverse menu offers something for everyone to enjoy. The only downside is the ambiance—it’s fairly simple and not particularly upscale, but the food more than makes up for it. The incredible dishes and welcoming vibe make Sinchao a must-visit. Highly recommended for anyone looking to savor exceptional Taiwanese cuisine!
評論3:
[Joey Esguerra] (5分, 4 months ago): Food – Great presentation! The taste is unique and delicious.
Service – Superb! The staff are very helpful and even offered a pillow for back support, which was such a nice touch.
Atmosphere – Gives off a luxurious vibe with a lovely view that really elevates the dining experience.

It’s definitely on the pricier side, but if you’re feeling a little boujee on your trip, this place is worth the splurge. And don’t skip the rib eye steak fried rice — it’s their best seller for a reason!
評論4:
[mmats01] (5分, a month ago): I wanted to eat something on the higher end while visiting Taipei. I had my share of street food and cheap eats, but I wanted to see what the Michelin scene was like. I looked up a few places online and realized that I've never been to a Michelin rated rice shop before so I stopped by.

The restaurant is absolutely beautiful, and it's a wonderful place to have a nice meal in.

Service here is polite and well trained. They notice the little details and details matter.

I ordered seafood shrimp cake as an appetizer and the truffle fried rice with bluefin tuna as the main. Both dishes were excellent with lots of flavor. The rice was cooked well, not too oily and the tuna was seasoned and cooked perfectly.

I would recommend this place to anyone, especially those looking for a higher end meal.
評論5:
[Michelle] (4分, 2 months ago): Came here for christmas dinner.
Ordered the famous steak, and I’d give it a 4/5 - the food was delicious, but it wasn’t hot enough. I know it would’ve tasted better if it was hotter.
The veggie was good, as were the mung bean tiramisu! Very unique tastes.

Very aesthetically pleasing interior!

餐廳名稱: 輪輪1976 和食茶酒駅/ Lun Lun1976/台北信義餐酒館/包廂餐廳
Google Map地址: 110405台灣臺北市信義區松壽路11號3樓
評論1: [XO Handsome] (4分, 4 months ago): Lun Lun 1976 is one of the many restaurants in Xinyi SKM Mall A11, Taipei.  Online reservation is straightforward.  This Japanese restaurant stands out with its train-like façade and interior design.  The private cabins with different minimum charges are just as IG-able.  The minimum charge of the cabin which can accommodate up to 8 guests is NT$ 6,000 for lunch on a weekday.  Servers are patient and efficient.  Food is reasonably priced and of good quality.  Highly recommend the crispy Japanese-Style Deep Fried Chicken (NT$ 260), the refreshing Cold Beef Noodles with Sukiyaki Style (NT$ 320), and the flavorful Japanese-Style Taraba Crab Congee (NT$ 1,080) which is only available in October and November.
評論2: [Florence Hung] (3分, 3 years ago): Love the flavors and the aesthetic of the restaurant. Their servers are very kind and their service is fast, however, the cooking is a slow. Their portions are small and some dishes are on the saltier side. This restaurant is definitely pricey and their minimum charge is one drink per person, so in addition to the meal, you must order drinks. Aside from everything, I had a good time here :)
評論3:
[Johnny Wang] (3分, 3 years ago): I'm not sure I'd consider their food necessarily Japanese, but it's certainly Japanese-influenced/centric, with staples like sashimi to fusion dishes like mentaiko gratin. With that said, very few dishes stood out to me aside from the gratin and their pork soup: not that anything was bad, but more that they were largely unremarkable.

Many of the dishes are also presented with seemingly unnecessary or out-of-place flair, but given the nice ambiance and vibe of the space, I suspect they're catering to the IG crowd. The old school railway aesthetic is, admittedly, rather eye-catching.
評論4: [Tiago Afonso] (4分, a year ago): Great little “studio gibhli” train vibe restaurant. 5 star staff and decor. Nice food. Couple of set menus for two people. Interesting twist to some dishes, overall would repeat and recommend if you like to try something different.
評論5: [A H] (5分, 2 years ago): They served very generous portion of food. This Japanese fusion cuisine is very tasty and special.

餐廳名稱: 饗食天堂 台北信義店
Google Map地址: 110台灣臺北市信義區松壽路12號ATT 4 FUN百貨6樓
評論1: [ABXY] (5分, a month ago): We booked the Eatogether Xinyi Restaurant buffet for New Year celebrations and hoped to see the fireworks from 101. However, our table could not see the view, but it was okay as we wanted to avoid the crowd. We arrived at the restaurant quite early because some roads were blocked. The reception staff was not very helpful, maybe she was tired. The lobster was served individually and the food was fresh. I really enjoyed the salmon. Even though there was a long queue, everything moved quite fast. Most of the staff were nice and cleared plates quickly. After dinner, we walked down and could see the fireworks very clearly. It was beautiful and enjoyable. 😊
評論2: [Cherish .K] (5分, 2 months ago): This is my second time going to this buffet, I went to the one in q square near Taipei main for the first time but xinyi own had a bit more of a selection and it was great.
評論3: [Riza Cabrera] (5分, 12 months ago): It’s one of the best value-for-money buffet I’ve tried Taiwan A variety of choices and dishes to enjoy. The atmosphere is lively, the environment is clean, and the chefs are very friendly. With such a wide selection of food and drinks.
評論4: [JungJung Chang] (5分, a year ago): My favorite buffet with a variety of choices and dishes to enjoy. The atmosphere is lively, the environment is clean, and the chefs are very friendly. With such a wide selection of food, I can always find something I love. It’s the best value-for-money buffet in Taiwan!
評論5:
[Paul Yang] (5分, 7 months ago): Quite a lot of food.  Very high quality.
Get the clam soup, prime rib, egg tarts, and chocolate fountain dipped fruit and cracker sticks

Sashimi was great

Afternoon tea price at 2:30 pm was 700 + 10%.  They promptly close at 4:30 pm so the workers can eat

Google Map地址: 110台灣臺北市信義區市府路45號4樓
評論1:
[Kristine Orias] (5分, 3 months ago): First time dining here and I thought we needed a reservation to dine. They allow walk-ins and the waiting time is actually fast.

Food was super good, and the service was really fast and they were able to attend to our every need/request despite the volume of customers. They even suggested better food combinations when we place our orders.

Overall, we had a really good time and will definitely come visit again when we come back to Taipei.
評論2:
[Samo Muránsky] (5分, 3 months ago): After some time in Taiwan I felt like having a taste of home, so I stopped here for lunch. The place markets itself as American restaurant, but the menu is quite heavy on Italian food and I can say this is probably as close as you can get to a proper Italian experience in Taiwan. I arrived at lunchtime without reservation but was able to get a seat at one of the high tables (which I prefer anyway) despite the place being fully booked. The key seems to be to arrive right when they open.

Burrata starter was spot on, rich and well-balanced flavour. Pappardelle pasta was also very good, but the sauce was a bit too mild in taste (the taste was on a creamy side, it lacked the meaty flavour and could use more seasoning/spice) - still a very pleasant dish but could be improved a bit. Cheesecake was absolutely delicious and I highly recommend to not skip it :)

Service was polite and pleasant. Service style is local, so expect the main to arrive either alongside your starter or shortly after. Check is also brought proactively without asking but you can still order more stuff and they will just reissue it.

Overall a great experience and I highly recommend this place if you're ever in need of some European food in Taipei. The lunch set me back 40€ (three courses + a large bottle of San Pellegrino) which is very reasonable for a restaurant of this type at such an exclusive location.
評論3: [Tuong Lam] (5分, 3 months ago): Food is really good and high quality. The drinks are all impressive too. Wine options are good too. Pizza is phenomenal according to my husband. Get here early tho or they will run out of stuff. All employees speaks fluent English. Nice and quiet option away from the bustling food court on a weekend
評論4:
[Deborah Reyes] (5分, 3 months ago): We ordered the linguine - it was ok, eggplant appetizer - great choice,
Thai salad was good, the cheese pizza was good - crust was nice and toasted, but the roast chicken took the prize!  It is rare to have chicken breast that is fully cooked yet, juicy! This dish alone is worth going back for.  Mall 101, 4th floor!
評論5:
[Jony] (5分, a month ago): I’ve eaten at Salt & Stone for at least 15 times now. The food is always consistent, fresh and really delicious. The service has never missed a beat. The staff are friendly and attentive and always willing to help.

However my last trip went way beyond my expectations! I took my family here for a Valentines/pre-CNY meal.

We ordered a lot of the great food we’ve always gotten but this time decided to try the lamb chops for the first time. The waitress told us it would take an hour to be prepared which we were comfortable with.

The lamb ended up taking longer than expected, which we actually didn’t mind since we were enjoying our wine and our chat. However the staff were so courteous, giving us updates and even providing a dessert as a show of gratitude for us waiting.

In the end we got our lamb chops which were so good! Tender, juicy and tasted really good without the gamey taste that you sometimes get with lamb. Worth the wait 😁

After we paid and were walking away one of the staff chased after us with a bag filled with amazing pastries. She wished us happy Valentine’s Day, happy CNY and thanked us again for our patience. It was one last surprise that made the experience even more unforgettable.

Thank you S&S staff that was working on Valentines Day 2026!

Google Map地址: 110台灣臺北市信義區信義路五段122號
評論1: [Alex J. Lin] (2分, 2 months ago): Pineapple fried rice has very little taste. Chicken satay is ok. No need reservation on a Friday dinner. Not a lot of people
評論2:
[Marcel Jelke Vercruysse] (1分, a month ago): The Massaman curry beef contained extremely low-quality meat that was very tough, and the sweet potato was completely dried out, almost like cardboard. The sauce did not taste fresh at all.

The Tom Kha Gai (chicken) soup was overly sour with a very strange flavor. The chicken in the soup was inedible, extremely tough (see photo).

The only dish that tasted good was the stir-fried pork with kaphrao.
The rice we were served was also very dry, with some grains being hard. Overall, a very disappointing experience.
評論3:
[Grace Chung] (1分, a year ago): When it comes to celebrating special occasions, especially birthdays, we expect an experience that is memorable for all the right reasons. Unfortunately, our recent visit here did not live up to those expectations and left us feeling quite disappointed.
From the moment we arrived, the enthusiasm of the occasion was overshadowed by the quality of the food. We ordered the green curry pork, anticipating a fresh and flavorful dish, but what we received was anything but. The pork tasted old and gamey, which prompted us to express our concerns to the waitress. To our disbelief, she returned to inform us that the chef claimed there was nothing wrong with it, stating that this was simply how the pork should taste. It was hard to believe that the chef could be so disconnected from the expectations of fresh, flavorful cuisine.
Things didn’t improve when we switched to chicken, which also arrived with an unappealing gamey flavor. All dishes, including the pork meatball soup, failed to impress and lacked the authenticity we were expecting. After several underwhelming unauthentic dishes, we decided it was best to simply refrain from raising our concerns with the staff any further.
To add to our frustration, this was meant to be a celebratory gathering. Prior to our visit, we had called to inquire about a complimentary birthday cake. The staff assured us that they could prepare one, provided they checked the birthday person's ID. However, when the time came to remind them about the cake, we encountered further issues. After waiting over an hour, we were finally presented with a plain chiffon cake— devoid of any candles or festive decorations. It felt like an afterthought rather than the celebration we had envisioned. If the restaurant was unable to provide a complimentary cake, we would have appreciated honesty during our initial inquiry. Yet, they insisted on checking the ID for a cake that was subpar in quality. They didn't ask for id when the cake was presented and I guess they didn't bother anymore for what they had offered.
Overall, our experience here was disappointing, particularly in light of the rave reviews we had read. The food lacked freshness and authenticity, and the service fell short of basic expectations. We left feeling offended and discouraged, with a resolute decision that we will not return.
If you’re looking for a place to celebrate, I recommend you stay away from Pikul. There are countless other restaurants that will provide not just good food, but an overall enjoyable experience
評論4: [Kai Yin Wang] (5分, 2 years ago): we have one of most impressive eating experience at this place. The atmosphere is amazingly relax and pleasant. We had both dinner and lunch here, the dishes are distinguish delicious. Curry soft shell crab, curry  pork and green papaya salad are all very very tasty. Also I had the best chicken wing which I will order every time. Manager Mr Lu gives wonderful service and he told us that he will add 30 more new dishes including green curry beef which is my favorite starting this March. I highly recommend this place for romantic gathering, friends or family gathering.
評論5: [C Neon] (4分, a year ago): This is a traditional Thai restaurant that serves many of the favorites such as curry, salads, desserts, and a few chef specials.   The dishes are tasty as good.  Presentation of the dishes are also well thought thru.   Overall we had a good experience

餐廳名稱: JK STUDIO 法式餐酒館 (台北信義店)｜預約制
Google Map地址: 110台灣臺北市信義區基隆路一段147巷5弄13號
評論1: [James Ng] (5分, 7 months ago): JK STUDIO dining experience is really first-class! The restaurant environment is elegant , and the lights are warm and comfortable, which is perfect for dating or celebrations. The food is exquisitely arranged and rich in flavors. From the appetizer to the main course, you can feel the chef’s thoughtfulness. The waiter is polite, so that you can enjoy your dinner easily. It‘s really worth recommending! ✨
評論2:
[lemon] (5分, a year ago): Enjoyed this meal!

For starters, I recommend the butter and bread, the bread is nice and crispy. The potato and clam chowder puff pastry is thick and flavorful, highly recommend.
For the main course, I recommend the linguine lobster, I really liked the flavors.

The only disappointment was the Lamb and Port wine berry sauce. The sauce itself was good, but the lamb meat was not as tender as I had expected it to be.

Also the desserts were delicious. I ordered the crème brûlée and chocolate cake. Both were absolutely delicious.
評論3:
[Debo Wang] (5分, a year ago): I came here with a group of three for dinner at 7 PM on a Saturday night. We were able to make reservations for dinner via their website the day of around a few hours beforehand.

As soon as we arrived, we were taken directly to our seat. The service throughout the meal was wonderful and they were very attentive.

We ordered a few appetizers to share:

-ESCARGOT: Nice and tasty. It came with toasted bread.

-FRENCH ONION SOUP: Definitely not authentic French onion soup. It tasted like Taiwanese fusion. It was ok, just a bit bland.

For my main I had the Boeuf Bourguignon as a combo meal. The combo came with salad, soup, bread and drink. All were very tasty.  The bread was nice and warm and the soup was a good creamy mushroom soup. The main was well cooked in a good sauce.

All in all, a great dinner option in the area that is a decent price for what you get as well!
評論4:
[Jennifer Fu] (5分, 8 years ago): What a pleasant surprise! We were afraid that we wouldn't be able to find a decent Christmas dinning place because we booked so last minute, but JK far exceeded our expectation! I must say that the salmon spinach ravioli was the best ravioli I've ever had and all of the meats we had were juicy, tender, and absolutely flavorful. We did have to wait a little because the table before us took a while, but the restaurant was kind enough to extend their working hours by 30 minutes so we didn't have to rush through our meal. Note that if you order steaks or any type of meat, it'll take a bit longer than the pasta or risotto dishes!

100% would come back again!
評論5: [Yvonne Liu] (3分, 5 years ago): The food in here is acceptable based on their price. I expect the food is a little bit better, but it’s not bad at all. The service is friendly and not too difficult to make a reservation. The wine in here looks good, unfortunately we didn’t try. I think maybe I will update the rating if I try some wine in here next time.

餐廳名稱: SHANN SHANN 小香 台北三越信義店
Google Map地址: 110台灣臺北市信義區松壽路9號6樓
評論1: [Angel Marie Isidro] (5分, a month ago): 🇵🇭A flavorful and well-balanced meal with bold seasoning and fresh ingredients. The crispy fish was the highlight, with great texture and a nice citrus kick. The stir-fries were aromatic and paired perfectly with rice. Generous portions and perfect for sharing — would definitely return😊
評論2:
[Critsy Y] (4分, 3 months ago): This spot serves solid Taiwanese comfort dishes, but in a more “posh” mall setting. Think comfy booth seating, polished decor, and service that’s totally on point. Yes, you’re definitely paying a premium for the environment—you can find the same classics for a fraction of the price at a tiny street shop—but honestly, I’ll take air-conditioning and comfortable seats over sweating it out any day.

The food itself is flavorful and well executed, exactly what you’d expect. It’s the perfect place to pop into while you’re already shopping, though probably not a place I’d go out of my way to seek out. And their “fancy” take on fatty pork rice with a little abalone on top? I’m loving it. :D
評論3: [Helen Chow] (3分, 3 weeks ago): Came on the valentine’s day, food portion is quite right for 2 people. A mordern version of local food, yet we prefer the traditional ones are way better. a few guests complained their dishes are too salty , and my egg cake came very sweet. the restaurant  also offers a wide range of wine.
評論4: [Gari Acolola] (5分, a year ago): Hands-down one of the best gastronomic experiences of my entire life. Shann Shann is a MUST when it comes to Taiwanese cuisine. Each dish we ordered was 11/10. The taste was unique and remarkable. Service is also excellent!!! Food was served really quickly. Our server Ruby was one of the best I've ever encountered. She was very alert and enthusiastic attending to us, and was very helpful sharing with us what to eat. She is truly a delight. We couldn't decide which dish we liked most because the whole meal was A+! We ordered the braised pork rice with abalone, steamed egg with shrimp and black truffle, sliced beef with ginger and onions, and bean curd roll stuffed with shrimp, and sugarcane oolong tea for drinks. Truly a restaurant worth coming back to.
評論5: [C Chang] (4分, a year ago): Pretty comfy dining space and good service. Waiters are attentive and pay attention to details, food is decent too. Not big portion and goos for small group.

Google Map地址: 110台灣臺北市信義區松壽路12號4 樓
評論1: [Maswassa Janchua] (5分, a year ago): Good korean resto in town. Has many yummy recipes. Perfect for group.
評論2: [Aleksandra] (5分, a year ago): Delicious rose dakgalbi, rice roll with radish and cheese was awesome. Half portion was more than enough for two people ✌️
評論3: [stephanie kao] (5分, a year ago): It’s very nice food and cheese love the most ! Rex Spicy Chicken is main recommend
評論4: [Joelle Mbatchou] (3分, 10 months ago): Had the rose creamy chicken which is promoted as a popular dish. Too creamy for me and the noodles were so hard to swallow. I had the half portion and that would have been plenty for 2 people.
評論5: [Michelle Natasha] (4分, a year ago): food is good, very authentic! must try

Google Map地址: 110台灣臺北市信義區逸仙路26巷17號
評論1:
[Olga Maevskaia] (4分, 5 months ago): Visited in September 2025.
Taiwan 🇹🇼 Taipei.

Reminiscent of an American-style bar, 30 Thirty boasts a relaxed ambiance with dark tones and wooden furniture, creating a laid-back yet inviting atmosphere. We chose this spot for its early 11 AM opening, making it perfect for a leisurely brunch.

FOOD:

The menu leans toward European cuisine with subtle Asian influences, offering a variety of wine-friendly appetizers. Since we weren’t too hungry, we opted for their highly praised squid with mentaiko. The squid was tender and perfectly cooked, while the mentaiko inside added a slightly crunchy, juicy burst of flavor. The spices were well-balanced, and the dish was an absolute delight, leaving a strong impression.

SERVICE:

The service was friendly and attentive, with our dish arriving in about 20 minutes, making for a smooth and pleasant experience.

VERDICT:

30 Thirty is a fantastic spot with a cool, relaxed vibe, though it might get noisy and crowded in the evenings. While we only tried one dish, the quality was impressive, and it left us eager to return for more. This restaurant is a great pick for a chill meal with flavorful, well-executed dishes. Highly recommend giving it a try!
評論2:
[Samuel Bostrom] (2分, a month ago): Takes at least 30 minutes for each dish to come out (hence the name). We ordered separate dishes so we were sitting around for over an hour waiting then watching one person eat then repeat. Ended up leaving before the last person got his meal because they "lost" his order.
Food was okay, aesthetic but overpriced. I ordered a mascarpone toast for brunch which also came with prosciutto and fruit (weird combo but my fault).
The waitress gave us 2 menus for 4 people, told us she'd bring more then forgot.
Don't come if you dont have a reservation and don't order separately.
評論3:
[Artemis Han] (5分, 3 years ago): The food was delicious. We were surprised to see whiskey bone marrow on the menu, and it didn't disappoint. The garlic bread goes very well with the dish. I also love the teppanyaki chocolate cake.
The service can be a little slow during busy hours. We enjoyed our dinner here.
評論4:
[Chengkang Lee (Ethan)] (4分, 5 years ago): The bistro was not fully occupied during our visit in the afternoon, so the overall experience was great, in terms of service, vibe, and food quality.
We ordered dishes intermittently, and as it's a friend reunion, we didn't find the service time too long to be endured.
The cocktail was good as well as the beer.
評論5: [9emy L] (4分, a year ago): Fairly speaking the cuisine here is great - you taste the ingredients- they are fresh, tasty, spot on seasoning - probably only the dessert 🍮 pudding one disappointed me - it was relatively small but pricy - forgot to take a photo. It was a 8/10 meal 👌

餐廳名稱: INPARADISE 饗饗 微風信義店
Google Map地址: 110台灣台北市信义区忠孝東路五段68號46樓微風信義
評論1: [othniel wang] (5分, 3 months ago): I love how the place has such high ceiling and warming decorations, the food was amazing from the Chinese cuisine to the European cuisine. Not to mention their salmon sashimi melt in your mouth great choice and quality of fish along side with other sashimis as well. It was a great experience the creamy pasta show inside the parmesan cheese wheel. Definitely was a great experience was just a little upset didn't get the window view, the reception said it was full turns out there are some empty spots left just perfect for 2 persons. Overall the food was not to doubt and the desert is not too sweet light for closing the night.
評論2: [Dave Chen] (5分, 2 months ago): A last minute reservation resulted us having some of the poorest seats in the restaurant, but still a five star experience. We got there at 5:30p and our window was until 9:30p. Amazing! Food was fresh and excellent, service was frequent, views were immense and satisfying. Worth the price of admission for the food, time, views, and service. Would definitely visit again.
評論3: [Ada Chiu] (4分, a month ago): We visited for Lunar New Year Eve lunch and the food selection was great. High quality food and beverages. Dining time was 11:30am-2pm. The layout of the restaurant is unconventional, with buffet areas separated into 3 different sides.
評論4:
[TAN KC] (5分, 4 weeks ago): Buffet - International

Just at ar breeze top floor. A upclass buffet. Big and has a huge spread.

Managed to get in. Spread is great. Varieties is big. Had a great time there. Service is great.

Yes celebrated a birthday there. :)
評論5:
[Cery and Jennifer Coker] (5分, 5 months ago): Came for family reunion with 19 people. We booked our reservation using app "klook" and pre paid

Reservation based with time slots and limited to 2 hours - arrived early since the clock ticking on time on the dot

Great choices of good selections. Mostly arr Japanese styles of food. Sushi, seafood, grill lamb, few different Chinese style dishes and can't left out the deserts

We sat where we got a greta view of 101 building

Google Map地址: 110台灣臺北市信義區忠孝東路五段139號2樓
評論1: [Christen Johansen] (5分, a month ago): Just a short walk from Taipei City Hall Station you will find this small, but excellent steak house. The service is efficient and friendly. The meat cooked to perfection. Not sure about that big TV screens showing sporting events, but the volume was set very low, so it didn’t affect the overall experience that much.
評論2: [Vannessa Adrienne] (3分, 2 months ago): A stylish steakhouse in Xinyi with well-grilled, juicy steaks and a warm, wood-toned interior — perfect for date nights or special dinners. The service is friendly and attentive. Great choice if you’re craving quality Western cuisine near City Hall.
評論3: [Chelsea Yong] (5分, 6 months ago): It was as a short walk from W Hotel Taipei, easy to access to the restaurant. What I love about this restaurant is the ambiance and food portions are fantastic. All foods are delicious except one thing, the food server tends to rush and they don’t smile, the guest servers are great. Perhaps need to train the food servers on hospitality skills. Overall experience is pleasant.
評論4: [Kenna Marcelo] (5分, 7 months ago): The service is great and very welcoming. We were led to our table and given an English menu. The staff spoke in great English which they didn’t have to but is greatly appreciated. The steak was the perfect size and quality. It was perfectly cooked. The hokkaido scallops were delicious. They even cut open my lobster for me! Oh and the bread and truffle butter… yum!
評論5: [Andrew Ho] (5分, 4 months ago): Excellent food and atmosphere. Service is up-to-mark. If you want excellent western food in Taipei, this is the place to visit.

餐廳名稱: 稻舍食館 微風信義店
Google Map地址: 110台灣臺北市信義區忠孝東路五段68號4 樓
評論1:
[raymond ho] (4分, a month ago): I came to this restaurant everytime I visit taipei.
The menu dont have a lot of selection. They mainly sell set meal which I think is value for $.
The main thing to eat here is the 猪油拌饭 (pork lard with rice).
They recommended to swirl the warm pork lard 2 rounds on the rice and 1 round of light soy sauce in the rice, mixed and eat it.
I normally swirl a lot more, lol.
The 红烧肉 (braised pork in dark soy sauce, in the pic) is a must try. It's soft and the skin melts in your mouth. You can feel the collagen sticking on your lips.
After your meal, order a pot of oolong tea and enjoy it with you friend.
評論2: [pcm lim] (5分, 4 months ago): We stumble upon this restaurant when visiting breeze. Love the food. Not much difference from Singapore style cooking, may be we copued from Taiwan. Food presentation is good.  The pork oil on the rice is delicious. Service is good.
評論3:
[Dexter C] (5分, 4 months ago): Decided to try this place near our hotel as our last lunch in Taipei.

Surprisingly, the food is good and very filling. We had the black date roast chicken and the roast pork set.

Also found the osmanthus dongding oolong tea to be so good that we bought some back home (had to stuff them into the luggage last min).

Lunch crowd from office can filled up the restaurant. Advise to do reservation.
評論4:
[Sheng Yang] (4分, 9 months ago): Salty egg fried shrimp is great.
Rice is great, with lard and soy sauce. Simple and delicious.
Like all the small dishes too, especially the tofu.
Mullet roe cocktail has been cooked to perfection. Best way to enjoy mullet roe so far.
Tried braised pork, it’s also great.
Will definitely come back.
評論5:
[Richard Chen] (5分, 2 years ago): Very authentic traditional Taiwan cooking. Best part is the soups and braised foods. They say to pour 2 circles of pork oil and 1 circle of soy sauce on the rice, but you should double it to really taste the flavor. The chopped fruit yogurt took away from the fried dough. It would taste great on the side of ice cream or on top of snow ice. Best dessert is the turtle made of peanut dough, chewy and sweet and savory.

I don't like how everyone is transitioning to digital orders, but at least this one's workable unlike others who glitch out.

Google Map地址: 4樓, No. 11號松高路信義區臺北市台灣 11073
評論1:
[HL T] (5分, 5 months ago): We stumbled upon this restaurant while on holiday in Taipei and decided to give it a try for lunch. With only two of us, the waiter suggested the tasting menu for two, which proved to be an excellent choice. The food arrived promptly, and we were treated to a delightful culinary journey.

The dishes were expertly prepared, with standout options including the coffee pork ribs, which were fall-off-the-bone tender, and the rich, flavorful curry chicken with soft, juicy chicken thighs. The eggplant stir-fried with minced pork and chili was also a hit. While the Thai-style green papaya salad was a slight letdown due to the papaya being slightly underripe and hard, it didn't detract from the overall experience.

The set came with a unique and refreshing bottle of oolong tea infused with carbonated water, which was a lovely touch. What truly elevated our dining experience, however, was the exceptional service. The wait staff were attentive, checking in frequently to ensure everything met our expectations. The service was prompt and efficient, adding to the overall positive experience.

Overall, we highly recommend this restaurant for its delicious food, unique offerings, and top-notch service. It's definitely worth a visit!
評論2: [Jessica] (4分, 2 months ago): Overall, positive experience! Initially I was disappointed because the three dishes we specifically went for weren’t available (beef, fish, and 肉骨茶. They said it was a quality issue from upper management. We were quite happy with what we ordered. Everything was tasty and service was good. Would go back!
評論3: [Serena HENG] (5分, 7 months ago): only waited for about 10-15 mins for a spot, atmosphere and decor in the restaurant looks super unique, looks like a european restaurant! service was also rrly good despite many people during rush hour. the food is really good and unique and price point was kinda okay! (2259 NTD for 7 dishes), free drink for a review tho! would come here again!
評論4:
[Audrey Clara 陳依青] (4分, 3 months ago): We ordered six dishes, most of which are in the top 10 favorites.

Our favorite was the Northern Thai fried fish. It was really delicious! The sauce had a nice balance of tangy and spicy, making it perfect to eat with rice.

However, the coffee ribs, which are a specialty at the Nanshan branch, didn’t suit me or my friends. The combination of coffee and ribs didn’t work well for us, and the coffee flavor was too overpowering—tasting more like honey coffee.

The chicken curry and water spinach were just okay, nothing special.

For the rice, the turmeric rice was so fragrant 👍
評論5: [Tang Jessica] (5分, 6 months ago): Good service & delicious food.

餐廳名稱: GYUU NIKU ステーキ專門店

評論1: [Don Stuart] (5分, a week ago): Ok so boom.  Here we are. Sitting down. No wait. They serve some nice little welcoming drink and snack.  And then averil Levine complicated comes on. Vibes. And then we order our food off the QR code. Not a big fan of QR codes. But the food was proper. The meat lava thing was pure buss down. 評論2:
[AperolSpritz J] (5分, a month ago): We arrived around 8pm on a Friday evening. The place was busy. Luckily we did not have to wait long to be seated.
The Japanese styled restaurant serves mostly rice bowl(丼）with a focus on beef dishes. Deep fried side dishes are also available.
Food is delicious and substantial. Price is surprisingly affordable. Drink menu is simple with a few beers and umeshu choices.

The only complaint was that our order took a long time, 30 minutes to be precise. I cannot imagine combing here solo and having to wait half an hour for food by myself.
評論3:
[Jim Wong] (5分, 2 months ago): After a long day of shopping in Xinyi, my college-age daughters and I were pretty tired and just wanted a quiet neighborhood place away from all the crowds. We were craving Japanese food, but not sushi, and were really glad we found this spot.

The food was really good and hit the spot, and the place had a calm, relaxed vibe that made it easy to just sit and unwind. The staff were friendly and patient, and even though we don’t speak Mandarin, they were still super helpful and made us feel comfortable.

It was exactly what we needed after a busy day, and we’d definitely come back.
評論4:
[Janine] (5分, 12 months ago): Came here for the first time today. It’s cool to see them doing everything by the bar - from ingredient preparations, cutting meats, to cooking.

We had the oxtail soup, the volcano protein don, and the fried steak fillet buridon. I personally think that there are lots of other great places for the first two, but the fried steak fillet here was good! Excited to come back to try their other things like the steak buridon with fish roe and egg 黒門市場丼, the ox tongue buridon 鹽蔥牛舌丼, as well as, their fried food, sides, and plum wine. Looking forward!!!

We just walked over, so we didn’t really have to go out of our way and it was convenient. It’s a bit sinful, but all of the rice bowls were nice “comfort food” to have. If you’re around the area, it’s a good place for a good meal.
評論5: [Terrence Wu] (5分, 8 months ago): Fantastic gyudon with great quality meat. We even enjoyed their fish! It is a bit smoky in there for those who are more sensitive to it, but overall a fantastic restaurant.

餐廳名稱: 欣葉台菜 信義新天地A9店
Google Map地址: 110台灣臺北市信義區松壽路9號8樓
評論1:
[Yang T.C.] (5分, 3 weeks ago): If Shin Yeh Taiwanese Cuisine did not exist, something essential in Taiwan’s culinary landscape would feel missing. This is the kind of food you continue to crave even when you are abroad—deeply comforting, unmistakably local, and rich with tradition. The A9 branch at Shin Kong Mitsukoshi Xinyi Place offers an especially refined experience. The restaurant serves a wide array of classic Taiwanese dishes, from time-honored banquet favorites to beloved home-style specialties, all prepared with consistency and care.
The atmosphere is elegant yet welcoming, making it suitable for both family gatherings and business meals. Compared with other Shin Yeh locations in Taipei, this branch feels the most polished and comfortable, with spacious seating and attentive service that never feels intrusive. It manages to preserve the warmth of traditional Taiwanese dining while presenting it in a modern, sophisticated setting.
For visitors who want to understand authentic Taiwanese cuisine—or for locals seeking a taste of home at its best—this location stands out as one of the finest representations of Shin Yeh’s legacy.
評論2:
[Marie C] (4分, 2 months ago): Located in A9 of Mitsukoshi Shinyi, it was a surprisingly slow night here. Am always used to find them crowded.

Our old time favorites from their original location along Lin Shen Road are all here, the steamed minced pork with salted egg yolk, fresh not fried spring rolls, the tenderest beef pan fried with sate sauce on a bed of spinach, and the yummiest fried sesame balls.

I only gave a 4 for food because the steamed pork had gristle and the almond jelly had too much soup tonight and not the usual amount of jelly and fruits.

The 4 star rating for atmosphere is because there was a very noisy private party going on in the private room and we were seated right outside. There were many empty tables so a wiser decision would have been to seat guests farther away. But it is a small matter and we didn’t ask to transfer, all in all a small blip and glitch in a good dinner experience.

Will I come again? Of course yes. Clean, lovely, elegant atmosphere with excellent Taiwanese cuisine and great service.

Wide menu with pictures. Fine dining dishes as well as home cooking, and snacks like fresh spring rolls (these bear no resemblance to the fried finger sized spring rolls; they are not fried and the size is around the circumference of a man’s wrist and about 5 inches in length) and cuapao (Chinese sandwich with braised pork belly garnished with cilantro, crushed sweet peanuts on a steamed rectangular bun - also known as Taiwanese burger) are also on the menu.

We spent over $1000 per person because of the steamed crab in glutinous rice and the Buddha jumped over the fence soup, otherwise a simpler meal can be had for much less than Nt$500/person is easily doable here considering the wide menu range.

Wheelchair accessibility is also good with elevator access.
評論3: [Sundeep Sikka] (5分, a month ago): Attended a fancy business dinner so didn't snap many photos. The service was top-notch and the Taiwanese cuisine was truly authentic. Everything was exceptional. This was my first time here, and I've heard that this restaurant is a must-visit for genuine local food.
評論4: [Cinee C.] (5分, 2 months ago): Our must go to restaurant every time we visit Taipei. Authentic traditional food with good service and ambiance. Service can be slow at times when it gets busy. Fredh ingredients. Menu on the pricer side but worth it for the view when booking private room.
評論5: [Dim Sum] (3分, 3 months ago): Shin Yeh is a long-standing Taiwanese institution, though the food now feels somewhat commercial, like a casual dining chain. We visited the Shin Kong Mitsukoshi A9 outlet, which gets busy at dinner. The food was below the standard of the main restaurant. The Shin Kong Mitsukoshi A9 outlet is located in the CBD and it is very popular, so booking ahead is recommended.

餐廳名稱: 星期五美式餐廳 信義餐廳

評論1:
[Hayley] (2分, a month ago): I rarely post negative reviews, but this experience at TGI FRIDAYS Xinyi during Chinese New Year Eve fell significantly short of professional standards. While the frontline staff seemed to be doing their best, the management of the evening was poor.


Logistical Failures: Despite an 8:45 PM booking, we waited in the cold while the staff struggled to manage pre-booked vs. walk-in guests. We opted for outdoor seating to save time, but the area was poorly maintained—covered in plastic with heaters that were never turned on.

Poor Value for Money: The food quality did not justify the price point. Garlic Veggie Pasta ($340 NTD): One of the few vegetarian options; it arrived excessively salty and garlicky. Caribbean Seafood Rice ($580 NTD): Plain and underwhelming compared to the menu presentation.

Hygiene Standards: The facilities were unacceptable. The ladies' restroom faucets were non-functional, and the bins were overflowing with waste. Being redirected to an employee bathroom is not the standard expected for a flagship location.

Environment: We ate in the cold next to uncleaned tables piled with dirty plates from previous guests.

I value transparency and hope this feedback serves as a necessary call for management to better prepare for high-traffic holidays. This was not the standard of service or quality I associate with the brand.
評論2: [Nick ten Cate] (5分, a week ago): Great food and quick service. Baseball game was on and the vibe was very good. Everybody cheering while enjoying the nachos and chicken fingers.

Google Map地址: 110台灣臺北市信義區松高路19號6樓
評論1:
[JC] (3分, 2 months ago): This place clearly knows how to play the high-end Japanese dining game, the kind where tweezers, smoke, and dramatic plating do most of the talking.

The setting is pleasant and polished, but don’t expect a jaw-drop moment. It’s nice… just not pause-and-take-a-photo-of-the-room nice. The real story is the fixed menu, which turned out to be a surprise, not necessarily the good kind. We were served a parade of meat cuts I would probably never order on my own, each one prepared with impressive technique, precision, and a lot of theatrical flair.

And to be fair, the chefs work hard. Every piece of meat has its own method, timing, and ritual. You can feel the effort and craftsmanship. But somewhere between the camera-ready presentation and the last bite, the flavors didn’t quite keep up with the visuals. This is one of those meals where your phone eats better than you do.

Service, however, was genuinely excellent! Attentive, warm, and professional without being stiff. That part deserves real credit.

Prices sit firmly at the high end, which would be easier to justify if the meat itself felt more special or indulgent. As it stands, it felt like paying premium prices for an experience that prioritizes concept and aesthetics over taste.

Would I recommend it? Honestly… I’m on the fence. If you love culinary performances and Instagram moments, you might walk out happy. If you’re chasing unforgettable flavors, this one may leave you wondering what all the hype was about.
評論2: [dashu oz] (4分, 2 months ago): Staff are very hospitable, classic japanese woody decore that justify the price range. Wagyu beef quality is no doubt very good. However, pieces consumed seemed too expensive. Value for money wise, many choices in the Xinyi shopping precinct.

評論1:
[Man-Chen Kao] (5分, 2 years ago): It’s a gem in Xinyi District, Taipei. The staff is courteous and food is beautifully presented.
Not to mention that their NT$1380 teppan course offers A5 wagyu steak and lobster. Very impressive. Definitely will come back❤️
評論2: [Ho Alvin] (5分, a year ago): **Good value for quality** – the ability to enjoy both seafood and premium beef at this price point is truly amazing.
評論3:
[郭杰] (5分, a year ago): In the vibrant heart of Taipei's East District lies a hidden gem that has taken the culinary scene by storm - 吾界
Teppanyaki. This newly opened no-menu teppanyaki restaurant offers a dining experience like no other, exceeding all
expectations with its exceptional quality and value.
At 吾界Teppanyaki, the emphasis is on high CP value teppanyaki that truly impresses. From the exquisite A5 Wagyu
beef imported from Miyazaki, live lobsters, South African abalones, to the freshly delivered Longdan grouper from
Penghu, every ingredient is carefully selected to ensure a premium dining experience. What sets this restaurant apart
is not just the quality of the ingredients, but also the impeccable freshness and taste that elevate each dish to new
heights.
Led by a masterful chef with exceptional skills, the culinary journey at 吾界Teppanyaki unfolds with a delightful ten-
course meal that leaves diners fully satisfied. Priced at just $1380 per person (plus 10% service charge), the value for
money is unbeatable considering the premium ingredients and expert craftsmanship that goes into each dish.
For those looking to celebrate a special occasion, 吾界Teppanyaki goes the extra mile to make it truly memorable. The
dessert chef will artfully arrange a special plating and light up the moment with a candle-lit birthday cake ,
showcasing their thoughtful and customer-centric approach.
In conclusion, 吾界Teppanyaki is a culinary haven that promises an unforgettable dining experience. With its top-notch
ingredients, skilled chef, generous portions, and thoughtful service, this no-menu teppanyaki restaurant sets a new
standard for excellence in Taipei's dining scene.

評論1: [christina chen] (5分, a month ago): Unassuming little store recommended by our foodie cousin so we knew it would be amazing! It did not disappoint! Everything we had was so delicious! My favorite was the shrimp and asparagus hand roll. I’ve had many of these in my life but this was the best one by far with the crunchy texture inside. The crab/ikura/potato salad shell is a must order as well. The uni hand roll was sweet and not fishy. We were in love with the pork katsu Sando as well. The pork was super juicy and thick. I could barely bite it. I like that they offered katsu sauce or salt and pepper to season. It gave different flavors to the one dish. It’s enough for 2 to share and be quite satisfied. We will definitely be coming back!
評論2:
[Ken Chou] (5分, a month ago): Came here via verbal recommendation from my cousin and this place exceeded my expectations in every way.

The food was just superb, we ordered the uni and the shrimp and asparagus hand rolls (both were excellent, especially the shrimp and asparagus), the katsu sando, mentaiko and their featured crab sando, and they all exceeded expectations in the best way possible.

Reservation strongly recommended as we saw many walk-in parties get turned away due to the small size of the restaurant. This place is an absolute must try!!
評論3:

評論4:
[iris y] (4分, a year ago): Not your authentic Japanese restaurant, more of a Taiwanese/Japanese/American fusion place. That said, the dishes were all delicious albeit in a non traditional sense?

My sushi roll had mashed potatoes and drizzled with lots of sauces… definitely more North American than Japanese. The saving grace was definitely the tempura shrimps, tasted fresh and delicious.

The soy sauce on the table was too sweet for me to go with the sashimi, and so was the salmon roe. It tasted much sweeter than those I’ve had before, which made me question whether they were real or artificially made.

Overall, I appreciate the service. Staffs were kind and helpful in answering all our questions, but I’m not sure if I’d return… I guess if I’m in the mood for fusion Japanese food?

p.s. they offer a free mentaiko potato for those leaving 5 star reviews… a gimmick I whole disagree but oh well
