評論2: [Sheryl Liu] (5分, a year ago): If only there were six stars to give! The service was really attentive. We ordered beef and duck breast for the main course, both options were fantastic. The food amount was fulfilling and the taste was great. For the appetizer, I strongly recommend the refreshing seasonal fish. The soft-shell crab was not as impressing. However, all other dishes were great. The space between each table was comfortable. We love the overall experience!
評論3:
[Kai Wang] (4分, 2 months ago): All the dishes were quite delicious, just the portion is not very big to begin with so may have to order more if you’re with a group! We basically ordered everything on the menu. The desert tiramisu was yummy, with a hint of peanut?
They didn’t have the lasagna so be sure to ask when you call because not all the items will be available!

Just a bit disappointed that for a bistro, they are charging water and there was no tap water option. If you’re hoping for coffee, tea, there won’t be but there’s a wide selection of alcohol. We thought going for brunch might mean other beverage selections, have to check more carefully next time.

It’s downstairs so if it’s sunny day, you might feel like you’re missing out on the sun but the decor and environment is cozy!
評論4: [David Thompson] (5分, a year ago): This spot is such a hidden gem! The basement dining area is modern, well-lit, and has a cozy feel. My menu choices were beautifully presented and tasted just as good. We’re looking forward to coming back on a weekend when it’s a bit livelier to soak more of the atmosphere!
評論5: [Vivian Linwebber] (5分, a year ago): Comfy and relaxed were the first impressions when we arrived this basement dining hall.  It wasn’t like what I was expected (filled with young crowds and noisy), instead it was quiet and peaceful.  Initially we were greeted by a young maitre d'  who seemed concerning about our wish to dine in this restaurant and kindly offered us to checkout the menu before leading us to the basement (the dining hall); where was beautifully decorated ( be mindful your head as the staircase’s ceiling was a bit low for taller people), the seating area was carefully divided for different groups of guests.  We couldn’t figure out the region of dishes, perhaps it’s a fusion style as the menu covers around the world.  The price is a little dear, fine dining range, but the table setting was a bit like steakhouse （clothe serviette wrapped cutleries).  We order two set menus with the duck and fish. Duck was thick, beautifully presented (needed a little more flavour), the fish was superb, freshly prepared, matched well with the French green sauce. Good selection of wine for our main. Dessert were Tiramisu and Crème Brûlée; very safe choice. I’m looking forward to seeing if the team would take a challenge to offer higher skilled of desserts as a highlight of ending the dining experience. Overall, it was a pleasant experience and very full belly.:)

Google Map地址: 106台灣臺北市大安區文昌街80號1樓
評論1:
[kelly chiang] (4分, 2 years ago): Overall food was nice but the portions would be a bit small for someone w a bigger appetite. All mains come w a bowl of rice and they said that u can ask for more if u want more. The food is served with nice plating and it’s really nice to have an open kitchen experience without all the smoke and oiliness.
We ordered the beef cheek, beef tongue, ratatouille, and pulled pork. The beef cheek was tender w nice gelatinous texture, which paired well w the savory n acidic red wine sauce. The beef tongue was super tender and the cocoa base sauce gave a curry like umami which was really nice and interesting. The mashed potato was very silky smooth. The touch of crispy onions was amazing! It added some flavor, texture, and fun vibrancy to the dishes. The pulled pork was very flavorful. Ratatouille was nice w the tomato base sauce n hot cream. The onsen egg was a really nice touch of creaminess. However, I think the veggies could’ve been more thick cut for more texture, juiciness, n freshness from the veggies themselves.
評論2: [Jessica Kan] (5分, 3 years ago): There are just a few seats, like a small bistro.  We were sitting at the bar and facing the cook. We ordered the roasted chicken leg set, it was really yummy, The meat was tender, the veggies were easy to bite and the flavor was good. The dishes were served very quickly. It's a good lunch experience.
評論3: [Angie Chen] (5分, 2 years ago): Delicious food and the dish makes you feel like at home.  I would definitely come back again.
評論4: [Pata Tim] (5分, 5 years ago): Easy to find location, decent price and portion. Very tasty. Highly recommend the beef and ratatouille, would definitely go back just for those two.
評論5: [陳宜倫] (5分, 4 years ago): An unforgettable,relaxing place.🍷🍸🍴

Google Map地址: 106台灣臺北市大安區敦化南路一段270巷6號
評論1:
[Bob Chau] (5分, a year ago): Good food!

Attentive warm service.

Cozy atmosphere.

Fair prices.

The weekday set menu for additional $180NT is good value. You get a drink, soup, salad, and really nice dessert.
評論2: [Sam Huang] (4分, 6 years ago): I really liked the drinks we got. One was apple flavored, the other was berry flavored. The set menus were pretty good overall. Salad and soup were both good. My main course was a steak, which I must admit was a little disappointing for how much it cost.  The lamb was cheaper and I think it tasted better. The desserts were totally awesome though!
評論3:
[Cecile Kang] (4分, 8 years ago): A bit pricey, but the food quality was quite good! The pork shank was delicious and truffle fries was just right. The two risottos, black truffle and mushroom & pumpkin with soft shell crab, were both creamy and flavorful, highly recommended! The capellini with squid was so so, relatively normal dish compared to other amazing dishes.
In terms of dessert, strawberry tart with grapefruits was really nice and tasty, and it played a great ending of this meal!
評論4: [Tammy Liu] (4分, 5 years ago): Came for lunch and was surprised of the interior ambience, even with all the windows around, it was dark, dark ceilings, dark tables, dark decor, more of a night bar ambience.  Service was very good.  The pasta and risotto were okay, average. It got kind of noisy with about 70% full.  Ventilation   was not very good, smelled greasy.  Prices were a bit on the high end.
評論5: [Cynthia Yu] (4分, 11 months ago): The chicken soup was very rich in chicken flavour and so yummy . We added in clams and pork belly with vegetables. That was so good. Big Portion . You need to reserve the soup at reservation.

Google Map地址: 106台灣臺北市大安區仁愛路三段143巷25號
評論1: [Cameron G] (4分, 4 months ago): Decent overall. We weren't big fans of the fried veal but liked the fish and bread.
評論2: [James Hicklin] (5分, a year ago): We have dined at L’Age twice, and had a great experience both times.  We have sampled many items from the menu, and everything has been great.  It is not a large menu, yet it has a great variety of flavorful fusion dishes. Nice ambience and music with charming service.  Highly recommended to others.
評論3:
[Janno] (3分, 3 years ago): The staff were amazing, very friendly and engaging! Nice environment, but food was just okay.

Went here to get some steak, but apparently they only serve an 8oz one which was quite pricey. There are alternatives for this kind of steak. The smoked tuna and ham salad we had didnt taste good, though. Soufflé was good enough but their wine selection is quite decent though.

Probably visit here once or twice, maybe for the ambience and maybe the wine selection, not so much for the food. 3/5.
評論4: [Austin Farwell] (5分, 3 years ago): This place is special! Great preparation with surprises in every dish. I was super impressed with each course. Not the cheapest date but well worth it for the cooking and quality of ingredients. Service was charming as well!
評論5:
[Joey Su] (5分, 4 years ago): This bistro is a hidden delight. The menu may seem simple, but the food is anything but. Every dish is amazingly seasoned and the flavors are absolutely rich. I recommend getting a few main entrees and appetizers to share, and get some carbohydrates to round things out. I highly recommend the lamb.

Do take note that the tempo of this restaurant is a bit slower than the average. Dishes come out a bit slower, but they are very well crafted. You can tell the owners wanted guests to relax and take their time enjoying the food. They do not have a time limit so this is a great place for a date or catching up with old friends.

Google Map地址: 106台灣臺北市大安區安和路二段71巷15號
評論1:
[Auguste “施安康” Sérayssol] (5分, 3 months ago): We had such a wonderful experience at Divino! From the moment we walked in, the staff was incredibly warm and welcoming. A special mention to Velma, who has always been the nicest friend and an absolutely amazing server. She took great care of us and gave us excellent suggestions throughout the meal.

The food was outstanding, we enjoyed perfectly cooked gnocchi, a delicious carbonara, and an amazing steak done just right. We finished with a beautiful panna cotta topped with a lovely red-fruit coulis. Everything was full of flavor and beautifully presented.

Overall, a very memorable experience. Highly recommended! 🙌🍽️
評論2:
[Pei-Ru Shih] (5分, 8 months ago): Absolutely fantastic experience! The food was outstanding—my favorite was the grilled octopus. It was perfectly tender, paired with a delicious sauce and creamy smashed potatoes.

The atmosphere was relaxed and welcoming, and the chefs were incredibly friendly. Sitting at the chef’s table made the evening even more special, as we got to interact with the chefs and watch the cooking process, which added a fun and unique touch.

A big thank-you to our friend Velma for introducing us to this place and making it such a memorable night!💕
評論3:
[Winterkönig] (2分, 7 months ago): The food was visually impressive, but the taste was rather standard.. Unfortunately, the herbs cooled down very quickly, possibly due to the strong air conditioning.  On the positive side, the staff were incredibly friendly and provided excellent service.

However, I don’t quite understand the hype around this place.  Maybe it's more about Instagram worthy photos than actual culinary quality?  Also, the place is small and narrow, and the toilet is surprisingly dark and cramped, which made me wonder if they’re trying to hide the dirt.  Either way, at least I can say I’ve tried it this type of modern presentation.  I’ll stick to my mama's La Cucina for an authentic Italian cuisine ."
評論4: [Harold Florand] (5分, 7 months ago): Incredible experience at Divino Taipei, the food and the wine were fantastic. Everything was very tasty and authentic Italian food which is not always the case in Taipei. Special thanks to our waitress Velma for taking such good care of us, we will definitely come back!
評論5: [Kewpie Wu] (5分, 6 months ago): Love the fact that it’s got attention to the details as well as the chilled vibe at the same time! The staffs are really friendly and mindful, the food itself is as authentic as I could imagine - highly recommended!

Google Map地址: 106台灣台北市大安区仁爱路四段27巷25號12F
評論1: [lee leo] (5分, 4 months ago): I stayed at the Kimpton Da An Hotel and had breakfast at The Tavernist every morning — it was fantastic! The food was fresh and delicious, and the team was genuinely warm, attentive, and full of positive energy. You can really feel their great teamwork and hospitality. A wonderful way to start the day! Highly recommended.
評論2: [sschwartz1488] (2分, 2 months ago): I came her with my girlfriend and another couple for a nice dinner in Taipei. We ordered the set menu as well as two pork main dishes. The food was fine, but for the price, it is not even close to being worth it. Our bill was $350 USD for four people which by Taipei standards is pretty insane. It's a nice hotel, but I would never recommend anyone to spend their $$$ here.
評論3: [Kin Cheung Ng] (3分, 2 months ago): I spent a night there, during reservation I wasn’t clearly informed that only a fixed set dinner was available, which was a bit disappointing. The food overall was tasteless, with the exception of the sea bass and beef. The waitstaff introduced each dish very quietly, and even with full attention it was very difficult to hear them. Overall, the experience was fair, but given the relatively high price, it didn’t feel like good value for money.
評論4: [JC77] (4分, 6 months ago): Food was good, drinks were great, the wine was at perfect temperature. The restaurant is cozy, I want to go back to try the terrass when the weather is less hot. Service was friendly
評論5:
[Rex Sandoval Jr] (5分, 3 months ago): The Food: The Servers, particularly Lyn had recommended us the lunch set and walked us through the choices. She set the expectations and the food blew us away. The ribeye was literally melt in my mouth. The seafood was fresh and perfectly cooked. The dessert was to die for. It was an excellently curated by the chef.

The Service: This is what separates a good restaurant from an excellent restaurant. I highly commend Lyn for the excellent service. She was very attentive to our needs and was very funny as well. She takes EXCELLENT photos. She also asked us where we're from and how long we'll be staying in Taiwan. She even gave us recommendations in Taiwan. Her service is unmatched. Big props to Lyn for making our time there very delightful.

The atmospehere: The restaurant was situated on the 12th floor. It has good views. But an insider tip that Lyn told us, there's a part on the balcony that has Taipei101 as the backdrop. The lighting of the place really set the mood. It screams a mix of hominess and luxury. Only good interior designers can do that. Big props to the atmosphere as well.

餐廳名稱: Ombré敦南店｜大安區餐酒館 - 多國料理餐廳 - 台北早午餐推薦
Google Map地址: 106台灣台北市大安区北市敦化南路一段161巷70號
評論1:
[Johnson Deng] (5分, a year ago): Got lucky on a weekend and went in solo. I say lucky because man it’s really crowded for brunch and dinner.

All the entree dishes here are on par with other high end brunch places. Presentation is great, portions are actually generous. Definitely a weekend splurge—but order ahead.

Had the truffle bacon fries and beef tongue risotto. Loved the fries—first time I didn’t feel like I needed extra condiments to eat them.

Knocked off a star for the risotto: Risotto didn’t completely absorb the stock and soften all the way through: grains were a little bit “dry hard” in the very center. To be explicit: it was undercooked, not “al dente”.
Cream Stew and balsamic glaze were really tasty and savory.
評論2: [Eugene] (4分, a year ago): The food presentation and the atmosphere are great although only the mushroom soup taste good for me. A bit pricey. Recommend if you looking for a good vibe place to chill.
評論3:
[Sophie Kwok] (5分, 2 years ago): Came here for brunch on a Monday and it was 90% full. Remember to book in advance.
We had a salmon set and a pork chop set, and both were great. I prefer the salmon over pork because the marinade and seasoning of the pork is more like Taiwanese flavour than western one that goes with salad and bread etc., but the texture was great.
It’s slightly on a pricey side for brunch, but given the quality and portion it is acceptable. 10% service charge required, so we ended up spending 1000+ for two people.
評論4: [Daniel Lo] (4分, 3 years ago): I decided to come here after reading a few reviews about how this was one of the best brunch places in Taipei. We were definitely not disappointed the risotto and the French toast were incredible. however, I will say that it is on the pricier end. you definitely will have to make a reservation if you want to come here.
評論5: [PinHao Bill Chen] (5分, 5 years ago): Love ombré soooooo much. The meals are absolutely amazing, tasty, and smell. The restaurant is decorated comfortable and classical. The flavor of appetizers and main dishes are strong and would be such a great idea to pair with wine or a cocktail. I highly recommend the appetizer, shrimps with mushrooms in spices, this dish will notify your stomach to be ready for a great dinner moment. Last but not the least, the waitresses here are kind and are happy to take photos for customers. I have a great time with my family and next time I will bring my friends here!

Google Map地址: 106台灣臺北市大安區臥龍街11號
評論1:
[Jeff Tan] (4分, 3 years ago): Reasonably priced Japanese restaurant. Overall food tasted pretty good, portion size was decent for the price.
Restaurant can get a bit cramped for space, overall not a bad place for a quick meal.
評論2: [Husnul Khotimah Matoha] (5分, a year ago): Excellent value for the curry rice combo meal, even with the single meal, the portion is already quite big! The miso soup that comes with it was also so good!!
評論3: [Wayland] (5分, 2 years ago): Set meal is excellent value. I ordered extra salmon nigiri because I figured that the set meal at that price couldn't possibly be enough food for me. Boy was I wrong 😂
評論4: [Steven Chen] (5分, 8 years ago): They serve fresh and delicious sashimi with a lower price than some other Japanese restaurants and it's totally worth the money!
評論5: [Claire] (5分, 8 years ago): I live near here🙆🏻 Quality has remained as business picked up😇I love its sashimi ❤️

Google Map地址: 106台灣臺北市大安區和平東路二段118巷2弄1號1樓
評論1:
[Mevalin] (1分, a month ago): I cannot speak Chinese. Their menu translates “pork pizza” in Chinese into “smoke salmon pizza” in English, so I got the wrong pizza. We were super hungry and in a hurry so I just accepted it. I pointed out the menu mistake to them and said, I’d just take it this time. Then that’s it. No apology nor any intention to take responsibility. It doesn’t seem like they were rude or anything, it seems like they were just young part-time kids who didn’t know what a decent restaurant should do.

Note that they have a process of confirming the order with the customers. But again, I cannot understand Chinese. What’s the point …

We planned to take some home for our helper and she cannot eat pork so we had to buy sthg else for them. That pork pizza wasn’t good. It had some bone fractures in the minced pork, very disappointing. It took us 5 days to try to finish the food we didn’t like before we throw the rest away …
評論2: [Lydia Harris] (4分, a month ago): Overall satisfaction. Not really breathtaking, but no complaints. We entered hungry and we left not hungry. Good food, good vibes.
評論3: [Jessica Ting] (3分, 2 years ago): The pasta was quite bland, and the cuttlefish was tiny. But the business lunch set meal price is quite reasonable considering the soup and the drink are included.
評論4: [Nik] (5分, 3 years ago): Nice food, drinks and dessert in very reasonable price!! Highly recommend!!
評論5:
[T T] (2分, a year ago): Penne Bolognese: flavors of tomato and meat not integrated.  Needs to be cooked much longer.  (My homemade version is better).

Pizza: edges too crispy but the rest undercooked.  Cheese not browned.  Probably sauce is too wet.  Cheese was not evenly distributed (no cheese -> burnt)

Google Map地址: 106台灣台北市大安区忠孝东路三段282號新光三越Diamond Towers 二館4樓四樓
評論1:
[Mano Recto] (5分, a month ago): An exceptional dining experience and one of the highlights of my Taipei trip. From the very first bite, it was clear that this kitchen operates with precision, creativity, and discipline. The opening sequence — including a playful yet thoughtful edible “cork” — immediately established the restaurant’s confidence and sense of humor without drifting into gimmickry.

Each course was carefully composed, beautifully presented, and thoughtfully paced. Flavors were refined, restrained, and purposeful, allowing technique and ingredients to shine. The ambiance was calm, elegant, and immersive, and the service attentive without ever being intrusive.

This is Michelin-level dining in the truest sense: originality grounded in craft, clarity of vision, and consistency throughout the meal. A destination restaurant I would gladly return to — and one that fully deserves continued recognition.
評論2: [Karen Ng] (5分, 3 months ago): I truly enjoyed the entire experience. The culinary concept is thoughtful and well-executed, offering dishes that are both creative and satisfying. Each course felt intentional, with flavors that were balanced and memorable. A special thank-you to Vanessa (hope I got her name right), whose service elevated the evening even further. She was attentive to every detail—so observant that she even noticed I’m left-handed and adjusted the table setting accordingly. Her professionalism and warmth made the meal feel even more personal and enjoyable. Overall, a wonderful dining experience, and I look forward to seeing how the restaurant continues to grow its unique concept. Highly recommended for anyone looking for refined cuisine with genuinely thoughtful service.
評論3:
[Mikki B] (5分, 2 months ago): The food was good and well executed. Each dish was a work of art and delicious. Service was polite, though pacing could have been better, one dish arrived before I had finished the previous course, which should not happen at a 2-Michelin-star level.

While tables were not particularly close, the acoustics made it easy to hear conversations from other tables, affecting the sense of privacy.

We were given a signed A4 copy of the menu and drinks pairing, which was a nice gesture. However, without any human interaction to accompany it, the gesture felt incomplete—human connection matters, and putting a face to the signature would have made it meaningful.

Overall, good food, but certain experiential details fell short of what the rating promises.
評論4: [Jason] (4分, 3 months ago): Each dish was creatively and artistically presented, with a mixture of textures and minimal use of sauces, which heightened the distinct flavor of each ingredient. Most exceptional in presentation and taste were the amuse-bouchées (one presented in a bowl among wine and champagne corks and a mini-garden adorning a crunchy phyllo basket), the sea cucumber and cucumber, the Maine lobster and chicken, and the add on pasta with an extra generous portion of Australian truffles grated by Chef Alain by our table. But the one dish that was phenomenal was the “soup and bread,” a mushroom cappuccino and truffle doughnut – such a tasty unique combination!
評論5: [Siao Stan] (5分, 2 months ago): One of the most innovative restaurants in Taiwan ~ creative seasonal menu, excellent drink (non-alcoholic) pairing, all-white decor and attentive service.  Price is reasonable for 20-course Michelin rated dinner.

Google Map地址: 106台灣臺北市大安區安和路一段102巷28號
評論1: [Thomas K.] (5分, 2 months ago): Great venue to book for a private lunch event. Very attentive staff and excellent food.
評論2: [Ivy MT] (5分, a year ago): It’s a Christmas dinner for us and we really enjoy the vibes there. We ordered our dishes already but we see other tables ordered French fries, so we follow. I have to say, the sauce is so heavenly and it definitely add one highlight to our trip! The service is so homecoming, we’ll definitely come back again.
評論3: [Roni Hung] (5分, a year ago): You can have a classic Peruvian gourmet and cocktail here such as ceviche and pisco sour. From starter to dessert were really good. I especially like the lamb. The service attendants are friendly. I had a great time with my date.
評論4:
[Heather Cline] (4分, a month ago): I wanted to love Pico Pico. Interesting menu, leaning towards Mediterranean flavors / proteins.

Drink (sadly only one because of service):
I ordered the Chartreuse Swizzle to start and it was delicious. But it was also served about 2 cm from the top of the glass and was more ice than liquid. Regardless, it was delicious.

Food:
Very good.
1) Started with the tomato + cheese salad. Greens were fresh, tomato’s were nice but it was too heavily dressed and served with a strangely sweet topping of breadcrumbs / nuts.
2) Asparagus with spicy cheese sauce and fried egg. Best dish of the night. The asparagus were perfectly cooked, the sauce was creamy with the right balance of salt and a little spiciness (kind of reminded me of pimento cheese sauce). The fried egg was also cooked to perfection and the table side presentation of cutting the asparagus and mixing in the warm, creamy egg yolk to make the sauce was splendid. Creamy, rich, delicious with a bit of a kick to not be too rich or redundant on the palate.
3) squid with salsa verde. Squid was perfectly cooked. Not rubbery at all (braised or sous vide before for tenderness). The salsa was tasty but I wanted MUCH more as it only was enough for about 1/2 the squid on the plate. Also the sauce could have been brighter (more acid—lime?) and the dish was la king salt. A finish of flakey salt would have been wonderful.

Lastly I must discuss the service. It was disappointing and I clearly had a very different experience than other diners. After taking my order, I waited ~15 -20 minutes for my cocktail and had almost finished it when my salad was brought. When the server cleared my salad I asked for the wine list. It was never brought to me.

I then waited another 10 minutes for my asparagus dish (~20 minutes between courses) and wasn’t surprised when the couple who sat next to me got their asparagus dish at the exact same time (despite being sat as I was finishing my salad).

A glass of white wine would have balanced the creaminess of the second dish so well but the wine list never came.

Then came the delicious squid. Still no wine. Still no server to check on me.

I finished my squid and was then brought a glass of white wine (?) which I sent back because a) I had now finished my meal and b) it had been 30+ minutes since I asked for the wine list.

Also, I don’t eat pork so the menu was limited. I inquired about 2 dishes to see if the pork could be omitted (clams with chorizo and broccolini with carbonara sauce) and was told by the server that wasn’t possible (perhaps that’s house policy but, if not, she didn’t even check with the kitchen). Also rhe only time anyone filled my water glass is while I was waiting for the bill.

I wanted to love this place but left feeling a bit disappointed. However the menu is intriguing and the cook was to perfection with some tricky ingredients (asparagus and squid) so I’ll give it another try.
評論5:
[Maite M] (5分, 2 years ago): Absolutely delicious! I had high expectations for this restaurant, and it certainly did not disappoint. The beef was cooked perfectly, the lamb was tender, and the beetroot sauce created a perfect combination of flavors.

However, there is room for improvement in terms of food serving times. It took over 15 minutes to receive the second dish. Despite that, I thoroughly enjoyed the atmosphere, and our waitress Jin was truly awesome.

Google Map地址: 106台灣臺北市大安區大安路一段183號
評論1:
[Ally Virlynn] (5分, 2 months ago): Both the ribeye steak and lobster were fantastic! Portion of ribeye was big. Carrot soup was really amazing. I was looking forward to the Popovers but they tasted a tad underwhelming to me. Kids love the cream spinach. Garlic broccoli tasted fine.
The corn was very nice. Fries were good when they were hot but not so good when they turned cold.

Staff were friendly and polite. Service is very good.

Recommended to try it!
評論2:
[Jason Long] (3分, a month ago): I prefer rich/fatty food.

Steaks were pan fried, which is fine, but there is little to no crisp on the outside. Also they seem slightly under seasoned and could probably use more butter for a richer taste.

Carrot soup was okay, but also wish it were richer.

The popover bread had a burnt taste.

Fries tasted fresh.
評論3: [白腹中華人] (5分, 2 months ago): Food was great! The place is nice and cosy. Lots of couples and families. The refillable fries is generous and comes fresh. Went there for a date night and love the place~ will patronise again.
評論4:
[Joey Esguerra] (5分, 4 months ago): All I can say is it’s definitely worth your money!

What we ordered:
1.	Grilled Lobster
Juicy, fresh, and surprisingly big portion.
2.	Steak
No photo could do it justice. The meat was soft, a little chewy but flavorful and well seasoned.
Both meals came with a carrot soup, though it tasted more like pumpkin — either way, really good and filling!
3.	Wagyu Burger
Portion was huge. All I can say is the juices were dripping with every bite. Pure comfort food and honestly food for the soul.
評論5: [Auguste “施安康” Sérayssol] (4分, 2 months ago): Great experience at Le Blanc in Taipei. The steak frites were well done, and the red carrot soup was really delicious. Simple food, perfectly executed. Would recommend!

餐廳名稱: 樂子the Diner 瑞安店（台北大安區必吃特色早午餐 / 台北好吃聚餐餐廳推薦 / 大安森林公園附近美食／大安區寵物友善餐廳）
Google Map地址: 106台灣臺北市大安區瑞安街145號
評論1: [Anna] (5分, 4 months ago): Their yellow exterior caught my eye. I tried their bacon with burger patties, scrambled egg, fries, cherry tomatoes, and mushrooms. It tasted great. I appreciate that their ketchup was freshly served and not from a bottle. I wasn’t a fan of mushrooms, but this meal definitely satiated my hunger.
評論2:
[Ms. Cheng] (5分, 6 months ago): I’ve never been to the Diner 瑞安店 in person, but it has become one of my favorite places to order from on UberEats. When I’m craving a real American breakfast in Taipei, this is the first place I turn to.

The food arrives consistently fresh, hot, and well-prepared, which isn’t always the case with delivery. My absolute favorite is the 地中海蛋捲 Mediterranean Omelette — flavorful, satisfying, and packed with just the right balance of ingredients. I also really enjoy their Potatoes O’Brien, which are cooked perfectly with that mix of crispy edges and soft, flavorful bites.

What I appreciate most is the consistency: every time I order, the quality is the same — generous portions, delicious flavors, and a comforting taste of home-style breakfast. Even through delivery, the food never feels rushed or sloppy; it feels like care goes into every order.

For anyone looking for hearty, reliable, and genuinely tasty American-style breakfasts in Taipei, the Diner 瑞安店 is my go-to recommendation.
評論3: [Karl Finke] (5分, 3 months ago): Neat American style food, good service. Some of the menu gets really quite interesting with the PBJ burger and the cilantro one, quite nice!
評論4: [DoraEva] (4分, 10 months ago): This is where Diner begins.  Being a long supporter of Diner, this has been one of my favourite burger place.  I did not order burger in this visit.  In stead, I ordered ribs and other side dishes.  Among these, nacho is the most satisfying treat, and rib is not.   I would recommend to stick to other brunch items like burger, sandwich, eggs.
評論5: [Eva Beekers] (5分, 3 months ago): It's so hard to find genuine tasting American breakfast food in Taipei. This place actually does it well. I liked it so much that after discovering it I went back again the next day for a meeting.

Google Map地址: 106台灣臺北市大安區忠孝東路四段216巷33弄10號
評論1:
[Gerline Soo] (4分, 3 months ago): Walked past and decided to try it given it has so many Michelin awards :)

They had some interesting dishes and we enjoyed our experience.
評論2:
[Totoro E. T.] (3分, a year ago): I have to say the places clean and open, The tables are big and great for large party.
As to the food, everything tastes average nothing stands out and nothing that want me to reorder the dish.
At the end of the meal, our party was talking because we have not seen each other for a long time.

評論3:
[Justin Chu] (4分, a year ago): There were a few interesting dishes that were delicious. However at that price point, I was expecting much more.

A lot of dishes were just average. I do not mind paying NTD 200 for a plate of peanuts if it was good but it turns out just average. The surprising good dishes were the sausages, prawns and vegetable
評論4:
[Alice Chiu] (2分, a year ago): Very disappointed with the food, both taste-wise and value-wise. Not a single dish that I would want to order again. The environment is extremely loud; we would have to shout to each other in order to have a conversation and had to keep asking others to repeat what they said. The only good thing is the service.

Expensive but 👎🏻
評論5: [Stephanie Wong] (5分, a year ago): Dine during Lunar New Year’s eve. Maybe this is the factor contribute to the mediocre dishes we had. Had been highly anticipated but turn out not as good as all the reviews. Must come back during usual business hour.

餐廳名稱: 義大利餐廳 TRASTEVERE 大安區 忠孝復興站 美食
Google Map地址: 106台灣臺北市大安區復興南路一段107巷7號1樓
評論1:
[邊季（吐司邊邊）] (2分, 10 months ago): Nestled in Taipei, Trastevere proudly carries the legacy of Sabatini, a name synonymous with classic Roman dining since 1958. This connection, which even extends to the "Sabatini" stamp on the plates and bowls (a detail that initially seemed a touch curious), certainly sets a stage of anticipation. You settle in, thinking you're in for an authentic Roman holiday on a plate.
Our group, in a noble quest for a comprehensive taste, made the bold decision to order basically the entirety of their set menu. A marathon of dishes ensued, a parade that showcased the breadth of their offerings. This approach, while ambitious (and requiring a good appetite!), gave us a thorough sampling.
However, despite the impressive array and the clear nods to its Roman parentage, the food itself proved to be the most underwhelming chapter of our visit. When benchmarked against the truly authentic, soul-satisfying flavors one encounters in the trattorias and ristorantes of Rome itself, the dishes at Trastevere Taipei generally fell short. That spark, that depth of flavor that defines genuine Roman cuisine, seemed to be somewhat lost in translation.
It's a peculiar feeling to see the esteemed "Sabatini" name on your plate, a promise of quality and tradition, and then find the food doesn't quite sing the same glorious Roman opera. The ambition is there, the lineage is clear, but the execution, on this occasion, didn't transport us to the bustling, flavorful streets of Trastevere, Rome.
The Verdict:
If you're seeking a pleasant Italian-ish meal in Taipei with smart tableware, Trastevere might fit the bill. However, if you're chasing the robust, authentic taste of Rome and have high expectations based on its Sabatini connection, you might find, as we did, that the experience is a bit underwhelming. It's a restaurant with a famous Roman name, but it seems some of that Roman magic might have gotten a little jet-lagged on its way to Taipei. Maybe we are sad because we had high hope for this restaurant.
評論2: [蔡柏康（CATMARINE）] (5分, 2 years ago): I had the pleasure of dining at this exquisite Italian restaurant, and I must say, it was an extraordinary experience from start to finish. The service was impeccable, with attentive staff who catered to our every need. The pasta dishes were a true masterpiece, perfectly cooked with delectable sauces that delighted my taste buds. The pizzas were equally impressive, boasting thin, crispy crusts and a medley of fresh, flavorful toppings. This restaurant truly captured the essence of authentic Italian cuisine, and I left with a satisfied palate and a desire to return for more unforgettable dining moments.
評論3:
[Cameron G] (2分, a year ago): We ordered sets but didn't find the dishes too great for the price. I think the ingredient quality and flavor could be improved.

Appetiser: Salad with duck breast: 3/5
- Decent but the presentation could be improved

Tomato cream soup: 4/5
- Pretty good, I might only suggest adding a garnish on top, like parsley, but it's not required

Spaghetti with beef: 3/5 (not pictured)
- The flavor is a bit plain for my liking, maybe this dish could have spices added.

NZ steak entree: 3/5
- it's kind of weird, and wet, and the presentation isn't so good. Quality is just ok too.

Desserts: 3/5
The homemade ice cream is great! I think other desserts are ok, we had tiramisu and Panna Cotta.

The service is great, and I appreciate the staff.
評論4: [Howard Tsai] (5分, 3 years ago): I took my dad to Trastevere for lunch to celebrate Christmas today. It was such a delighted dining experience. From the atmosphere to the food and then the excellent services. Everything was done to the perfection and we were just so pleased with everything Trastevere offers. For sure we will be visiting Trastevere again in the future!
評論5:
[Patrick Sammet] (5分, 3 years ago): We passed by a couple of weeks ago and today finally came for what proved to be an amazing dinner for my wife's birthday at Trastevere.
While there are a handful good pizza places in Taipei, Trastevere is one of only two Italian restaurants I would recommend for fine dining.

The pizza was quite okay, which means a lot given the rather low Taiwanese standards when it comes to pizza. What surprised us really a lot, was the pasta and the desserts, both of which tasted amazing and were outstanding. My wife had the tiramisu and I had the cheesecake, either of which we would warmly recommend. The appetizers were also lavish and were a worthy start for what proved to be a great culinary journey.

Definitely recommend this restaurant~

Google Map地址: 106台灣臺北市大安區四維路28號
評論1: [Danielle Veluz] (5分, 3 months ago): I celebrated my birthday with a friend here and had a really great time! Service was excellent - no complaints. The staff were all super courteous and made sure the explained all the dishes clearly to us. Atmosphere-wise, the lighting was a little too dark for my lighting but otherwise very comfortable. Now let's get to the food-we got the 9 course meal with 2 additional amouse bouches (not in menu). Wonderful flavors all around and interesting use of local ingredients and inventive flavor combinations and textures. The sourdough bread + beer butter and fermented chili butter was my fave, I wish there was a way to just buy it on its own!! Duck was fantastic except I wish the skin were crispy lol. On to the main - the wagyu was just.. ok for lack of a better term. It was wagyu alright but it was a bit too plain for my liking (not the restaurant's fault, maybe I have a less than refined taste in beef). Overall though still a wonderful meal and I would go here again but I'm looking forward to trying a different tasting menu. Cheers to Mume!
評論2:
[Audrey yeo] (3分, 3 months ago): First visit to MUME Taipei. Reservation was easy, and the team was very accommodating with a last-minute change of date. Warm welcome on arrival and a refined, inviting ambience. The bar was particularly well presented, and the course was clearly introduced.

Food presentation was excellent, with well-portioned dishes and clear explanations throughout the meal. Service was attentive and professional, and the wine recommendations were well paired.

While the overall experience was polished, the flavors were average and none of the dishes stood out by the end of the course. Given the reputation, I was expecting more depth and memorability in taste.
Overall a pleasant experience, but I’m unlikely to revisit.
評論3:
[Season Yeung] (5分, a month ago): I celebrated Valentine’s Day at MUME in Taipei. Reservation was easy, and the team was very accommodating with my time change. I received a warm welcome on arrival and the ambience was very modern.

Food presentation was excellent, with well-portioned dishes and clear explanations throughout the meal. Service was attentive and professional. I had the non-alcohol beverage pairing which matched well with all the dishes.

I really enjoyed my experience and I would definitely dine at MUME again when I next visit Taipei.
評論4: [Cutesoprano] (5分, 2 weeks ago): It's hard to book a table for solo diner in a michelin star restaurant. But lucky, MUME offer an option for a solo diner like me. I'm having a great night with overall experience. I book the chef's table with the half glass wine  pairing. My 1st favourite is the yilan's red prawn. I will say this food is like love at the 1st bite. LoL. But it is the best i can describe for my experience. The sourdough might not be the best, but i love the idea of pickled chilli butter! LOVE IT! It is well paired with the sourdough! Wine pairing especially very well paired with the yilan abalone and wagyu short rib. As for desserts, i'm amazed that chef even thinking of making burdock as a desserts. Something i never imagine, but it actually working well with chocolate and banana. But tbh, with a combination of chocolate and banana, there is no way it will be failed. Too bad the wine pairing doesn't include any dessert wine. But i think in taiwan it's not common to drink sweet wine with desserts. The staff are helpful, the sommelier especially giving me some good recommendation as well. I'm actually worried that it might be difficult to communicate with me as i don't understand any chinese. But all the staff are still very helpful and make sure i'm having a good time at MUME. Thank you so much for the experience. If i ever comeback to taipei, i will surely coming back to visit MUME.
評論5:
[C C] (4分, a month ago): I was expecting to give a five star yet this time in the new menu their daring selections of daily Taiwanese ingredients challenged themselves. The easy to get such as squid was even less tasty than that on the street

The service was polite yet superficial. When asking them the questions of the food they could not answer; when giving them suggestions that they didn’t get it. when asking for the manager they insisted to get all the details of our queries before inviting the manager. The problem was I explained the queries yet she couldn’t get it at all. The young staffs were good looking and they remembered how they were taught about the food yet without passion nor the real understanding of what they were talking about. For such a Restaurant we still prefer to be served with the cutleries rather than selecting the proper cutleries from keeping opening the drawer.

The fourth star was given to their passion, efforts, and the creativity in culinary, and the manager’s enthusiasm

餐廳名稱: UNCLE RAY 串燒·牛飲·咖喱 台北大安
Google Map地址: 106台灣臺北市大安區敦化南路一段160巷48號1樓
評論1: [Denis Chabot] (5分, 2 years ago): Extreme good food alert!! Everything is cooked to perfection and with taste… quantities are reasonable and so is the price… I recommend to book though as it is getting packed quick!! I beg you to try the scallops and the squid as well as the 月見控肉飯
評論2:
[Vortrox92LG] (5分, a year ago): In my opinion, Fries with Truffle Mayonnaise ($93) always went more well with each other than ketchup. The Chips were amazingly fried and tasted better than at least 90% of those.

Fried Chicken Skin ($58) was salty and crispy and tasted kinda different from the coating on typical fried chicken. The authentic texture of chicken skin was so tasty and if you try dipping it into the truffle sauce, it will turn out perfect.

Cumin Lamb Ribs ($69) tasted quite delicious, juicy and tender. There was only one skewer but it was the lamb with lowest price I had ever seen from all the restaurants.

Berry Mulberry Fizzy Drink ($58)  was quite sweet and slightly sour. I highly recommend this drink because it’s really one of the best fizzy drinks I’ve ever had.

In conclusion, the portion wasn’t too much but the prices were quite low compared to most similar restaurants. The service was quite nice and fast.
Overall it’s definitely a 10 out of 10!

FYI, you scan the QR code to order and pay after the meal. Besides, if you pay in cash, you’ll get a 5% discount for your bill, which means only 5% service charge instead of the original 10%.
評論3: [Jessica Ting] (3分, 2 years ago): We came here because my friend highly recommended it, but my dining experience was just average. We ended up spending slightly over NTD $2000 (for two people), but for that price, I prefer to go somewhere with either better food quality or a more decent ambience place.
評論4: [Vu Jessica] (5分, 12 months ago): The restaurant space is cozy, the owner and staff are very friendly and cheerful. Delicious food, reasonable price!
評論5:
[Tiffany Hwang] (5分, 3 years ago): Such a chill place! Food is nice, beer is fine~ and they have some games to play with to win drinks! The owner here is so nice and friendly~ thank you Ray for having us!
Definitely gonna come again!!!!

餐廳名稱: The House Taipei 屋內（台北大安區雪茄酒吧餐酒館推薦／東區夜晚美食調酒／科技大樓站美食）

評論1: [Sherry Liu] (5分, 2 years ago): Undoubtedly a hidden treasure in Minsheng Community! The drinks are truly delightful, and the fish and chips are delicious too! Out of the 6 drinks me and my family tried, we only described the desired feelings and flavors to the bartender for 3 of them, and the results were absolutely an enjoyable experience. Will definitely come back in the future!
評論2: [Brian Wan] (4分, 5 months ago): nice bar, easy and relax.
評論3: [Henry Tseng] (5分, 2 years ago): Great drinks and food. Service is so great. Music so good jazz night. Love this place!

[L. Filip] (4分, 2 years ago): Waiter didn’t speak English and they weren’t serving food at all.

Friendly and nice otherwise.
評論5: [Asia In Europe] (1分, 10 months ago): Walked in and was told closed when not. Not great.

餐廳名稱: BRUN不然-信義店(捷運大安站)
Google Map地址: 106台灣臺北市大安區信義路四段103號2F
評論1:
[Obsessed DCO] (5分, 3 months ago): It was by surprise a gem for me on my first morning back in Taipei, travelling alone seeking a break. The cafe situated on a busy start on the second floor took a nice mix bringing a sense of calm in the busy city.

Great service and fantastic food, but most importantly their coffee was rejuvenating. Perfect taste for the morning that I needed the caffeine kick after a whole day travel.

This will be on the top of my list with my loved ones coming back to Taipei.
評論2: [Jen AF] (5分, 3 months ago): We had a quiet and chill brunch at Brun! Our orders were delicious! I love their bacon and eggs with sourdough🤗 winner!
評論3:
[George Yeh] (2分, a month ago): We had brunch this morning and next table had a cat on top of table. Does the restaurant staff understand some customers might be allergic furry friends? And cat on table = hygiene issue. Don’t come here if you’re allergic to cats and dogs.
Food was okay. Service was poor. Staff were cold.
評論4: [Mohammad Alanzi] (5分, 4 months ago): This place is inside another place. Go inside PAZZO and take lift to second floor. The food was good and overall it was great. The eggs needs seasoning but all was ok.
評論5:
[Chanyanat S.] (5分, a year ago): It was my first time at Brun, and it’s so hard to get a reservation! But it was totally worth it—I think it’s now my favorite breakfast spot in Taipei.

The restaurant is pet-friendly, so I brought my cat along. The waitress was super nice and even greeted our kitty, which was a fun touch.

All the food tasted amazing, but one thing to note is they don’t offer dish adjustments. For example, we wanted to add more scrambled eggs, but that wasn’t an option.

Overall, I loved the experience and will definitely be back!

Google Map地址: 106台灣臺北市大安區仁愛路三段88號
評論1:
[Eric Chou奧克蘭寶哥] (5分, 5 months ago): What a legendary restaurant in Taipei city centre area!

It's small, it's ordinary but it's the taste of memories.

If you or your family have a military background, this is definitely the restaurant for you
Within this tiny place you get to enjoy flavours from different part of China, I mean, decades ago.

After all these years they are still priced reasonably, the portion is still good for even one person to eat alone or for two to share more.

No one single item will disappoint you, just go ahead and order. If you have no idea what to eat, asking around or wait for clerks to recommend.
評論2: [Tim Chen] (4分, 3 months ago): Came here for lunch on a Sunday, was second in line even though came at 11am which is when the restaurant opens. Dish were served relatively quick with two types of free rice at the side free for refill. Sweet and sour pork, the shrimp egg and the kimchi beef dish were really delicious, the spicy tofu was pretty mediocre. They do serve a complimentary soup if you didn’t order they also have free hot tea near the door. The place had about 10 parties waiting in line by the time we left which shows how popular this place is. Would recommend for anyone wanting to eat some 外省 dish.
評論3: [Patrick (rose2020)] (5分, 3 weeks ago): Came here before 17:00,  already many people queued, traditional Chinese dishes, very friends staffs!
評論4:
[Kate Shao] (5分, a year ago): Traditional and popular military village cuisine in Taipei. They offered all-you-can-eat rice with affordable price.

My favorite dish was stir fried bitter gourd with pork! Braised tofu and duck blood was tasty to go with rice. Stir fried spinach with shrimp paste was fresh and delicious. They also provided vegetarian dishes.

There were two kinds of rice to choose from: Penglai rice and Zailai rice. 100% recommended to choose Penglai rice. Cause the texture was suitable for Taiwanese.
It also came with a large bowl of soybean sprout soup for free.

Efficient service and reasonable price. Pretty crowded on weekends.
評論5:
[CH Loh] (4分, a year ago): Family style restaurant. Minimum 1 dish order per person. Portion size is large and I over order. Taste was ‘ok’ for me but maybe it was what I ordered. Other tables look like they had more yummy food 🤣

Rice and tea is free flow, and there’s also a free soup. 2 dishes for me cost NT420. Not the cheapest but the place is very wholesome.

餐廳名稱: Agusto 牛排龍蝦餐酒館 大安店｜台北美食義式餐廳 生日聚餐約會推薦
Google Map地址: 106台灣臺北市大安區忠孝東路四段216巷27弄15號1樓
評論1:
[YA Li] (5分, 9 months ago): Great spot for Italian food in Taiwan. The pasta was fresh, the pizza nicely done with a crispy base, and the tiramisu was a pleasant finish. Overall, the flavors were balanced and satisfying without being overly rich.

What stood out most was the service — especially from staff member 穎. She was attentive, polite, and made the whole experience smoother and more enjoyable. So far, one of the best service experiences I’ve had in Taiwan.

Would definitely come back. Worth a visit if you’re in the area.
評論2:
[Angie Yeo] (5分, 6 months ago): Chance upon this restaurant so dropped in for lunch. It was not crowded. Service was attentive and great. Food is good too.
Only thing is they dont serve wine by glass otherwise will be nice for solo traveller like me
評論3: [Derek Chau] (5分, 7 months ago): Was craving something other than the numerous street markets and decided to come to Agusto for some Italian food. The ambience is really nice and food was top notch. The mushroom soup and Hokkaido scallop risotto was just what I needed to rebalance my taste buds. There was a hint of licorice that I’m personally not a fan of in the risotto, so I think they used Thai basil rather than regular basil. The scallops were cooked perfectly though. Price was very fair as well. Would definitely come again.
評論4:
[Justin Hayworth] (5分, a year ago): Everything was fantastic!! Superbly fresh seafood and lamb.

Masterful dish design and presentation.

Warm ambiance.

Best meal I had in Taipei!!

$70 USD for 5 great dishes. What a gem!!
評論5:
[A Hawaiian Foodie] (1分, a year ago): Came on a Friday night at around 8pm and almost all restaurants nearby were full. So I appreciated the staff of this restaurant for letting me in despite their shortage of manpower.

However, in terms of food, it was below average. The baked vegetables were drawn in water when served (probably due to the high temperature of baking the veges) and the squid pasta was lack of flavour. The dried-age steak was quite tender but not meatful and juicy enough, also the sides were a bit pitiful. I didn’t understand why the other reviews gave 5 stars for the food, as the dishes clearly were not up to par.

As much as I appreciated the efforts of the staff, I believe only 2 waitresses covering the entire dinner period was clearly insufficient. Therefore the finished plates in my table were unattended to, my water was not regularly refilled, my wine order was missing, and no change of plates for my entire meal.

Given the setting and price range of this meal, I expect much better services and food - clearly it was far from satisfaction!

餐廳名稱: 十二月 粥品 • 茶飲 • 私房菜｜台北大安店
Google Map地址: 106台灣臺北市大安區大安路一段19巷10號
評論1:
[Clement Tay] (5分, 3 months ago): It was full house, but we sat down for a meal as the staff called to confirm with someone but did not pick up the call (great service)

Went in December And the chicken porridge was amazing and so comforting for the cold weather. The texture is the mushy kind which I love, similar to hongkong congee.  Flavour is terrific, chicken has the sesame oil frangrance and so tender.

The 青龙菜 is great but taste slightly different than Singapore, I think probably it's due to the water being used. Maybe spring water from the mountains.

Ordered the fried chicken also and it's close to 咕噜肉 or orange chicken, fantastic as well
評論2: [S L] (4分, 4 months ago): We ordered chicken porridge, crab porridge, yam with duck, egg tofu, and a vegetable dish. We did not like the crab porridge. It came with a small crab and it was frustrating to eat as we had to pick out the crab meat. On top of it, there were shells in porridge and parts of it tasted bitter. We liked everything else so I would still recommend this restaurant.
評論3: [Jialeng Lee] (4分, 8 months ago): Tried the garlic stir fried beef and crab porridge- both dishes delivered on flavor. The beef was fragrant and nicely seasoned, while the porridge had a rich, comforting depth with fresh crab. Slightly on the pricier side for what you get, but if you’re looking for a satisfying meal that leans toward indulgence, it’s worth considering.
評論4:
[Tony Fu] (5分, 2 years ago): Excellent quality porridges and accompanying side dishes. Pleasant environment with cool AC, still very enjoyable on a warm day.

We ordered two single serving orders, with squid and fish, both are delicious.

The popular fried tofu is highly recommended. We loved the pickled cucumber as well.
評論5: [Diana Hsu] (4分, 10 months ago): Just happened to walk by this place, so we decided to stop in and have dinner. There are a couple of vegan congee options. You have to order at the counter. Service was good.

餐廳名稱: PAPA RESTAURANT （台北師大店）／師大美食/師大義大利麵/師大簡餐/師大披薩/師大手工烘培/師大歐式麵包/師大下午茶/師大聚餐推薦/師大商業午餐/師大包場

評論1: [Tina Hsu] (4分, a year ago): The food was delicious, but the portions were a bit small. Drinks were unlimited. The staff kindly helped with the two little kids, which was very sweet and adorable.
評論2: [Silvianie Dji] (5分, 2 years ago): A family style resto. They serve western food with affordable price. One suggestion, it would be great if the menu got the picture on it, would be easier to order, especially for those who don't read chinese.
評論3: [Rol Rex] (2分, 2 years ago): No recommended, cash or app pay only  … Italian foods but not international services. They ask for payment in advance, when ask for receive they told me after meal .. what kind of logic … will not come back … don’t expect Italian bread… very local ..
評論4: [Tzi Ling Chew] (5分, 3 years ago): Amazing “jade” pasta! Chef told me it was spinach pasta, which is so rare to find in Taipei. It was a simple aglia olio dish but the flavors all come together very nicely, was not too greasy! The meal also comes with a bun so be prepared to feel stuffed. For the price of 198 (drinks included), this is definitely an underrated gem in Shida area.
評論5: [賴昀心（Amazing Grace）] (2分, a year ago): The food with rice is to light tasty and the spegatti is too sault.The service there is not good.

Google Map地址: 106台灣臺北市大安區信義路二段200號
評論1: [Michelle Huang] (5分, 4 months ago): Fantastic food. I had a vegetarian Greek omelet (without green onion, onion, and garlic), and my friends had hummus, french fries. and a pasta. My Greek omelet is full of vegetables and utterly delicious! And l saw the sign next to the entrance that this is a "women and children friendly restaurant", which means the facilities and ingredients are extra safe for kids and women, and they don't use any plastics that leach out Endocrine Disrupting Chemicals (EDC). The only thing that I don't like was the music was a bit too loud.
評論2: [J C] (5分, 3 months ago): Sitting at the bar seats overwatching the chefs and staff working the kitchen was mesmerizing. Food was great, service efficient and friendly. Place can get very busy. Do make an appointment during popular hours.
評論3: [Ю] (4分, 6 months ago): The Mediterranean style/taste was good. The selections were good. I enjoyed the low sodium taste. It could get crowded. Reservation is recommended. The restaurant is big, bright, and clean. The people who worked there seemed nice and professional.
評論4: [Ting-Wei Wang] (4分, 3 months ago): Lovely place to have some casual food- a bit different from all the typical street food around this area! People were very friendly, Jenson who served us was super helpful and very considerate. This is a place that we can relax, sit and talk while enjoying the food (very tasty!) in this chaotic, popular district.
評論5: [Michelle Ness] (5分, 6 months ago): After a couple weeks traveling throughout Asia we needed a change in flavor for a night. This was great! We ordered the Toasteria Salad with a local cheese option, octopus, cheesy potatoes and chicken shawarma. It was all delicious and we only wished we’d had space for more. It was pricey but given how little we’d spent at food markets and little restaurants we could afford the splurge. The staff were so friendly and helpful. It exceeded all our expectations and if we make it back to Taiwan we’ll come back again. Pretty good endorsement to seek out Mediterranean food in Taiwan!

評論1: [K “Momo80” K] (5分, 4 months ago): Great local cuisine restaurant with a bit of a different flare to local delicacies. Great choices and everything was delicious. Highly recommended. Always nice to come with more people because then one can try a lot of dishes.
評論2: [L Y] (5分, 12 months ago): A local friend brought us here to this quaint izakaya store tucked away from the main roads. Serves up great food and drinks (I don't drink alcohol but my companions very much enjoyed the highball and made 3 repeated orders each). Service is friendly and personable (as with the usual Taiwanese culture). Interior design exudes an element of old charm and it's a vibe. Highly recommend visiting!
評論3:
[K Liu] (5分, a year ago): Been coming to this establishment for years and have always been super impressed with the quality of the food. If I can give this place a 6 out of 5 star, I would. It’s that good.

Everything dish is created to overwhelm your taste buds with delicious explosion of flavors. Everything goes perfectly with beer. I like everything on the menu, but if I really had to choose these are my go to dishes:
腐乳鹹酥雞 (Fried chicken)
主廚雞油飯 (chicken rice)
地方花椒麵 (spicy noodles)
桔醬豬五花 （grilled pork belly marinated in home-made honey sauce)
串烤牛小排 (grilled boneless beef rib)
厚切黃金脆薯 (fried potato wedges)

The charming old school decor creates a warm, cozy dining experience; perfect for informal gathering with friends and breaking ice on hot new dates.

The service staff is energetic and enthusiastic; always ready to make your dining experience unforgettable.

As this restaurant is super popular, you should call at least 3 days in advance to make reservation on weekdays, and 1 week in advance on weekends.
評論4:
[Patrick Ho] (5分, 6 years ago): A very local traditional looking an good late supper shop which my friends and me search on google by chance.

Around 10 we want to have some good food and drink to eat and chat, and after searching the restaurants around DaAn district, we find this one is very appealing to us. Not only it’s decorations are traditional Taiwanese one, but most importantly it’s operated by a bunch of young adolescents. You can see the staff have put a lot effort in decor details, from wall paintings to menu and chairs/tables. You really feel like go back to the 80s or 90s. And the food is also good too, especially their signature spicy noodles! They use thick noodles and dry cooked it, make you want to eat bowl after bowl. We have ordered some other food and not bad too, together with rice wine make us have a good supper night.

It’s always good to support youngsters effort, and this restaurant has it own strong identity too (especially the Lennon wall to support the neighbor city). It opens till late and if you are finding for a good place and food after 10, this is a very good option for you.
評論5: [Quincy Ng] (5分, 8 years ago): This is the best Taiwanese cuisine I have ever had in my whole life!! No matter it's food or drink, it all comes with good taste!!! Every single dish surprised us, raised our eyebrows wide!! The atmosphere is comfy and so well decorated, Taiwanese-Japanese style. The staff are all very helpful and polite!! Nothing, I repeat, nothing can be complained.

評論1:
[Gustavo Orellana] (3分, 3 weeks ago): Buttermilk is an American-style restaurant with some really strong highlights, but also a few misses. Overall, I’d rate it around a 3.5/5.
I did feel it was a bit overpriced for what you get. The “seasonal vegetables” were underwhelming, just one type of leafy green and a tomato sliced into four pieces. The soup was okay, but nothing special.
That said, they absolutely nailed some dishes. The buttermilk biscuits were very tasty, the ribs were amazing and full of flavor, and the blueberry pie was superb, definitely one of the best parts of the meal.
It’s the kind of place where some items shine while others fall short. I would go back, but it’s not my favorite spot in town
評論2:
[Sam Wood] (3分, 4 months ago): Been here several times. Always left very full. I liked most of what I ordered EXCEPT the fried chicken. As much as I like some things, overall I feel the food is not up to the price level.

The service is very attentive. Great job. When busy, inside can get quite noisy; some customers yell like they’re sitting outside.

Burgers: 4/5. Great taste/toppings, beef patty is big but can be cooked dry sometimes, bun was crisp/chewy and delicious

Fried chicken: 2/5. Was quite looking forward to this, but honestly most bites had more breading than chicken. Two sauces are provided, one is quite spicy (good), the other is white gravy (I found it too heavy with just fried chicken…maybe if there were a biscuit served alongside…). My favorite thing about this dish may have been the watermelon :/

Carrot cake: 5/5. Oh yum. Huge size. Probably supposed to be shared. Whiskey caramel sauce makes it even more rich and delicious.

Popcorn shrimp: 3/5. Interesting choice pairing a few shrimp with breaded/fried pineapple and actual popcorn.

Key lime pie: 5/5. Oh yum. Rare to see this in Taiwan. The sugar crumble crust was excellent, along with the thick pie filling.

Caesar salad: 3/5. Avocado salad 5/5.

Crab cakes: 5/5. I would get these again. The tartar sauce is great.

Blueberry pie: 5/5. Maybe my favorite dessert here.

Fish and chips: 5/5. Surprisingly good (compared to the fried chicken I had). The tartar sauce is great too.

Macaroni and cheese: 5/5. Fresh baked and tasty.

Pork ribs: 5/5. Especially like the watermelon bbq sauce.
評論3:
[Paul Robert Gilroy] (4分, 10 months ago): We had the meal delivered and in spite of travelling it was really really good Fried Chicken. So American style with real southern US style biscuits. It was nice to have "外帶晚餐" with no plastic garbage 👏.
I will make it a point to visit the restaurant next time.

評論5:
[雪倫Sharon] (5分, 3 weeks ago): This is the only branch left of my favorite pasta restaurant! 🥹 I ordered the following dishes:

🍝Yuzu Pepper Clam Pasta $278

👉🏻Set B (with $208 appetizer + $100 drink) $208
✅Sea Urchin Cream Seafood Omelette +$30

For more food reviews, follow me on IG: yayosharon_food

Google Map地址: 10491台灣臺北市中山區八德路二段332巷16號
評論1:
[Jessica Chou] (5分, 3 weeks ago): If you’re looking for an affordable yet refined donburi spot in Taipei, I recommend YORUよる_by Dennis Wang. After earning Michelin Plate recognition for three consecutive years as a fine dining restaurant, YORU has transformed that same spirit into high-quality Japanese set meals starting from just over NT$300, making it much more accessible.

I tried the Mapo Fried Oyster Set. The oysters were crispy on the outside, tender and juicy inside, and surprisingly light without feeling greasy. The mapo tofu was an unexpected highlight — well-balanced in flavor, rich but not overpowering, and it didn’t overshadow the delicate taste of the fried oysters. The combination worked beautifully.

I also ordered the Yoru Kiln-Roasted Beef Short Rib Set (NT$430). The kiln-roasted aroma was incredibly fragrant, with a subtle smoky flavor that became more enjoyable with every bite. Each set comes with the main dish, three side dishes, and a bowl of soup, making the meal both satisfying and well-rounded.
評論2:
[Ben K] (5分, 2 months ago): Second visit. I think the one dish worth getting here is the beef tongue over rice (gyutan don), though the beef curry rice is also not bad. Can't speak to the rest.

The gyutan is a tender medium rare, with a nice grilled crust outside. The bowl gets maybe 5-6 slices with some yuzu chilie paste. (We use this paste at home and it's awesome on beef, pork, chicken, fish. Love it.) the rest of the set meal is good, but the star of course is the beef. I swear there used to be more meat in the bowl, but it could just be me.

They use a custom-built charcoal oven to grill the meats--the person was very happy to explain how everything worked--and it has an "air curtain" in front of it to control the smoke and smell, since it's a very open kitchen.
評論3:
[Henry] (2分, 3 months ago): Would NOT recommend.

Decor for bougie on point. Pretentiousness of kitchen staff with their tweezers: legit. Food quality? Meh at best. Ordered the tonkatsu ramen. They warn you that the cut of pork might be slightly pink in the center. Mine was past well done, white throughout. It was chewy and tough. The flavors though. I wish I could write more about the flavors. I really do. Because there was no flavor, I don’t have anything else to write. Same goes for the ramen. Bland. So little flavor anywhere. If I had been here before I would have known to have gotten the pork with extra tonkatsu sauce because I am assuming that’s where the flavor lives. It certainly wasn’t in the food. I see them sprinkling salt on their dishes so I’m not sure where the umami went. The restaurant also does not have any condiments of any kind on the tables. Salt or shichimi would have gone a long way.

While I understand that they have reservations and the seats are limited, there were still two empty seats next to me the entire time I was there. With about 15 minutes to go in our allotted time, the waitress came over to try to resolve our bill. She just stood there. I had to get up, go to the locker to get my wallet. Then pay. There was no subtlety about reminding us about the time remaining. We ended up leaving well within our allotted 60 minutes.
評論4: [Albert Yau] (5分, 2 months ago): Ribs is hands down the most delicious. The flame grilled and charcoal crispy texture is a perfect blend. Soup is miso flavor. Temperature is hot. Portion is normal. Egg is perfect.
評論5: [Pat tala] (4分, 4 months ago): The beef rice was delicious, but the fried pork rice was a bit too dense and chewy. Overall, it was delicious. The atmosphere was great, and the staff provided excellent service.

餐廳名稱: 阪前鐵板燒 台北中山北店

評論1:
[Kristin Chin] (5分, 2 weeks ago): First time trying teppanyaki at Itamae Wagyu Teppanyaki Taipei Zhongshan N. Branch (阪前鐵板燒 台北中山北店) and the dining experience was wonderful.

The ingredients were very fresh and the A5 wagyu literally melts in your mouth. Watching the chef prepare everything right in front of us made the meal even more enjoyable. Service was also attentive and professional.

At TWD 1880 per pax, I think it’s really worth it for the quality and experience. Highly rated for a reason — reservation is required as it’s very popular. Highly recommended if you’re in Taipei!
評論2:
[Yesle Hong] (5分, 2 months ago): So worth coming here! Since we love good Wagyu and seafood, this was the perfect choice for us as a couple.
First of all, the hospitality was excellent. The staff were very friendly, and I had a personal reason to be careful with certain cooking styles. They did their best to accommodate my request, which I really appreciated. All the food was fresh, and the portions were generous—we were completely full by the end of the meal. The dessert was also delicious.

Although they didn’t speak much English to explain each dish, it was clear that they tried their best to take care of us, and we were very happy with their service. You can pay by card, and they continuously refilled free tea, which was a nice touch.
評論3: [Gary S. Y. Tan] (5分, a month ago): Must visit in Taipei for Teppanyaki.  The restaurant offers a visually stunning, multi-course teppanyaki experience using fresh premium ingredients.  Nice vibe and cosy atmosphere here.   Reservations is a MUST as they are mostly fully booked most days and most time slots.  Special shoutout to chef Jill who took the trouble to explain in English specifics about each dishes, ingredients and how to enjoy each dish.   Thanks also to service staff Sandy for her attentive and top notch service. We will come back here again if  we visit Taipei!

BOOKING REQUIRED

評論1: [Yeng Quek] (5分, 4 months ago): Enjoyed our meal at King Ping Tea Restaurant. Overall a pleasant, friendly service. We ordered steam pork soup dumplings, shrimp floss wrapped in fresh lettuce, fried rice and braised beef noodle. Good taste, not too salty and good quality ingredients.
評論2: [BOON LEONG TAN] (4分, 7 months ago): On 2023 Jan during Chinese New Year, we are came to Taiwan for tour. Today we are at Taichung and with the cold morning we have a dim sun at this restaurant. Having a great breakfast with variety of choices.
評論3: [Kevin Ang] (5分, 3 months ago): Xiao Long Bao focused restaurant with lots of other main dishes and sides. A popular place.

The tables are pretty cramped and service is attentive
Would come again if visiting Taipei again

餐廳名稱: butcher jr. by lanpengyou 中山

評論1:
[Matt L Yan] (4分, 9 months ago): Great little shop for quality burgers and tasty fries.

Ordered the Bacon JR (double beef) and avocado (beef) + Large fries with JR sauce.

The beef patties were very juicy and tender, paired exceptionally well with the other ingredients. The avacado burger tasted very fresh. My only reason for giving 4 stars instead of 5 is that the beef patties are very salty, especially in a mostly-meat burger like the Bacon JR. The fries and JR sauce were awesome, herb seasoning was super delicious.

Seating area inside was decently spacious with 4-5 tables and a high top bar, perfect for any party size up to 6 people per table.
評論2: [Calogero Damanti] (5分, 3 months ago): Amazing burgers, and the fries and cheese balls are pretty good too. Recommend the Jr. Sauce, it's very good!
評論3: [Ben Shimon] (5分, 2 months ago): Excellent burger! Super soft bread and really tasty meat. Lots of unique burger options too. Get the JR sauce for your fries. So good we ate it all before we could take a picture
評論4:
[Andre Kuo] (5分, 8 months ago): Probably one of the best burgers I have in Taipei!!!! I had a salted caramel burger with chips and wings on the side, everything was just spot on!!!!
What is even better is that they offer patty swapped from beef to pork which is absolutely friendly to those don't take beef like me:))
評論5:
[Jonathan Koh] (5分, a year ago): Extremely juicy burgers paired with no-frills milkshake. No fancy gimmicks, just burgers done right. The vibes were immaculate as well. Loved their choice of music.

Seats are very limited so I suggest to order take-away if you do not want to queue.

評論4: [mic leow] (5分, 11 months ago): Fantastic ambience. Friendly service by our server , Ben Zheng and Ru Wun. Great recommendations on the food options. Service is top notch and food is exquisite. The bread basket is served warm and bread is fluffy.  A very sastiying meal!
評論5: [Jessica] (5分, a year ago): Gorgeous place for an anniversary dinner. Price/portion is great (almost too much 😂). Taste is great and staff are lovely. They took a photo for us and gave us a rose since we were celebrating. Definitely recommended!

餐廳名稱: 梅子台灣料理餐廳 林森老店

評論1: [Hazel H] (5分, 2 months ago): We had an amazing experience at Umeko. Each dishes was unique on its own and the flavors were exceptional, unlike anything we’ve tasted elsewhere. The staff were attentive and service was great. In the middle of the meal, the ladyboss came by and her warmth made us feel genuinely welcomed. The meal was truly memorable; we enjoyed all the dishes and will definitely be back again. Highly recommended!
評論2: [Peterson Tan] (5分, 8 months ago): This is an absolute gem! The food is simply great, offering authentic and delicious local Taiwanese flavors. Beyond the fantastic dishes, the restaurant itself is clean, and the nice music creates a very pleasant dining atmosphere. The serving staff were incredibly friendly and helpful, making the whole experience even better. Highly recommended for anyone looking for wonderful Taiwanese food and a delightful dining environment!
評論3:
[Sebastian Tan] (5分, 8 months ago): From its inviting interior décor and heartfelt hospitality to its mouthwatering dishes, Umeko Taipei offers an authentic Taiwanese dining experience that truly lives up to its glowing reputation.

Don’t miss the signature crab rice, crispy fried squid balls, and the golden oyster omelette—each accompanied by carefully crafted condiments that elevate their flavors to new heights. These are not just dishes; they’re delicious stories waiting to be savored.

The service is exceptional, on par with the finest 5-star establishments. The staff are remarkably thoughtful and attentive, creating a warm, welcoming atmosphere that reflects genuine passion and pride in their work.

Whether you're a first-time visitor or a seasoned explorer of Taipei’s food scene, Umeko is a must-visit destination that captures the soul of Taiwanese cuisine.
評論4: [jingxi yew] (5分, 3 months ago): Very good crab rice, fried brinjal, chicken really good, with excellent spring rolls! Meal rounded off with sweet and bouncy mochi! Highly recommend
評論5: [Anthony Lorbach] (3分, 3 months ago): Waitress was very pushy and brought rice even though we said no.  It appeared on our bill.  They have an English menu and they are quick.

評論1:
[joe burr] (1分, 11 months ago): I don't know how it got a 4.8 rating. Nothing special and the broth was not very good. Very light. Price is on the high-end for this type of food in Taipei. I got the spicy beef shank noodle soup and I would say the beef was average, but the broth was below average. Service was barely noticeable. There are other places to go, which I will search for. I was quite disappointed as I was looking forward based on the Google rating here.

No complaints about your beef, I just felt it was average, didn't say I wanted saltier beef.  Just providing my opinion here and letting people know what to expect.

I don't know what you mean about charged based service? I expect friendliness, prompt service.  Your staff member seemed uninterested and bored.  Just my opinion.  Have a nice day.

"Health consciousness" hmmm... Taiwanese cuisine includes high sugar bubble tea, deep fried high fat foods, lu ruo fan, pig blood, instestine, the list goes on.  You don't make any sense contradicting yourself.
評論2:
[Hejin Zulvy Izzabilla] (5分, 9 months ago): I stumbled upon this restaurant while looking for a toilet, but ended up having lunch here—and honestly, it turned out to be one of the best decisions I made during my time in Taipei.

For just NT$220, I got a generous portion of beef noodle soup. I wished the broth had been a bit saltier, but it was still good—I actually ended up finishing it all. The meal also came with pickles and iced tea, which was a nice bonus.

The staff were very helpful, the place had free Wi-Fi, and they spoke good English too.

Highly recommended if you’re looking for a quick and pleasant break from the hustle and bustle of Taipei’s busy streets.
評論3:
[Marshall Sherrell] (1分, 10 months ago): I ordered the spicy garlic beef noodle soup. It was...fine I guess? But it did not have much flavor, while at the same time it costs significantly more than nearby restaurants which serve tastier food for less money.

I was going to give the place 3 stars because I mildly disliked the food and the high price. However, upon seeing the owner of this restaurant insult the people who give low reviews on Google, I downgraded to a 1 star since they lack professionalism.

Here is a video I took while inside the empty restaurant in case the owner would like to question whether I really dined here, as they did for other low reviewers 😅
評論4:
[SS Jj] (5分, 2 years ago): I just finished eating 4 dishes here - sous vide pork tenderloin, slow cooked beef 🥩 shank, signature boiled egg and beef bone broth soup. The food was such good quality, it’s a nice place, attentive, friendly service and great value! and as a bonus - they even let me eat my salad I brought from outside!

I had a question about the pork at the end and the boss came out to talk to me, my expectations were certainly exceeded, I’ll surely go again when I have the chance.
評論5:
[Max Chang] (5分, 2 years ago): Stumbled upon this noodle place when trying to figure out what to eat! What a nice surprise!

For such affordable price range, we had the nice noodle with nice protein, soup, tapas and dessert! Awesome

The cook on the items are good, from the meat to the noodle, all the way to the lava cake! Such a pleasure to get to try this restaurant! If you had the chance, don’t miss it!

餐廳名稱: YABI KITCHEN 台北誠品南西店
Google Map地址: 104台灣臺北市中山區南京西路14號2F
評論1:
[Kelly] (3分, 4 months ago): Abit of each flavor from SEA regions - Thai’s pineapple fried rice, Penang’s fried kway teow, and hainanese chicken but neither tastes authentic. Not awful but just fusion.
Esp the chicken which came buried in flakes n spices.
評論2: [Jeffrey Hung] (3分, 3 months ago): Taste is good, kind of fusion, yet some dishes like 仁當牛肉 and 肉骨茶 are unavailable. Probably try new dishes next time to experience more authentic  food.
評論3: [pcm lim] (5分, 4 months ago): Went to Eslite to buy books and stumble upon this restaurant. Ordered the bakutteh, satay, coffee ribs, fried kangkong and char kway teow. Taste like back home. A bit pricey though. Cost us about SGD$60.
評論4: [Michael Wu PhD] (5分, a month ago): Wonderful restaurant with a southeast asian fusion spin to the dishes. I will definitely come back again with my friends and family.
評論5:
[Vortrox92LG] (5分, a year ago): Pataya Fried Rice ($295):
It’s their chef’s special fried rice and the taste actually hits different with a mixture of saltiness, umami, slight spiciness. The fried rice is well seasoned with various spices and covers some squid inside.

Milk Tea (Teh Tarik) ($130) (Cold/Hot):
The origin of this milk tea can be traced back to Indian-Muslim immigrants in the Malay Peninsula. In my opinion, not going to lie this is one of the best milk tea I’ve ever had, which tastes sweeter and creamier than Taiwanese Pearl Milk Tea.
There’s got to be something special added to the drink.

This southeastern Asian fusion restaurant is located on the second floor of Eslite Spectrum. You can reach here by the MRT and get off at Zhongshan Station.

To sum up, the southeastern Asian dishes they have made are absolutely authentic and impressive with great portions.
The service is pretty nice and efficient and the prices are moderate.
Overall, it’s a 9.5 out of 10!

FYI, you scan the QR code to order and the 10% service charge is included in your bill.
