You can leave your name and mobile number here for them to call you if there are no seats. Heard some patrons say the 酥炸水晶鰻 (if they have it for the day) is great though I have not tried it personally. Tried their seafood don and it is great. Worth the short wait.
評論2: [Teddy T] (4分, 7 years ago): We are so lucky having the seats to experience the meals finally. The meals are soooo good I like it. But the environment is not really good... u have to share table with others and crowd.
評論3: [Max Lam] (5分, 8 years ago): We came here on a Monday evening, around 7pm. We did not have to wait for a table as the restaurant wasn’t full yet. If you wish to come later at night especially on holidays then you probably have to wait a bit. The food has ridiculously high quality considering its low price level. Salmon sashimi was top notch and the shrimp sashimi was also near perfection. Don’t get deceived by the compact sized sashimi rice, you will get really full having it. The sashimi ratio to rice was close to 2:3, I mean that’s a hell lot of sashimi you get for that price range. Worth coming.
評論4: [James Pan] (5分, 6 years ago): Nice experience here. Fresh and delicious. Do go there early on weekends or lines are long and taking times to wait.
評論5: [Derrick Tai] (4分, 7 years ago): The sashimi is fresh, should be the best course at that day. I would say it's a good Japanese restaurant but not that great as I expected.

評論5:
[Juliana C] (5分, 2 years ago): Yummy food with many different cooking receipts.  Big varieties of beer selection is amazing.  Very nice atmosphere.  Relax and enjoy the beautiful night.

Google Map地址: 104台灣台北市中山区松江路182號2樓
評論1: [Veronica] (5分, 3 weeks ago): Every item we ordered was a hit. As a non-vegetarian, I didn’t feel like there was something missing at all. The salad with tofu was tart and v refreshing - a great appetiser to kick off the meal. The mushroom with egg dish, one of their specialties, was the favourite at our table. Reminiscent of the stir fried egg white and crab dishes at Cantonese restaurants except that this was fully vegetarian and very yummy. Would also recommend the tea based tofu and the yam vermicelli soup - especially enjoyable with cold weather. Service was attentive and friendly as well. Wish I could’ve tried more dishes but will definitely stop by again the next time I’m in Taipei.
評論2: [Dree Chua] (5分, 5 months ago): The mushroom donburi was so bussin. The taste was so vivid that I kept going for more. The portions are generous here. We thought donburi meant it has rice, but actually it doesn’t, so we ordered a bowl of rice to pair it with. The white cabbage and tofu skin was nice too, but the mushroom donburi stole the show for us.
評論3:
[Mandy Kuo] (5分, 2 months ago): December 2025
Loved everything about this place! The food, dining environment, service, and that there's no dining time limit. Super attentive service - the male server with glasses went above and beyond making sure our dietary restrictions towards refined sugar were accommodated given sugar is such a commonly used ingredient. Loved the taro vermicelli, fried mushroom, steamed and fried stinky tofu, and stirred fried bamboo. The tofu pudding was also lovely and nicely served in a wooden pot. Definitely coming back whenever I visit Taiwan!
*****
December 2024
Amazing food! Our party of 10 all thought the food was delicious! My personal favorites were the red tea taro steak (which fooled my father who thought there was pork in there!), taro rice noodles, tofu with tea sauce, and bay cabbage with sesame oil. Service was attentive and truly appreciated the main server, a super thoughtful lady, paying attention to my husband's dietary restrictions making sure to exclude added sugar to the dishes we ordered. The restaurant had a charming interior and also allows large groups to dine without time restrictions unlike many other busy restaurants. There's an elevator to the second floor where the restaurant is so wheelchair accessibility is quite good. Highly recommend this place!!
評論4:
[Sebahat Ç.] (5分, 10 months ago): Hands down the best vegan experience I’ve ever had. I was honestly a bit nervous about the flavor, but everything completely blew me away.
The pumpkin in orange sauce was a dream, the tofu (which they kindly made eggless for me) was perfect, the tempura mushrooms were next level, and even the rice, finally, rice in Taiwan that wasn’t bland!

Every single dish went above my expectations. I genuinely want to eat here every day, and I don’t say that often.

I know the photo I took doesn’t do it justice (it was a quick shot), but trust me: flavor-wise, this place is sublime.

If you’re vegan and in Taipei, do yourself a favor and go.
評論5:
[le ca] (4分, 2 years ago): A very high-ended vegetarian restaurant that was recommended by a friend, and it wasn't disappointed.  Many people think vegetarian food would be boring and tasteless, but the dishes we had were so delicious and we were so impressed.  You could tell the owner/chef had put a lot of hardwork and thoughts into making those dishes.

However, the price is on an expensive side.

-
A Japanese restaurant hidden in an alley 🍣

A group trip here is definitely worth it! You can try so many dishes. Highly recommended are the caterpillar sushi, raw beef salad, Matsusaka pork sikyo-yaki, and pumpkin croquettes. The caterpillar sushi has perfectly seasoned vinegared rice and shrimp, making it very refreshing. The seared beef, with its Maillard reaction and the addition of scallions and purple onions, has a richer flavor and isn't greasy even after eating a lot!

The Matsusaka pork is marinated in miso beforehand, but it's not too salty. The searing method preserves the tender meat, making it perfect with rice or drinks. The pumpkin croquettes are a must-try for pumpkin lovers; the smooth pumpkin puree filling and light batter are excellent! 🏷️Prawn Caterpillar Sushi: NT$430

評論1:
[The Straight Fork] (5分, a month ago): ***best lasagna in Taipei***

Bolognese lasagna - clear star here. It’s rich, comforting, perfectly layered, and just deeply satisfying.

Reginette ai Frutti di Mare - fresh, herbaceous, not overly sauced. Each seafood is individually cooked to perfection and not a dump-all, one pan approach.

Insalata Invernale - thoughtful in the blend of garden veg mixed with yummy dressing. Salad alone nets you all the macro/micro nutrients for the day.

The space feels homey, and the staff’s smiles are honestly contagious.  It’s such a refreshing contrast to a lot of new dining spots in Taipei where service charges are automatic but the service itself feels nonexistent and robotic.

The fundamentals are so well dialed in (quality ingredients, technique, harmony, chefs touch, and consistency….our 3rd visit) It  wouldn’t be surprising to see this place creeping into Michelin-level territory.  加油👏
評論2:
[Jay See] (5分, 3 weeks ago): The Lasagna Bar is a real one. A true hole-in-the-wall tucked into a quiet Minsheng alley. Some people complain about the tight space, but this is Taipei. If you’re afraid of elbows and close proximity with strangers, there’s plenty of louder places serving worse pasta.

What matters is the food, and the food delivers. My wife and I ordered the Vegetariana and the Siciliana, opened a bottle of Barbera, and settled in. The flavors are deep and thoughtful, the kind you don’t rush. If I’m being honest, I’d personally go for lasagna sheets a touch more al dente and a cheese crust pushed a little harder. I like texture and crispy cheese crusts. Not a criticism, just preference. Next time, I’ll ask if they can meet me there.

The room feels like eating at someone’s residence. You’re shoulder to shoulder with strangers, staff sliding past, dishes being washed in full view. No curtain, no performance. Everything’s out in the open, and it works because nothing here is pretending to be something else. Watching plates get scrubbed while finishing a great meal feels right.

This is comfort food without laziness. A place that knows exactly what it is and doesn’t care about scaling up or pleasing the wrong crowd. The kind of spot you hope stays small, crowded, and honest. Lasagna Bar deserves its flowers. Quiet neighborhood and a loud soul.
評論3: [高先生] (3分, 4 months ago): Their bread was tasty just hard as a brick. The meatballs in canned tomato sauce were as you’d imagine them to be. The tiny serving of lasagna which is probably roughly 10 USD wasn’t anything special. The pistachio ice cream was pretty good and creamy. The interior design is like dining in someone’s kitchen which is neat for a group of people. You even have to pay for water haha sorry but we aren’t in Italy.
評論4: [Justin Wang] (4分, 4 months ago): Small bistro offers many selections of Italian cuisine not only lasagna. The reservation is recommended for weekend since we are walk-in and sitted in the bar next to kitchen.
評論5:
[Ratatouille] (5分, 4 months ago): Honestly, I was pretty skeptical coming here since there hasn’t been a decent lasagna spot in Taiwan yet. We came for lunch and were lucky to have made a reservation — seating is quite limited. The restaurant actually has two connected spaces with the kitchen in the middle.

Their menu is bilingual and lists the ingredients, but if you’re not familiar with Italian dishes or pasta names, I’d recommend looking up photos first. The menu descriptions alone don’t really do the dishes justice. Prices are definitely on the higher end, so I wasn’t expecting too much at first.

We ordered the lasagna (of course), prosciutto e fichi, and ravioli — and wow, everything was incredibly flavorful. The taste still lingers in my mouth, and it’s honestly one of those meals that sticks in your mind long after.

I’d recommend sharing the dishes since the portions are slightly larger than typical servings in Taiwan, and the flavors are on the heavier, richer side — perfect for splitting so you can try more without getting overwhelmed.

The only downside is the ventilation — you can smell the kitchen smoke, and your clothes will probably pick it up too. Not a huge issue, but worth noting.

Highly recommend this spot, and I’ll definitely be coming back.

評論1:
[Daniel Wright] (3分, 2 months ago): I don’t usually leave negative reviews, and I understand that restaurant kitchens can get hectic, but from a customer’s point of view this place felt genuinely unclean. We had multiple issues during one visit, which made it hard to enjoy the meal and sat awkward at the bar.

First, our rice on our initial order was hard and tasted like it had been sitting in the warmer since the day before and I know that flavor well. They kindly remade our beef/egg donburi, but the second plate came with a hair in it and I’m sure it’s the same rice because they looked in the rice warmer for a good 5-10seconds and scooped the same rice to put over our beef/egg donburi. My nigiri also had a gray hair on top. At that point we didn’t feel comfortable eating anymore. I could tell the staff didn’t seem happy about the complaints and they were apologetic, so we just asked to take the food to-go and ended up throwing it away. Even the takeout packing was sloppy, the beef and egg were flipped over with the rice sitting on top and I just threw it away next trash can I seen. I did eat all the food just cause it wasn’t awful but they have got to do something about their hair. I truly don’t know how the guy wearing a bandana got a hair in our food the second time of the remake and the sushi guys grey hair in my plate. Idk, just bad luck I guess.

It’s disappointing because this place was highly recommended. I can see why people rate it high though..the fish itself is good quality, the nigiri portions are generous, and the soup that came with the meal was excellent. The anago, though, needed a little more cooking.

I think the kitchen and dining area could really use a deep cleaning, and simple things like wearing hairnets or hats would make a big difference. For a restaurant with a 4.9 rating, I honestly expected more attention to cleanliness and consistency. I hope they take reviews seriously because the potential is there, but this experience left us disappointed.

評論1:
[Lee Iris] (5分, 3 months ago): 不荤主义者 Taipei offers one of the most thoughtfully crafted plant-based dining experiences in the city. It's the Ding Tai Febg Vegetarian/Vegan heaven!

From the moment you walk in, the atmosphere feels calm, warm, and intentionally minimalist! It's perfect for settling into a slow, lingering dinner.

The menu focuses on clean, well-balanced flavors w all nature ingredients, and each dish feels like it’s designed to highlight ingredients at their best. Presentation is elegant without being pretentious, and the portions strike a nice balance between satisfying and not overly filling.

Service is attentive in a low-key, unobtrusive way. Staff are knowledgeable about the dishes and happy to offer suggestions based on your preferences, which adds a personal touch to the meal! Highly recommend this place!!
評論2:
[Sebahat Ç.] (5分, 3 months ago): I’d visited the Veggienius branch in Taichung before, so when my friends suggested lunch at their Taipei location, I was excited to see how it compared. Making a reservation was incredibly easy, you can book directly through Google in just a few taps.

The restaurant is close to MRT Exit 6, though the entrance is a little easy to miss since you have to go up a flight of stairs. Once you’re inside, the space opens up nicely. It’s roomy, the atmosphere feels calm, and the seating is arranged in a way that gives each table a comfortable amount of privacy.

The food was just as tasty as I remembered, flavorful, well-prepared, and served quickly. The staff were attentive and friendly. The menu is mostly vegetarian with some vegan options; I only wish they offered a few more vegan variations because the dishes they do have are great.

One thing to keep in mind: they’re cash only, so be sure to bring some with you.

P.s. if you write a review, you get a VIP card for future discounts

評論1: [Apriandy Angdresey] (5分, 5 months ago): A fun yakiniku spot where you get your own grill at the table. The most interesting things is of course the small shuttle bus that speeds by carrying your order, it’s entertaining to watch. Staff were super nice and welcoming, which made the vibe even better. However, the downside is that the food takes a while to arrive.
評論2:
[EmmaN CrisAnto] (5分, 6 months ago): It is my first time to try this kind of dining experience 🥰 very unique yakiniku restaurant with your own table for bbq and your orders will come with a little shuttle bus from their conveyor belt 😍😍😍 very cool and the food was also great 😋
Their service is good and i will come back here next time 👍👍👍
評論3: [B W] (5分, 9 months ago): This is a pretty cool spot in Songshan District if you’re craving for some bbq. They’re not all you can eat, but just order more meat if it’s not enough because their pricing is pretty good for bbq. I got the 美國安格斯板腱牛/美國安格斯翼板牛 combo set with rice and side dishes included for $489 and I was satisfy with it. Don’t come here and expect top tier quality meat, but the price you pay is good enough for what you get. The employee’s was also very helpful as well. I’ll definitely come back next time.
評論4:
[Jack Lim] (3分, 9 months ago): Walked in… and it was crickets. No one greeted us — no “hello,” not even a nod. After a minute, we tried walking in a bit. That finally triggered someone to say, “Please wait at the counter.” So we waited… for over 5 minutes… while 90% of the seats were still empty.
(We were first-time visitors, by the way.)

While waiting, we noticed a bunch of lockers and thought, “Maybe we’re supposed to put our bags there?”
We opened one — oops, someone’s bag was inside. We closed it immediately.
Two minutes later, a staff member came out from the kitchen and told us not to touch their personal lockers.
To be fair… how would anyone know? No lock, no sign, and they’re sitting right next to the public waiting area like part of the furniture. Honest mistake! 😅

🤖 After ordering, our food arrived via auto-cart — very cool and futuristic!
But then… nothing. No explanation, no one checked on us. Maybe that’s the point of the robot delivery.
Luckily, there were some instructions written near the seat — but only in Mandarin. Good thing I could read it!
It quickly turned into a “mystery grill challenge” — we had to figure out how to cook everything, locate the spoons and chopsticks, and grab our own utensils.
(My friend cranked the heat too high and torched his meat. That crispy beef became the highlight of the night.)

🔥 Thankfully, the ingredients were fresh and the meat quality was decent. Once we figured things out, it was actually quite fun — but you definitely have to take the lead.

💰 Prices were reasonable, and the overall experience felt more like an interactive food mission than a restaurant meal.
Just don’t expect attentive service — you’re the chef, the server, and the grill master here. 😄
評論5: [Albert Yau] (5分, 2 years ago): Yakiniku with a modern twist. A mini train capsule transports your dish directly to your table. A mini grill for a single person seating. Portion can be selected thru the ordering app. Meat quality is normal. Chicken soup is hearty.

評論1:
[John Lim] (5分, 6 months ago): This is a truly authentic Hong Kong-style restaurant, and it ranks in my top three Hong Kong-style restaurants.
The Hong Kong-style milk tea is rich, smooth, and comes in a generous portion.
The salted fish and diced chicken fried rice is aromatic, full of wok hei, and absolutely delicious. Prices are reasonable, making it well worth recommending!
評論2: [JOY CHEN] (4分, 3 years ago): good service and of course food is good. Nice they keep the building and interior design as original and Hong Kong style. Can't travel to HK but can enjoy Hong Kong food. The sandwich and radish cake are good 👍
評論3: [Sophia Shih] (3分, 3 years ago): I ordered curry seafood ball, beef chow fun, HK milk tea and ham toast. The milk tea was authentic and smooth. I liked that the sugar was not added, which can be adjusted by ourselves. Others were not bad.
評論4: [Miyan Miyan] (4分, 2 years ago): Really authentic in comparison with the HK taste of food. I find many so called hong kong style restaurants in taiwan with food that tastes different, so this one is a nice find if you want authenticity.  A bit pricey but I’d definitely go back again!
評論5: [Carmen Lo] (2分, 7 years ago): A so called "Hong Kong style tea house",  good HK style milk tea, but the pineapple bun with butter is not up to standard. The pineapple bun is small and not soft, in addition, not crispy topping, think butter but cost TWD60. A bit pricey compare with HK.

Google Map地址: 105台灣臺北市松山區市民大道五段173號
評論1:
[Daniel Santoso] (5分, a year ago): Great omakase. all ingredients are fresh and served well. the sea urchins (Uni) in this place is so good. It has a slight briny taste only. it tastes slightly sweet savory flavor with buttery texture.

the portion of their nigiri is not too big, but the fish slices are bigger than the normal portion of nigiri.

since I love savory and salty food, I like all of the menus that are grilled. it is served hot and the fat is so smooth and melt immediately in your mouth.
評論2:
[Wei-Shien, January Chang] (4分, 6 years ago): 💫tl;dr - The fish quality is extremely good and the nigiri is well made. It is indeed a worthy try.

🌟Revisit: Yes!

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Food: Really impressed by the fish quality and the skill set of slicing and “wrapping” nigiri. Personally I have tasted many Japanese restaurants before, however only a few of them can produce such firm and well wrapped nigiris. The texture and taste are really something amazing. It is worth trying.

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Prices: I chose NT$1,000 set today. The place offers 3 sets- NT$800, NT$1,000 and NT$1,500. NT$1,500 is only reserved only. Set price equal and above NT$1,000 would present with oyster.
NT$1,000 set served 12 dishes not including the free self-served miso soup. Uni nigiri and salmon sashimi were the most impressive dishes. Though the portion was not big and just about 70-80% fullness after the meal it was overall pretty satisfactory.

⭐️⭐️⭐️
Environment: It is like a street food setup, a “stall” next to the expressway. Therefore it could be a bit noisy and the air pollution could be bothering. Also if the weather is not good it could be quite uncomfortable. Cool day w/o rain would be the most suitable day to visit.

⭐️⭐️⭐️
Location: It’s situated next to the viaduct and by the pedestrian. No major public transportation can reach. It would take about 10mins walk from the nearby bus station. The easiest way would be riding scooter and parking in the lanes.

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Others: The staff was quite friendly and helpful. The chef was really concentrating and professional at making the nigiris. In terms of the fish quality and the cooking skills I would give out 5 stars w/o hesitation yet the environment and service could be relatively simple and even a bit crude therefore 1 star was deducted.
評論3:
[First Last] (3分, 8 years ago): I feel conflict about this "restaurant". The quality is great, but not the quantity or price.
It's a market stall, so no AC, but lots of fans.
It was $800 for a set, no other options, soup is free, beer is $150 per glass.
At this price I can dine at all you can eat Japanese restaurants that has AC and decor.
It's hard to recommend this stall mainly because of the price.
評論4:
[Foodie Attorney] (3分, a year ago): The quality of food in this omakase sushi restaurant was good, which should be expected because of the relatively high price  ($2,500 NTD per person). Unfortunately though, it was one of the worst omakase experiences I’ve ever had due to the chef’s unwelcoming behaviour.

Customer service is paramount in omakase. After all, “omakase” directly translates to a customer putting themselves in the host’s care. Unfortunately, customer service is where this restaurant falls well below standards.

I’ve dined in dozens of omakase sushi restaurants in Japan and in my home country, from relatively affordable to Michelin Star standouts. Chefs typically fall into two categories in terms of personality: chatty and personable or quietly focused. At this restaurant, I experienced something unpleasant and unexpected — a Japanese sushi chef/owner who was constantly conversing, drinking, and joking with one half of the customers (in Mandarin), while completely ignoring the other half. Despite being conversationally proficient in Japanese, the owner only spoke with my wife and I to introduce each fish in Japanese. Since there were only five customers in total, the chef’s behaviour left my wife and I feeling alienated and unwelcome. At best, this behaviour can be seen as poor customer service. At worst, it can be perceived as unintentionally, or perhaps intentionally, being rude.

On a more positive note, the quality of fish was generally high. The fish contrasted nicely with the well seasoned and warm vinegared rice. Some of the nigiri garnishes (e.g. yuzu kosho and pickled shallots) were interesting as well. The quality of the kaiseki dishes was also generally remarkable.

To summarize — If you value customer service at all as part of your omakase experience, which you should if you’re paying $2,500 NTD per person, I can’t recommend this restaurant at all.
評論5: [Blair Ho] (5分, 2 years ago): Japan quality! It was even better than the one I had in Tokyo last time. Must go In Taipei!

評論1:

評論2:
[C Rabbit] (5分, a year ago): The food was quite delicious and reasonably priced, but it seemed the restaurant was understaffed, so serving food and ordering dishes was relatively slow.

Also, I suggest the restaurant add a hook in the restroom; it's quite inconvenient to take off your coat without a place to hang it.
評論3:
[Allen Chien] (4分, a year ago): One of the oldest restaurants in the Minsheng community, this shop has moved from its original alleyway location to a main street location, but the craftsmanship remains unchanged, mainly serving home-style dishes.

The stir-fries are all common dishes; we ordered leafy greens, shredded pork with shredded vegetables, three-cup chicken leg, and sweet and sour pork ribs.

The stir-fried cabbage and water spinach were cooked to perfection, making them good choices for leafy greens. Personally, I found the shredded pork with shredded vegetables too salty, even with rice; the sweet and sour pork ribs were quite good, with a pleasant sweet and sour flavor; the clams weren't overcooked, with just the right amount of freshness; the three-cup chicken leg was essentially frog legs cooked in three cups, similar to chicken; the Hakka stir-fry was a bit of a disappointment, too salty, which didn't feel like Hakka cuisine. The tofu casserole and water bamboo shoots were average.

Overall, the dining environment is very nice, but the ordering process is quite slow. There's a wide selection of dishes, allowing you to choose your favorite home-style dishes.
評論4: [Je ming 振銘] (5分, 2 years ago): Free tea is provided. The vinegar-flavored sea fish is incredibly crispy, with a satisfying crunch when you bite into it. It's very fragrant and has a very unique texture; it's the first time I've had this kind of cooking method, and I highly recommend it. The eggplant and sausage dish features very tender and fresh large intestines.
評論5: [Chen Esther] (5分, 3 weeks ago): The dishes were authentic, but I prefer lighter flavors, so I found the taste a bit strong, but it was still delicious 😋 The owner was very nice, and the bento boxes were also tasty :)

評論1: [MR Lee] (5分, 3 years ago): Took my mom and auntie here for a quick lunch. The value of the lunch set and freshness of seafood were exceptional.  We had 烤海鱸魚肚 & 花鯧，it came with 4 dishes of appetizers and soup; plus dessert. Definitely recommend to take both desserts if you have the stomach 😋😋; cheesy mochi and brown sugar tofu bean curd. The cashier(maybe the manager) was also very friendly.  The food was delicious, although some dishes were sold out already.  Overall, it was a great experience and a place for small gatherings.
評論3:
[Phil C.] (4分, 3 years ago): I am giving it four point three out of five.
This was my second time visiting the restaurant. The restaurant maintains a very good quality of their food and service.
Among the items I ordered this time, I want to recommend deep fried chicken cartilage, and beef tongue stew. The crunchy texture of and the spices on the cartilage make it a perfect match with the draft beer. And when you start feel thirsty for having deep fried stuffs, the beef tongue stew came served to ease your thirst.
There was one thing very interesting. I found there were five rolls of toilet paper in the toilet. I think the owner must need strong sense of security when having nature calls.
評論4: [D] (2分, 3 years ago): Just a ordinary restaurant with unknown cuisine.  Some fancy skills as you can tell from the photos.   Not an izakaya restaurant.   Not a Taiwanese restaurant.  Owner's innovation or homemade omakase.   Just crunch your hunger 😂.

餐廳名稱: 富錦樹台菜香檳 敦北店
Google Map地址: 10548台灣臺北市松山區敦化北路199巷17號1樓
評論1:
[Welly Lee] (4分, 3 months ago): We tried several dishes here and overall had a delicious experience.

• 🦪 Stir-fry oyster with Chinese doughnut: Easily the highlight of the meal. The oysters were fresh and perfectly cooked, and the pairing with the crispy doughnut was brilliant — the textures and flavors worked beautifully together.
• 🌿 Cordia and white water vegetable: Slightly crunchy with a touch of sweetness, making it refreshing and light.
• 🐖 Pork neck with squid and stinky tofu: Flavor was good, but the pork neck pieces were cut a bit large, so they came out a little chewy.
• 🥭 Green mango dessert: Pickled green mango served over shaved ice. Unless you’re a big fan of green mango, this one may be worth skipping.

⚠️ Note: The restaurant has a NT$1000 per person minimum, so be prepared to order above that amount for your table.
評論2:
[Scott Nguyễn] (1分, 4 weeks ago): Worst meal we had in Taiwan. The food was either so salty (oyster & pork), or bland and fatty/oily (dongfa pork). We couldn’t even finish and decided to give up more than half and ran to Raohe night market right after.

The service was extremely subpar. We were given a table next to the bathroom, despite there were so many other tables available. The staff spoke very little English and didn’t even try to communicate. We asked for sparkling water, cold but without ice - was given lukewarm sparkling water lol.

We ate a 1-Michelin star Chuan Ya the previous night, they tried their best to explain the dishes to u. Here, they just drop the food on our table and run away?!?!?

What ashame. Not at all deserving of the star.
評論3:
[Matthew Tao] (5分, 2 months ago): This was my first time at Fujin and I’ve only heard about it through the Michelin guide! Wanted to try a Michelin star restaurant without breaking the bank. I’m from America and we ordered the 4 set menu and added a lychee pork to offset the (1,000 TPE) per person.

I have to say the experience here was amazing! The food was soo delicious and super light and easy on the stomach yet providing such richness in flavor and pairing that has been amazing for my friends and I.

Lilly was an AMAZING server who went out of the way to make us feel welcomed, appreciated and cared for. She gave us outstanding service and truly went out of her way to make us have the best experience at this restaurant!

Overall, this meal is a thoughtful dialogue between past and present. It doesn’t try to reinvent Taiwanese cuisine it refines it. Every dish feels rooted in memory, yet polished with contemporary technique and balance. A truly memorable experience that shows how tradition can evolve without losing its soul. I can’t wait to come back and bring others here as well!
評論4: [edwin Ilagan] (4分, 2 months ago): We came here to celebrate our wedding anniversary. And it did not disappoint. The food was great. We especially liked the braised tenderloin beef, sweet and sour pork with lychee and the braised dongpo pork. The braised beef tenderloin and the braised dongpo pork are new and yet they taste familiar. The braised beef reminds me of the beef pares I had in Manila. The braised dongpo pork, on the other hand, reminds me of pork humba. All in all, they served us the best Taiwanese food we’ve had. The desserts, on the other hand, were not good. We had the sweet peanuts and almond tofu and none of them were delicious. It’s such a shame because all the other food we ordered were great. Even the guava juice is refreshing. It balances out all the richness of the food we ordered.
評論5:
[Critsy Y] (5分, 3 months ago): There are a few Fujin Tree locations - at least two in Taipei from what I can tell - but this is the one that actually holds the Michelin star. Some people assume all branches automatically share the accolade, but that’s definitely not how it works. I’ll review the Taipei 101 location separately.

We arrived and were seated right away after the hostess checked the book. The vibe is modern, clean, and casually chic. Service was on point — well-trained team, attentive without and friendly. They started us with a little amuse bouche of watermelon radish that woke up our appetites. We ordered the minced pork with chives, century eggs & peppers, boiled chicken with sauce, the braised Dongpo pork with candied dates, and the Pu’er Tea sweet dumplings for dessert. Everything was genuinely delicious. When the Dongpo pork came out, the server actually paused to let me get my photo, then took it back to the kitchen to have it cut.

Thoughtful touches like that go a long way. This location truly earns its star. I can’t say the same for their other one. I’d happily come back and would recommend this branch without hesitation.

評論2:
[Patrick Selvan] (5分, a year ago): If there was a 10, I would give this lovely place that score.  I was super tired after travelling and wanted to have a good meal and sleep. Wanted to have the Risotto for some strange reason and saw the review for this place.  Food is fantastic, ambience was very cosy and music very soothing.  It was my lucky day and met David who is the owner and Fei Fei who’s the head chef.  I had the spicy beef risotto with peppercorn that was served Szechuan style, the pumpkin soup with bits of bacon, the cranberry cocktail and caramel pudding what was literally super awesome. Definitely recommend to anyone who is here in Downtown Taipei area.

Came back the second time on Boxing Day and it was even better. I had the 13 Flavoured Rib Eye Risotto and the Transparent Beauty Cocktail(did not have a description but it had Rum I think). Very crowded during lunch which speaks volumes for the quality and taste of the food 🤗

I ordered the house special pasta and it was delicious.  The spice was good and not overwhelming.  The presentation was excellent with big portions.  You get free drink or soup if you order the main entree before 11:20am!

Lastly, it is cash only and serving utensils and water are all self-service!
評論4: [Jie-Ting Jiang] (5分, 3 years ago): Excellent creative cuisines and super friendly staffs. The dining area and the kitchen are both super tidy. Everything’s well arranged. Such an amazing restaurant. Great dining experience. Highly recommend.

評論4:
[台灣金針菇] (5分, a month ago): Zhan Yuan Fu's mushroom and chicken soup has a simple yet satisfying flavor that grows on you, making it the kind of place you'd want to return to. The signature mushroom and chicken soup has a noticeable aroma as soon as it's served; the mushroom flavor is strong but not pungent, the broth is clear yet not thin, and it's smooth and mellow. You can taste the natural sweetness of the chicken that's been slowly simmered, without being overly salty or greasy – a soup you can enjoy for a long time.

The chicken portion is generous, the meat is firm but not dry, and still retains a springy texture. It pairs perfectly with the broth-soaked mushrooms. The mushrooms themselves are well-prepared, with a soft texture and a subtle sweetness, adding to the overall flavor of the soup. Chili peppers are available for you to add yourself; they are fragrant and have a distinct spiciness. Adding a little to the soup immediately enhances the flavor, making it both warming and satisfying.

It's worth mentioning that the black tea can be enjoyed continuously, a great bonus for those who like to have a drink while eating. The black tea is not overly sweet, refreshing and helps cut through the richness, a perfect complement to the mushroom and chicken soup. Overall, Zhan Yuan Fu's mushroom and chicken soup takes a down-to-earth approach, without too many frills, but the soup base is consistently good and the seasoning is just right, making it a comfortable and satisfying meal.
評論5:
[Lin Brian] (5分, 3 months ago): The second floor of Terminal 2 at Songshan Airport features a food court offering a wide variety of restaurants in a spacious and bright environment. It gathers many well-known eateries, providing convenient dining options for passengers and the public while waiting for their flights.

A half-day trip to Songshan Airport is recommended. Besides the observation deck for watching planes take off and land, you can also stop by the food court for a meal and rest. Furthermore, EVA Air has set up an aircraft simulator and engine model on the second floor of the food court in Terminal 2, offering a unique hands-on experience.

In addition to the food court on the second floor of Terminal 2, there is also a food court on the second floor of Terminal 1, near the international departure area. So, you can visit either Terminal 1 or Terminal 2.

[鐘阿邦] (5分, 2 months ago): This was my first time visiting. I ordered the sashimi-only donburi (is that a Taiwanese pun? But "donburi" is actually pronounced "don" in Mandarin) for NT$270, and upgraded to a set meal for an extra NT$120.

After being seated, there were edamame as an appetizer on the table, and hot tea was self-service. After ordering, the staff informed me that there was a complimentary drink (probably included in the set meal?), and I could choose a foil-wrapped bottle from the refrigerator.

The set meal and soup arrived quickly. In winter, a few sips of hot soup are always a good start; the broth wasn't too salty, unlike the overly salty typical Japanese style. The side dishes were also quite delicious, although the cabbage was a little too soft.

The chawanmushi (steamed egg custard) was also very generous, with a scallop, salmon roe, and clams. The karaage chicken was very tender, with no cartilage; it would have been even better with chili peppers.

The main course, sashimi donburi, wasn't huge, but the sashimi slices were thick, fresh, and delicious. The center seemed to contain tuna flakes, and the shrimp roe was a large sphere. Overall, it was above average.

By the end, I felt quite full, even for a male adult. It was great value for money. However, there aren't many seats for dine-in, so reservations aren't possible; you have to wait in line. There are waiting seats available at the entrance.

評論1: [Willy Lo] (5分, 3 years ago): Lovely experience. I had the 55days dry aged 24oz T-bone steak with salad and dessert bar. The steak is really delicious. The flavor of the steak is very condense and strong. The meat is so tender. The chef grilled it perfectly. Dessert is excellent! Everything is really yummy. Will definitely go back.
評論2:
[yuan yuan shih] (2分, 3 years ago): 5 stars for the food n 1 stars for the service.

We asked some questions and the waiter asked us to read again on the menu by ourselves. Rude to my parents. Mayfull might as well just generate a QR code for us to take orders. Easier.

We had the 28oz wiv lobster.  Semi-buffet is the second best in town. price range about $3500/person. Had a nice time only because we chill well.

will visit again but not anytime shortly.
評論3:
[Michael Liu] (5分, 3 years ago): Food: 5/5 Averaging 1,300NT per person
Service: 5/5
Value: 4/5
Food - is spectacular and reasonable for the price, the only reason the value - isn’t 5* is because the waitress insisted on charging us for water, they don’t provide tap water.
Service - is still great! They were very polite and even willing to wait for our party to leave after hours.
Interior Design is beautiful! Will definitely come back and will definitely recommend, such wonderful experience!
評論4: [Violet Bauer] (5分, a year ago): Pristine service in a fine setting without serious dress code! Choose from set menu with aged steak and scallop/oyster appetizer and a choice of soup. The salad bar was pleasantly abundant with lots of vegetable options and delicious desserts.
評論5:
[Chloe Tsui] (5分, 6 years ago): A wonderful steak house which never fails me👏🏻

Visited here a few times, the pics are from three visits.

We tried several kind of steak: the aged steak taste really different if you order different date, some taste like blue cheese; the Wagyu is also good but not good as aged steak (the thinner one).
You can get a buy one get one free for any seafood if you ordered aged steak. Seafood platter and lobsters are all good. ($1800for 2)

I will totally recommend the salad bar to you, only $800/person, with delicious salad, appetizers and dessert. Not many choice provided, while the quality is high. Tiramisu is my favorite, I always reserve my tummy for it!

Google Map地址: 114台灣臺北市內湖區內湖路二段103巷76號
評論1: [Thong Kurus] (5分, 3 months ago): A local, corner dai chow place. Everything from soups to stir-fry, hotplate, steam, fried, grilled, local '3 cups' marination. Ordered a few of their signature dishes to try. We had fried you tiao with fresh oysters, stir-fried green beans with deep fried pork intestines and 'dong po' pork - classic uncut version of braised pork belly, before it becomes lu rou fan! Fed us a family of 6, 4 adults and 2 kids after a late morning stroll and exercise at the nearby park. It was really well prepapred and cooked to perfection. Portions were medium sized. Just slightly too salty to my liking, but bearable with white rice and some brown rice tea which was served on the house. Good, kind, friendly service. Must visit if you are in the Neihu area!
評論2: [Karen Chan] (4分, 2 years ago): Delicious local Taiwanese cuisine experience! The oyster with fried dough fritters is a the star of the show, and the restaurant’s specialty. The other two dishes are tasty and flavorful. The vibe is typical, family style and cozy, filled with neighbors and locals. Don’t forgot to order a Taiwanese beer to get the total experience!
評論3: [SuN MoSs] (1分, a year ago): No longer what it was. Food bland and tasteless. Allows drunken, loud, and disorderly people continue drinking and destroy any type of dining atmosphere. Avoid.
評論4: [Henry Hsu] (5分, 3 years ago): Affordable food, generous portion and friendly efficient service
評論5: [Frans Oilinki] (5分, 4 years ago): This is a gem of a restaurant. The cooking is better than in many expensive restaurants. Dishes are made with high quality ingredients, prepared with love and appreciation, and service is fast. Big tables to enjoy your food with your friends or family. Passing close by, I came to eat here after some time. After eating here I feel so happy! It is like more than my body was nourished!

評論1:
[Yuan Chang] (4分, 5 years ago): A well-known place mainly for its donburi (among other dishes). Although the place is often quite busy, service is very fast — and hence turnover great. If you're visiting for dinner, the street-side parking spaces around are free.

Their seafood stock is very fresh, and the portion generous. It is a bit unusual in using warm unflavored rice.

Extra point for service (I'd give 4.5): no vegetarian menu, but waitress was quick to suggest ordering bowl of rice and natto — make your own natto don. That worked great.

Reasonable prices with the fresh seafood is probably why they're packing the place all the time. Recommended.
評論2: [Kate Shao] (4分, 5 years ago): Sashimi was fresh and awesome! The famous one was 無敵海鮮丼 but I ordered 生魚丼飯 which was full of raw fish and provided one raw egg yolk. Big portion for female. One donburi including Taiwanese Miso soup(many meats inside), donburi (hot rice) and free hot tea. Cause the restaurant is popular, you need to wait at least 30min but it's worth it! Cash only. Nice service.
評論3: [Wilkie Keira] (5分, 6 years ago): Yummy seafood bowel, especially like uni bowel. Complimentary soup full of fish and veggies. But not traditional Japanese miso soup. More like al the scraps into a soup, but still fresh and yummy. I come regularly when I wanna valuable seafood sashimi bowel. Highly recommended.
評論4: [Allen Suei] (4分, 7 years ago): Nice Japanese restaurant with local style, food are nice and services are above average. 評論1:
[Johnson Chou] (3分, 3 years ago): After read most recent reviews, we would give it a try.
Yes, some of reviews are true, the reception and  waitstaff not energetic, too cool, that’s how I feel. However, one of waitress came out from nowhere, her service totally gain back confidence of this place services.
The wait of brunch is reasonable, and the presentation is not bad, food quality is average, food portion might be bit small.
This place vibe is good and design is simple but up to date.

評論4: [Sharon Daniel] (4分, 4 years ago): Not many options for vegans but vegetarians can find a few things on the menu. I had the mushroom and vegetable pasta which can be made vegan. They added extra garlic and chili pepper at my request. The Spaghetti Alla Puttanesca has anchovies. The vegetarian breakfast meal has eggs. For 90 nt you can add bread, soup, and drink to your meal. For an additional 50 nt they will add a side salad.
評論5:
[amy tsai] (5分, 8 years ago): Excellent food and service. Highly recommend and would definitely visit again! Staff was very friendly and knew the menu well.

I'm glad I randomly walked into the restaurant. Would come again to try other dishes!

I forgot to take food photos cuz it was too good

評論1:
[Merve Shen] (3分, a month ago): Unfortunately, this restaurant doesn’t have an English name listed on Google Maps. With over 1,400 reviews, we decided to give it a try.

The space offers bar seating by the chef’s counter, where you can watch the chefs prepare the dishes, as well as rectangular tables suitable for groups of four. It’s clearly a popular Japanese restaurant among locals in this area.

That said, the food didn’t quite live up to the hype. The sashimi wasn’t as fresh as expected—still acceptable in taste, but nothing impressive. The chicken dish took quite a long time to prepare and ended up being below average in flavor.

On the positive side, the complimentary salad and edamame were surprisingly good and nicely done.

Overall, while the restaurant has a strong local following, I personally found it quite overrated given the high expectations.

餐廳名稱: 義沐 Eat MU 台北內湖店
Google Map地址: 114台灣臺北市內湖區瑞光路583巷29號1F
評論1: [Adi Rainbow] (5分, 2 months ago): Strongly recommend! Amazing food, affordable and the service is great- they fixed my issues after ordering on QR code. English friendly. I’m a blogger and this is at the top of my list of lucky findings :)
評論2:
[JF Lee] (5分, a month ago): From appetizers to desserts, everything was delicious! The menu is diverse, with set meals for 2 or 4 people available. The service was excellent. I would definitely come back to try other dishes!

For appetizers, I highly recommend the Thai-style spicy and sour squid. The sweet and sour flavor was very appetizing, and the squid were very tender.

For the main course, I chose the garlic and spicy beef spaghetti. You can choose between thin or wide noodles (the photo shows wide noodles). The garlic flavor was incredibly rich and intense, and it was a little spicier than expected, about mildly spicy. The beef was medium-rare, not too tough, and had a nice oily flavor.

The only dish I wouldn't recommend is the tortilla; the crust was a bit dry and hard.
評論3:
[ChunER] (5分, a month ago): A two-person feast, the food was above average, and the service was fast. Although the price was a bit high, it was worth the visit!

- Walnut Bread/Chef's Soup: The garlic sauce on the bread was delicious, the corn and potato soup was just average.

- Scallops with Truffle: The truffle flavor wasn't very pronounced, but the freshly seared scallops were very flavorful.

- Garlic Spicy Short Ribs Spaghetti: The garlic sauce was mildly to medium spicy. The steak was ordered medium-rare but felt a bit tough.

- Squid Ink Squid Spaghetti: Rich in seafood, the squid, shrimp, and scallops were fresh, and the squid ink sauce was rich. Overall, highly recommended!

- New York Cheese/Orange Milk Chocolate Cake: Both cakes were delicious and fragrant. The orange milk chocolate cake had a great sweet and sour taste paired with the crispy texture.
評論4:
[Wang Jessie] (4分, 4 months ago): ⭐️Double Feast Set Menu $1780

>Walnut Bread, Corn and Potato Chowder

>Seared Scallops with Truffle

>Pesto Short Risotto Risotto

>Garlic Creamy Shrimp Spaghetti

>Dessert: Mont Blanc, Orange Milk Chocolate Cake

⭐️Grape Yogurt Fruit Salad $370

⭐️Banana Oreo Chocolate Ice Cream Mochi Pancake $300


A popular restaurant in Neihu Science Park, specializing in pasta, risotto, brunch, etc.


The double set menu is very generous in portion size, perfect for three people. The added grape yogurt salad is refreshing, with ample fruit and a rich flavor from the cashews and walnuts, making it a delightful appetizer for both adults and children. The appetizers were relatively ordinary, featuring fresh scallops with a little salad, but the scallops and salad weren't combined well, which was a slight disappointment.

The main course, pesto short rib risotto, had a perfect balance of soft and chewy rice. The slightly chewy short ribs paired well with the risotto, and as you slowly chew, the rich pesto sauce unfolds in your mouth. Highly recommended for pesto lovers. The other dish, garlic cream shrimp pasta, lived up to its name, with a strong garlic flavor, accompanied by creamy sauce and five peeled shrimp. It's a dish that those who enjoy strong flavors will appreciate. The only downside is that the garlic flavor is very strong; it might not be recommended for dates or those who are more concerned about their image.

For dessert, we chose Mont Blanc and orange milk chocolate cake. Both were slightly less elaborate than the main courses. The Mont Blanc was sweeter, a safe choice that wasn't bad but not particularly amazing either. The extra chocolate ice cream on the mochi pancake was very rich, and the mochi inside the pancake was soft and chewy without being too dry. Recommended for those with a large appetite!
評論5: [KunBin Cheng] (5分, 3 weeks ago): There are plenty of seats, and it seems to offer a variety of Western cuisine, including American brunch, pasta, thin-crust pizza, and Spanish paella. The food quality is good! Of course, the prices are also on the higher side, but you can save 100 yuan by registering as a member.

評論1: [Chengkang Lee (Ethan)] (4分, 6 months ago): Plenty selection of food. Some are good and some don’t. However the service we got today from the lady was great and she made recommendations and checked on us quite often without bothering us too much. Good interaction.

評論1: [Jay] (5分, 2 years ago): Great food, service and atmosphere. While not authentic Sichuan cuisine, but the fusion spin tasted great. The Sichuan style Mapo Tofu was not too spicy but had a good numbing sensation and the tofu was so soft and the sauce was good. The fried calamari was fried well, nice and need crunchy and not too oily. The Fried Rice with Salty Fish and Chicken Strips was incredible, would recommend, the crispy rice on the bottom was so good! Would also recommend the Seafood with thick noodles in Hakka style, the noodles were just the right thickness and the chew was perfect (could use more seafood though). Not pictured, I also had the Sichuan style spicy beef in pot, which was good but the beef was too fatty for my liking, and the braised fried tofu (which I liked a lot, would recommend).

[Korius] (3分, 3 years ago): for this price, i expected much better food. the cherry duck salad is good, but everything else ranged from acceptable to subpar. the beef was terribly tough and sinewy, the salmon fried rice was disappointingly bland, and the seared fish (not in the photo because it came much later) was dry and tough.

i appreciate that the environment is spacious and the servers friendly, but they really need to work on the food.

The wait staff were very fast and efficient.  The food was all beautifully presented and perfectly cooked.  Every dish was delicious.  Highly recommended!

評論5: [Michelle Cheung] (5分, 6 years ago): The Lunar New Year Eve dinner is excellent—especially the seafood dishes and chicken soup! And service is so friendly.

Google Map地址: 114台灣臺北市內湖區內湖路一段372號
評論1: [Anna Chen] (1分, a month ago): DON'T order the squid!!! 🦑 I never give bad reviews but I was truly insulted by the quality of food and service here. I ordered the squid lunch set. Upon first bite, I tasted a weird flavor. I thought maybe I just wasn't familiar with the sauce. After the third bite, I could not handle the taste anymore and spit it out. I asked the server what kind of sauce they use and they said just a sauce for the grilled squid. They seemed annoyed that I even asked. Then they took it to the kitchen to "taste" and said there was nothing wrong. I asked if they tried it and they seemed irritated that they had to repeat themselves. They tried to gaslight me by saying nobody else had a problem with this dish, but when I walked around the restaurant to go to the bathroom, I saw that nobody else ordered it so of course nobody else had a problem. I asked the other guests at my table to smell the squid and they all stated that there was a strong stinky smell protruding from the squid. Clearly spoiled. Even the "spine" in the squid shrunk beyond recognition, a sign of being frozen for too long. The squid had a nasty taste and smell. Nobody else dared to try it. They offered me another main meal and I chose the chicken, which is $40 less than the squid. However, they charged me for the squid meal that I barely ate and said the chicken was "on the house." So I paid $300 for a $260 meal plus a stomachache. Not to mention the 10% service fee. It would have been a different story if they took responsibility for their spoiled food, but they didn't and instead gaslighted me. I'm not a chef but I know when food has gone bad, especially seafood. To serve that to customers without remorse is unforgiveable. I will not be returning here again.

評論1: [Jeffrey] (5分, 3 months ago): A warm neighborhood bar with a refined and quiet atmosphere — the perfect little spot to unwind. The wine selection includes carefully chosen red and white wines from various regions, including bottles directly from wineries. The owner thoughtfully introduces different varieties or simply chats with guests. You’ll also find a curated selection of beers, Japanese whisky, and even peated options, making it easy for everyone to discover their own favorite drink.
評論2: [Storm Avalanche] (5分, 3 months ago): Spectacular delicious Beef Noodle Soup..Very cool vibe..small cozy..It's a hidden Gem with great service and Delicious food...The local beer is good too
評論3: [Peter Waldkirch] (5分, a year ago): Whenever my wife and I visit her family in Taipei we stay in Neihu, and I always look forward to spending time at Chen 3. It has very good drinks and food, but the friendly owner (hi Angela!) and welcoming regulars are what gives it cozy neighbourhood bar vibes. It’s always nice ending a day seeing the city with a drink here.
評論4:
[Dru Lu] (4分, 2 years ago): Lovely little neighbourhood bar that’s pretty much the only option near by. It gets pretty wild with all the regulars over the weekend :)

The menu is simple with many Taiwanese favourites. The braised beef three treasures are perfect drinking food. In hindsight, should’ve ordered some braised pork’s instead of mid squid.
評論5: [Angel H.] (5分, a month ago): Food was great, the vibe was nice, and the service was AMAZING 👏🤩🙀 Definitely coming back if I visited Taiwan again!

餐廳名稱: 擁灣晨食所 台北內湖店 |團購早餐會議|下午茶點心|早午餐|
Google Map地址: 114台灣臺北市內湖區內湖路一段437巷4弄4號
評論1:
[H. Yuan (阿谷)] (5分, a month ago): I had a burger again, this time with a full refill, making it even bigger and tastier! 🤣

(Although less healthy)

Takeout only 😭 The burger bun isn't your typical breakfast kind; it's more like McDonald's, finer and firmer!

The lettuce was generous, the tuna topping was average, but the egg was exceptionally delicious.

The handmade burger platter was exceptionally delicious and huge!

It was noticeably thicker and juicier than a regular burger bun,

(even though I asked for no mayonnaise, no peanut butter, and no bacon).

It's really good for NT$109.

I hope I can have it again tonight 🤣 I hope it's as delicious and big as it is, and that I can compare it with other burgers in the area.
評論2: [吳佳頤] (5分, a month ago): Every dish was delicious! The fries and hot dogs were also very good.
評論3:
[Bonifacio Chu] (5分, a month ago): I had it at the original location in the Three Gorges area before, and the taste was unforgettable. Now they've opened a branch in Neihu, and the quality is just as amazing!

I highly recommend the "Handmade Beef Cheese Burger." The owner said it's truly handmade, no wonder it's so tender and juicy!

Friendly reminder: Please eat it while it's hot, as it bursts with juice when you bite into it.

Please eat with care =)
評論4: [曹語諾] (5分, a month ago): The burgers were super delicious, affordable, and made with great ingredients! 👍🏼👍🏼👍🏼
評論5: [韓天] (5分, a month ago): It's quite delicious, especially the handmade beef burger! Highly recommended!

they have much,beverage for free
can refill soft drink,rice and soup
評論2: [Jabroni Anthony (Basic Gamer)] (1分, 2 months ago): Ordered the curry rice dish plus a drink. My goodness was it terrible. Half the plate came cold/room temperature. The rest was luke-warm. Biggest waste of 400NTD. You can find better food at a 7-11. My biggest regret was not checking the reviews prior to eating here. Never again!
評論3: [Ben Johnson] (5分, 4 years ago): My family and I are big fan of Joyfull! The food is served quickly and seats are always available!
評論4: [Anthony] (1分, 2 years ago): Very bland flavor on all the dishes we ordered. They also forgot one of our main dishes. Maybe it’s good to come with kids since the drinks/rice/soup are unlimited. Other than that, skip this place.
評論5: [Chinwen Hsiao] (1分, 4 years ago): Great for after shopping, nothing else.

評論1: [He Andy] (5分, 3 years ago): It’s really good restaurant. The vibe is great. I think that could be celebration or YEP place. The dish is tasty. There is a parking lot for customer.
評論2: [034] (5分, 2 years ago): not bad
評論3: [Daniel Hsu] (4分, 2 years ago): Delicious food
評論4: [Shang-Wei Kuo (KSW)] (4分, a month ago): My boss treated me and my colleagues to a meal here. It was decent, nothing special. The decor was a bit dated, but the food was pretty good. There's a multi-level parking garage nearby, so parking is convenient.
評論5: [謝玉枝] (5分, 6 months ago): "The signature steamed grouper immediately caught everyone's attention as soon as it was served. The fish body was crystal clear, and the aroma was overflowing after being poured with hot oil. The fish meat was tender and smooth, with a firm texture and a hint of sweetness. Combined with the fragrant scallions, ginger and soy sauce, it fully demonstrates the essence of Cantonese cuisine: "clear but not bland, fresh but not greasy." This dish is not only a feast for the taste buds, but also a must-order dish at the banquet, which symbolizes good luck." "The shrimp dumplings are crystal clear and the shrimp are chewy; the siu mai are plump and solid, with fragrant meat and fresh shrimp; the radish cake is crispy on the outside and tender on the inside, with a savory and fragrant aftertaste. These three classic Cantonese dim sum, every bite is authentic and satisfying!" With such a high level of ingredients and environment, the price is super value and it is very suitable for family gatherings or entertaining friends!

評論1: [N Z] (4分, a month ago): For the New Year, IKEA Restaurant offered two discounted combo sets. We chose the combo that included a beef steak, half a chicken, and Swedish meatballs. In my opinion, it tasted good!
評論2: [Doreen Leong] (2分, 4 months ago): I often go to Ikea Xingchuang for food and shopping. Today I decided to visit Ikea Neihu. Roast beef was the weekend specialty so we ordered - NT$269 for 150gm. Unfortunately almost half the portion was dried and not edible. It was disappointing.
評論3: [LINN HTUN ONE] (5分, 7 months ago): Delicious food worth savoring — no need to rush your mealtime. Just be prepared for line up during public holidays.
評論4: [Ben Johnson] (5分, 4 years ago): I usually go there to get furniture and home appliances! They offer variety of stuff which cost you less compared to other malls! The food was pretty average and to be honest, the chicken tastes like tyre! I like the meatballs and gravy 😋! Wish they could rearrange the menu to something far better than the current menu! Staff look emotionless and working like robots! No smile, a serious face with permanent frown something like this ---> 😐
評論5:
[Ana Calderon Solis] (4分, 11 months ago): IKEA’s food is always a safe option when you don’t know what else to eat. I used to order the plant based balls but now they changed the mash potatoes for pasta (only for the vegetarian option) and that’s something is a NO for me (I want mashed potatoes).
I recommend to go around 2pm, otherwise the place will be totally packed.

Google Map地址: 114台灣臺北市內湖區瑞光路513巷22弄15號
評論1: [Paul T] (5分, a year ago): Great food shared with everyone. A banquet with Hakka and Sichuan flavors, dish after dish , they were very tasty! We enjoyed it very much!
評論2: [Sacha Holland] (4分, 8 years ago): Been here a couple of times and it's really very good. Not a fan of the cold hors d'oeurves at any restaurant, but the small fish and cucumbers with fish (apparently flying fish eggs) are ok. They had strange, tiny bread bowl with chicken filling. You can leave the "bread". Everything else is pretty much amazing. Soft shell crab, fish in lemon grass sauce, chicken soup. The broths were soooo tasty. Not as spicy as I've heard for this region, but just about right for me.
評論3: [Jimmy Jih] (5分, 8 years ago): Strongly recommend this unique twist of Haka and Sishuan food! The service was solid and the staff pleasant to speak with.  Would recommend getting the tofu. It goes amazingly well with rice. Their blanched chicken with tangy citrus dipping sauce I'd also recommend.  Take a look at the pictures I've posted! You can't go wrong with the selection.  Go with a small family to try more dishes.
評論4: [Leroy Chen] (5分, 4 years ago): I have been visited this restaurant since year 2009 and keep coming back. This place is definitely a highly recommended restaurant to whom like spicy and hot meals.
評論5: [DANNY LIN] (5分, 7 years ago): Excellent Hakka style food especially love the vermicelli with taro soup. Best ever and free refills too.

Google Map地址: 100台灣臺北市中正區濟南路二段18-3號
評論1:
[Charles L] (5分, 2 months ago): Perhaps one of the best cooked duck I've ever had!
This place is a hidden gem.
I appreciate when dishes take 30mins to cook, it really shows that they aren't cutting any corners by pre-cooking anything.
評論2: [Mark Ong] (5分, a year ago): I am very glad I found this diamond. One of the best meal I had for my trip. I kicked myself for ordering a 10USD pumpkin soup but I am very delighted from the end result. Multiple layer of goodness could be felt across each spoonful of goodness. You first felt the creaminess then the sweetness of the pumpkin. Aroma oil of shallots kicks in following the savoury crisp of croutons accompanied with almonds slices favour. All in one go. As if your taste buds took the elevator. Sea urchin risotto is not my favourite as it's not full of favours. Although the scallops are very fresh. Everything was great. The chef is of skill.  Definitely on my return list.
評論3: [Giovanni] (5分, a year ago): Incredible french cuisine, the chef's work is so close to a French cooking chef. Everything was delicious, we got mushrooms salad that was perfectly dressed, a tasteful rib-eye and a wellington cooked to perfection. The staff is super prepared and kind, very efficient. Wellington has to be ordered in advance.
評論4: [iris y] (5分, 5 years ago): Been eyeing this place for a while now but never found the time to make reservations. Came with my parents and they both were extremely satisfied with the food. All the meats were cooked to perfection. Though I regret not getting my moms beef cheeks cuz they were so tender and the mash tasted like butter! Lamb risotto was pretty good and the lamb to my surprise wasn’t dry at all. The salads were definitely their must tries. Loved the warm salad!!!! Definitely my favourite dish of all I tasted. Will be back to try out the desserts next! P.s. there’s a max 1.5 hr dining time. Might not be ideal for dates
評論5: [Jonathan Niu] (4分, 5 years ago): Ambience and food presentation were quite good.  Expected a bit more from the truffle dish as it looked amazing but the truffle shavings were quite light in aroma and flavor.  The risotto itself was quite nice as the rice still maintained a bite, which I've found to be more rare in Taipei. Uni risotto with scallops was quite good, although it's quite strong in flavor.  I've seen a lot of people recommending the chicken wings, but tbh didn't really enjoy them that well, couldn't quite place what kind of sauce it was but didn't enjoy it. Fries were OK.  Quiche salad was not bad but would probably not order without the +appetizer meal option.

評論1: [C Cc] (5分, 5 months ago): ​This is easily a top 5 curry in Taipei (in my opinion)! If you're near the Chiang Kai-shek Memorial Hall, you absolutely must try it! The beef is incredibly tender—it melts in your mouth with every bite. Just thinking about it makes me hungry! 🤤😍
評論2: [gloria djap] (3分, 3 months ago): Found this place near Chiang Kai Shek that has a good rating. The food was okay, but for the price, I don’t think it was really worth it. The waiting time was also a bit long. I ordered the curry set with soup and a drink. 評論3: [Kyle Rice] (5分, 2 weeks ago): Great curry, very unique flavor (different from most curry places in Taipei, with a lot of customization options), and good portion size for the price. I got the lunch special for 250 NTD; it was definitely worth the price.
評論4: [Jin Jun] (5分, 10 months ago): Cozy place with pretty good food. Staff is very friendly. I already mixed the curry before taking the photo so it looks messy but presentation had no problems.

Google Map地址: 100台灣臺北市中正區濟南路一段2-1號
評論1:
[Gustavo Orellana] (5分, 3 weeks ago): Su Hung is a Taiwanese restaurant with multiple Michelin stars, and you can definitely taste the quality in their dishes. Everything we tried was very flavorful and well executed.
That said, two dishes completely stole the show: the Taiwanese burger and the sweet and sour fish. Both were absolutely amazing, perfectly balanced, rich in flavor, and truly memorable. Honestly, I would come back just for those two.
A fantastic place for high-quality Taiwanese cuisine and definitely worth visiting.
評論2: [Bernie] (4分, 3 months ago): A close friend, James Hsu took us to an incredible dinner at Su Hung Restaurant, which has been recognized as a Michelin Restaurant for three years. The quality and presentation of the food were impressive, and the flavors truly delighted us. However, the portion sizes were a bit smaller than what the price suggested, making it feel a bit pricey for the quality. This place is quite popular in Taipei, and securing a reservation can take several months. They offer two dining options: a casual setting on the ground floor and a more elegant fine dining experience upstairs. Keep in mind, there’s no free parking available, so finding a spot can be a bit challenging.
評論3: [A.P. Li] (4分, a month ago): Its said they send their chef's back to Shanghai every year to learn and train more, so the menu is always changing and developing. They do every dish properly and right. It may not be "an abundance" of food, but every bite left me satisfied and content. Just make sure you have a reservation, because you might not otherwise be able to partake.
評論4: [F1 Racer] (3分, 4 months ago): they have some good dishes, overall taste is okay,  but if you factor in prices, it's just average. This restaurant was Michelin guide listed, not to be confused with Michelin one star, but are charging Michelin 1 star inflated prices. The taste for the sweet and sour ribs is excellent, however the portion of the ribs were poor compare to what they charged.  I counted about a total of 10 pieces, because ribs are mostly just bone, You're not getting much meat. The steam buns were good, nothing to complain about, but not wow.  The Sichuan dish has good taste, but lacked peppercorn which is traditional and customary in authentic Sichuan cuisine, giving it the numbing spiciness but otherwise it's fine. Again good enough but lacks a wow factor. The Daikon pastry stuffing did not impress, it was too little, and too bland, it was wrapped mostly by flour dough. Customer service was also not up to not only the price they charge but the fact that they're Michelin guide listed. they simply lack training, and the understanding of customer orientation, some of the staff seem to not want to be there, but only are because they have to be there. The staff lacked knowledge of their own dishes, and when questions were asked, the answer was a I don't know, leaving the customer not knowing what to do with that, what or how to continue to order. None of the staff was new, which says they don't have excuses not knowing their own menu, which is the primary job of a wait staff.  My dining experience could have been better by more attentive and knowledgeable staff.
評論5: [70times 7] (4分, 6 months ago): Delicious Shanghai style food!  Service was terrible!  There were three attendances, one male and two female ones.  The younger waitress was extremely attentive and diligent.  The other two were very lazy.

Google Map地址: 100台灣臺北市中正區漢口街一段15號1樓
評論1:
[Benjamin Shim (derpbenji)] (5分, 3 months ago): I’ve been here every single time I’m in taipei and I must say I have NEVER been disappointed. A criminally underrated Italian restaurant tucked away in a corner near Taipei Main Station lies one of the best experiences I have ever had.

To start off, the 2 person dinner set for TWD1480 is extremely worth it. It comes with two soups, one appetizer, one salad, two mains, two desserts and two drinks. I could never find value like this especially from where I’m from, where this would EASILY cost upwards of TWD3000.

Special mention to the Pesto Risotto which literally changed my life. I have never eaten risotto this good and I am seriously contemplating moving to Taiwan just to eat this more regularly.

Also, wonderful and excellent service from the staff here who are very attentive and extremely friendly.

Would I come again? I think the correct question to ask is how many more times am I coming back before I leave.
