評論4: [yingkai kong] (5分, 3 weeks ago): Had a really pleasant dinner here! The food was flavorful and well-presented, and the portions were satisfying. Service was attentive without being intrusive. The ambience made it a great spot to chill and catch up with friends. Would definitely come back.
評論5: [zlingyen] (5分, 5 months ago): The dining experience was absolutely amazing.The service was excellent.The meals are delicious and fresh.It was a great value for money.In conclusion,I hope the restaurant keep their warm service and tasty dishes forever.

餐廳名稱: 龍門客棧餃子館 (林森店)
Google Map地址: 100台灣臺北市中正區林森南路61巷19號
評論1:
[Nikireview] (4分, 5 months ago): Ordered the dumplings and the luwei, both were amazing! The luwei was especially good and definitely worth the wait (we queued for around 20 minutes).

For the luwei, I recommend getting the 豆皮,豆干, and their meat selection. The ayi can portion it out for you, just let her know how many people are in your group.

As for the dumplings, they were served piping hot and incredibly juicy inside! If you order them, I suggest eating them first before moving on to the other dishes, so the soup inside doesn’t get absorbed by the skin.
評論2:
[Justin Kan] (5分, 3 years ago): This unassuming place serves one of the best dumplings that I have ever had in Taiwan. The dumpling skin was thick, chewy and moist… and the meat in it was delicious. I would definitely come back here again for the dumplings.

There also seem to be many braised items available. I didn’t get to try them but I saw many customers ordering them. Will definitely want to come back here again to try them. But expect a long wait… but it’s really worth the wait.
評論3: [Jiehan Chuang] (5分, 5 years ago): I would give them five stars, it’s no.1 dumpling restaurant in my heart at Taipei. Their dishes and soup all tasted well and beef noodle also sweet and smooth, most impressive is that dumpling’s dough is thick and with chewy, stuffing still very juicy. If you come to Taiwan suggest you should come here to try it.
評論4: [Lindaaa] (5分, a year ago): This is family restaurant but look clean the taste is so good of the noodle and dumplings as well with the Taiwan beer. Recommended
評論5:
[Neil Yeh] (5分, a year ago): Best spots: Beef Noodles, $300 Lu-way, Pork Dumplings 10 pcs, Taiwan Beer 1 bottle

This is the best way to enjoy Provincial Taiwanese cuisine here.

餐廳名稱: 雙月食品社 濟南店
Google Map地址: 100台灣臺北市中正區濟南路一段7號1樓
評論1:
[Christopher Lee] (5分, 3 weeks ago): We threewent to the famous Xiaolongbao restaurant “G,” only to be turned away at the door.
They said they accept Google reservations only. Oh well…

There seemed to be a few empty tables inside, yet they were firm about seating reservation guests only. Perhaps it was for smoother operations, or simply out of fairness to those who had booked in advance. As travelers, we couldn’t know the full story — but the disappointment lingered.

With closing time approaching, we didn’t have the luxury of hesitation. I quickly checked the map and found another well-known spot not too far away. We naturally picked up our pace.

That’s how we ended up at Shuang Yue (雙月), a Michelin Bib Gourmand restaurant known for its excellent value.

The clam and chicken drumstick soup was clear yet deeply flavorful.
The chili chicken drumstick soup had a gentle heat that kept us reaching for another spoonful.
The braised sea bass was tender and delicate.
And the spicy sesame noodles struck the perfect balance between nuttiness and heat.
👍👍👍👍👍

Highly recommended — every single dish.

Looking back, not getting into “G” turned out to be a blessing in disguise.
We ended up having a truly wonderful meal at Shuang Yue.

Travel often rewards you in the most unexpected ways.
評論2:
[Joey Lu] (5分, a month ago): If you are looking for soul-warming Taiwanese comfort food, Moon Moon Food (Jinan Branch) is the place to be. We ordered a massive spread to try their signature items, and everything lived up to the hype.

The Highlights:
* Signature Clam & Chicken Leg Soup (蛤蜊燉雞腿湯): This is the star of the show for a reason. As you can see in the photos, they don't skimp on the portion size—the bowl is overflowing! The chicken leg meat was incredibly tender and chunky, and the addition of crisp mountain cabbage makes the broth naturally sweet and refreshing. The clams added a nice hit of umami without being overpowering.
* Fresh Oysters with Garlic Paste (蒜泥鮮蚵): These were impressive. The oysters were plump, huge, and tasted very fresh with zero fishiness. The simple dressing of soy paste, garlic, and green onions was all they needed. A must-order for seafood lovers.
* Braised Milkfish Belly (紅燒/滷虱目魚肚): The fish belly was cooked perfectly—silky fat that melts in your mouth and tender meat. It was savory and paired beautifully with the rice.
* Spicy Sesame Noodles (椒芝麵): These noodles had a great chew to them. The sauce was a rich, fragrant blend of sesame and a kick of spice (Sichuan pepper numbing sensation) that coated every strand.

The Sides & Staples:
* Braised Pork Rice (滷肉飯): A solid bowl of Lu Rou Fan. The pork was fatty and gelatinous (in the good way), creating that sticky, savory coating on the rice that defines a good version of this dish.
* Braised Assortment (Lu Wei): We tried the Braised Radish & Carrots which were stewed until soft and flavorful, soaking up all the braising liquid. The mixed platter with tofu skins, kelp, and braised egg was a great palate cleanser between the heavier meat dishes.

Verdict:
Despite the frequent queues, the turnover is fast and the food is consistently excellent. It feels like a home-cooked meal but elevated with high-quality ingredients and precision. Highly recommended for the chicken soup and fresh oysters alone!
評論3:
[Super Fragrance] (4分, 3 months ago): Ordered spicy sesame noodle, clam and chicken soup, braised tofu and tia pu la plus dou ge. All of $400.

Braised item and tofu was so so. Clam and chicken soup was a lot. Although some of the clam was empty. Chicken was tough not suprising but soup came with so much cabbage. It was mildly sweet and refreshing.

Spicy sesame noodle has the most unique taste. Very tasty and numbing. Addicitive. But it is kinda like a guilty pleasure because i feel that the texture is too dry. Even when coated with oil it is still too dry but the taste is too good.
評論4: [Sharon Yap] (5分, 2 months ago): Came here 2 nights in a row. Conveniently located a stone throw away from our hotel. The food here is really tasty and very well priced. My DD loves it and wants to come back again tomorrow! I highly recommend the pepper chilli chicken and pork soups, their flavour is simply unmatched.
評論5: [Htway Htway Htut] (4分, 2 months ago): Overall experience is nice. My fav must be their sea bass fish and oysters. Recommended 👍 dining time is limited and if you’re almost spending 50mins then they will come and remind you 😅 but their foods is nice and people are very crowded so…can understand about their limited dining time. If you want to taste good food,you can try this shop but if you want to chill and relax and not recommended because of their limited dining time 😊

Google Map地址: 100台灣臺北市中正區牯嶺街43號
評論1:
[C T] (4分, 2 months ago): We arrived just before 11am on a Saturday and it was pretty quiet, plenty of seats to choose from. Although after awhile it starts to get busy, so best to come here before 12.
We ordered their original soup dumplings (小籠湯包 $180NTD)and also their prawn and loofah dumplings (蝦仁絲瓜湯包 $260 NTD).
In general, I found the top part of the dumpling skin a bit too thick, and also the filling especially the meat from the original one breaks up really easily and not too smooth. The fillings are well seasoned. It's not the most expensive but also not the cheapest, $180 for 10 dumplings, I expect the quality to be a bit better.
The restaurant and toilet is clean, staff is friendly too.
評論2: [Ameily] (5分, a month ago): Had to wait in line for a bit on a Sunday early afternoon to get seated - the dumplings were great, NOT a big fan of the shrimp shumai - but the fried rice definitely beats DTF
評論3:
[Giang Tran] (4分, 4 months ago): We finally made it to this famous xiao long bao spot, and it was absolutely packed with customers! Despite the major crowd, the auntie working there was super helpful and managed to get us seated quickly, which we really appreciated. However, the food itself was a bit hit-or-miss for us. We tried both the crab meat and the pork xiao long bao.

Honestly, we found the overall flavor profile a bit lacking. The biggest disappointment was the crab meat xiao long bao; the filling had a strange, slightly sand-like texture that wasn't pleasant. The standard pork xiao long bao were just okay. Ultimately, while the service was fantastic, we concluded that perhaps the flavor here is a bit too local for our personal taste preferences.
評論4:
[akira yabe] (5分, a year ago): It’s good 小籠包and炒飯 casual shop
Taste is fantastic, staff are very friendly and price is reasonable.

If you want to enjoy nice 小籠包 in Taipei, we can come here
評論5: [CR H] (4分, a year ago): If you’re looking for authentic xiaolongbao and dumplings in Taipei, this place is a must-visit! The xiaolongbao have thin, delicate skin with rich, flavorful broth inside, and the dumplings are perfectly juicy. The quality is top-notch, making it a great spot for those who appreciate traditional flavors. Definitely worth a visit for an authentic local experience!

Google Map地址: 100台灣臺北市中正區青島東路21-4號1樓
評論1: [Cassity Holly] (4分, 7 months ago): Full house even on a Wed night. Must have a reservation. For this price and no service charge, I cannot say any thing about food quality. They are decent, more homy style. Some food are like a quick pull together of outside snacks… like the chips, popcorns in the salad… cookies in the dessert…
評論2:
[K Chang] (5分, a year ago): Food is very tasty and portions are big, compared to the usual French cuisine. The style is not exactly French to me. More like mixed of western style since it used many local ingredients. For example, the tea served in the end is very Chinese. I’m surprised that no coffee and French dessert were served.
Btw, salmon Wellington was fantastic.
評論3: [Michael Lee] (5分, 2 years ago): Small shop with quality food served with passion. Little crowded space but big windows bring in outside neighborhood resembles relaxing French countryside atmosphere. Staffs are very dedicated and service with heart. Advanced reservation is recommended.
評論4: [Kelvin Lau] (5分, 8 years ago): This is the restaurant that serves good quality of French dishes..Without menu provided on Sunday I came for lunch, I was asked to choose one of the  main course selections on a little menu sheet...The  ambience was great and cozy.  It costed me 800 dollars...Very enjoyable lunch.  Don't ever eat too much breads at the beginning...Leave your stomach a room for your main course. Duck breast was so tender, juicy and tastes great...Strongly recommend it.
評論5: [Jonathan Wang] (5分, 2 years ago): First time to this hard to book restaurant. The presentation is Michelin style, and food is good, I would call it a lower cost version of RAW or Logy. For 800 NT per set, you get way more than what you pay for.

Google Map地址: 100台灣臺北市中正區林森南路61巷17號
評論1: [StiMania] (5分, 2 years ago): If, only if you don't care about service but simply wanted to have delicious "sand pot", It's IT. The hot pot like "Sand Pot" were so delicious and warm, especially there's only 10°- outside. We had meat ball and fish head one. So great to go with rice or Shanghai style veggie rice. Also had some stir fry dishes which were also good. Most of all, the price was so right which worthy of every penny. Guess will come back more to enjoy great "sand pot"
評論2:
[K Chang] (5分, 2 years ago): Both food and services are great. Casserole dishes are the specialty in this restaurant. House made sour Napa cabbages are used in the stews, instead of regular cabbages.
Kung-pao dishes are great. Fried tofu dishes are ordinary.
評論3: [E] (4分, 4 years ago): We came for the infamous gigantic meatball. It was great! The rice clay pot was good. Veggies were fresh. But the oysters were not fresh unfortunately.
評論4: [お小賴] (2分, 5 years ago): Not the same as it used to be, disappointed with most of the dishes, especially the lion head ( Taiwanese meat ball) hotpot. No complaint to the meat ball itself but I disliked the soup.

餐廳名稱: Nola Kitchen 台北林森店(二號巷子口內、喜來登飯店後方)
Google Map地址: 100台灣臺北市中正區林森南路2號
評論1: [Anna] (5分, 4 months ago): Their food was absolutely delicious. I had chicken and waffles and tried their orange sunrise drink. It was a meal that I definitely enjoyed. The staff was very kind to assist me in getting my order.
評論2: [Joyce Lim] (5分, 10 months ago): Love this place. Great food and vibe. Not the cheapest - ntd1940 for 2 mains, 2 add-ons, 2 drinks, 1 dessert. Flat iron steak risotto -- tasted like rice in Chinese broth but so good. Steak was tasty and reasonably juicy too. Fish and chips was not bad, fried on the spot, love the thick and evenly cooked potato wedges. Add on salad and pork chop weren't big portions, but were well executed. Loved that the salad dressing was put on the side. Tie guan yin cocktail was creamy, sweet, floral, and had enough alcohol. Wheat ale was smooth. Tiramisu didn't taste authentic but was enjoyable, not a huge portion but just nice as an after dinner dessert. Restaurant played Jay Chou songs, familiar 2000s tunes. Service was adequate. Loved the vibe. We were seated near the door, couple of flying insects around us. Overall a pretty decent eatery for those who miss western food in Taiwan.
評論3:
[Sihan Goi] (5分, 10 months ago): Had dinner here with wife after a disappointing yet expensive lunch at Jiufen. Wanted more meat and some beer near our hotel, so decided on this. It claims to be a Louisiana restaurant, but I'd say it's Louisiana inspired. It's like saying Olive Garden is an authentic Italian restaurant. It's enjoyable, but probably not authentic.

Had a Flatiron steak on risotto, a fish and chips with a side of pork chop and garden salad, I had a wheat ale which is 1 for 1 all day, and my wife had a tieguanyin cocktail. We enjoyed everything. The steak was tender, dinner medium rare, and the risotto was tasty as well, but let's not kid ourselves - it's more like mui fan than risotto. We enjoyed it though. Fish and chips was standard, can't really get that wrong. Pork chop and garden salad was also pretty good. I liked my wheat ale, which was kinda like Blue Moon. My wife loved her cocktail too. Service was OK, but they close quite early and had to send my 2nd beer before I finished my first.
評論4: [Daren Chin] (5分, a year ago): This was my first time checking out Nola's Kitchen, and the food did not disappoint. The fried chicken and waffles were a delight, and the gumbo was creamy and flavorful. I will come back and check out the rest of the menu.
評論5: [JM] (5分, a year ago): One of my favourite meals in Taipei! The chicken and waffles & jambalaya are really tasty. Particularly loved the fried chicken. Staff was really friendly and helpful as well

Google Map地址: 100台灣臺北市中正區延平南路61號2樓
評論1:
[Tita Pizza] (3分, 3 months ago): The pork chop tasted authentic and flavorful — just note that it’s on the oily side, which is typical of traditional Taiwanese-style pork chops. If you’re not used to that, it might feel a bit heavy, but it’s true to how it’s meant to be cooked

We got the pork chop noodles and chicken leg rice -- total of NTD 360 for 2 dishes

The place itself is very charming. It feels like stepping into a restaurant from a 1980s movie, with that nostalgic, old-school atmosphere.

It was also packed with locals, and we seemed to be the only tourists. When we asked for an English menu, the staff were warm and accommodating; they even had a server walk us through it at our table. Really appreciated the hospitality :)
評論2: [Vee Tom] (5分, a month ago): 10/10 this was amazing. will be back whenever i’m in Taipei. pork is perfectly crispy and peppery. the dry noodles with pork chop is my favourite. super casual atmosphere that adds to the beautiful flavour of the food
評論3: [Amanda Li] (4分, 5 months ago): Made it there for dinner 10 min before their closing time, the old lady took my order at the entrance and went to kitchen to prepare the food before I even had the chance to pay. Not surprised that the hot plate of food arrived at my table within minutes of me sitting down. She showed me where the utensils were under the table, and politely smiled and wished me to enjoy my meal. I really love the courteousness I've experienced in Taiwan, such a contrast to the attitudes we get in America.  I ordered their famed pork chop rice combo, and it certainly met my expectations of what Taiwanese pork chop should taste like. The meat was still on the bone, thick slaved, fried in a crispy batter and flavored. The accompanying braised pork rice, veggies, pickles and ginger slices all created a wonderful flavor combination along with the pork chop. Even the bowl of bamboo shoots soup was well prepared. I had to finish eating my meal quickly since everyone was packing up for closing time. But I enjoyed every rushed bite and savored it as long as I reasonably could. May try to come back for another meal before heading back to USA later this week, because I certainly won't get a meal like this back in Seattle !!
評論4:
[Benedict Isles] (5分, a month ago): Dong Yi Porkchop is your authentic Taiwanese pork chop. The facade and interiors look like a scene straight from a Chinese martial arts action film, with an old Auntie manning the front desk.

Overall, super love the food! They were very affordable too. The crew was helpful, even though we were kinda lost in translation, they made extra effort to serve.
評論5: [John Lim] (5分, 3 months ago): Old school Taiwanese food with retro vibes. Food and drink are great, nostalgic food like banana boat is great. Although advanced in age, the servers are fast and accurate. Will return again 👍

餐廳名稱: 孫立人將軍官邸（陸軍聯誼廳）
Google Map地址: 100台灣臺北市中正區南昌路一段136號
評論1: [Johnny Hsu] (4分, a year ago): Honestly, it’s so lucky that we have a chance to have lunch in this such historic place, and it maintains very well. On the other hand, foods are average level.
評論2:
[Daniel] (4分, a year ago): Food was ok 👌 location is beautiful and very difficult to book a table. I found this place a little bit overpriced hence for the food only 4 stars.

Overall: 4.5 stars
評論3: [Families LC] (5分, 3 months ago): I thought I was at a southern cozy home with families, not hustling Tpe city. Be sure to enjoy its garden for guests.
評論4: [Kate Shao] (5分, 6 years ago): Good and unique place for wedding in Taipei! It's convenient (near to MRT 中正紀念堂 station). Japanese style house was beautiful. Staffs and service were friendly. Chinese cuisine was nice, especially the chicken soup and pudding. Had a awesome and wonderful wedding experience.
評論5: [Vivian S.] (5分, 3 years ago): It's a tranquil restored japanese house that used to be the place for soldiers to meet their future wives back in the day. The japanese garden is nicely maintained (probably not to japanese standards but still pretty). The koi pond is filled with beautiful large and small koi fish. The green house houses a Chinese restaurant with slightly higher end prices where you can dine with greenery all around you. We opted for the bookstore tea house where there's a minimum order of 2 drinks. For lunch there's a sashimi set and japanese steak set that includes dessert and a drink for 780. For the afternoon tea you have a light meal of udon soup or creamy Japanese style udon and some other dishes with a coffee/black tea for 350. An espresso is 120 and coffee is normally 150. Food is alright but it's quite nice to dine next to the japanese doors next to all the greenery. You can reserve a tatami room if you have a larger groupm Credit cards accepted.

餐廳名稱: 雙月食品社 青島店
Google Map地址: 100台灣臺北市中正區青島東路6之2號
評論1: [Ariel] (5分, a week ago): We arrived 10mins past it's opening time and waited for 30mins. The welcoming staff林韋樺was clear, efficient and helpful. The flow of ordering was fast. We ordered and paid before being seated so once we sat down, food arrived. Ms 葉宣岑was detail-minded in making sure our orders have completely arrived. Even though the wait was long, the fact that food was great bang for buck made it worthy. We enjoyed the dry noodles, chicken soup and oysters. The mesona and black fungus drink was surprisingly nice (not sweet). A little bonus is the pleasant fragrance in the washroom. The observed dining time is 20-30mins per table. Will come back but maybe arrive before it opens to minimize the waiting time in the queue.
評論2:
[Rachel Lee] (5分, 2 weeks ago): Good food and excellent service.
Brought my parents here for the signature chicken soup and the staff gave us some recommendations.
We didn't order the dried noodles and one of the staff asked us to try. She offer us the dish as a complimentary and it was really good! 10/10 would come back again!
評論3:
[조영우] (5分, 2 months ago): Moon Moon Qingdao Branch.
I’m not exactly sure why, but although they seem to operate until 2 p.m., we were still accepted—so either they don’t really have a real break time, or they were just flexible. For reference, we arrived around 1:10 p.m. and entered after about 30 minutes of waiting.
While waiting, a young female staff member in front of the restaurant was incredibly kind and attentive, which immediately put us in a great mood.
The space is a bit small, but that’s pretty typical for Taiwanese restaurants, so we didn’t mind at all.
As for the food—everything was absolutely amazing. We ordered eight dishes, and the total came to around 600 TWD. Every single dollar was worth it.
Highly recommended.
評論4:
[Pineko] (5分, 2 months ago): Moon Moon Food was such a lovely Michelin-recommended spot in Taiwan and definitely worth a visit 🌟🇹🇼 Expect to queue outside on the sidewalk according to time slots — we waited around 30 minutes to an hour ⏱️ Before entering, you place your order and pay, and once it’s your turn they call you inside. The shop is small, cute, and cozy, and they were playing Jay Chou songs the whole time which I absolutely loved 🎶💕

We ordered the signature set which includes all their popular dishes ⭐
The spicy and sesame noodles were a highlight — the texture was unique, super chewy and fun to eat 🍜✨ The sauce was rich and flavorful. The bowl is a bit small so I’d recommend getting one per person.
The chicken soup with clam was fragrant with a slight ocean aroma that helped balance everything out 🥣🌊
The boiled oyster was tasty though the oysters were a bit small for my taste 🦪
The fish was soft, mild, and nicely cooked 🐟

Overall, great flavors, memorable noodles, cute vibe, and worth the wait if you’re in the area ✔️😊
評論5: [Minze Chien] (5分, 5 months ago): A great gem that is well known! We came first time in August with my whole family. Everyone loves their foods. We also tried Uber Eats ordering from them in August. Absolutely love everything they have. This time is the second time we dine in. We ordered the herb chicken soup, oyster and dry noodles along with small dishes. Great service. People are super nice here. They even serve herbal tea while you are waiting. Expect to have some waiting time as it is very popular. Highly recommended.

餐廳名稱: Second Floor 貳樓微風台北車站店
Google Map地址: 100台灣臺北市中正區北平西路3號2樓
評論1:
[J T] (5分, 2 months ago): Absolute 5 Star experience.

We arrived late with only 10 minutes to order before the kitchen closes.

We were served by the very professional and helpful Miss Rachel. Fantastic service and recommendation. Order done and we were seated to a wonderful window seat all on under 8 minutes.

All the dishes recommended were fantastic.

The breakfast set comes with 2 drinks with the coffee being refillable AND choice of a different hot beverage upon refill. This is almost unheard of.

Both Pasta We ordered were cooked to perfection. The mushrooms were prepared in a crispy manner and infused thoroughly with truffle favors.

The sausage pasta was cooked with a wonderfully balanced cream sauce , rich without being overwhelming halfway through the meal.

As it was the Winter Solstice , 冬至， the restaurant offered free Tang Yuan for all of us what a pleasant end to a fabulous dining experience.

The crown jewel experience is the view of Taipei 101 from the window seats. This only is worth the meal price and more.

This restaurant is A must visit for anyone coming around the 101 area.
評論2: [Shelley Lee] (5分, a year ago): I seldom hand out 5-star ratings to restaurants, but this second-floor restaurant truly deserves top marks! Even though it can get quite crowded during dinner, the staff is consistently polite and cheerful. As you can see from my meal photos, the dishes looked exactly like they did on the menu—what a pleasant surprise! The portions were generous and the food was absolutely delicious. I have to give a special shout-out to Leo, the server who attended our table. His big smile and passionate, upbeat attitude made our dining experience even more enjoyable! This was definitely one of the best services we’ve had in recent memory!
評論3:
[Vortrox92LG] (5分, a year ago): Truffle Fries ($250):
They were nicely fried and tasted so amazing with the truffle and the cheese shreds.

Crispy Calamari Rings ($290):
The rings were so chewy and crunchy but a lot tastier with the special tangy ketchup. These were definitely the best calamari I’ve ever had from the bottom of my heart.

Kale Juice ($200):
This was my first time drinking kale juice which was not as thick as it seemed and tasted kinda sweet.

In conclusion, the restaurant has always been crowded whether on weekends or weekdays noons and nights so reservation
in advance is highly suggested. Despite the high prices, the foods were just perfect and got large portions.
Overall, it’s a 10 out of 10!

FYI, you scan the QR code (English available) to the place order and 10% service charge is included.
評論4: [Simon T Bramley] (3分, 2 years ago): Very convenient if you’re in Taipei Main Station, and need decent vegetarian options - of which there are quite a few. The food was better than I anticipated, so that’s a plus. The ordering process was a bit of a nightmare, however. You get full colour menus, with a supplement in English, but you then have to scan a QR code on a receipt that takes you to an online menu that is very hard to navigate, especially if your first language isn’t Chinese. Even the waitress needed time to help us order, which seems counterproductive. Adding to your order after the fact made it even more complicated. It also didn’t help that alongside the noise of other customers, the music was a bit too loud. It really put us off a bit, but was saved by the very decent food.
評論5:
[Scott Ng] (5分, 7 years ago): Nice cafe with good ambience and food. More importantly the service is great. Especially their service staff called Cindy. Very professional and friendly too. I ordered their pasta and egg Benedict with beef. Pasta is just right and the sauce flavorful and savory. The egg Benedict with beef is the highlight. Egg is soft and moist in the center as you cut it open. Beef is moist and tender too. The homemade bread is also excellently done. Crusty on the outside and soft in the inside.
Overall it is an excellent dining experience. However, the cafe can be very popular and lunch is packed with people hence can be noisy. This is the only minus I find. Too bad I didn't try their signature drinks but it should be of a good standard too.

Google Map地址: 100台灣臺北市中正區青島東路7之5號號
評論1: [Jingwei He] (5分, 2 weeks ago): It was a very nice dinner when I visited Taiwan. The three cups chicken and sweet sour porks are so good. The restaurant is just crossed street from Sheraton where I stayed, so convenient!
評論2: [Jenny Liu] (3分, 6 months ago): Food was a bit too salty for me!!! Best dish was the Fried Tofu!! Soooooo good!!!
評論3: [S Ong] (5分, 9 months ago): Found this restaurant from Google map. It was not located at the main road and you really need to use Google map to guide you. The serving portion was right, the price was reasonable and we enjoyed the food.
評論4: [Kanna Ogurie] (4分, 7 years ago): Interior is very clean and the servers are fun and nice, but not always attentive. The food is not the cheapest, but the quality is good. I recommend the Kung Pao Chicken.
評論5: [charlene cabioch] (5分, 2 years ago): Amazing food and service!! Thank you so much for the good recommendations of the meals : tofu, lotus, rice and soupe 🍲❤️✨

Google Map地址: 100台灣臺北市中正區仁愛路一段1號
評論1: [Ni Cheng (Rina)] (5分, a year ago): The spaghetti with garlic shrimp was absolutely delicious, with perfectly cooked pasta and juicy shrimp. If you are looking for a cozy place with excellent cuisine and friendly staff, this is highly recommended!
評論2: [Wei Jen Huang] (1分, 5 months ago): For below reply, you mistaken me to someone else. I only order one ice coffee. I never had any verbal disputes with anyone in the restaurant in anytime.
評論3: [Chi-Chuan Yeh] (5分, a year ago): Very delicious food！ Nice place for meeting friends！
評論4:
[Tracy] (5分, a month ago): ⭐ A hidden gem restaurant is tucked away inside the National Taiwan University College of Medicine Library! 😂📚✨

I wasn't there to study, I was drawn in by the food.

The meat sauce crepe with vegetables was insanely fragrant.

The chickpea salad was so refreshing it felt like a healthy advertisement.

The curry had perfectly balanced spices.

The pesto seafood risotto was delicious.

The berry pancakes were a total dessert lover's dream! ❤️🫐 The pancakes were crispy on the outside and soft on the inside, piled high with berries—the sweet and sour flavor was just right. Add ice cream and it was a total win; one bite and my mood increased by 200%.

The environment was quiet and comfortable, not rushed at all, super chill—a true hidden gem in the library.

✨ Conclusion: I didn't come for the books, I came for the food. Highly recommended! 🔥🍽️🧇
評論5: [Chen Angela] (4分, 3 weeks ago): The restaurant, hidden inside the library, was quite hard to find. The paper-wrapped seafood trio became too salty after the second bite. The caramel crispy milk tea looked good, but tasted rather bland.

餐廳名稱: 大樹義麵坊-2026平價排隊美食 台北中正義大利麵 焗烤 商業午餐 外送推薦 熱門義式料理餐廳 訂位推薦 團體學校安親班訂餐外送 聚餐包場 臺大醫院 中正紀念堂附近美食 PTT Dcard
Google Map地址: 100台灣臺北市中正區林森南路45-1號
評論1:
[Paul Yang] (5分, 8 months ago): 5/5 — Vegetable pasta with green sauce
3/5 — corn soup
5/5 — clam pasta with green sauce and cheese (got this twice) —
4/5 — scallop and shrimp pasta with white sauce.  The scallops and shrimp were good, but the white sauce wasn’t that flavorful, and the pasta was pasty.  Maybe I would have liked it with green sauce.
Best pasta I’ve had in Taiwan.  Green sauce and cheese on top were great.  Got clam for meat.  Corn soup with bread on top was ok.  Bread slices with pesto sauce was pretty good.

Update 7-13-23

rigatoni with tomato meat sauce is ok.  4/5
The menu said beef in chinese which usually means beef slices, but it was ground beef which makes sense as a pasta sauce.  Cheese on top is good
評論2:
[Achmad Rizal] (5分, a year ago): I really like the baked rice. I ordered  6C雞肉焗飯. The green sauce tastes very good.
For the bread, it is just like normal bread.
評論3:
[Tina Hsu] (5分, 2 years ago): Tastes great.
You can choose any type of sauce and noodles.
Cash only. No service fee.
評論4: [Marcus CYL] (5分, a year ago): Good food, good vibe, good people.
評論5: [Mac B] (4分, 3 years ago): Decent, but nothing amazing. A very affordable price point for a set meal, complete with drinks and soup.

Google Map地址: 100台灣臺北市中正區羅斯福路二段14號
評論1: [le ca] (5分, 2 years ago): First meal in Taipei and it made a very good start.   It is a very small local place and semi self service, ever dishes we ordered tasted good and would love to go back to try more dishes.  Highly recommended.
評論2: [Wez360] (5分, 2 years ago): I enjoyed the food. The hot and sour soup was really good. All the food was tasty, thought I prefer Din Tai Fung’a xiaolongbao. These weren’t far behind as the soup dumpling skins were quite thin and QQ.
評論3: [Joann Bee] (5分, a year ago): 11.4.24: Amazing! Food was amazing. I've been here twice, and I love their 小笼包 (160 NTD) and 白菜年糕 (180 NTD). I tried their shao mai, but it was so dry. These two dishes made up for it. There is also an English menu for foreigners.
評論4: [Lily Tsai] (5分, 2 years ago): The xiao long bao and the pork chop fried rice here are excellent. Actually, all the food here is tasty and quick. Utensils and sauces are self-serve. No restrooms, but you can ask for a key to a bathroom next door. It’s close to Mandarin Daily News, which is convenient either before or after kids get out of class there. The staff are friendly and easy going. It gets busy during lunch. Highly recommend getting a pastry (bing) as those come out immediately (or get it from the window for on the go). Cash only. We’ve eaten here many times!
評論5: [彭TJPeng] (5分, 3 years ago): Omg what the heck how have I been living in Taipei for the last 15. Years and never been to this joint. Forget about Ding Tai Feng   This place is the real deal like Holyfield.  The quality of the ingredients and the craftsmanship of the food and flavor profiles is superb. The best fried rice I’ve ever had. And the best sweet dumplings as well as veggie dumplings as well. Also the soup dumplings is the shizzz YO. We got there around eight and a lot of the dishes were already sold out

餐廳名稱: 台北喜來登大飯店-請客樓
Google Map地址: 100台灣臺北市中正區忠孝東路一段12號17樓
評論1:
[Wayland] (4分, 3 months ago): Excellent Taiwanese food, especially the soups. The hand pulled chicken with crab was very innovative. There was only one dish I thought was exited poorly, the sesame chicken rice wasn't very different from what I've had before and the chicken was dry.

I almost gave 5 stars but the service could be improved. Nothing terrible about it but if the server is going to prepare and dish every course on the side and serve to us (instead of communal sharing), they need more than one server because it was very slow.

The room offers good privacy.
評論2: [jcl] (5分, 4 months ago): Taipei's Guest House (請客樓) offers a refined dining experience perfect for sharing. We successfully split a set menu and added a la carte dishes. The signature "Whispered" appetizer and the fragrant Sesame Oil Chicken Rice are absolute, must-order highlights. This distinctive, high-quality Sichuan and Huaiyang cuisine provides a truly memorable and sophisticated meal.
評論3: [Daniel Wang] (5分, 4 months ago): Very nice place and food is delicious and display is nice. As one of us knows the place very well so we got several complimental food, almost can't finish all dishes. This place really have its level and keep it well. I like spicy and they do have many dishes with right spicy and taste wonderful
評論4:
[Ashley Hung] (5分, 7 months ago): This was glorious. I totally get why people rave about guest house. Some highlights:

The thin beef jerky was so crispy and flavorful. Beef stir was surprisingly so spicy and so good. 水煮魚 is always a fav and this one did not disappoint with the 嘛. Mapo tofu oh my god. I’m not one for tofu, not even mapo tofu, but I could eat endless amounts of this one. Rice noodles just kept ending up in my mouth… I was so full at this point and yet I couldn’t stop. They also perfected the 麻油雞飯 flavor wow
評論5:
[Be Present] (5分, 9 months ago): As per its name, a presentable place to indulge guests.

Food is good.  Our favorite (must ordered) is the sea cucumber, flavorful, bouncy and chewy.  The highly recommended applewood infused short ribs is well prepared though the meat could be better selected so it won’t have too much tendon/tough part.   The hawthorn juice is delicious.

Amazing service - helpful in highlighting special dishes; apportion soups and juice on the side and serve to each guest.

Overall great service, good food.  Pricing is on the high end.  Averages NTD 2090 per head for lunch.

Google Map地址: 100台灣臺北市中正區懷寧街64號
評論1:
[Stefanie Gu] (4分, 3 months ago): We stumbled upon this place during a night walk in April and finally decided to give it a try since it’s super close to our hotel. We were immediately greeted upon entering and seated quickly. The ambience was pleasant, and the place felt very clean.
The menu was impressive—probably the biggest and heaviest we’ve ever seen! I appreciated the pictures inside, which made choosing easier (though it still took us a while). Our eyes were definitely bigger than our stomachs at this point.
We ordered the following:

1.  Roasted Peking duck ( 2 ways - wrap and porridge)
2. Oyster omelet
3. Stuff melon with meat
4. Fried Tofu Chicken Wings
5. Radish/Turnip Cake
6. Braised Pork Belly

Everything taste delicious especially perking duck wrap. Service was attentive throughout, and overall, it was a great experience.
Would recommend if you’re nearby and want a comfortable spot with a wide variety of options.
評論2: [Ueda Etona] (5分, 2 months ago): You can enjoy elegant seasoning that allows the natural flavours of the ingredients to be appreciated. Some people describe their taste as weak, but I suspect they have been poisoned by cheap, salty flavours masked with excessive salt. The various types of xiaolongbao are, of course, delicious, but do try the soups and seasonal specials too. The service was a little slow, and we sometimes struggled to place our order, but the service itself was friendly and attentive.
評論3:
[Jirapat Mos] (4分, 4 months ago): Food: Everything tasted ‘Okay’. Nothing is memorable enough for coming back. The pork belly dish was quite a turn down for us because the meat had no taste. If they did a better job at seasoning the meat, it’d come out quite perfect. The bun was really good though.

Service: The staffs were very nice but due to lack of staffing or attention?, sometimes it was very hard to call them for ordering
評論4: [Shaine Serrano] (5分, 4 months ago): Absolutely loved this place! It's truly worth visiting for the delicious Taiwanese food. The service was kind and attentive, making the experience even more enjoyable. Every dish tasted wonderful, and the flavorful Xiao Long Bao was particularly memorable. I highly recommend it—you won't be disappointed!
評論5: [Jora Dimayuga] (5分, a year ago): The dumplings are perfectly steamed, with a deliciously rich broth inside. I also tried other dim sum options, and they were just as impressive—fresh, flavorful, and expertly made. The atmosphere is cozy and inviting, perfect for enjoying a meal with the family. The service is friendly, and the overall experience is top-notch. It’s definitely one of the best spots for dim sum in Taipei. Highly recommend trying their signature dishes and sharing the feast with your loved ones!

Google Map地址: 100台灣臺北市中正區羅斯福路一段61號
評論1: [George Chiao] (4分, 2 years ago): Definitely not a fine dining Definitely a delicious local homie gathering place. The pork knuckle is so good, crispy outside, tender & juicy inside and very flavorful. I think they might brine the meat to add flavor and remove the meaty smell before roast it. The rest of dishes are also flavorful but not super impressed. I think this restaurant might have been here for more than 40 years. We ordered more dishes after pounding pour after pour of high-proof spirits. The alcohol might burn out my palate for tasting food. I think you can have pretty decent food here for $500 per person if you plan your orders well.
評論2:
[Andy Hsia] (4分, 7 years ago): Restaurant has been around for decades and showing some age. The food is still good but no longer what it was. This taste is still familiar to what I remember and for those who have been here you will get a sense of nostalgia. Most customers definitely have been coming for most of their life.

Good Hunan cuisine, not many options is Taipei so this is worth a visit if you are looking to try some spicy food. Not sure there is an English menu so probably need some help ordering.
評論3:
[Wayne Cheng] (5分, 2 years ago): The restaurant is open at 11:00.  We arrived at around 11:40am on a Wednesday morning,  and it was already packed.
Anyway,  food is tasty, and the price is reasonable.
評論4: [helen Liao] (3分, 4 months ago): We ordered vip room.  It's quite in the room but It is very loud outside.  Waitress didn't replace clean plates often.
評論5: [Mike] (5分, 3 years ago): Antique.  Come and have a taste of the classic 60’s

Google Map地址: 100台灣臺北市中正區寧波東街1號
評論1:
[KTLL] (5分, 3 months ago): The Peking roast duck is costly but delicious!
Since it’s super popular, I recommend calling ahead to make a reservation and pre-order the roast duck (it takes one hour to cook).

Hope the owner will hire more wait-staff. The two waitresses were swarmed with serving a full house during lunch time.
評論2: [michael rabinowitz] (4分, a month ago): Remember when  it was on ChungHua Lu in the 80's and 90's. Reliable.  now still mighty fine.   Staff helped us get a lunch with crispy duck.
評論3:
[Matt L] (3分, 8 years ago): This restaurant is just OK. If you want Beijing flavours - then this will be fine if you're in the area and are really hungry (and so can't make it anywhere else). The staff appear to speak no English, and the menus (pictures attached) are only in Chinese.

The restaurant itself almost transports you to the mainland (the decoration and even the clothes the waitresses wear). The food is fine but not superb (I noticed that many westerners give the place high ratings, but having lived in Beijing for quite a while, I can only give this place three stars). Most of the clients seemed to be locals. Prices are strange - some things out expect to be cheap and really expensive and vice versa. A meal for two came to 900ntd (quite expensive for what you get, considering what else is around).

The guotie 锅贴 (fried dumplings) were good, as was the huiguorou 回锅肉. Other things so so.
評論4: [C C] (2分, 6 months ago): Pity its taste has far lost the genuine Peking style. It had been good when I was a child and we went often as living in the neighborhood. The attitude was not as patient. Oh well.. hope everything could improve to remain its name
評論5: [Arthur Chen] (5分, 4 years ago): Good place for a old fashioned hotpot in a cold overcast windy day, great food, and warm service.

餐廳名稱: 詩篇咖啡餐廳Psalms Cafe & Restaurant
Google Map地址: 100台灣臺北市中正區中山南路20號B1
評論1:
[Becket Hui] (4分, a month ago): The restaurant is tucked underneath the Central Library and serves a range of Taiwanese dishes. We went during late lunch and ordered the beef noodle soup along with some tea. The place seems pretty popular as all the side dishes and desserts were already sold out by then.
The beef noodle soup was quite good. The beef was tender, and the noodles had a nice bite. The flavour, though, felt a bit underwhelming. Overall, it’s a decent spot for a casual lunch.
評論2: [Bernie] (3分, 3 months ago): Our good friend James Hsu took us to Psalms Cafe & Restaurant, nestled inside the National Central Library in Taipei. The cafe is well-loved for its fresh, delicious food, serving breakfast, lunch, dinner, and a variety of refreshments, especially coffee. The coffee is rich, creamy, and absolutely tasty, all enjoyed in a warm and cozy atmosphere.
評論3: [Doris Chin] (5分, a month ago): Psalms Cafe is such a peaceful and comforting place. The atmosphere is calm and welcoming, and you can really feel the love and intention behind the space! The food and drinks are delicious, and it’s the perfect spot to relax. Definitely a place I’ll keep coming back to.
評論4:
[t l] (5分, 11 months ago): Very popular place!  Excellent food and nice and efficient staff.  Menu in Chinese as well as English.
Ordered beef noodle soup, tomato flavor.  Also deep fried fish.
評論5: [Dave Mariani] (4分, 2 months ago): Food was very good, service was ok, brought our drinks after we were done eating.

Google Map地址: 100台灣臺北市中正區中山南路21之1號
評論1:
[Brandon C] (5分, 2 months ago): Can only really comment on the drinks because that was what we came for. My taiwanese friend recommended this place as it's the og bubble tea and it really didn't disappoint. What separates it from other bubble tea places are the size of the tapioca balls. They're much smaller here which I thought was really cool.

Also recommend the side order of radish cake. They're like lor Bach go at dim sum, and they are really well made with a tasty sauce. There is a minimum spend here and there was a queue as soon as it opened at 1130... We showed up just before it opened and we were 10th in the queue just to give you a sense of it.

When you arrive, enter the queue and you'll be given a number. You can wait outside the restaurant in the hallway where you'll hear your number called. Sit down and scan the QR code at your table, and place your order. Go pay for your order, and that's when your order will go through. They do accept card. Also there is a minimum spend per person.
評論2: [Uyen Bui] (5分, a month ago): I felt not too sure when walking int this restaurant because I just thought it was a boba shop but it was indeed a restaurant. I felt weird when just ordering boba only but seemed like other customers were doing the same things. Overall it’s a great place to visit but in my opinion the drinks didn’t impress me. Good to give it a try but probably won’t come back!
評論3: [Stuart Gibson] (5分, 3 months ago): Went here for our xxth anniversary. Was recommended for its traditional Taiwanese food. Didn't disappoint was very enjoyable.. a little touristy but that didn't reflect in the cost
評論4:
[G] (4分, 5 months ago): A pint of bubble tea what is there not to love!

Even though this was classed as the originator of bubble tea it tasted great but nothing special that sets it apart with competitors. I did appreciate the huge portion that we got which was something different but I definitely hit my bubble tea quota for the week..

Way to order was pretty confusing especially for tourists like myself, the place was pretty peaceful however.
評論5:
[Layar Jaring] (5分, a year ago): Most bubble tea places have a generic sterile decor. But not here. Here, they have an excellent environment. I like the way it's designed to be inviting, comfortable, relaxing  place for you to sit and rest.

Staffs can be brusque though but maybe because we don't follow their 'booking' / waiting for tables rules. You need to first take a number at the entrance. Then wait patiently for the staff to come assign you a table. Don't walk straight to a table and plonk yourself at the table and expect your drinks and food. Once you get your assigned table, use the qr to order, then go to the payment counter and pay. Then only will your food and drinks be sent to you.

Being a branch / franchise of the creators of the bubble tea does create some expectations and I must say I enjoyed their concoction. The fact that you can adjust the sweetness of your drinks is an added bonus. You can't go wrong no matter what you order. Their drinks are excellent. I can't comment on their food as I didn't order any, but visually, (observed my neighboring tables) they look delicious.

Google Map地址: 10641台灣台北市大安区信义路二段16號1樓
評論1: [Charley Yip] (5分, 2 years ago): This place lives in an alley facing a school. We have been to the restaurant for a couple of times already. The food is delicious, highly recommend the salmon rice ball, the waiters and chefs are really nice! When you enter the restaurant, there are two tables that you can sit. Then you take 2 steps up to the bar table. There in the bar table you can see how the chefs cook which it’s really cool! Really like to come here again soon!
評論2:
[Jimmy Lee] (5分, 6 years ago): I visited during a weekend lunch hour (around 12:30). Although I didn't make any reservations, I still managed to get a table without any waiting (a table for 2). The restaurant is pretty small (but still comfortable), so don't expect it to be the best dining atmosphere ever. I ordered the most expensive combo (around 1500NTD/person), which came with: a salad, sashimi, grilled shrimp, four sushi, pork, a small portion of eel on top of rice, miso soup, and a home-made dessert. The service was phenomenal and the food was fresh and well prepared. The meal was NOT served all at once, which allowed me to enjoy and experience. There's only one thing I would complain: the air conditioning was blowing straight to my head, but that didn't bother me that much.

I would definitely recommend this restaurant and I will suggest you make a reservation since it is pretty popular during lunch/dinner hours. This store takes cash only and it is not very suitable for party over four (unless if you make a reservation for the entire bar side of the restaurant with then it will work perfectly). I'll definitely revisit some days and try their 1500/2000 chef's menu (with dish varies daily).
評論3:
[Karen Wang] (5分, 3 years ago): Omakase sushi was delicious!

Love the little details of this shop: the different ceramic sets for each customer, the tea-lights underneath each teapot, the homemade pickles including some non-Japanese but delicious flavors like basil tomato. Also the two turtles hanging out in an aquarium in the corner lol.

Set menu also comes with chawanmushi, a grilled course, a main course, soup, and dessert.
評論4:
[Meag F] (5分, 3 years ago): This restaurant is down an alley off the main road, right across from the NTNU building that dominates the end of that block.

I called the restaurant 30 minutes before closing to see if I could squeeze a last minute reservation in, and they were so kind to let us come so late. We didn't feel rushed at all 🙂.

Sushi and donburi were beautifully made and very delicious. I got their recommended cheesy mentaiko salmon donburi and was very impressed with how they were able to pack so much umami into the dish. Would definitely go back!
評論5:
[Michael J Del Toro] (4分, 7 years ago): This is a very traditional Japanese restaurant. There are both cooked dishes and sushi/sashimi. They have grilled meat & seafood and some traditional Japanese cooked dishes.

Going early we found ample seating and took place at the sushi bar. We were greeted courteously. Only downside is they don’t speak English but there are photos for the non sushi/sashimi dishes so you can just point.

We ordered a pork donburi and sushi and sashimi with a halibut hand roll. The sushi offered is limited (Cobia, salmon, scallop, sweet shrimp, snow crab, etc) but very fresh. They will also sear it if you don’t want it “sushi raw”. Teas is served and you can buy soda, beer and saki.

Salmon & salmon belly Sashimi came first and was excellent. Very fresh and well prepared. Next up was the pork donburi. Never had it before but it was very good and I am told by my Japanese friends it reminded them of Japan.

The next dish were the sushi seared and normal style. Again the first was very fresh and prepared in traditional sushi manner.

Cobia and hand roll were last. The Cobia sashimi is served on a bed of onions with a delicious dressing and scallions with traditional Japanese fish flakes. It is a great combo. EDIT: 2nd time portion smaller and no dressing. FAIL

The salad palette cleanser that comes with the sushi plates are excellent and consist of pickled radish and cucumber with tomatoes dipped in an Italian inspired dressing. One of my favorite parts.

I don’t normally order the hand rolls but I am glad I did. It was wonderful. dessert was a green tea mocha  and a great way to round off the meal. EDIT: hand roll was half size 2nd time. FAIL

Overall the food, service and atmosphere was good and I rate this a good Japanese restaurant in Taipei. In Taiwanese it is OVERALL: 還好

EDIT: Order salmon rice ball 2nd time: all rice & no salmon. Chef pointed out tiny piece smaller than baby fingernail. Only piece in rice ball as photographed.

Maybe don’t go again. If they recognize you they might not give you as good food.

餐廳名稱: 陶板屋 台北重慶南店
Google Map地址: 100台灣臺北市中正區重慶南路一段58號2樓
評論1: [Usada Klinbhiboon] (2分, a month ago): The dining experience here is quite baffling. They offer a coursed meal that doesn't fit the cuisine at all. Although the staff are attentive and the prices are budget-friendly, the ingredient quality is disappointing and reminds me of a fast-food chain. It seems the high volume of positive Google reviews is driven by the restaurant's request for feedback rather than the actual food quality.
評論2:
[Bylur Xu] (5分, a year ago): Lovely place to be at to catch up with friends as the restaurant offers a choice of 7-course meals to keep both your mouth and tummy busy at work.

I took on the NT668 set and the ordering itself took the staff a good 10-15mins to confirm all our orders.

The dishes were meticulously prepared and well presented to us but the staff team was pretty much short-handed during the evening’s full seating.

Would recommend this place for persons who wants a good place for long conversions and good food.
評論3: [Leticia Dubas] (5分, 3 weeks ago): The food was great and service excellent.
評論4: [Stephen Chin] (1分, 2 months ago): We booked a lunch with friends and despite promising us they could cook a meal taking care of gluten and allergies, they were unable to do this. Most of the set meal could not be eaten once we checked sauces. They also have no ability to modify the food since most is precooked. Finally, after a lot of conversation in Chinese they messed up and brought out a suspicious looking brown sauce with our steak, which of course contained wheat once we asked. If you are celiac or gluten intolerant don't eat here.
評論5: [Kimii. Hayashi] (5分, 8 years ago): I was invited to have lunch there as a reunion with an old friend. I thought it would just be a slightly fancy Taiwanese-Japanese meal. Turned out it was a 7 courses meal upon tight scheduled reservation, and the serving following each course is super fast unless you asked them to slow down. Each course is highly unique and super creative.! Even with seemingly small portion, I was so full!

Google Map地址: 2之4號, No. 2之4號林森南路中正區臺北市台灣 100
評論1: [annie chen] (5分, a year ago): We came here couple years ago and have always wanted to visit again. This time my husband was feeling well, the server offered to him some modified dishes. We ordered two sets of the food. The presentation and the taste of each dish were great. Again, we loved it and enjoyed the atmosphere in the restaurant. Servers were attentive.
評論2: [Dave Ong] (5分, 4 months ago): Recommend to take the omakase it is value for money and very excellent chief
評論3:
[Jack Chou] (5分, 2 months ago): Have been to the restaurant many times. The sea food was terrific, great sushi and difference set meal. The shef is really good at cooking for amazing food. most important !! The prices are reasonable. Recommend to enjoy.^^

Coming again and feeling as usual, so good of the delicious food , grateful service.

so wonderful food

perfect Restaurant！！！
評論4: [G H] (3分, 2 years ago): 3rd visit, and we found that it's not good for value. Sukiyaki set as you see in the photo is $610 after tax, with about 5 pieces of beef.
評論5:
[CD] (3分, 8 years ago): I expected a bit more for the price.  The Japanese salad was 280 NTD and it was pretty basic and not too big.

The tuna sushi was excellent as was the squid and shrimp.

The avocado roll was just below average.  The roe on the rice was smashed into the rice and disappeared.   The avocado had no flavor.  Perhaps the fruit wasnt ripe or in season - if that is the case it shouldn't be available on the menu.

Service was good and the sake was nice.

I probably won't come back as there are many other choices for this type of cuisine in Taipei.

餐廳名稱: 姥姥酸菜魚 微風北車店
Google Map地址: 100台灣臺北市中正區北平西路3號2樓
評論1:
[Lau Guan Zhen (Sean)] (5分, 12 months ago): If you’re a fan of sour fish stew (酸菜魚), Laolao SuanCaiYu is a must-visit! The broth is deliciously tangy and full of flavors, with just the right balance of sourness and spice. The fish is tender and fresh, perfectly complementing the pickled cabbage.

The restaurant offers a comfortable, modern atmosphere with quick and friendly service. Portions are generous, and the prices are reasonable for the quality and taste. They also have a variety of side dishes and appetizers to pair with the main dish.

A great spot for anyone craving a hearty, flavorful meal in the heart of Taipei. Highly recommend giving it a try!
評論2:
[EightNine (EightNine)] (5分, a year ago): The sour fish soup and side dishes (especially the fried pork) were all delicious. We preferred the non-spicy version to the spicy version.

You can help yourself to dry ingredients like hibiscus flowers, orange peels and dried plums to steep your own hot tea, which was very special.
評論3:
[Vortrox92LG] (5分, a year ago): I had a Suan Cai Yu individual set meal (Boiled Fish with Pickled Cabbage and Chilli) that typically included a bowl of rice, 3 side dishes, and a dessert which was grass jelly. However, you could replace something with another thing in the digital menu. For instance, I substitute the side dishes with an extra dessert, so the set meal became with a bowl of rice and 2 grass jelly. Besides, you were also able to adjust the spiciness or add extra sourness to your Suai Cai Yu. The broth with extra sourness was even more sour than the lemonade in my opinion.

The boiled fish had amazing texture and the portion was greater than others. The rice went so well with the fish and the broth in a bite.
The meat ball and the pork slice in the soup were tender and delicious. Furthermore, the wide bean noodles in the broth had always been my favourite things in Suai Cai Yu. The smooth and flexible texture with spicy taste was the best.

The delectable Grass Jelly was definitely a great calm after the sour and spicy meal.

The Winter Melon Punch with Chrysanthemum wasn’t as sweet as it usually was, but the taste was still good.

Last but not least, the service and the food were excellent, and the price was moderate.
Overall I would give it a 9.5 out of 10!

FYI, you self serve the water near the kitchen and place your order with the QR code.
評論4: [Percy Cheung] (4分, a year ago): Been here a couple times, the service is sometimes good sometimes so so. We usually have to order extra spicy sauce with the sauerkraut fish. Food quality is pretty consistent. I like the make your own drink station they have.
評論5: [J.David Hwang] (4分, a year ago): It’s a restaurant soecialising the  popular sour pickle fish soup.  It’s quite tasty but a little too sour for me to fully enjoy the soup itself.  It’s delicious to go with with rice.   The price is above average but business is great!

Google Map地址: 100台灣臺北市中正區青島東路6-1號
評論1:
[Ally Virlynn] (5分, 2 months ago): The shrimp rice bowls (both with and without the soft shell crabs) were absolutely delicious! There was no fishy smell. The sauce was perfect! All the sunny side up eggs were cooked to perfection as well. The vegetables were a tad bland but it was still acceptable to me. The free brown sugar red date wolfberry hot tea tasted so good.

The lady serving us was friendly and accommodating. All the food items were served pretty fast too although it is stated in the menu that the soft shell crab shrimp bowl might take some time as the crabs are deep fried after order is taken. The soft shell crabs were very crispy as well. Basically, everyone in my family loves the food so much.

Recommended to try out! We will definitely visit again the next time we are in Taipei.
評論2:
[Juztin Png] (3分, a year ago): was recommended by a local friend to check out 忠青商行 in Taipei, so I went with a group of friends. When we arrived, we were told there would be a wait, which was fine, but I must say, their table management needs improvement. We waited around 40 minutes for a table of four, and despite the restaurant being full, they kept calling in tables for two from the queue, rather than combining tables to seat slightly larger groups.

The staff seemed overwhelmed, with three or four people on the floor managing mobile orders, yet they still couldn’t keep up with food delivery and table turnover. This led to longer waiting times and showed a clear need for better organization.

Payment was another issue. Their app lets you select cash or credit card, but we found that not all credit cards, especially foreign ones, are accepted. Additionally, they only offer local Taiwan payment apps, which tourists can’t use, and they don’t have a POS machine for physical credit cards. This made payment frustratingly inconvenient for us as tourists.

The food itself was good, but we were disappointed to find that many of their signature dishes were sold out by 12:30 pm, even though they open at 11 am. If these dishes are popular, it would make sense to prepare more.

Overall, while I’d recommend trying their food, be prepared with cash if you're a tourist, expect a wait if you’re in a larger group, and have alternative dishes in mind just in case they run out.
評論3:
[Darren] (4分, a year ago): Went to the branch store located at Taipei HSR Station, where you have to order as a set. Got the Shrimp Rice w/ sausage and egg, as well as the Dry Noodles w/ Chilli Peppers and Minced Pork. The shrimp, sausage, and egg yolk made for an intense flavour bomb, but it quickly became too saucy and surfeiting at the end. the portion was just decent, but this was a good thing as my partner lost her appetite for the bowl about three-quarters in. The noodles faired better, but while it was tasty in all honesty it's just noodles with minced meat. The sides included in the set were alright - the worse were the tasteless veges and the best was the soup that had a very strong shellfish taste.

Overall, our meal came up to 631NTD. A good option if you wanted to try the Michelin Bib Gourmand Shrimp Rice dish but do not fancy joining the long queue at the main store. Personally, go for it if you want to try, but the meal wasn't that great that I would return (to neither this branch or the main).
評論4: [Art Yuji] (4分, 6 months ago): This was my first experience at a traditional Taiwanese restaurant. The dish was topped with smoked pork, rice, and braised cabbage. I ordered a rich chicken soup, and side dishes of braised cabbage and braised radish. Payment was possible with Line Pay, so ordering and payment could be completed from the table. It was smooth. The service was good. There's also no lunch break, so it's easy to just drop in. There's seating in the middle that can accommodate 24 people, and takeaway and delivery
評論5: [Natalie Betricia] (4分, 6 months ago): The food was nice, but the shrimp rice was a bit on the sweet side.  The chicken was yum! The staff were great. However, it is really crowded.

Google Map地址: Floor 2, No. 22館前路中正區臺北市台灣 100
評論1:
[Hannah Chiang] (5分, 3 months ago): Had an amazing lunch at Junto. Service was impeccable. Food was delicious and thoughtfully prepared. Each dish comes with a theme/story and combines fresh ingredients, chef’s creativity, and artful presentation.

The staff takes great care to make sure you are comfortable and explains the ingredients and stories behind each dish. It makes for a very enjoyable dining experience and we cannot wait to return to try the dinner menu which will showcase more entrees vs dim sum bites and therefore a whole different set of cooking skills according to the exceptional maître d’ Terry who warmly welcomes each guest.

We really feel like we were treated so well during our visit. See you soon!
評論2:
[Kamlesh Chhetty] (5分, 3 months ago): ​We visited Junto on December 1st, 2025, and overall had a very pleasant experience. The culinary creativity on display was definitely the highlight. The restaurant offers a set menu with truly great creativity, presenting a sophisticated and modern take on Cantonese cuisine. The servers were consistently attentive throughout the meal, which contributed to a comfortable dining atmosphere.
​It is worth noting that the restaurant offers a smaller selection of dishes for lunch compared to dinner. More notably, there were a few minor service points that kept it from being a perfect experience. The staff did not proactively inquire about our beverage preferences; we had to ask to see the wine/drinks menu ourselves. Similarly, we were not informed about the additional options or possibilities to upgrade certain dishes on the menu, which felt like a missed opportunity to enhance the meal.
​While I highly recommend booking in advance and experiencing the cuisine, I would advise keeping your expectations slightly below the highest tier you might have for a Michelin-recommended establishment. The food is excellent, but the service details could be refined to match the quality of the kitchen.
評論3:
[Michael Choi] (4分, 2 months ago): Visit Junto today for lunch with 3 of my friends. We ordered the lunch tasting menu and added the extra  Lava Sesame Ball for dessert and ordered the Gin with dried Tangerine peel. (The drink is very tasty.)

In general, it is a solid meal for the price. However personally I think there are room for improvement, especially as a recognised Michelin mentionee restaurant.

Today's Lunch:

Radiant Blend Juice - refreshing and a good start for the meal.

Crispy Sweet Potato and Taro Seafood bites - for serving, the waitress poured sauce lightly onto the crisps, it provided an acidity and sweet flavour to lighten the fried potato and taro, however maybe the strips are too thin and short, the sauce struggles to cling into the fries, and lots of the sauce landed on the plate.

Untold Secret Jelly Cube - This is a pork dish and is one of their signature dish, the seasoning is very nice on the surface if the meat, but didn't fully penatrate into the middle, so the middle of the meat is a little blend.

Seafood Dumpling and Steamed Egg with aged Tangerine Peel Gin - a very light and nice dish. Before serving the Tangerine Gin is lightly sprayed on to the dish, the steamed egg is smooth and silky, the dumpling skin in tender and filling is complex, but I failed to feel the connection between the gin spray and the overall dish.

Crispy Roll stuffed with Porcini Chicken - I felt the roll mimicking the 蘿蔔絲酥餅, the taste of the stuffing is good, however within the 4 pieces, 2 of the pieces are slight burnt at the bottom.

Mustard Cheese Glutinous Rice Dumpling - mimicking the 鹹水角 it is presented with a Van Gogh painting, but the dumpling is not rested enough and it's burning to the lips. However the subtle cheese is beautiful.

Chef's signature Char-Siu with Mountain Pepper Oil -  this is another signature dish, and the same as the Jelly Cube, the sauce have different layer of taste but is not too sweet, but like the Jelly Cube, the middle of the meat lacks penetration and it depends on the sauce to give it taste. Personally the Char-Siu can slice a little thicker like traditional Char-Siu.

Imitation Shark Fin Soup - This is one of the best 碗仔翅 I have tried, the ingredients are slices thinly and the soup temperature is just right.

Salt and Pepper Fried Shrimp - the shrimp is fried just right, but seasoning is a little on the soft side.

This is the forth deep fried dish already and at this point we felt it's one too many deep fried dishes.

Stir Fried Chicken with Dahingpoa Pepper - This is the only dish that I can say I don't fully understand. The chicken is blend (I can taste pepper oil and chilli) but then the taste is not strong (or is it suppose to be so subtle?) The deep fried green garish on our plate is like 川椒雞 but are not consistent and not crispy enough and it soft on presentation.

Thick noodles in Abalone Ginger and Scallion Sauce - The noddles are tender and nicely cooked, the sauce is coated evenly on the noodles. Simple and nice dish.

Ginger Milk Pudding with Caramel Crunch - After the heavy meal (10 courses), this is very refreshing, smooth and tasty. With the caramel it's already very nice, adding the caramel added an extra layer of taste.

Lava Sesame Ball - when it arrives and we open it, lava already solidified, however it taste beautiful.

Just a couple of points on presentation (1) Suggest all use table cloth because the wood table cloth is all scratched, (2) provide more sharing chopsticks, there is only 1 pair for us to start with to share among us 4.

The waiter and waitress are friendly and explained the dish well.

Just some personal feeling and comments, as I said solid menu and I will definitely go again and try the dinner menu, which looks appealing also.
評論4: [chloe dennis] (5分, 2 months ago): Worth the travel for miles! The tasting menu shows meticulous thought in its design across each course, with every dish expertly crafted to highlight both flavors and presentation. The crew displays an amiable attitude, adding to the overall enjoyment of the dining experience. Each table is illuminated by a spotlight directed at its center, enhancing the atmosphere and highlighting the meticulous care with which guests can capture their dining experiences, or, capture the food. My personal favorites are watercress soup, crispy taro and egg white dumplings. Highly recommended! Do remember to make reservation.
評論5: [Chi Pham] (5分, 3 months ago): Had my birthday dinner here and it definitely didn’t disappoint! We went for the 15-course tasting menu, which took about 2.5 hours and was full of creativity and surprises. Every dish was beautifully presented, and the staff explained each course with care. I especially loved the jelly cube - I still remember how the flavors burst in my mouth - and the crispy sweet potato and taro seafood bites, which was fried but not oily at all, just perfectly crispy and fun to eat. Thank you also for the birthday cake ^^

評論1: [Valerie Adams] (5分, 2 weeks ago): Absolutely delicious! I ordered the hot and sour noodles and the shrimp tempura. Everything was cooked perfectly and came out quickly! The staff are extremely friendly as well. I’ll definitely be back!
評論2: [Real honest reviews] (4分, 2 months ago): We came around 1:40 p.m. for lunch and waited about 5–10 minutes. The place was packed with tourists, which made sense given how close it is to the main train station. We ordered four different kinds of shumai along with a wonton soup. The wonton soup was a miss. The classic/original shumai, though, was really good, especially with the chili sauce. While it seemed like the shumai were pre-made or frozen rather than made in-house, they were still quite tasty. Our second favorites were the black truffle shumai and the shrimp dumplings (although the wrapper was too soft and not the right texture). I wouldn’t order the spicy shumai again, as it wasn’t my favorite.
評論3: [Swee Ping Lim] (4分, 3 months ago): I had a wonderful experience at Lei Hou Restaurant! 🤩 The food, especially those delicious dumplings, was simply amazing. 🥟 The service was top-notch, always attentive and friendly. 🥰 While the atmosphere was good, the incredible food and service truly made my visit memorable! ✨
評論4: [Sean Seah] (4分, 2 months ago): The Siu Mai rocks. Glutinous rice was good but the Har Kow was a little dry. The skin is just a tad thick. Service and great with a quiet env.
評論5: [Lebao Yaj] (5分, 3 months ago): It was great, the best I've had outside of America so far. There shrimp rolls were good too. Definitely try it out. I wish they had regular pork dumplings though

Google Map地址: 103台灣臺北市大同區承德路三段206之1號
評論1:
[M. Eren Akbıyık] (5分, 3 months ago): I cannot overstate how fantastic of a lunch this was.

Flavorful and fantastic lobster roll, smoked salmon, as well as a deeply enjoyable clam soup. Great idea with the tahini/katsuyobushi broccoli salad.

However, the fish (threadfin) was truly exceptional, and one of the best fish cookery I have ever seen, well executed ichiyaboshi combined with a perfect cooking and a blowtorch finish that made the whole fish basically into creme brulee.

Worth changing your way to drop by here, have a lunch with some sake.
評論2: [TST] (3分, 2 years ago): We ate here in 2020 and really enjoyed the food. We came on this trip for the fish which was grilled perfectly and was really fresh. It was the service that didn't sit right with us. The lady was unfriendly when we asked about the fish we wanted. Maybe they were having a bad day. Hopefully when we come by the next time they can be more personable.
評論3: [Jessica Kan] (4分, 2 years ago): The restaurant is a very japanese style bistro. There are many fried things you can choose, but we are in a rush, so we ordered a Hokkaido pork bone ramen and a sashimi rice bowl. The soup of the ramen was really fresh and flavorful and the sashimi was really good 👍, hopefully we can have more time to enjoy the meals next time.
評論4:
[Kane] (2分, a year ago): The sushi is underwhelming and the prices are higher than in other, much better Japanese restaurants. We tried a few other dishes including the prawn sushi, which was mostly rice. We also tried the egg tofu which was tasteless. The salmon sushi was okay.

The staff weren’t so friendly, however, I think I would struggle to be friendly if I was serving subpar food all day.
評論5: [Big Ben] (2分, 4 years ago): average and overrated. A place for filling up your hunger, ok. An awesome one… not quite so, in my opinion. Especially, their roll is kinda disappointed.

Google Map地址: 103台灣臺北市大同區迪化街一段329號
評論1:
[Joyce Wong] (1分, 2 weeks ago): Reply to owner's reply: Level 2 was almost empty but staff didn't use it. I was next in queue and more than 15pax left the restaurant from Level 2.

Don't come without online reservation. Got queue number at 2.30pm and we are next in queue. At 2.50pm, there were many empty tables but realised shortly 6 tables were setup for the staff lunch and other tables were meant for 3.30pm lunch appointment. When I asked if we have a chance to get a table soon, staff informed that it's already indicated that the tables will only be available soonest at 3.30pm when we get our queue number at 2.30pm, so we just have to wait till 3.30pm. Upon my pushing again, staff said 1 reservation was cancelled so we got seated at 3.15pm. I also observed that the tables of 4pax were all reserved by 2pax each. I cannot reasonate with their reservation system so I won't recommend anyone to walk in.
評論2:
[Shirleen Tan] (5分, 5 months ago): I would say this is a fanciful Taiwanese food. The restaurant is located in a very pretty alley.

The presentation of the food is really eye pleasing. It makes me happy just by looking at it. The food is amazingly delicious and the atmosphere of the restaurant is fantastic too. Very nostalgic vibe.
評論3: [Emilia Liu] (5分, 5 months ago): Very traditional Taiwanese style cuisine. I love the retro decor, full of history and very well preserve. The food was delicious too. Definitely will come back again
評論4: [Michael Wu PhD] (5分, 2 months ago): Delicious traditional Taiwan restaurant with a spin. They are famous for their rice.
評論5:
[Sam W] (5分, 2 months ago): The foods were excellent. We enjoyed every dish we ordered. Though I particularly liked the Sea Bass with Loofah and Mushrooms, Fermented Tofu Fried Chicken and the Braised Pork. I love that they give you a jar of soy sauce and lard for the rice. I have not had that since I was a little kid.

The service was very friendly, they gave us a private room for party of 5 instead of make us wait for a table. They did tell us the time limit is 90 minutes. Maybe a thing in Taiwan as another place had told us the same. Plenty of time because of the quick service and we didn't plan to linger.

A friend back home recommended this place. I felt compelled to buy them a souvenir because of the recommendation.

評論3:
[H] (3分, 10 months ago): Ordered the Sicily coffee and a garlic oyster pasta.

The Sicily coffee was surprising as it was refreshing. I had some sweet lemon juice in the coffee. This went well with my window seat as it was very therapeutic.

Can't say the same for the garlic oyster pasta. It was lacking strong garlic flavours for an aglio olio. For the oysters, there were quite a few so the portions are decent.

However, I kept biting into "sand" or the black dirt that exists in shellfish. That ruined my experience.
評論4:
[serena chin] (4分, 10 months ago): They only take cash and have a QR code queuing system. Recommend to get a number before exploring the area.

Will recommend the Neapolitan pasta! Did not get to try their pancakes because it was OOS.

Cute little space
評論5: [Joe D] (5分, a year ago): I'm so glad I came back to Waku Waku after failing to get a table on a very busy Saturday with a very long wait. We were able to walk in and be seated immediately for lunch at 2pm. Ordering via QR code was very easy (thx to Google Translate) and our food was brought out very quickly. For our mains we had the fried chicken pasta and the salmon mentaiko pasta. Both dishes were generously portioned with perfectly cooked al dente pasta, and we found ourselves scraping up every bit of sauce off of the plates. Aside from pasta, I REALLY enjoyed the fresh baked bread (crusty yet chewy, with a  fire mentaiko spread) and the chicken wings (roasted instead of fried, fall off the bone, lightly sauced and topped with pine nuts). A couple of delicious refreshing summer drinks completed our meal: an iced Sicilian coffee and an iced daffodil tea. Looking forward to trying their other locations!

評論1:
