Anyway, it’s a nice place for a quiet afternoon with friends and family.
評論2: [G Reyes] (5分, 2 years ago): I think they’ve perfected the grilled chicken special brunch. Comes with all the sides. Asked for fried egg instead of scrambled and it came perfectly done sunny side up. It was quiet when we came but not complaining- very relaxing atmosphere to enjoy lunch.
評論3: [Alice Chiu] (4分, 3 years ago): Very friendly staff. Cozy place. Busy during dinner hours. Reservation is recommended.
評論4: [Dan P] (4分, 2 years ago): Had a very good meal. Chicken pine nut pesto pasta. Well prepared and tasty. And creme caramel for dessert.
評論5: [Kevin L] (5分, 4 years ago): Very friendly staff that makes me want to come back again.

Google Map地址: 103台灣臺北市大同區重慶北路一段62號2樓
評論1:
[em emmm] (3分, 10 months ago): Updated: 04/2025
The quality dropped again, and went around dinner time, many dimsum dishes are sold out, so you have to book in advance. If they can maintain the quality consistently, it would be even better. 🙏🏻

I first tried this place in 2020, and it was insanely good—like really good. The 腸粉 (Steamed Vermicelli Roll) was next level, with that perfectly soft, slightly chewy rice roll wrapping around crispy bouncy shrimp. The 叉燒 (Red BBQ puff) was another standout—the pastry was beautifully layered and flaky, and the red BBQ pork filling was generous and flavorful. The 蝦餃 (shrimp dumplings) had that perfect juicy, snappy shrimp texture, and even the 牛肉球 (meatballs) were huge and well-seasoned.

That being said, the quality here can be a bit inconsistent. I’ve been back many times, and I have to admit I was disappointed a few times—especially after the pandemic, when the place seemed to go downhill for a while. It even closed temporarily for renovations, so I decided to give it another shot.

Good news—it’s back on track! Not quite at the peak level of my first visit, but way better than during that slump. The 蝦餃 (shrimp dumplings) wrapper was a little too soft and soaky this time, and some of the fried items were a bit greasy.

One thing that has never changed, though, is the service—absolutely top-tier. The staff is incredibly friendly and attentive every single time. I’ve been here more than ten times now, so I really hope they can keep maintain their standards as before.

Reasonable price for great dim sum in town! CP 很高 👏🏻
評論2: [Real honest reviews] (1分, 2 months ago): We arrived around 1:30 pm, and the restaurant wasn’t full. However, a middle-aged woman refused to serve us. Extremely unfriendly attitude.
評論3:
[stephane dao] (2分, 5 months ago): I didn’t upload a photo because there’s really nothing to show. I recently moved to Taiwan and am delighted to explore the culinary culture of this “island of treasures” (its nickname in Chinese). However, this so-called Cantonese restaurant is Cantonese only in name.

I’m Cantonese myself and have lived in Hong Kong, Shanghai, and Paris — I’m a big fan of Cantonese cuisine whenever I can find it. To put it briefly, this restaurant is on the same level as the Cantonese restaurants in Paris: not very authentic and overpriced. For a Chinese restaurant in Taiwan, that’s quite astonishing. True food lovers should book a ticket and go taste genuine Cantonese cuisine in mainland China.

評論5: [Luke Walker] (2分, a week ago): Decent food, but time between ordering and the food arriving was way too long. (>30 minutes). Spent two hours here and received 6 total dishes.

評論1: [Clinton Yew] (4分, 2 months ago): Nice cozy place with private room at the back and more space on the second floor. The clams pasta is al dente. The steak is great. But the steak with the pasta was a bit tough. The kitchen is however not primed for large groups. We happen to meet a full capacity crowd and our steak took an hour to arrive.
評論2: [Hsiaoping Wu] (5分, 9 months ago): It was a friends’ gathering night, and the place is pleasant to talk  and nice decor for photo taking. We have ordered seafood platter, pasta, pizza, French fries and three different desserts. The food taste’s authentic and nice portion during our visit. it is a great place in a famous historical street.  The restaurant has been really successful for 10 years with high quality ingredients and consistent food quality! No issue to visit soon!
評論3: [Teresa Liao] (5分, 2 months ago): We had a most wonderful dining experience here! The food was amazing! I loved every bite and I truly believed it was my favorite meal during our stay in Taipei!
評論4: [Benjamin Ehrenberg] (1分, a year ago): It’s food but it has nothing to do with Italian cuisine. It’s bland and tasteless and the pizza is buried in cheap cheese but lacks any taste. The noodles are slightly better, handmade but too thick and undercooked.
評論5:
[陳建榮] (3分, 2 years ago): My friends and I came in in the Saturday afternoon and looked for a quite place to relax. We really appreciated the staff re-arranged our table to the ground floor, however, I think the oder and smoke leaking from the kitchen was as well annoying😵

Drinks and cakes are ok but I think the environment doesn’t meet my expectation…

Google Map地址: 10353台灣臺北市大同區承德路二段14號
評論1:
[Elena Fasolo] (1分, 2 months ago): ​I rarely write reviews this negative, but my experience here was genuinely dangerous. I suffered severe food poisoning that resulted in a high fever, vomiting, and severe gastrointestinal distress with very debilitating symptoms. It was nearly an ER-level emergency and completely ruined my vacation, as it took me one week to recover.
​Beyond this, the "fine dining" atmosphere felt pretentious. They enforce a strict "one drink per person" rule, even if you only want water or tea. While the ordering method -using a separate sheet to mark items from a priceless menu with pictures -is common in parts of Asia, here it is frustrating, especially for international tourists. The names on the sheet often do not match the picture menu, making the process confusing.
​Ultimately, the food was unremarkable and extremely pricey - aside from the fact that I couldn't eat anything else for a week after dining here. I cannot recommend this place.
評論2:
[Teedee Hong] (5分, 4 years ago): My first time hopping and I love the vibes here!🥰

Lucky enough that our random walk-in ended up with last table left, so better should call for make a reservation!

I didn’t expect much about foods as we just wanna find places to drink after our dinner. But then those cold dishes serve us with over expectations taste.

So delicious with some fusion in it that make them really different when compare to another Taiwanese beer places.

The sprout dish is what surprised us most. I am not a big fan of sprouts but this is no any foul smelling at all.

Another dish I like is sweet potatoes in porridge. This bowl just need to be double orders!!! 😍

The drinks are all great, especially cocktails!!!

評論4: [TungChen Wu] (4分, 5 years ago): Environment and atmosphere are awesome, no doubt!! Food is great also, happy to have such Taiwanese side dish restaurant to spend drinking session. However, it's too pricey, the cost is too crazy.
評論5: [Huang Sophia] (5分, 3 years ago): Nice restaurant with traditional Taiwanese cusine. I am absolutely delighted by the pig-blood-cake. I am very happy with the food. The price is reasonable. The drinks are a bit pricey, but it doesn't bother me that much! The atmosphere is chill. It's great for friendly get togethers!

評論1:
[Andrew L.] (5分, 4 months ago): While window shopping, we stumbled into this incredible restaurant and were seated immediately—no waiting required! The service was exceptional. We were personally met by the supervisor, Alison, who took our order and offered fantastic dish recommendations. Every plate was absolutely delicious.
The total came to roughly $60US, which was incredibly well worth the price. This delightful meal easily stands out as the most pleasant experience we had during our visit to Taipei. I highly recommend this restaurant; you are guaranteed a joyful and satisfying dining experience.
評論2:
[Liau Yuan Wei] (5分, 4 months ago): Came here on our last day in Taipei to have lunch before taking the Purple MRT line to Taoyuan Airport. We had these dishes (Minimum spend of 400 NTW per person):
1. Garlic Peanuts: Got this as my mum loves to munch on peanuts
2. Chicken with Classic Scallion Oil: Best cold chicken ever it was so juicy!
3. Steamed Halibut with Taiwan Pickled Seeds: Very fresh fish but this halibut has a lot of fish bones so be careful
4. Claypot Tofu with Seafood: Pumpkin based sauce tofu, got this as my brother can eat tofu every meal
5. Stir-fried Seasonal Vegetable
6. Steamed Garlic Shrimps with Rice Noodles: Not bad, reminds me of my mum’s cooking

Overall not bad food quality and taste! Recommended for any tourists around the area.
評論3: [Greggy Chua] (5分, 2 months ago): Delicious food with exceptional service, always attentive to giving water. Recommended dish salted egg squid, 3 cup chicken. The abalone chicken soup is not that tasty. Minimum 400 TWD per person required to spend.
評論4:
[Edward Cheson Sy] (5分, 11 months ago): Jin Zhu serves delicious Taiwanese food with outstanding and speedy service.

Stir-Fried Oysters with Douchi - 4/5, these oyster are cooked in a black bean sauce then served with youtiao bits for extra texture. The server warned us about the dish having a salty flavor but the dish itself isn’t salty. It’s just that fresh oysters have a sea-like salty flavor which was natural.

Sweet and Sour Chicken - 5/5, their version of the dish was certainly different from the deep red colored dishes from other restaurants, but the dish was still prepared very well. Crispy chicken nuggets coated in a slightly sweet brown sauce. My kid loved eating this!

Three cup Tofu - 5/5, delicious tofu in a subtle ginger sauce. This might not be enjoyable for everyone because of the heavy ginger flavor. Nevertheless, I loved it.

The service here was very excellent. They gave my kid color markers to play with and the food was served quite fast.

The white rice was also refillable, which was perfect because it compliments every flavorful dish we ordered.
評論5:
[Karina Adianto] (5分, 7 months ago): We had our last dinner in Taiwan at Jin Zhu. The interior of the restaurant was beautifully decorated.

We ordered:
Pork fried rice
Stir fried seasonal veggies
Scallion on chicken
Crispy fried pork
Braised pork combo: pork belly & knuckle

The food was great, properly cooked and seasoned. I could tell, all the ingredients & meat were high quality & 'without flaws' products.
It was served beautifully, too. The service was also flawless.

Google Map地址: 103台灣臺北市大同區承德路二段213號
評論1:
[Jason Johnson] (5分, 11 months ago): Friday 11th April 2025

On our travels to Taipei Taiwan we came across this wonderful Hong Kong 🇭🇰 restaurant, which is only a 10 minute walk from the Ningxia Night Market, or 6 minutes from the MRT Minquan West Rd station “red line”.

If your craving some authentic Kong style food this is the place to visit, we ordered Silky Egg with Char Siu on rice, Crab Meat Siu Mai, Black Sesame Steamed bun, Phoenix claw, Beef with stir Fried noodles, Raddish cake and Tempeh Pineapple 🍍 Rolls.

Ordering is easy via the QR code enabled Menu system which provides photos and english option. Food is great and we really loved all the items. My favourite items where the Pineapple Rolls, Black Sesame Bun, Silky Egg with Char Siu on rice and beef with stir Fried noodles.

I almost forgot to mention that we were served a delicious chicken soup to start the meal and it was super refreshing 😋. Great food flavours, plenty of options, friendly staff and good vibes! We really enjoyed it and I hope you do to.
評論2:
[martin de geus] (5分, 10 months ago): I had been wanting to go to this restaurant for a while, but kept on forgetting about it.
I'm glad I finally paid it a visit. We had one of the silky egg dishes (beef on rice), and a couple of dim sum dishes as well.
Before we had even made our order we were already served with complimentary soup and tea. After we made our selection via QR code (menu in English and with photos available), the dishes soon started arriving on our table. The silky egg dish was divine (maybe on of the best eggs I've ever tasted), the dim sum were some classics and some new (for me) dishes. The crab yolk siu mai and steamed bbq pork buns were great, the fresh shrimp bean curd rolls were good too, and the tempeh pineapple rolls were a surprising yet tasty choice.
I will come back here to sample more dishes and I would urge you to give this restaurant a chance as well.
Prices were very reasonable and the service was good and fast.
評論3: [Elsa] (3分, a year ago): Way too sweet for my taste but maybe that's how all dim sums are. The smooth eggs dish was covered in oil and too salty, it was too heavy. Blanched vegetables are good though.
評論4: [Herman Lam] (5分, a year ago): Authentic Hong Kong taste in Taipei run by Hongkongers. We tried shrimp dumpling, iced lemon tea, Singapore fried rice noodle,  BBQ pork with fried egg rice. All are up to our expectation. Will come again for other dishes.
評論5:
[黃國書] (5分, 3 years ago): The restaurant reminds me of my trips to Hong Kong! They serve authentic dishes such as french toast, pork with scrambled eggs. Their service is also nice and prompt.

More than that, I support Hong Kong! I hope one day they can resume their democratic political status!

評論2: [Davide Scimia] (5分, 2 months ago): good tasty food .really well cooked . a Taiwanese flavor and kind of home cooking .
評論3: [Stewart Wan] (5分, 2 years ago): It’s very delicious. We enjoyed a nice lunch here, ordered not many dishes because the portion is not small and only two ppl. Must order the 炒水蓮, the best of the best! 臭豆腐was really memorable, garnished with garlic and chilli… highly recommended but need to reserve in advance at dinner window, good luck for lunch window to get a table nar!

Google Map地址: 103台灣臺北市大同區貴德街75-2號1樓
評論1:
[Luca bigler] (4分, 5 months ago): I was curious to try this restaurant suggested by a friend and was a nice experience.
Food was delicious ( unfortunately this month can’t drink alcohol, so didn’t test the many wine in the list)
The chef running alone the kitchen was impressive, so we don’t care of the waiting time.
Was just too noisy and windy from the AC and kitchen area.
評論2: [Junze Zheng] (5分, a year ago): Experience on bar top  is very chill & interesting. The lamb dish is one of the best I've tasted! That said, I might choose to skip the ice cream . Melts a bit too fast
評論3:
[György Adler] (4分, 4 years ago): We had high hopes with this establishment but eventually it turned out as a slight disappointment, maybe because I "ranked" the restaurant merely on its prices.

Upon our arrival we have been introduced to a blackboard, where the menu can be read. I guess the menu changes too frequently so this solution must be for the sake of environment protection.

The staff's attitude seemed promising in the beginning, however later some flaws came to the surface, including:
- As I came from work, I had two big bags with me. It would have been pleasant if the staff offers me the boxes to hold luggage instead of just indifferently watch my struggle with the bags and umbrellas.
- Since it's a French restaurant, the wine selection is sizeable. However, the staff didn't bother to introduce or recommend us some wines and we specifically had to ask for the wine menu.
- My wife asked for the empty bottle for "professional purposes" at the end of our dinner. Although we got a paperbag for the bottle, it remained unwashed.

However the dirty dishes has been taken away almost immediately after we finished a course, so it wouldn't be fair to judge the staff's attention solely on the aforementioned glitches.

Part of the dishes were a let-down as well, especially the rum soaked sponge cake. The rum flavor was extremely dominant, suppressing nearly every other flavor, so after a few bites it seemed like one's eating a bottle of rum spoon by spoon. In light of the amazing lava cake, this dessert was indeed disappointing.

The main dishes, cattle belly and roast duck, on the other hand were amazing, however this former wasn't really outstanding. The duck tasted amazing, it was seasoned and cooked perfectly, whereas the garnish was also magnificent. The home-made sausages were also on the spot.

Overall, due to the flaws I mentioned, I can not be fully satisfied and would expect a bit more "care" for this price.
評論4: [周澄融] (5分, 6 years ago): Incredible restaurant. Excellent cuisine crafted with amazing ingredients and impressive skills. It’s not as expensive as other bougie French restaurant in Taipei city, yet it has perhaps more refined, delicate taste and better portion. The environment is also well organized: clean, cozy, stylish and calm. Worth going for special occasions.
評論5:
[Maximilian Pleyer] (2分, 10 months ago): Its a small and narrow corridor like Restaurant and I brought some friends to entertain them.
Overall we were rather disappointed.
One bottle of white burgundy was fine but served after appetizers were served.
Appetizer portions were tiny & poor value:
1. Asparagus ~ 1 Single Stick cut in 4 pieces 🤐
2. Esgarcots Bourgignon ~ lack of Herbbutter & Bread 🤐
3. Caramelized Brussel Sprouts tasty but tiny
Then we waited for 45 minutes for our Maincourses and after complaining we decided to order another Salad Nicoise to help us over the very long waiting without explanations or apologies.
Finally the maincourse arrived:
1 Salmon & 1 Whole Fish ~ good taste and fair portion.
Right away, while finally being able to enjoy our maincourses, the female server was pushing us for desserts and after my reply to let us finally enjoy our maincourse she replied thatcwe have to decide now because the kitchen will close.....🤬🤬 real nice and considerate service.
When paying NT$ 7700.- / Euro 230 - you should certainly expect more especially in a city where service quality is normally very good.
I will surely NEVER AGAIN bring my friends there to be disappointed and to lose face on top of it.

Google Map地址: 103台灣臺北市大同區錦西街82號1樓
評論1: [J L] (2分, 3 years ago): Got the pork katsu. Tasted too sweet, soggy, and undercooked batter that tastes like pancake mix. The rice was not fragrant and undercooked. Salmon rolls was alright but sushi rice too sour.
評論2: [CK Visionpartner] (5分, a year ago): Good taste, both of the raw fish rish bowl and the seared salmon roll, and not to miss the steamed egg
評論3: [Natasha Morla Tanuputri] (5分, 6 months ago): Food tastes amazing
評論4: [Hang On Arvo (2boys_brunch)] (3分, 3 years ago): Nothing fancy.
評論5: [ㄚ鴻遊趣] (4分, 2 weeks ago): It's basically a good place for a relaxing meal. There aren't many seats, but it's not stressful either. The sashimi occasionally has some gristle, and the other dishes are a bit salty.

Google Map地址: 103台灣臺北市大同區哈密街89號
評論1:
[Duke Chu] (5分, 10 months ago): This curry rice is insane—
it’s incredibly delicious!
I highly recommend the curry rice, meat, and shrimp cake.
The croquette and cabbage are just average.
You can even get extra rice and curry sauce!
The curry sauce is authentically Japanese and super tasty.
I totally recommend everyone to come try it.
I ate two bowls of rice in one go!
評論2: [Carolina Day] (4分, a year ago): Very clean and bright place. They went and pushed 3 tables together for us which was very nice. We ordered sukiyaki - egg, meat, broccoli, cabbage and kimchi on rice. 120NT each bowl. The tea is only pre packaged poppers.
評論3: [Peter Chen] (5分, 5 years ago): Good value for the nicely portioned meal. NT100-120 for most dishes with most expensive at NT200 for the steak which looked pretty good (haven’t tried it yet though). Plenty of veggies which seems and tastes fresh. Rice is cooked well and chewy. Meat portions also pretty generous for the price. Not many tables so better to do take out. Not much space inside so better to order ahead for the take out. Would be fun to do take out and eat at nearby parks.
評論4: [DIVA Yang] (5分, 4 years ago): Simple food and yet healthy and delicious. Ideal for portion control. The shop is small with 15 seats and has no restrooms. No reservations needed. Accept walk-in and cash only.
評論5:
[Chen Aini] (4分, a year ago): Very comfortable and clean environment for people enjoying tasty food.
it's nice can refill rice and curry

Google Map地址: 103台灣臺北市大同區塔城街33號
評論1: [Radu Acsinia] (5分, 2 weeks ago): Excellent place for sushi with a great location next to Dunsin street. The staff is friey and the place has a cozy atmosphere, which means you can see all the action of the preparation. The fish is so fresh and also plentiful, makes for a fantastic meal.
評論2: [lee yongqing] (5分, 5 months ago): I'm so full! The salmon fish head was amazing, oil just oozing out, flesh so firm, so fresh. Then vegetables was amazing too. Cooked to perfection, not too stringy, firm and easily eaten.
評論3: [HC] (5分, a year ago): Nestled in a quiet corner, this Japanese eatery offers a cosy ambience. The dishes are crafted with fresh, high-quality ingredients, and the generous portions make it a delight for both the eyes and the palate. The friendly and attentive staff add to the welcoming experience. For an extra treat, try to snag a counter seat and watch the chef's masterful techniques in action—it’s like dinner and a show! With its excellent food and reasonable prices, this spot is truly great value for money and a must-visit for lovers of Japanese cuisine.
評論4:
[Seabass Sterel] (5分, 2 months ago): The deep fried chicken was excellent, the salmon in the 3-piece sashimi combo was to die for and the eel was awesome too.
Did not expect that for a place on a busy street!
評論5: [Ed Fung] (5分, a year ago): I swear... this is one of the best Japanese restaurants I've ever been to.  It's small and cozy with very fresh seafood.  Tonight, I had the donburi with fish roe, fresh crab meat, scallops & crab tamale, veggies with sesame sauce and deep fried oysters.  Everything was to die for!  I felt like I was tasting the ocean.  The seafood was THAT fresh and tasty.  Pretty sure I'll return before I leave Taipei.  Just an amazing family run restaurant!

Google Map地址: 103台灣臺北市大同區寧夏路81號
評論1: [KT] (3分, 7 years ago): This place is just alright. I had better sushi in the past before so I couldn’t say it will be the best sushi for me.
評論2: [Alice Chiu] (4分, 3 years ago): Very friendly staff. Nice Taiwanese-Japanese style dishes.
評論3:
[Po-kai Chang] (5分, 7 years ago): Awesome Japanese food in Taipei.
Fresh sashimi, raw beef, sushi.
評論4: [Tina Yang] (5分, a year ago): Great food, fresh ingredients, perfect portion. We accidentally broke a bowl, the server didn’t frown, instead, he immediately told our kid to be careful. Then swept the floor. We really appreciate him.
評論5:
[A Ye] (5分, 4 years ago): I loved the Seafood, Beef also served very well. All dishes are really prepared with much of beautiful decor so its not only food but also ART thats what we like.
We were having dinner at seperate room but you may use main Hall where there are many tables. COZY.

Google Map地址: 10347台灣臺北市大同區迪化街一段177號
評論1: [Gabriel Lazarus] (5分, a month ago): Feeling overwhelmed with the street around, I ducked in “The Carp” and was so happy I did. I kept it simple and had the medium spiced pork noodles, which were amaaaazing. I topped it off with the chicken and abalone soup, which has a lovely light flavor. Sat outside, which was serene and particularly welcome due to the frenetic neighborhood surrounding the restaurant.
評論2:
[Shirleen Tan] (5分, 5 months ago): I ordered the premium set meal and it was a great dining experience in The Carp.

The mullet roe fried rice was so flavourful and not to mention that the appetiser, mullet roe candy bar is really a bomb.

Overall a fabulous dining experience and i have enjoyed soaking myself in the fragrance of the wonderful meal and environment.
評論3: [E Shyong Tai] (5分, 6 months ago): Lovely meal by a chef who uses dried mullet roe in most dishes.  Restaurant is in a street that is famed for dried goods.  Both her father and grandfather ran their business selling preserved seafood.  Fantastic shelter from the sweltering heat outside
評論4:
[Christian Larsen] (4分, 11 months ago): As others have pointed out, the nine sampler set is super delicious and varied. And the fried rice is just so delicious. A must try in my book. The accompanying miso soup was ok. The three piece dessert was really well made, although not my taste preference.

The place is super cozy; I really enjoyed the atmosphere. And the staff is friendly and helpful!

On service, one comment is that I was asked to pay when the dessert was served - not in advance of the meal and not after. This ruins the experience somewhat.

In summary, I’m flagging The carp as a favorite place to eat in Taipei and hope to visit again! With a wish that maybe next time payment can be handled a bit more elegantly.
評論5:
[YuuJinn] (4分, 6 months ago): Reunion with friends here when everyone's going back to Taiwan for lunar new year.

The cuisines are creative and amazing good, we love every dish that we ordered.

Also take away some karasumi snacks to enjoy with family, that's tasty also.

餐廳名稱: 叼饞重慶麻辣炸物-台北大同店
Google Map地址: 103台灣臺北市大同區蘭州街107號
評論1: [Claudia 961018] (5分, 4 months ago): So good~
評論2:
[JL] (5分, 2 weeks ago): I've been back to this place three times already, and it'll definitely be one of my favorites from now on. The squid is super delicious!

The chicken is good too, and their chili is amazing!

Every time I come, I have to wait 40 minutes.
評論3: [愛吃勾勾] (5分, 2 weeks ago): This time I bought spicy chicken, squid rings, and tofu skin. I highly recommend the squid rings (they're incredibly delicious!), super fresh and with a bouncy texture, like they were just caught from the sea! The spicy chicken is also amazing! It's different from regular fried chicken; this is my favorite fried food lately!
評論4:
[CC C] (5分, 2 weeks ago): I saw it on Gomaji, so I decided to try it. The wait was 30 minutes, which is quite long. You can wait inside, otherwise it's very windy outside.

I chose the cumin flavor, and it was super delicious!

I think the tempura was the best.

The vegetables were okay.

The tofu skin was also super crispy and great! There were a lot of fried chicken pieces!

However, I didn't really like the simplified Chinese characters on the packaging.
評論5:
[Mann] (5分, a month ago): Reservations via Line are very convenient.

You can specify your preferred taste in the notes.
The food is really delicious; I recommend trying it.
The owner is also very nice.

Google Map地址: 103台灣臺北市大同區承德路二段120號1樓
評論1:
[Brandon C] (5分, 2 months ago): My favourite meal in Taiwan so far! The beef roll was incredible. Amazing texture and so much flavour. The dumplings were really well made and prepared and the spinach was a nice clean side to add to the meal.

The Xiao long bao reminded me of my childhood when my mom used to steam it for me. When food is able to elicit an emotional response like that you know it's good.

The people working here were so nice. They could tell we couldn't speak mandarin and were patient with us. They have a really solid number queue system which is displayed outside so you know when it's your turn to go inside. Definitely recommend!!
評論2: [Annabelle Ambrose] (4分, 5 months ago): The beef roll slaps hard! The scallion pancake was just okay. I think I needed some soy sauce and garlic. You have to get your own chopsticks from a drawer at the back. No one will tell you this though. The woman kept on telling my order was too big… who cares if I pay. I want to try different dishes. It was busy when I went at lunch time and I waited about 20 mins to be seated.
評論3:
[お小賴] (5分, a year ago): This famous restaurant used to be closer to the Nakayama MRT station, moved to this new location in 2024.

Always a line outside during the chow time, after tried this place for the first time, now I understand the reason.

Great value for money and all the dishes were amazing, the soup dumplings were a highlight for me, so juicy and sweet and seasoning was spot on. Knife sliced noodle made their noodle dishes tasted even better because of the chewy, Al dente texture. Lastly their spring onion pancake... Man I will say no more, you go try to find out.

This is the place where I will happily bring my overseas friends to have a taste of Taiwan.
評論4: [Boo] (4分, 2 months ago): Beef and fish roll were really good. Rest were average. Service was not great.
評論5: [Victoria Huang] (5分, 3 years ago): I love this place. My all time favorite place to get vegetable dumplings + beef roll. Their fish roll is also good. I’ve also tried their veggie roll but I think it’s too heavy on the sauce and a bit too sweet. I’ve also tried their noodles which was good but I probably wouldn’t get it again. I consistently go back despite long wait times because their beef roll hits the spot every time. Definitely get there as early as you can or prepare to wait a while.

餐廳名稱: 富春食堂 無菜單料理
Google Map地址: 103台灣臺北市大同區太原路48巷7號
評論1:
[Kb -] (5分, 3 weeks ago): Absolutely the best sashimi I have ever had (believe it or not, including Japan!). The freshness and flavours of each individual slice shines through. Really well priced, much better than what you'd get in the restaurants. If you're lucky, you'll find the chef playing the piano whilst you're eating.

Can be a bit tricky to find the restaurant as its down a quiet lane.
評論2: [Cassandra Chua] (5分, 4 weeks ago): The kind of place that brings a tear to your eye - best chirashi-don I’ve ever had in my life. The sushi rice, the way the sashimi is sliced, the aburi, the perilla leaves - everything is unpretentious and prepared to the highest standards. I don’t think you’ll be able to get it such standards even in Japan. I had the 富春 deluxe / signature chirashi-don and it feels like an omakase in a bowl. Whenever I visit Taipei, I’ll come to this restaurant at least 4 times on a 7-day trip, and I’m not even exaggerating 😆 The chef owner is so modest about his top-notch culinary skills. So glad to have found an absolute gem in Taipei in this family-run restaurant. 真的太谢谢你了，老板老板娘！下次再见哦！🙏💯😋
評論3: [S Chen] (4分, 5 months ago): Thick slices sashimi with rice for lunch meal deal. Grilled salmon head with salt was delicious.
評論4: [Nicole] (5分, 3 years ago): Great lunch, fresh, the bowl may not look big but totally full for me, the fish cut very thick, will definitely go again! There's also nice free soup ! Yum!
評論5: [Thomas Pong] (5分, 7 years ago): We booked a table with omakase one day in advance, and it's totally worthwhile. Great seafood and friendly cook. A definite must try!

Google Map地址: 103台灣臺北市大同區民生西路198-4號
評論1: [Brian Colvin] (5分, 3 months ago): This was our last meal of our Taipei trip, and it did not let us down! We got quite an assortment of dishes and all of them were wonderful. I could not pick just one favorite item. It is probably good to go here with a group so you can try a bunch. The service was excellent, they kept our table full and made sure we had everything we needed. This was the perfect end to our short trip to Taiwan, Thanks! (also, thanks to Johnnie for taking us to this spot for our farewell meal!)
評論2:
[Anisah 思思] (4分, 2 years ago): There are two different restaurants operating , one for lunch and a different one for dinner.

This review is for lunch.
We went once at night, they don't have the same menu- no fried rice, the ones we craved, no adegashi toufoo.

Satisfies your cravings for Japanese cuisine.  Nothing fancy just good food at reasonable prices.

Get down at MRT Shuanglian Station, just a short walk.
Lots of restaurants all along the way.

Must order :
Fried rice
Salmon belly shashimi
Grilled eel
Tempura shrimp - this is very good, not many restaurants can do this well.
Agedashi towfoo

Kirin ichiban beer

Yummy , food coma after !!!

We went again , this time after 6pm to find that a different restaurant is operating in the same space. The menu was almost similar but the prices were much higher at night. Also they didn't have fried rice at night.
The shashimi portions were do much smaller and more expensive.
So best to go for lunch and not dinner.
評論3: [Joel Yeo] (4分, 5 years ago): We dropped by this place nearby Ningxia Night Market宁夏夜市, there is another wet market at the row of shop, where this shop can be found at the 1st entrance of the market, the order person was abit impatient and sounded restless while we take a look and ask if there were dine in options. Quite a number of customers,  we got our seats anyway, cleanliness moderately acceptable as it is in a market, wait quite a while for our salmon order to arrive, 9pcs for NTD240. The color was abit lighter than in the display glass, however still tasted good or above average. There were many order and customers waiting for their fish also. All in all, reasonable price for such fish, just abit unhappy with server.
評論4:
[Aline BDI] (5分, 6 months ago): Generous And Tasty food
English speaking
評論5: [i . am . kenny] (4分, 8 years ago): Salmon and Swordfish is fresh. Spotted a bone in my Salmon.

Google Map地址: 103台灣臺北市大同區民樂街6號
評論1:
[Alexander Creed] (5分, 3 weeks ago): Food has an uncanny ability to make people happy - speaking from personal experience. I long for, look forward to, joy-making culinary experiences. In that spirit, 江牛樓 is a smile factory. A joy machine. Serving dishes of pure happiness.

I was grinning with ecstasy through every minute of my first, second and third consecutive daily visits to 江牛樓. Yes - I am obsessed with these beef noodles. And for good reason. These guys are THE beef noodle masters. From the unctuous, umami infused broth. To the beef that melts in your chopsticks as you try to gobble it down. To the dramatic, smoky side dish with a healthy chunk of wasabi. It is joy personified.

The smile on my face lasted long after I left - and so too did the mouth-watering taste of the broth. And somehow, the staff at 江牛樓, while serving Michelin star quality food, remain completely down-to-earth, welcoming and delightfully unpretentious. After my third (and sadly final - for now) visit I felt like I was leaving family behind. Until I return to Taipei, I will have a void in my heart where the staff at 江牛樓 and their beef noodles will always remain.

If I could only have one beef noodle for the rest of my life, it would be this one. In fact, if I could only have one dish for the rest of my life, it would also be this.

Try it - you will not be disappointed.

谢谢你. Thank you. Thank you. Thank you 🙏🙏🙏
評論2: [Michael Henley] (5分, a month ago): Every bit of our food was full of flavors. We had both beef noodles and A5 steak. Amazing beef flavor and texture that melts in your mouth. Noodles that gives a unique chew that's hard to find, broth that is delicious to the last drop, and the beef in the bowl just feel like the warmest hug from home.
評論3:
[Chi Pham] (4分, 3 months ago): Arrived at 3 p.m., got seated right away on the 2nd floor, and the food came out in about 10 minutes; so for a moment I wondered if I’d walked into the same place other reviews mentioned. Maybe it’s best to avoid peak meal times.

There are only two noodle options, and we weren’t offered the duck slices so I’m not sure how those are - but the beef in the noodles was plenty and incredibly tender. The noodles were nicely chewy, and the broth was deep with a bit of fatty richness floating on top. The wasabi boosted the flavor so much. The atmosphere felt rather westernized, with a US/UK playlist playing in the background.

NT$290/bowl.
CASH ONLY.
評論4:
[Kwan] (5分, a month ago): Deceitfully clear soup was packed with flavor, it was so good we came back for seconds ✌️

Clear soup is definitely the winner but the braised beef soup was good as well. Both types of noodles were good but the thick ones takes the 🍰

The smoked duck was tender and the skin was surprisingly crisp
評論5:
[Luke Low] (5分, 10 months ago): Worth the wait. Queued at 10.30am already got a queue. Since 1 person so able to get the 1.40pm slot.
Oh the soup is looks oily but it’s not. The chilli oil does not burn down the throat. The beef is super soft.best there is option to add in Wasbi to the meat, very interesting experience. Feels like eating sashimi… ordered thin noodles and they are firm yet can clip very easily. the experience is very personal, only 8 seats in front counter. Finish then will call in the next group. Very enjoyable eating experience.

Price wise is TWD400 (if I remember correctly) the beef ramen, duck breast TWD150.

餐廳名稱: Parko Parco 牛肚包義大利餐酒館
Google Map地址: 103台灣臺北市大同區承德路三段80號1樓
評論1: [Jennifer Fu] (5分, 2 months ago): I loved visiting Parko Parco’s new location! The old location was small and cozy, but the new location is so much more classy, and still very cozy! Everything tasted just as good as I remembered, and the seasonal crab squid ink pasta was phenomenal. Really really wish they’d serve their sister store’s cannoli and gelato here though 🥹 definitely felt like some desserts would’ve wrapped up the night even more perfectly.
評論2: [Matt Masui] (5分, 5 months ago): I was blown away by their panino. The dough was perfectly crispy, and the tripe with chili sauce was incredible. It was so good, I ended up ordering two more! The terrace seating is lovely, with a quiet view of the park. I’ll definitely be back next time I’m in Taipei.
評論3: [Inès Zitouni] (5分, a year ago): This place is so cute! Feels like Italy 😍 Every dish was so tasty and the wine pairing was perfect. We had the red wine of the day which was Circo Rosso and apart from the regular menu they also had daily specials 😋 The staff was very helpful and knowledgeable and took good care of us throughout the meal 🙏 The parts of meat that are cooked here cannot be found in many places in Taipei! Very special place ✨
評論4: [Joseph Ahdoot] (5分, 3 months ago): One of the best Italian restaurants in all of Taipei. It’s good to see a restaurant that is more bread based and does not change the original recipe. The bread is freshly made on the premises and the beef is wonderfully tender
評論5:
[Kayo Chang Black] (4分, 2 years ago): It's a cute and cozy Italian restaurant. The food is pretty good, the Bolognese sauce is fantastic. We were a little shocked about the coca powder on the Bolognese penne dish but it was okay (my husband didn't like it though). Maybe it would be nice to mention the coca powder on the menu.

The mango, serrano and arugula salad and burrata cheese salad were excellent. The only thing I didn't like as much was the roasted chicken leg. It was a bit dry,  like they cooked it and reheated it.

There's no bathroom in the restaurant so you have to go across the street to the public bathrooms.  Other than that, the place is delightful.

餐廳名稱: Moni .curry｜咖哩餐廳。擠花甜點。貓貓酥
Google Map地址: 103台灣臺北市大同區西寧北路63號1樓2樓
評論1: [An Le] (5分, 3 weeks ago): My friend had cheese and bacon croissant while I had cheese and egg. The portions are generous and also delicious. The owner and staff are really nice. They came to ask if I would like to sit inside because they were afraid it could be too hot outside. It's difficult to find a stop with quiet outdoor seating in Dihua Old Street, so I highly recommend this restaurant if you also enjoy sitting outside like me 😄
評論2: [Nico Robles] (3分, 3 months ago): 3 stars. Moni Curry in Taipei was just okay for me. The curry tasted oddly sweet, which threw me off, and the karaage didn’t hit the Japanese-style flavor I was expecting—it was a bit of a letdown. Still, the serving was big enough to fill me up after a long walk, so at least it did the job.
評論3: [Jess] (5分, 2 weeks ago): A quaint little spot for afternoon tea and the perfect place to rest your feet after exploring the nearby Dihua Street. The decor and tableware are absolutely adorable, and the service is impeccable — warm, attentive, and thoughtful. A lovely hidden gem for a slow, cozy afternoon.
評論4: [Kylie Dugan] (5分, 3 months ago): Delicious meal that is more than generous! The rice is well done, the egg and chicken are cooked perfectly, and the service is responsive. English menu is also available.
評論5: [Uğur Pınaraltı] (5分, a month ago): When you order a meal you get a complimentary souo before your meal, i enjoyed meal, as a Turkish person of course it was a bit strange for our dish taste, but still finished the whole plate.

Google Map地址: 103台灣臺北市大同區南京西路338號1樓
評論1: [Lóránd Gyenge] (5分, 6 months ago): The food is really-really delicious. I'm visiting first time in Taiwan and the food everywhere is really great and specia,l but here the components are in perfect harmony and each bite is a pleasure.
評論2:
[Adrick Loh] (4分, a year ago): I came here 3 times during my stay at the hotel beside this shop. We came here mainly to get a drink. Must recommend the Yuzu flavored beer and liquor. Less than NT$200.
Food wise it’s a bit of hits and misses. Overall I would visit it if I am staying nearby. Staffs are really helpful! Good job.
評論3:
[Emily NG] (5分, 9 months ago): Love the udon but the non alcoholic drink selection is quite limited as my daughter doesn’t take alcohol and sparkling drinks.
Minimum order is at least a drink and she doesn’t like any of those.
評論4: [Adam H] (4分, 7 months ago): The food here was good, and service was fine. They did not have any English menus, or signage, so we were caught off guard when it was cash only (servers didn’t tell us until we tried to pay). At least 1 drink per person is required also.
評論5: [Tien Phong Pham] (5分, a year ago): There is no grilled food at noon, but this noodle dish really suits me, the staff is enthusiastic.👍❤️

餐廳名稱: 無所洋食- 蔬食日式洋食--義大利麵、咖哩、中式米飯、拌麵 (Vegan Pasta & Curry Rice)
Google Map地址: 103台灣臺北市大同區迪化街一段358之1號
評論1: [Cito Pilini] (5分, a month ago): Delicious vegan food, nice place, friendly service, fair prices... I really enjoyed this place. You have a lot of choices!
評論2:
[Jacelyn] (1分, 6 months ago): I rarely leave negative reviews, but I feel this experience should be shared.

I first tried to make a reservation through LINE, but received no response. When I arrived, I was told the restaurant was fully booked. A simple reply to my message would have saved me and my family the trouble. I asked if we could dine in quickly since there were empty tables at least an hour before the next reservation, but they refused. I then requested takeout instead.

Unfortunately, from the moment I paid, the attitude shifted noticeably. When I asked politely if there were nearby places we could eat (as we’re not familiar with the area), the staff dismissed us, saying they were “too busy.” When I requested extra cutlery because we were sharing sets, the staff rolled her eyes and told me I’d have to pay extra, only giving the exact number of forks based on the sets ordered. Being met with eye-rolling and curt replies multiple times was really unpleasant.

On top of the service, the food itself was a disappointment. It was slimy, tasteless, and far from what I’d expect given the high praise this restaurant often receives. As someone who has been vegetarian since birth, I genuinely do not understand the hype.

Overall, I tried to approach the staff with patience and understanding, but the experience left us feeling unwelcome and disrespected. Both the service and food fell far below expectations.

[Louis_Foodie Wong] (5分, 10 months ago): 📌Taipei

"Affordable Eats: Dadaocheng, Taiwan"

After exploring Ximending the next day, our affordable eatery took us to Dadaocheng, a traditional old street in Taipei. Besides traditional dried goods, there are many small, everyday shops. We strolled around until sunset, then headed to the Dadaocheng pier to enjoy the sunset and snacks. One of our favorites was Osaka-style okonomiyaki (@yanyanokonomiyaki)

🇯🇵Japanese-style Seafood Osaka-style Osaka-style NT $250 The okonomiyaki was generously filled with toppings, and the cabbage was crisp and crunchy. Overall, the taste was great, just a bit pricey 😋👍🏻👍🏻

📍Yan Yan Okonomiyaki
Address: Dadaocheng Wharf Container Market, Datong District, Taipei City, Taiwan

評論1: [L. L.] (5分, a month ago): Good value buffet for dim sum and many other Chinese dishes for $720NT/person. We arrived 11:05 am for lunch, but it was already packed full. Fortunately, they gave us a table in the corner. There are many selection of dim sum and various meats (bbq pork, roast duck, Beijing beef, etc). We felt that dim sum and other dishes were good, but not the best. However, there's many appetizers, salads, main popular dishes, desserts, fruits, drinks, and even ice creams. They even have 5 different hot tea pots you can enjoy.  Overal, it's a great value for the variety and good quality of buffet!  Make sure to make reservations.
評論2:
[Rachel T] (5分, 3 months ago): Fantastic experience! Great value and quality. This became one the highlights of our trip to Taiwan! Food was great and I love the selection of tea, chicken soup was fantastic, the Fried Orh Ni ball, steamed Dimsum and the baked pastures as well as the traditional desert.  The starfruit was so sweet!
Around about 800 TWD per pax. But must make sure reservation is Made as this place is super popular!

We will be back when we come to Taipei again.
評論3: [Rei Rei] (5分, 3 months ago): The variety of food is really extensive! Especially like the fried rice, hor fun and the fried stuffs. The siew mai with abalone was so so. Desserts were good! U will be served a pot of tea once seated. The prawn inside the prawn rice noodle rolls didn't taste fresh. They don't allow photo and video taking of the food in display. 評論4:
[Wisanu Tuntawiroon] (5分, a year ago): Dimsum Kitchen at the Shilin Official Residence location is a fantastic spot for dim sum lovers. One of the most impressive aspects of this restaurant is its extensive menu. From classic favorites like shrimp dumplings and BBQ pork buns to creative new dishes, there’s something to satisfy every craving.

The variety of dim sum is remarkable, making it easy to try a mix of traditional and unique options. The dishes are beautifully presented and freshly made, with flavors that balance authenticity and innovation. The crispy turnip cakes and xiao long bao are must-tries, and their dessert selections, such as mango pudding, provide a perfect sweet finish to the meal.

The restaurant is spacious, clean, and welcoming, with a cozy yet lively atmosphere. The service is attentive and efficient, even during busy hours. It’s an excellent spot for families, groups of friends, or even solo diners who want to indulge in a dim sum feast.

Food wasn't consistent, I tried all the dumplings and there was this dumplings that has 2 pieces in a tray, it was supposed to had egg in it since my brother had came to eat here before but I didn't get the egg inside in one of the dumpling and I also got the egg inside in another dumpling. I tried their fried rolls too but mine wasn't taste like curry & had a lot of fillings but my brother's was really bad + tasted like curry with flours. The herbal soup was really weird & bad too. The most okay foods was their pao, siomay, egg tart & fried dish that has meat inside i dunno what's the name. And to get the salted egg pao, you need to queue fast and can only get 2 mini pao 🫠
Overall I won't come here again if they don't serve haagendasz for the icecream.

評論2: [Christine Brynn Khalil] (5分, 5 months ago): As a California girl that has been living in Taipei for almost 4 years, I can confidently say this place is above and beyond most of the competition. This isn't just a pasta shop, which many places in the area will do an "ok" job at - the ingredients are fresh, and present crunchy, chewy, tender, spicy and umami in symphony. I've been here a handful of times and am consistently impressed.

評論4:
[Amber Tran] (2分, a year ago): Sure the food was not terrible but I don't get the hype.. We got the beef ball and chicken pasta, both were mediocre. The chicken pasta's sauce was bland and a bit on the watery side for pasta sauce in my opinion and the beef's one was basically gochujang, pretty boring and unnecessarily overpowering. I don't understand the tiny pieces of veggies either, pretty uncommon on a pasta dish (we ended up eating with a spoon since it was not very practical to eat with a fork).
For appetizer we got the seaweed roll, which was very disappointing since the tempura batter was hard, not crispy as expected.
I guess the highlight of the meal was the cantaloupe soda, which was from a can (also unexpected).
To sum up, the experience felt almost like self-service. We ordered by checking off dishes on a paper menu, and we also helped ourselves to free water and napkins at a station. Additionally, we arrived before the restaurant's opening hour (5:30 p.m.), and waited outside with a few other groups. However, when it was 5:30, the staff remained inside, chatting, and we weren’t sure when it was okay to enter. It wasn’t until a delivery person arrived that my husband followed him in and asked the staff if we could come in. Only then did we realize that we could. Despite having a glass door, which allowed the staff to see us waiting outside, no one made an effort to welcome us in when it was finally time for the restaurant to open.

評論2:
[Theresa Tan] (2分, a month ago): I would like to provide feedback regarding our recent dining experience at your restaurant.

There were eight of us dining before a flight, and we were seated at different tables. We were informed that there was a minimum individual spend of TWD 200. We have order a 2 person set meal and additional main dish, our table’s total bill amounted to TWD 1,395. However, we were told this did not count as a shared meal and that each person was required to order a main dish. The waitress told me we need to order another dish of at least 139 to meet the 个人消费。

While we understand and are fine with a minimum individual spend requirement, we find it unreasonable to impose an additional requirement to order a main dish, especially when the minimum spend was already met. This led to excessive food being ordered that we could not finish, resulting in unnecessary food waste.

More importantly, we observed that this rule was not applied consistently, as other tables were not subjected to the same requirement. This inconsistency made the situation particularly frustrating and unacceptable.
Attached 2 photos of our bills at 2 different table

We hope you will review this policy and its enforcement to ensure fairness and a better dining experience for all customers.

評論3: [Always Hungry] (4分, 2 years ago): Had an event for 8 person there. The cocktails were great, we even had 3 bottles of sake. Food was a big disappointment, had a lot of leftovers. The service was very nice maybe because there were not alot of people on a weekday night.
評論4: [Sharon Seow] (5分, 2 years ago): I was recommended this place by a friend. The name already gave me good vibes due to its uniqueness. The interior design didn't disappoint. Foodwise, the szechuan spicy noodles and the szechuan popcorn chicken were right up my alley with the correct texture and right amount of spice that gives a kick but doesn't kill the tastebuds. The non-spicy truffle chicken rice was a hit with my tween who left me with just a spoonful to try. There are other interesting side dishes like the intestines, blood and fish cake that one can try alongside the mains. To top it off, the service staff were attentive and friendly which makes this visit an overall excellent experience! Located at Shihlin, it is very easy to get there. This bistro makes a great stopover while visiting the night market. A fun place to rest the body and reward the stomach before experiencing more of Taipei!

Google Map地址: 11141台灣臺北市士林區中山北路五段470巷8號1樓
評論1:
[LD CM] (3分, 9 months ago): Having arrived in the Shilin area early for official business at the nearby Shilin Residence, I thought it most fitting to begin the day with a hearty breakfast at a reputable establishment. Sadly, my visit to the Shilin Kitchen within the Renaissance Taipei proved to be far less agreeable than anticipated.

Upon entering, I was received by a male host whose demeanour could best be described as impatient and indifferent. One expects, at the very least, a modicum of courtesy and attentiveness in such establishments. Unfortunately, none was afforded. The interaction felt rushed and perfunctory, lacking even a semblance of hospitality.

The marble-top tables, though ostensibly luxurious, were presented bare, no placemats, no tablecloths, leaving every scratch, mark, and smear cruelly exposed under the morning light. It gave an impression less of minimalist chic and more of carelessness.

As for the breakfast itself, one could only describe it as entirely lacklustre. The scrambled eggs were a disappointment, texturally unappealing and lacking in seasoning. The pre-cooked fried eggs, sitting forlornly under a heat lamp, were equally uninspired. Fruits, which should bring freshness and colour to a morning plate, were limited to a paltry selection, perhaps two or three uninspired choices.

There was no sense of thought or culinary pride in the offerings. The entire affair felt as though it had been assembled out of obligation, not care. It was the kind of buffet one might expect at a lesser hotel chain, not from a property under the Marriott name.

Thankfully, I was in possession of a dining voucher, which slightly softened the blow. Had I paid full price for this uninspired experience, I dare say I would have regretted it most bitterly.

A deeply underwhelming breakfast marked by indifferent service, poor presentation, and a conspicuous absence of culinary inspiration. I would not return, nor recommend it, unless, of course, one is armed with a voucher and exceedingly modest expectations.
評論2:

I'd come here just for the salad bar buffet
評論3: [Edward Lu] (3分, 2 years ago): We went there for “main course” plus “semi buffet” dinner. The four main courses we ordered were all very delicious. 評論4: [YiHsuan Tseng] (5分, 3 years ago): Had a dinner gathering with my family. It’s my second time visit the restaurant, the location is quiet and spacious, not many people here. 評論5: [Yen-Chung Chen] (5分, a year ago): We had a nice experience for the breakfast there. The decoration of the restaurant is fashion and makes us had happy mood.
