Aircon nice and cool!

Recommended!
評論4: [Shuffle Dance Sweet Art] (5分, 6 months ago): 9/20/2025, Last lunch in Taiwan. At Shilin station I accidentally came across an Italian noodle shop. I went and ordered a bacon noodle, hmmm the meat was rich in flavor and when I ate the noodles it reminded me of the days I was still in Europe so the taste was great. I hope when I return to Taiwan I can visit this place again.
評論5: [Sasa Wu] (1分, a year ago): We were there this weekend and the experience is so not good. The staffs don’t even smile when we entered the restaurant. Then she gave us a table beside the toilet even though she saw we have a baby with us and there are lots of empty tables on the other side of the room.  Many people came later and all of them were placed on the other side while we are the only persons on the toilet side (i feel discriminated).Then to prevent my baby from crying and disturbing other people, i gave her my phone to watch youTube, then the waitress went to the back and turned the music so loud that my baby can no longer hear what she’s watching that made baby irritable. And when she serve the food, she didn’t even smile or atleast nod when we said ‘thank you’ to her ( friendly huh?) The food is fair but we will never come back and will never recommend this to other people. This place is worse!!!

Google Map地址: 111台灣臺北市士林區中山北路五段470巷8號1樓
評論1:
[SH W] (2分, 6 years ago): If the ambiance and atmosphere matter to you, this is a beautiful restaurant with big space and friendly staffs, but nothing worth for the food and money (Taipei is a city full of delicious food, here is not one of them). The worst are: Taiwanese kimchi $280NTD - no, too sweet and tastes really not fresh, you could def get a plate way better than this in a local restaurant even for free. BBQ Combination 燒味三拼 $980NTD is the worst in my entire life!! (Not kidding) How could you make BBQ combination this bad taste and the oil they used makes me sick?! I love BBQ combination and I always try them in every restaurant I go where serve it. This is beyond words I’d describe. We couldn’t finish it at all. The dimsum thing, looks pretty, but where did you buy it? - Highlight was the kale, really fresh and delicious, thanks to the farmers!
Don’t pretend it’s a high-end restaurant just because you’re cooking in a 5 stars hotel. Please have some moral responsibility to your customers, who support you. Thank you.
評論2: [王浩禾] (4分, 6 years ago): Beautiful restaurant, friendly service and good if not exceptional Cantonese basics (but no dim sum).  Cubed steaks, stir fried pork and chicken fried rice were very good, egg yolk fried shrimp less so.
評論3: [Claya Chu] (5分, 7 years ago): Nice pork stew and Shanghainese fried rice. The rest are mediocre. The facilities is brand new and staff are hospitable. Overall experience is good.
評論4:
[MY 3659 (MY)] (5分, 4 years ago): The food was so good. I had no time for the rest of the order. It’s definitely delicious!

This new chef is Taiwanese now,  no longer Hong Kong style menu. FYI
評論5:
[Ma Shun] (4分, 6 years ago): Cozy room, good location, warm staff.
This hotel is quite new as just been renovated.
Glad to stay here as it is within the walking distance to Shilin night market.

餐廳名稱: 荷麵亭 HASUMENTEI 士林店
Google Map地址: 111台灣臺北市士林區中山北路五段505巷42弄2號
評論1: [Peerawich Promjai] (5分, 11 months ago): Ramen is more than perfect! It's super tasty! Fried oysters and lobster salad are great too. Chef and staff are very nice and friendly. Would love to re-visit and try other dishes.
評論2:
[Yu-Ting] (4分, a year ago): After you enter, you order and pay. If you want softer or harder noodles, this is the time to say it.

This is another good bowl of ramen. The soup has intense umami, salty and spicy flavoring. I asked for a clear soup to add to the existing soup and it turned out very good.

Spicy Ramen: 8/10
Fried Shrimp Cake: 7.5/10
評論3:
[Jeff L] (5分, 2 years ago): Amazing Japanese ramen shop right next to the metro stop.

The soup is filled with flavor, the meat is outta this world, and the egg is made to perfection.

You can order two more servings of noodles for free, just make sure to raise your hand before you do.

Highly recommend if you’re in the area.
評論4: [Y L] (4分, 2 years ago): The food is quite authentic. The crispy layer of oyster matches it so well. The ramen is pretty good and if you don’t like any ingredient, they offer customization. It’s kinda salty to me, but they have iced water and you can help yourself, so I’m fine with that. The only thing that kinda bothered me, which is very personal, is that they have signs literally everywhere to remind people not to immerse themselves in cellphones but to focus on food (which is good) and finish and go. That caused stress when eating and I couldn’t relax and enjoy the food. Other than that everything is good.
評論5: [Linus Wong] (3分, 3 years ago): Better than a Taiwanese-flavored ramen shop, with on point noodles and a strong thicker broth, but disappointed by the chashu and salt-flavored pork soup base. Thought the soup might be a lighter more delicate base, but instead it was just extra salty, far too much to finish on its own. The pork chashu was a little ham-like and on the drier side. Not sure if that was on purpose or just overcooked, but definitely not worth remembering. The tangxin egg was also not as tasty as many other ramen shops. Fried oyster was pretty good tho, and service was fine.

餐廳名稱: 雞老闆 桶仔雞 士林店
Google Map地址: 111台灣臺北市士林區文林路101巷2號
評論1: [李昇祐] (5分, 5 months ago): Good
評論2: [Neo Soon Kian] (5分, 2 years ago): Food were amazing, service were good. Will definitely visit again!
評論3: [泰雅公主X雪怪Yetis] (5分, 3 months ago): Yummy
評論4: [Minh Anh Phan] (5分, 3 years ago): Good place to go, also the chicken is good
評論5: [lhb] (5分, 9 months ago): Good atmosphere

餐廳名稱: 天母盛鑫-士林聚餐餐廳|歐式料理|歐式餐廳|特色餐廳|必吃異國料理|異國美食|異國料理推薦|在地推薦餐廳
Google Map地址: 111台灣臺北市士林區中山北路五段729號
評論1: [KN Ng] (5分, 3 years ago): We went for bday celebration for my MIL. Food there was just beyond expectation, ambience and setting were slightly old school, but dont affect the whole dining experience. We had a great time the whole  evening. Highly recommended
評論2: [L] (5分, 6 years ago): Great service and atmosphere! Staff were extremely friendly and loving the freshness of the salad bar! We have been coming here for years, and never disappoint! Can’t wait for our next visit!
評論3: [Helena] (5分, 3 years ago): Been here more than 10 times! Perfect place for a family dine🥰
評論4: [May Sun] (5分, 4 years ago): Great place to have dinner/lunch with your loved one. Staffs are well trained and food is really tasty. The surprise might be found on the salad bar. Love it.
評論5:
[Melissa Tay] (1分, a year ago): Poor service. For the price, I expected the service to be better. Poor experience overall.
Roast chicken takes an hour to be served, after everyone at the table has finished their meal. We ordered pasta for kids and asked for it to be served first, was served second last.

Google Map地址: 111台灣臺北市士林區大東路133號
評論1: [Lawrence] (5分, 2 years ago): Good quality Japanese Ramen shop.  Scallop chicken Ramen is very good.  You can get extra noodles and soup for free.  (The only slight negative is that the soup does not seem to have much real chicken or pork soup stock.)  I will try the dipping soup noodles next time.  Looks yummy.  Also they give a free cooked egg for 5 star reviews nevertheless the restaurant is quite good.
評論2:
[John Lam] (5分, 6 years ago): I am from Canada and I live around this area for traveling.  I just tried their soup noodle for my lunch.
Service is excellent and the taste is excellent too.  They are very patient to explain to me the menu although I can’t speak Mandarin.  Add extra noodle is free.   I really enjoy the food.
評論3: [Alex Lai] (4分, 5 years ago): Japanese style ramen. You can choose thick or thin noodle as you wish. But when you order “free noodle” afterwards, the only option is the thin one. The price matches the quality. A nice choice nearby.
評論4: [julian tadulan] (4分, 6 years ago): Small place but their ramen will satisfy your palate. Fast service.Their Ramen was thick and rich in flavour that will satisfy your palate.
評論5:
[Thomi Kamilla] (5分, 3 years ago): Noodle, meat, and soup is good. Especially the spicy one. Worth to come when you are hungry and seeking for new taste.
A bit pricey, if only they give whole egg instead of half maybe it would be ok

Google Map地址: 111台灣臺北市士林區福華路150號
評論1: [Anik Bibib] (5分, a month ago): If you are craving authentic Malaysian food, you must visit Mr. Chee (Shilin Branch)! They hold a Halal-friendly certificate and use only Halal-certified meat sources, so you can dine with peace of mind. The location is very strategic, situated right near Zhishan Station. Their laksa soup is incredibly delicious, and you can really taste that all the condiments are freshly made. It is a highly recommended spot for any Muslim friend looking for a great meal in the area.
評論2: [Chusnul Khotimah] (5分, 6 months ago): All the menu are using Halal meat!! Also they don't sell menu with Pork and not using alcohol for all menu. So, for my Muslim friends, you can enjoy all the menu here~
評論3:
[Valeria Rios] (5分, 2 months ago): The food is amazing. Good spicy, good amount. The curry? Omg !!!!
They only have one vegetarian option as a main dish but is really good
評論4: [Rama Rizana] (5分, 2 months ago): We ordered some foods and beverages, especially the one that I had was Char Kway Teow. Very tasty. The staffs are very helpful and kind. Thank you.
評論5:
[A O] (5分, 4 months ago): Always ordered on Uber and love the sambal chilli as it is authentic to sambal in Singapore.

Food overall is good and fresh.

Google Map地址: 111台灣臺北市士林區中山北路五段505巷22號
評論1:
[Lorraine Frederica] (5分, 3 years ago): Update: they changed to a nearby bigger place. Better one. More space for guests to eat, with same price and same excellent service.

One of the best ramen i have tasted, in my opinion. The place is small and cute, there was a line but i didn't wait too long. The food came very quickly and the employees were amazingly helpful.

The broth is really creamy so beware if you dislike thick broth. The texture of the noodle is unique and a great pair to the broth. The place itself is clean and comfortable.

Price wise is so-so, price range $190-$250 approximately.
評論2:
[Sascha Pallenberg] (5分, a month ago): Absolutely amazing Tonkatsu Ramen in an authentic atmosphere. Came for the look frome the outside and stayed for some of the best Ramen in Taipei.
Very accessible for foreigners. They even have an English menu and help you with the Ramen machine at the entrance.

I'll be back!
評論3:
[Y L] (5分, 10 months ago): Superb dining experience! Having ate ichiran For 2 days consecutively and facing with ridiculous queue there, I would recommend u guys to come here instead. Portion is bigger, soup is as nice if not, nicer and meat is definitely better here. Pro tip: remember to add the garlic in the soup for the full ichiran taste!

Service n environment is 💪🏻 would come here next time instead
評論4:
[YiHsuan Tseng] (4分, 4 years ago): Nice location, friendly staff, clean table, cheerful ambiance, fast and customized service, and reasonable price.

But the very soul to the store, ramen, is just okay and nothing special. The store attracts long queue every time I passed through the road, but I do think it is overrated.

It is still a good choice and nothing wrong with the store, but it’s definitely not worthy of long queue waiting.

I gave four stars instead of three because there is no time limit for customers despite their limited seats and a long queue outside. Perhaps that’s the reason that makes the store seem to be this popular?
評論5:
[Sokyoul] (5分, a year ago): Great Ramen!
I took the Tonkotsu with extra egg. You have the choice of thin and thick noodles, I personally prefer the thicker ones. It tastes just like in Japan.

The shop is conveniently located directly next to Shilin Stn.

The stuff were super friendly. The lady helped me order in English at the machine since I don't speak Mandarin.
I was asked if I want to add more clear soup to dillute the spicyness.

I ordered the "mild" version, which was normal spicy for my European tounge. So for someone who actually wants mild, I would recommend ordering "not spicy".

All in all would recommend!

餐廳名稱: 味擂家みらい家日式料理 士林店（無訂位服務）
Google Map地址: 111台灣臺北市士林區福壽街1號1樓
評論1:
[chu kok wee] (4分, 2 months ago): Delicious Japanese bento-style set meals with a Taiwanese twist.
Taste is good and well-balanced, with generous portions.
Prices are slightly above average, but value is added with free miso soup and friendly service.
A satisfying and comforting meal overall.
評論2: [Natthaphong Chongcha 李俊宇] (5分, 10 months ago): A restaurant decorated with eye-catching Japanese design, very highly recommended for those who love Japanese food and expect its authentic taste once arrived. Plus, a service here is beyond awesome, they treat you really really well with optimistic attitude. And I’m looking forward to coming back here again despite the distance from my residence.🥹
評論3: [Noel Lau] (5分, 2 years ago): Amazingly good Japanese food with friendly services. You can sense that the food is lovingly prepared and with passion. The set meal comes with a free flow of rice and miso soup.
評論4: [Nguyệt Ninh] (5分, 6 months ago): The restaurant is my must-visit place everytime travel to Taiwan, my friends from countries love the dishes here. Their staffs are super nice and friendly, willing to serve all the customers carefully even though all the table are taken (very crowded). Food are fresh and tasty with no doubt. It would be better if they opened another larger branch for more convenience.
評論5: [Sophie Chang] (5分, a year ago): Every dish I’ve tried so far is super good!!! Personal favorite Japanese restaurant of the year🥰🥰

餐廳名稱: Skylark洋食‧芳鄰 士林中山北店
Google Map地址: 111台灣臺北市士林區中山北路五段565號
評論1:
[YiHsuan Tseng] (5分, 2 weeks ago): Convenient location near Shilin MRT Station. There’s outdoor seating where you can enjoy some sunshine. The portions aren’t big, but the food tastes good and the prices are quite affordable.

When it’s busy, there’s a time limit for indoor seating, but the outdoor seats don’t have a limit.

It’s a nice spot to meet friends who live nearby for a simple meal and a relaxed chat.
評論2: [Carter Madura] (4分, 3 months ago): The restaurant's aesthetic was comfortable, and I liked the way the food was presented. Gorgeous dishware, solid utensils, pretty appealing to the eyes. The food itself here was merely decent. For the price, I'd expect higher quality, but it wasn't bad. Due to this discrepancy, I likely won't be returning anytime soon. I didn't personally experience any of the horrible service stories I'm seeing in the other reviews; we were left alone throughout the duration of our meal. However, I too noticed that there was not any soap in the men's bathroom... 📮
評論3:
[Christine Brynn Khalil] (4分, 4 months ago): I really like this place for their family friendly seating and great menu options. The food is just ok though, and a lot of the quality of things like bread, salad, and sides are clearly very cheap + bland but designed to make it feel like you got a very deluxe set meal.
Their pasta dishes and baked rice dishes are best by far of anything on the menu. The service is weird too. They have bizarre rules about seating when arriving between meal seatings (e.g. between breakfast and lunch) and will force guests to wait at the entrance instead of helping them to a seat even when the whole place it's completely empty. They clearly don't prioritize customer comfort or service in this way, and it's rather confusing.
評論4:
[D G] (1分, 2 years ago): Ate here twice in one month. First time average. Second time horrible customer service. Bread is supposedly refillable and if you ask for refill they tell you to order via app so they don't need to refill. Menu has different picture vs reality. See the onion soup. No cheese soggy bread, no onions just sweet onion water. Didn't refill my water or give me my tea until everything was eaten despite repeatedly asking.

The bigger male manager with glasses is very nice but everyone else is very rude and condescending, especially to elderly I was with. They ignored her when she talked

Legitimately one of the worst experiences I have ever had in all of Taiwan

Also men's bathroom doesn't have soap. 🤮🤮🤮🤮🤮🤮🤮🤮
評論5: [Joe Tsao] (4分, 5 years ago): The taste was average at it's best. The ambience is that of a typical family restaurant - energetic and loud at Monday night. Servers are friendly, so it wasn't too bad of an experience.

Google Map地址: 111台灣臺北市士林區小東街31號
評論1: [Earn Chanikan] (5分, 2 months ago): Definitely need to try out their hotpot! I ordered beef hotpot and there are many ingredients added in both seafoods, vegetables and many meat balls! With only 190 TW dollar! They also offered free drink with unlimited refills and rice if you order fried chicken! Very popular among local!
評論2:
[お小賴] (4分, 6 months ago): A great student restaurant that serves big portion with a friendly price tag, you can up size your steamy white rice for free, the side dish lacks in green vegetables but still pretty decent and tastes great.

Main protein dish is amazing and the portion is massive.
評論3: [Rae Zyon] (5分, 11 months ago): Famous for their friend chicken chop rice, the chicken is crispy and breaded and seasoned perfectly, not too thick and the meat is tender and juicy. Comes with two sides of vegetables and pork braised rice. The rice is refillable, as the staff walks around with a tray of rice. Drinks are also free, just get a cup and dispense at the back of the restaurant.
評論4:
[Ethan Tan] (4分, a year ago): 時來運轉 is a no-frills eatery serving local set lunches such as rice with deep fried prawns. Each set comes with free-flow drinks (green tea, red tea, with or without milk) and only costs around $130+.
The eatery was quite well-packed with locals at 2 pm even though it closes at 2.30 pm for a break before resuming dinner service.
Taste-wise, the food was decently delicious. Service was polite.
評論5: [Richard Krahn] (5分, 2 years ago): This is a must stop if you are hungry and are in need of visiting a night market.  Lots of university students frequent this place as they have large portions, quality food, and reasonable prices.   You can also get extra rice for free if your meal doesn’t fill you.

餐廳名稱: Boulevard Cafe 林蔭美式餐酒食(供餐至20:00)
Google Map地址: 111台灣臺北市士林區至善路二段33號
評論1: [Jenny Hsu] (3分, 9 months ago): A mediocre place with Western inspired dishes, but don’t expect anything authentic. The hamburger is tasty, although slightly too sweet and it came out   wet and soggy. The risotto is your typical Western inspired rice dish that has been localised. The Caesar salad wasn’t actually a salad—it came out as an English/American breakfast plate (see photo). The food took awhile to come out.
評論2: [Rick Pennington] (2分, 10 months ago): The Boulevard Cafe is very close to the National Palace Museum and had great reviews at 4.6 stars, however this was not our experience today. The service and the food were a little disappointing. The pizza was ok and the burger was pretty blah… 😑
評論3: [Sharon Daniel] (5分, 3 years ago): This Cafe is located between Shilin and Miramar so you'll need to take a bus to get here. You can take the 255, 620, or 957 from Shilin MRT Station. From Jiannan Rd Station, you can take the 620 or 957. It has two floors of seating and it's best to make a reservation as it a nice comfortable place to stay for a while. The staff is friendly and made recommendations from the menu. They have an English menu which is always very accommodating. I ordered the Vegetarian lasagna which has so much cheese on top that you'll wonder if it has any noodles. It comes with one piece of garlic bread but I'd recommend you get an additional order on the side with 5 pieces. My friend had the Spicy Chicken pasta and it was very spicy owing to the Szechuan pepper and garlic. For dessert we had the very decadent chocolate brownie and the creme brulee custard. We ordered from the unlimited soda refill menu for our drinks. If you order pasta, you can get a set menu price which saves about 60 ntd. We stayed for a long time chatting and lingering over our meals without any pressure from the wait staff to leave. That alone is a reason to return to this establishment.
評論4: [A] (5分, 2 years ago): A short walk from the national palace museum. First time dinning here, was surprise with the quality of their burger and pizza. I guess reservation will be encourage for dine and drink in the night.
評論5: [Terrance Shih] (4分, a year ago): Nice vibe, good food, kid friendly with a good kids meal. Came for a late weekend lunch and we were seated quickly. It feels like it was opened by a group of friends than being very commercial, but the scale is impressive. Mexican burger is small but tasty and spicy. The fries has a unique treatment, so it's definitely memorable. No on-site parking that I'm aware of. The closest parking lots are all 500m+ away.

Google Map地址: 111台灣臺北市士林區天母東路7號
評論1:
[Kate Shao] (5分, 11 months ago): Local time-honored brand Shanghai restaurant in Tainmu. Spacious and clean environment.

Miniature pork steam bun was cute but tasted normal. Fang's specialty tofu was awesome! Soft tofu and it was appetizing.

Shredded beef fried rice had Wok Hei. It was absolutely one of my favorite fried rice! Deep-fried space-rib dressed w/ sweetened soy-sauce was impressive. Crispy outside and tasted like Taiwanese fried chicken.

Reasonable price and friendly service. We ordered six dishes and one soup for a total of NT$2500. Good for family gathering. Especially bringing elders. Next time must order donpo pork.
評論2: [Cherry’s Life in the U.K.] (5分, 6 months ago): Famous Shanghai restaurant in town. Dumping were just okay as I expected too much from review. Flaky scallion pancakes 蔥油餅 were amazing and smelled nice. Rice with veggie, Spicy fish were good as well. This place is crowded, better to make a reservation.
評論3: [Pang Cheung Sze] (3分, 3 years ago): I used to enjoy meals in this restaurant but the quality has slipped in the last couple visits. It is now serving very average food and charge high price. The Shanghainese dim sum is still good, but the hot dishes are really not the same as before.
評論4: [Janice Kuo] (5分, 6 months ago): have not been in this restaurant since 2000. Still great food as usual
評論5: [Mega Chan] (4分, a year ago): Decent food, decent atmosphere and decent service. Only thing I can complain about is that the food must have had a lot of msg, as I must have drank 10 cups of tea while having the meal.

Google Map地址: 111台灣臺北市士林區大東路54號
評論1: [owbert] (3分, a week ago): Unfortunately after the updated menu, no CP值. Overpriced. Favorite dishes are also gone. $350 for 1/5th curry
評論2:
[YiHsuan Tseng] (5分, 4 years ago): Excellent service and delicious food.
The staff is friendly and helpful, leaving me and my friend good impressions. We sit at the outdoor area to enjoy the fresh air in the middle of noon, and it is not that hot as we thought. The appetizer is one of our favorite here, and the other dishes are also fine. We enjoyed a pleasant lunch.
評論3:
[Wesley Wong] (4分, 2 years ago): You'll find a Thai restaurant inside Xilin Night Market. It's set up in an old-fashioned building that gives off a traditional vibe. What's interesting is that the tables aren't completely flat – they're a bit slanted, which adds a fun twist. The bathroom is pretty small, fitting only one person at a time.

Now, when it comes to the food, it's not as spicy as regular Thai dishes. They've made it less spicy so that it matches the taste buds of Taiwanese people. It's a bit different from the usual spiciness you'd expect from Thai cuisine, but it's done this way to make it more appealing to folks around here.
評論4:
[chunky chen] (3分, a year ago): The food is not bad,
but very pricey

The environment vibe is good, the service is good

No particular dish wow me, very average.

Overall 3 stars

Payment : credit card
評論5: [C] (4分, 5 years ago): I can’t say the Thai food here is authentic but it’s rather Thai fusion. Don’t get me wrong though it’s delicious nonetheless. What’s special about this place is that there’s an outdoor seating area in the back for hosting private events or even small weddings. The atmosphere is great. Ambience on point. Definitely worth checking out

餐廳名稱: 士林廣澤擔仔麵｜芝山捷運站美食｜士林排隊美食｜天母推薦人氣必吃餐廳
Google Map地址: 111台灣臺北市士林區福華路10號
評論1: [Chlotus] (5分, 4 years ago): This restaurant is like a fancy version of a noodle restaurant. It does not only serves noodles but also served some Taiwanese dishes and seafood. The noodle was tasty, and the owners were friendly. Strongly recommend this good place if you want to go to a simple place and chat with your friends in a good vibe which also provides tea for free.
評論2: [Sue G] (5分, 4 years ago): This place, our most favorite dinner venue for local Taiwanese home cook food. Just 2 min walking distance from our house, we called to order and just collect once the dishes are ready. For today’s meal, we ordered 2 types of taufoo. One for kids and mapo taufoo for adults. Their signature orange sauce pork which gives sweet and sour taste was truly a delight. The seafood taufoo was so good that it can match the one we ordered previously from Regent hotel. My husband said my mapo toufoo version is better than theirs but I liked theirs with the Szechuan peppercorn taste. Overall, my favorite!
評論3: [Kanit T] (3分, 2 years ago): Was in the area so tried this restaurant, the veg dishes and fried tofu were nice. Noodles were decent though nothing spectacular. Would give it overall more of a 3.5stars.
評論4: [ABILENE] (5分, 2 weeks ago): Incredibly tasty food and wonderful hospitality. The owner is very friendly and makes you feel right at home. A place I’ll happily visit again!
評論5:
[S L] (4分, 5 years ago): This restaurant is conveniently located next to a big carpark.  It's decoration is in the traditional home style.  We were the first customers and soon the restaurant is filled and it is only a Tuesday early afternoon.
Food is fairly priced and taste relatively good.

餐廳名稱: 蔦燒日式居酒屋-士林店
Google Map地址: 111台灣臺北市士林區中山北路五段505巷42弄1號
評論1: [Lisa Wang] (5分, a month ago): Just moved into the area recently and not gonna lie, we haven’t tried any restaurants yet. This restaurant’s food is scrumptious. We definitely will be coming back. The food was warm and the place felt comfortable and homey. There are both table seating and bar seating with a view of the open kitchen. A must try restaurant if you’re in shilin. Delicious, cheap, and convenient because it’s pretty close to the subway station. YOU HAVE TO COME HERE! IT’S DELICIOUS!😋🤤
評論2: [Ricky LK] (5分, a year ago): Visited with my friends, they book the place in advance. I think without booking, it may difficult to get the seat. The food is very tasty, everything. The price is normal for Izakaya. The serving portion is pretty huge compared to the price.
評論3:
[Lim Chen Chun] (5分, 2 years ago): I love this restaurant.
The food here tastes good.
The food portion is bigger than usual. It makes me feel so full!

The serve your food quite fast too.

My wife and I got the bar seating hence we can see 3 chef there preparing all the food.

Friendly Young chefs that are willing to chat with you.

I will definitely come back again.
評論4: [YiHsuan Tseng] (5分, a year ago): The teriyaki restaurant is fantastic. The location is super convenient as it is located just right out side of the MRT. The decoration is cozy and classic. The foods surprised us as the portion is over large lol we two people tried so hard to complete the dishes we ordered. They all taste fine and in reasonable priced. A good place to hang out with friends by midnight for sure.
評論5:
[Stefano Notarbartolo] (5分, 9 months ago): The place is full crowded - a great sign. Food exceeded my expectations,  great service,  helpful waiters who managed to squeeze me in although there was not enough place.
An instant favorite,  totally recommended.

Google Map地址: 11162台灣臺北市士林區大東路152號
評論1: [Kim S] (5分, a year ago): Absolutely loved this hidden gem.  There’s actually a speak easy inside the restaurant (upstairs).  The food was spectacular!  We had the spicy fried chicken and sugar cane shrimp roll.  Eat the sugar cane that it’s wrapped around!!  Hookah was also great!  Love love love.
評論2:
[Johnson Deng] (5分, a year ago): Small place. Not too many spots for couples to sit except at the bar. But the drinks—construction and taste, drink presentation were all top quality. I’ll come back because of the drink taste on some.

Fried platter was also great, I would’ve liked a lot more ketchup or a spread of other sauces to go with it. With how much I paid for the platter, I’m sure they would’ve still made money off me.
評論3:
[YiHsuan Tseng] (5分, 5 years ago): Been here for my birthday celebration last year (around Halloween) and it is a nice local bar. They played good music and offer creative drinks with fun games. For people come on their birthday, they treat them with special shots. This place is full of vitality at night but also got its balance for people to relax and chill.

Their drinks, however, is a bit Slim style. Maybe it’s why many girls fancy the place. The food is okay. It’s strongly recommended to make a reservation in advance. Otherwise, the queue will be quite long. Would love to come back for casual drinks from time to time.
評論4: [ah ya] (4分, 2 years ago): Nice and Cosy place. Great for gathering, there is a minimum spending requirement which is reasonable but it will be better to share during the booking to avoid any surprise for the customers.
評論5: [Weeraya Jajai] (5分, a year ago): The service that we had was really impressed from Mr.Kai ‘s service. He can speak english very well. The drink was good and the fried was good too. The cat is cute  and atmosphere was nice too.

Google Map地址: 111台灣臺北市士林區大東路49號
評論1:
[Philip Raymond Lim] (3分, 10 months ago): I’d give it three star not because it’s bad. The taste is good, the make is really basic. The ten herbs soup was tasty but the rib is all bones. Would have loved it maybe because I’m comparing it to how my grandparents would make it at home. For the chicken, I think it’s not as tender and flavourful but it fills me up. The noodle and vegetable is tasty but there’s lack of ingredient.

However, if you’re not a local and you want to get a taste of Chinese local cuisine… this is not bad already.
評論2: [Scott Pothier] (4分, 6 months ago): We stopped here because it was awarded a Michelin Bib Gourmand. The pork ribs themselves were tender. Broth was fairly plain, but still good! Service and atmosphere here were excellent! Things are efficient! Overall, a pleasant experience and solid choice in the Shilin Market!
評論3:
[Culinary Traveler] (4分, 5 months ago): Good flavor, the ladies from the staff were friendly.

While leaving the male staff made a sexist joke, that maybe he assumed I wouldn’t understand and only my companion would. This might be considered local humor but both of us found it disrespectful and unnecessary.
評論4: [Oanh Truong Buu] (5分, a year ago): We ate in a rainy day, ribs were really good but who hate the herbal smell is not recommended. My father said the goat meat noodles was good too. But they use one-time bowls, not very sustainable.
評論5:
[heifoonlei] (4分, a year ago): Generally,  there are many places that sell this soup and I have not try most of them. However,  I believed its taste should be similar if not the same.

The soup is lightly herbal and tastes sweet so it might not appeal to those who like a heavy dose of herbal taste in their soup.

So to those who has sweet tooth do drop in and savour the soup.

Enjoy

餐廳名稱: Creative Pasta 創義麵 士林店
Google Map地址: 111台灣臺北市士林區美德街32號近士林捷運站1號出口
評論1: [Liam Lindemann] (4分, a year ago): Sometimes you have to eat something familiar - pasta. Shilin branch of the pasta chain. There is an English menu, you then order online, but unfortunately the entries are no longer in English, so the detective work begins. The pasta dishes are quite good, considering the circumstances, and reasonably priced. Leftovers can be wrapped up.
評論2: [Scott Chen] (3分, 7 years ago): It is moderate. The service is nice, respecting to customers and solving issues efficiently. The price is reasonable. The meals I ordered was not too salty. I will try other flavors. Cash only.
評論3:
[Andy Lee] (3分, 2 years ago): I used to having the meal in creative pastas ! Unfortunately , the spaghetti which I order today includes the unknown fur !
I guess the restaurant sanitation is really horrible ….

Although they change the new one, I’m afraid that I won’t visit next time ….
評論4: [Steve Ni] (1分, 2 months ago): Don't be fooled. The worst fake lasagna ever. I don't recommend. Don't waste your time and money.
評論5: [Oscar Chang] (3分, 4 years ago): A Taiwanese influenced spaghetti restaurant. Decent price with interesting flavor.

Google Map地址: 111台灣臺北市士林區美德街66號
評論1:
[Lorraine Frederica] (4分, 4 years ago): The portion is more than enough for one people, next time i'm here i will choose to share than order for myself. The pasta itself tastes great, really generous with the sauce and the meat. The ambience of the restaurant is good, good cleanliness as well. The food was served quickly, and a bit crowded during my visit too.

Superb location, near Shilin MRT station.
評論2: [Michael M] (4分, 8 years ago): Decent Spaghetti, not overcooked and great, big portions. I had the vegetable pesto and it was pretty good, especially considering the price, I can't complain. They usually have a lot of space and also serve risotto and potato mash covered with cheese. Usually quite a lot of space and seats. Closing times a bit early.
評論3: [Vláďa Janíček] (5分, 7 months ago): Eat-x close to red line mtr. Good, tasty, big steaks.
評論4: [Lucas Tristan Chang] (5分, 8 years ago): This shop pasta was excellent full flavour n tasty. Cheap n in large size as average pasta at SGD$6.00...die must eat...but seating arrangement not ideal like HK eatery eat n get lost fast
評論5: [Finn Chang] (2分, 8 years ago): The pasta didn't taste good. It served better before, wonder if it has been traded off to another owner.

餐廳名稱: John的料理/士林官邸美食/無提供化妝室
Google Map地址: 111台灣臺北市士林區福林路197號1樓
評論1: [Rune Caboor] (5分, 2 weeks ago): So good!! Paid 115 NTD in total for the rice and the pork bun. Very good value for money!
評論2: [Matt Smith] (5分, 2 years ago): Fantastic little shop serving elevated but unpretentious Taiwanese classics run by a super friendly and generous family. Definitely worth a short walk to this somewhat out-of-the-way location for a delicious lunch or early dinner. We will definitely come back!
評論3: [J Z] (5分, 4 months ago): Quaint, cozy, comfy restaurant with a home style feel and vibes. Traditional Taiwanese dishes made by a chef that's experienced in fine dining. My kind of comfort food.
評論4: [Gregorio Villar] (5分, 11 months ago): I had an incredible experience at this homemade Taiwanese restaurant! The daughter who greeted us was amazingly friendly and spoke excellent English, making us feel right at home. Her warmth and hospitality were truly special. The food, cooked by her dad, was absolutely delicious—every dish was bursting with authentic Taiwanese flavors and made with so much care. You can tell it’s a family labor of love. I can’t recommend this place enough for anyone craving a genuine, heartfelt meal!
評論5:
[Renne] (5分, a year ago): The food was delicious and satisfying, and in addition, the phone service from the female staff was excellent.

I ordered through Uber Eats today. There seemed to be some issues that needed clarification, so the restaurant tried to call me several times. However, due to a problem with my phone, all calls went to voicemail, and I caused some inconvenience to the restaurant. Eventually, I managed to get through to the restaurant by calling them, but as a Japanese person who can only speak a little Chinese, I was worried about communicating. However, there was a female staff member who could speak English, and we were able to communicate smoothly. Despite it being close to closing time and a busy period, she was very polite and helpful. Additionally, there was a note in Japanese saying “Thank you ❤️” on the takeout container, which made me very happy. I wanted to express my gratitude, so I’m leaving this review. I wish you all the best in your future success🙏 Thank you so much 😊

Google Map地址: 111台灣臺北市士林區美崙街93號
評論1: [Linus Wong] (5分, 8 months ago): One of the best dumpling and potsticker places in the Taipei area! Also be sure to order the always changing traditional soup of the day!
評論2: [Yu-Ting] (5分, a year ago): The queue was worth it. The gyoza was good though next time I will skip the side dish. Press the yellow button to get a number from the printer next to the door.
評論3: [Dave Pearce William] (1分, 3 months ago): The potstickers are very oily, and the wait time around 10 minutes or so. Additionally, there’s a restriction requiring a minimum order of 10 pieces for certain items, which makes it less suitable for solo diners. Overall, not recommended if you’re dining alone or in a hurry.
評論4: [Liaw Kok Heng] (5分, 2 years ago): A small shop, not a touristic location and customers are mostly locals but serve delicious food with good service. Ordered fried dumplings, corn soup, soup of the day and refreshing drinks. All were very satisfying. Highly recommended if you want to taste local food.
評論5: [Yen-Chung Chen] (5分, 7 years ago): It is one of the most impressive dumpling restaurant we ever had. The boiled and fried dumplings are different, but both of them are very tasty. The restaurant is always crowded, you may need to wait for a while. Or, you can also buy the frozen dumplings and cook at home, and the taste is still great.

餐廳名稱: 雙月食品社 北投店
Google Map地址: 112台灣臺北市北投區光明路228號
評論1:
[Critsy Y] (4分, 3 months ago): This spot serves up solid Taiwanese home-style comfort food. Very humble setting, nothing fancy, just good cooking. You can order on the machine (super easy), or if that’s not your vibe, the staff will happily take your order. The service is really friendly and everyone was nice. There's a self-help condiments that you get yourself on the side bar and a sink to wash your hands before eating too.

We ordered all the Taiwanese classics between the two of us and had ourselves a pretty satisfying meal. I’m not even that into Taiwanese food (other than that fatty pork over rice… my one true love), but everything was tasty. I’ve been to their other location too, and the quality is consistently good.

It was a rainy evening, no line at all, and we were seated right away. Cozy, comforting, and reliable—exactly what you want on a wet night!
評論2:
[Linda Ann Woo] (4分, 2 weeks ago): Interesting spicy sesame noodles.
Fried tofu not bad.
Garlic chicken soup, chicken dry n badly bruised. Not as nice.
Braised pork rice not bad.
Slice pork sesame oil soup is the tastiest dish.
評論3: [Ada Erinal] (5分, a month ago): I've been there with my mom and sister the other day, and we loved it! The staff is so nice and kind, they explained us how it works everything and even answered our questions regarding dishes even if they were busy. We got the chicken soup and spicy noodles. The noodles were so good we ordered another noodle to try which was the one with the black trufles. Also very good but the spicy one was the best! I would've go there to eat all the time if I was staying near. I recommend it!
評論4:
[YF] (5分, 3 months ago): DELICIOUS! Home-cook style!
I love the steam fish with chilli, bamboo shoots, clam soup (Juicy fat clams with cabbage that makes the soup naturally sweet, YUM!), dry noodle (texture of noodle is good, a little al-dente. We had the truffle style which i am not a big fan off. Taste of truffle is too strong and fake for me)

Restaurant is very clean and they are super efficient. First come first serve basis. Order from the machine and payment is made at machine too. Food was serve very fast! Alcohol is NOT allowed. They sell some type of juice in bottle but they allow you to bring in your own drinks as long as it does not contain alcohol. This is a place where you go for a quick delicious meal and not linger around.
If you are looking for delicious quality home cook food at a great value, visit Moon Moon, you will not regret!
評論5:
[Jay Yu] (5分, 3 weeks ago): In this restaurant, you must get a ticket to make sure you are in the queue first then go to take your orders via the vending machine. Don't go to order without a ticket.
Besides of a bit chaotic above, the dishes were excellent.
I like the love-hate-pepper-seasame noodle (愛恨椒芝麵 is the phrase listed on the menu).
Their soy product was also a spot on (吸汁花干). It is kind of side dish, and the soy product is not like silk tofu but pre-dried and porouse inside. It absorpts lots of broth in it. Like it a lot.
Service and environment are excellent amd relaxing.
Only thing is there are many foreign visitors in this restaurant. In my visit, it's like 80% were foreigners. They may not know how it works in this restaurant, and a bit loud when talk. Please be a bit patience.

Google Map地址: 112台灣臺北市北投區泉源路19號北投晶泉丰旅2樓
評論1:
[Critsy Y] (4分, 3 months ago): Located inside the Regent Taiwan Hotel in Beitou, this spot dishes out some solid hotpot. Their seafood and meats are super fresh, and once you place your order, the salad bar turns into an AYCE situation. Same with the dessert bar—yes, you can build your own ice cream sundae, and yes, it’s as fun as it sounds. You can even mix up your own soda flavors if you’re feeling creative.

Service is very friendly and efficient and the ambiance is pretty nice. Your typical hotel restaurant vibe in Taiwan. Great for big or small groups.

A group of us came here and had a great meal—easy, tasty, and everyone left happy. The hotel also has an onsen, so next time I’m definitely pairing this with a spa day. Hotpot + hot spring? Yes, please.
評論2: [Vivian San Mei Liang] (5分, 4 months ago): Really good value and delicious set dinner with options of sukiyaki or shabu shabu. The salad bar has a great variety with fruits and ice cream. The staff are attentive and friendly.
評論3:
[Kelly Kong] (2分, a month ago): I came in here with high hopes after a day at some nearby hot springs, hoping to end with a delicious Japanese meal after seeing all the glowing reviews but I’ve never been more disappointed by a shabu shabu/hot pot experience in my life. We arrived around 7:30pm and service was incredibly slow, after we ordered it took us about 20 minutes to get all our shabu shabu pots when the restaurant was only 3/4 full. Once we started eating something just tasted like it was missing. I was excited to introduce shabu shabu to family having had it in Hokkaido and it being absolutely delicious but this was just… incredibly bland. I realized that rather than using dashi or any sort of flavored water for the broth, they just used plain unsalted water and no amount of sauce can make up for ingredients that are just blanched in plain water. It’s such a shame too since the salad bar has an amazing selection and the ingredients are high quality and delicious but nothing can shine though if it’s just boiled in plain old water.

Just very very disappointed in this as this has also been the most expensive meal (double the cost of Din Tai Fung) of a 1 week trip so far in Taiwan and also near the bottom of the list of worst meals I’ve had in my life since there is just no salt (and thus no flavor) to be seen anywhere in the shabu shabu pot. Plain old undressed salad is better than this. White rice is better than this. Boiled water is better than this because at least I wouldn’t be sad that I paid so much money for meat to boil in plain water.

Would not recommend coming here if you like flavor. Also very confused by other reviews here so maybe we just had a very off meal and they ran out of dashi since it was kind of close to closing times for them?
評論4: [Jeff Kao] (5分, 9 months ago): Stayed at the hotel for two nights and went to this restaurants for dinners two nights straight. First night we ordered hotpot (beef and seafood). The 2nd night we ordered sukiyaki (beef). We were thoroughly impressed with the quality of the food, service, and the atmosphere. The restaurant was a bit too cold for our liking, but we do understand the need to keep the resistant cool due to the type of food offered. The food sets are reasonably priced. The quantity was a bit too big for dinner, but you get your money's worth. Will definitely return for lunch the next time we are in town.
評論5:
[alna lau] (5分, 3 months ago): I had a wonderful dining experience at Mihan Beitou, located on the 2nd floor of Regent Taipei Beitou (北投晶泉丰旅). The restaurant has a calm, elegant atmosphere that perfectly matches the overall vibe of a hot spring hotel. The environment is clean, quiet, and comfortable, making it a great place to relax and enjoy a nice meal after a long day of traveling.

We ordered the hotpot set, and the quality of the ingredients really impressed us. Both the seafood and meats were very fresh. The beef slices were beautifully marbled and tender, and the vegetables were crisp and clean. After ordering the main set, we also had access to the salad bar and dessert area, which included fresh vegetables, sauces, drinks, and ice cream. Being able to build your own dessert and enjoy unlimited sides made the whole dining experience even more enjoyable.

The service was excellent. The staff were friendly, polite, and attentive. They explained the menu clearly, served our food promptly, and always checked in to make sure everything was fine. Even though the restaurant was inside a hotel, the service felt warm and personal rather than overly formal.

I came here with friends, and everyone left very satisfied and happy. What I really like is that the hotel also has hot spring facilities, so this place would be perfect for a full relaxing experience — soak in the onsen first, then enjoy a hotpot dinner afterward. I would definitely love to come back again next time I visit Beitou.

Recommended for couples, friends, and families. Indoor dining with comfortable seating.

Google Map地址: 112台灣臺北市北投區永興路一段47號
評論1:
[Lukas Dxb] (5分, 4 months ago): Amazing place with exceptional food. Service is just as you wish it would be!

Thank you team for making my dinner memorable with "exotic" outstanding food!

Must visit !
評論2: [Foo Seck Guan] (5分, a year ago): The Macha honey beer is special and good if one prefer sweet notes of the beer. We loved the chicken wings and chicken backside - yeah absolutely right. BBQ to a perfect crisp on the surface and tender inside. We also ordered the grilled vegetable which was an icing on the cake. Definitely recommend to anyone who wants to try.
評論3: [Ansabel Lim] (5分, 10 months ago): I ordered the salmon fried rice, mentaiko yam, grilled squid, yuzu pepper pork, matcha honey beer and guava juice. Loved them all! :) Food was served quickly too! I think it gets crowded, it's better to reserve!
評論4: [Daniel Ha] (5分, a year ago): Amazing experience! I was skeptical of the number of great reviews here but it did not disappoint! Macha honey beer is a must try. Other than that all their red marked recommended menu seems great. My wife and I tried so many great food in Taipei but did not expect to put this Japanese restaurant in our top 5!
評論5: [지윤차] (5分, a year ago): Very nice and cozy place, food was great. Ordered the miso eggplant, salmon fried rice, salmon rice balls and Hokkaido scallops. All food was 10/10. Would recommend the Matcha Honey Beer as well, smooth with green tea aftertaste, unique and delicious.

餐廳名稱: 長生塩人 北投車站
Google Map地址: 112009台灣臺北市北投區育仁路8巷3號
評論1: [A Leung (Andy)] (5分, 5 months ago): Great service and delicious noodle. Japanese fried noodle is highly recommended.
評論2:
[Jamie Chua] (4分, 7 months ago): We ordered the salt and soy sauce ramen. I like this kind of ramen, very simple, clean and refreshing taste, perfect for summer. This is not for people who want their ramen rich and flavorful. I wish they had the option to choose the noodle doneness, and they have thicker cut noodles. But either way, I will come back for this.
PS the seats are limited, so the waiting time will be longer. Limited options for side dish (only egg, extra meat and kimchi).
評論3:
[Edderss] (5分, a year ago): Best damn ramen I’ve had all year.
Soup’s been sitting in a bucket for 8 hours, straight outta beautiful bones.
Noodles so bouncy the NBA might as well just use them.
Service so lovely I’m inclined to just move in.
Man, can I just not leave??????
評論4:
[Kamy Yeung] (5分, 8 months ago): A small ramen shop with high quality food! We ordered salty ramen with good pork and chicken plus super delicious soup. Side dishes were so tasty as the starters. 😋

The shop is clean and tidy with simple decor.
We will definitely want to come again.

Staff is helpful to introduce the ramen to new comers.

We came at 7:30pm and only waited for about 30 minutes.  We are managed to sit near the shop and also found an almond drink next store to drink while waiting for the seats.
評論5:
[Chun-Chih Hu] (4分, 3 years ago): I got the Spicy Miso Ramen with double meat, their special ramen noodles, ajitama and extra helping of the noodles. Good flavor but under-seasoned aka not enough salt. They do provide salt, black pepper and shichimi togarashi on the table to adjust the seasoning of your meal to your liking. It is not spicy at all to my palette, the black pepper I added has more kick to it than the soup base itself.  Even with double meat, it was a rather paltry amount of meat added.  The chef can adjust the hardness of the noodles to your preference.  With all the extras added to make a decent meal, I wouldn't really call this cheap.  I ended up paying $295NTD for my meal and I could've gotten another helping of noodles to really top off as I walked out just satisfied but not full.

TL;DR -A good bowl of ramen that's catered to the Taiwanese palette but not cheap if you get all the fixings to fill up.  It is still worth a visit if you're right in the neighborhood.

Google Map地址: 112台灣臺北市北投區泉源路12號2樓之1
評論1:
[Brandon LaRouche] (4分, a year ago): Food was tasty. We ordered the eel with rice and crispy pork. They have english menus to look at and then you order using a QR code they place at your table, on receipt paper, when you sit down. You pay at the end.

Service was fast. Bathrooms were clean.
評論2: [Andrew Chwee] (5分, 3 years ago): An excellent meal at this restaurant. They didn't hurry us even when we were almost the last few customers in the restaurant. The cod fish soup was terrific. I love my Pork Tonkatsu - it may not be the best, but it's nice and crispy (almost without the porky smell. The fish set was slightly disappointing; although it was big, this fish tasted off. The green tea latte was quite delightful. The service is excellent and helpful, so if you are with kids, this may be the best spot to have a family meal.
評論3: [Janeki-neko] (5分, 4 months ago): Good food and fast service. The online ordering menu seems to only be in Chinese. It’s quite busy at meal times.
評論4: [Bob Cook] (4分, 4 years ago): A second floor Japanese restaurant that you enter through a slightly sketchy looking set of stairs. This branch of Ootoya is medium sized with nice Japanese food and views over the busy intersection. Service was mixed - mostly very attentive, but it would have been good to be told that the two of the top three dishes on the menu weren’t available. You order using the screen and extra rice etc can be requested using the screen throughout your meal. Once we got the food it was generally nice, good clean flavours, generous portions, crispy fried pork. The noodles better very chewy, my wife tried them and thought that they were undercooked. I’m not sure but they were certainly on the borderline. Overall a nice experience, with some reservations.
評論5: [Toh Bee Leng] (4分, a year ago): Opposite the Xinbeitou MRT Station. Hot and delicious food. Peaceful and comfortable atmosphere.

Google Map地址: 112台灣臺北市北投區中和街2號1F
評論1: [Grace L] (5分, 2 years ago): we ordered their set dinner.  the service was top 5 star hotel services. they explained their food well, and was attentive to customer's needs.  though it was a bit strange that this restaurant is located on ground floor which lacked a bit of privacy.  the food was nice, but only a few dishes were memorable.  can pass on the "free" french dessert.  the dessert that was included in all sets was alright. i wished they made their own ice cream.  their main course was their highlight!
評論2:
[Xiao Mi Tee] (5分, 11 months ago): I came here for dinner alone. The decor is lovely. I had the lamb chops, which included several other courses.

The very attentive and courteous wait staff change the utensils every course, but, being OCD, I didn't want to give up the ones I had already disinfected.

Everything was interesting and yummy. I wanted the lamb chops medium-well (do not judge! Remember I have OCD), but I think the chef decided medium was a better way to enjoy the meat - still yummy.

It wasn't cheap, but it was worth the price.
評論3: [Don Dizzlington] (2分, a year ago): More attention paid to the look of food than the taste, sadly, as I was looking forward to this meal. Large wait between the pre-prepared entrees and pre-prepared main, which was cold when served in an empty restaurant. Fish and beef are unseasoned to my western palate and very very small portions, nothing I would return for. Finally the mushroom soup tasted canned or made from porcini powder. Would avoid, at least for lunch.
評論4: [AT] (3分, 2 years ago): Food was good and passable. Staff was amazingly nice and helpful.
評論5: [Zichi 靜月梓（Archie）] (5分, 2 years ago): Highly recommended for breakfast and hot springs

餐廳名稱: 少帥禪園-台北北投張學良幽禁舊居
Google Map地址: 112台灣臺北市北投區幽雅路34號
評論1:
[Lorna Tan] (5分, a month ago): Love the ambience, architecture n the cute landscape figurines! Omakase lunch was delicious especially the herbal duck soup.
Consider upgrading the service staff uniform to be more aligned with the premium pricing n elegant atmosphere.
Can improve on the range of toiletries in the spa rooms eg hair conditioner, facial cleanser, etc. Will recommend to friends!
評論2:
[Elizabeth Shieh] (5分, 3 months ago): For an afternoon tea set, you also get a free ticket for footbath.
The minimum price per person is 500 NTD. For 6 person, a two two-person Afternoon tea set are enough.
Afternoon tea set is very thoughtful and delicious 😋
The water for the footbath is quite hot, the sunset is gorgeous 😍
評論3:
[Reynald Ong] (5分, 6 months ago): Marshall Zen Garden is a historic site that now houses a tea house, a hot spring spa, and a restaurant. 評論3:
[Vanessa Chia] (5分, 6 years ago): During our stay at The Gaia Hotel, we dropped by this restaurant that serves Sichuan and Hunan cuisine.

For starters, we were recommended a salad that was very unique. Once the Areca Flower Salad with Sesame Sauce (NT$380/ S$17.30) is served, the server will the toss the salad and portion it for you.

I was a huge fan of the Sautéed Green Beans with Minced Pork (NT$360/ S$16.40) here. It came with thick burrito skins for you to wrap the vegetable and pork in.

The next most impressive dish was the Chef Short Rib Specialty (NT$1780/ S$80.90). This huge rib would be served to you table side, which the chef hand wraps every day with straw (dao cao). You could smell the straw and when the server cut it open, steam would rise from the rib. We saw him effortlessly separate the beef from the bone, then chopping it and plating it with a side of the sauce it was marinated it or salt.

I can't not have desserts at the end of my meal but instead of having the usual cold type of dessert, we chose to have the Deep Fried Handmade Steamed Roll (NT$250/ S$11.40). It is basically a bigger and longer version of mantou. They also sprinkled fragrant shaved peanuts on top, and served it with a side of sweet condensed milk. So comforting!

評論5:
[雙胞胎] (5分, a month ago): The "Quanyuan Pavilion" restaurant in Beitou exudes a tranquil, Japanese elegance. To avoid the Lunar New Year travel rush, we had our meal there beforehand. The spacious underground parking lot evoked a sense of quiet solitude. The newly renovated space resembled a meticulously bound new book, every decoration radiating a perfectly restrained elegance.

Upon being seated, the waiters, with their warm hospitality, guided us into the six-course Peking duck feast. The tableware was exquisite and substantial, with two sets of chopsticks—one for the family and one for the guests. It was a thoughtful touch; in this space, everyone could gracefully maintain their distance from the food.

At one end of the table, my brother was playing with my child, while his little daughter, like a lost kitten, nestled close to me. Amidst the soft chatter and laughter of family conversation, the dishes were served one by one. The cold appetizers were served with a Japanese restraint, not for quantity, but for their exceptional freshness and rich flavor.

Then, the star of the show arrived: a golden-brown roasted duck, expertly dissected by the chef with the effortless skill of a master butcher. The moment I tasted the first bite of the roasted duck nigiri sushi, I felt an absolute conviction; any adjective seemed superfluous in the face of such balance. When the stir-fried baby rolls with scallions and ginger arrived, the server specifically urged me to eat them hot. The wildness of the sea intertwined with their tenderness, complemented by the unique sauce—perfect beyond reproach. My son boldly told his mother, "It's even better than Mom's!" At that moment, a subtle, almost dangerous humor hung in the air.

The meal concluded with a bucket of handmade tofu pudding—brown sugar, ginger juice, tapioca pearls, and peanuts. In its warm sweetness, our taste buds achieved a harmonious reunion. After this meal, we would drive to Yangmingshan to pay respects to our ancestors. Between the elegance of the Quanyuan Pavilion and the tranquility of the forefathers, I felt a sense of continuity in time. It was a perfect, undeniable day.

評論1: [Joyce Lim] (5分, 10 months ago): Best meal of our trip in Taiwan so far. Every course was tantalising, ingredients were high quality, taste was sublime. Highlights were the first dish (black beans were incredibly sweet), third dish (interesting), fourth dish (prawn so fresh, pork so tender), sixth dish (finished last drop of the soup). Worth the price tag of ntd1430 after tax. Complimentary entry to the adjoined Beitou Museum if you have a reservation for a meal at Yiran house. If I had to suggest improvements, it would be to add some crabmeat to the chawanmushi (couldn't find any), and source for better quality fruits (watermelon was bland). Regardless, was a fantastic meal. Highly recommend.
評論2: [Pauline] (5分, a month ago): Lovely dining space in a well maintained Japanese house. The course meal was tasty and well priced at 1,300 NTD plus 10% service charge. An interesting exhibition on beads through the ages and across the world was on at the museum's gallery. The museum gift shop stocked an interesting array of well crafted items. There's no entrance fee but visitors have to remove their shoes & put on the socks provided prior to entering the museum.
評論3: [Sihan Goi] (5分, 10 months ago): Had lunch with my wife after visiting the Beitou museum. No regrets. Pretty much every dish was very well executed, in terms of taste, portion, and presentation. Especially loved the prawn which was very fresh and tasty. Service was prompt and professional, ambience was bright and airy. Would try this again if I come again.
評論4:
[Megan Lim] (5分, a year ago): Genuinely delicious meal in a Japanese style restaurant. The dishes were delicate and extremely fresh, served in multiple courses.

We did a walk in for lunch and were very lucky to be able to secure a spot. Price wise it can seem pricy for 1100 Twd per person, but it's worth every penny!
評論5:
[benny hu] (5分, 3 months ago): Nestled on a tranquil mountainside in Beitou, this Japanese-style building occupies approximately 800 pings (about 660 square meters) in a serene and elegant environment. Formerly the Jiashan Hot Spring Inn built in 1921, it was transformed into a private museum in 1984, carrying the memories of Beitou's hot spring culture.

Designated a municipal historic site by the Taipei City Government in 1998, showcasing its unique historical value, the building underwent a complete renovation in 2008. While preserving its original wooden structure and Japanese charm, it was infused with new vitality.

Today, the museum combines exquisite kaiseki cuisine (by reservation only) with diverse art exhibitions and cultural activities, allowing visitors to experience the charm of history, art, and gastronomy interwoven in a space where nature and culture intertwine.

Their signature Crispy Spare Ribs is a must-try.  The taro "fries" were in fact pieces of taro nuggets.  The Sugar Braised Pork Liver was something new to me in terms of flavour and texture.  The pumpkin rice vermicelli had minced pork in it but you can order a vegetarian version.

They had big round tables for 8-12 people only so they appeared to be reluctant to serve parties of two.  Adding the fact that dishes come in big portions bigger groups say six people may have better dining experience.

Cash only.

評論4: [C Neon] (4分, 2 years ago): This is one of the original Taiwanese style restaurants in Beitou.  It's not a fancy place, but the people there are friendly and professional. I got the banana flavored ice, which is an homage to a past in Taiwan where they had to use artificial banana flavoring to sweeten ice because sugar was so expensive

評論5: [Esther Khoo] (5分, a year ago): the food was all really good and the people who worked there are all really nice and kind. there's a game to play to if you order the tofu which in my opinion is a must order when visiting. without expecting the most authentic Japanese ramen, the ramen is really good with a good variety of seafood and rich broth. the egg pairs with the ramen really well. the side were all really good as well and I highly recommend getting the mochi for dessert!!

評論1:
[Genta] (5分, a year ago): Dining at Piacere, the main Italian restaurant at The Gaia Hotel, was a delightful experience.

The ambiance is beautifully complemented by stunning garden views and a lovely vista of the hotel's lobby library, making it a perfect setting for both breakfast and dinner.

The menu features a wonderful selection of Italian cuisine, showcasing fresh ingredients and expertly crafted dishes. Every meal felt like a celebration of flavors, making it a highlight of my stay.

Whether you're starting your day with a hearty breakfast or enjoying a refined dinner, Piacere provides an inviting atmosphere that enhances the overall experience at The Gaia Hotel.

The combination of great food, beautiful surroundings, and attentive service truly sets Piacere apart. Highly recommended for anyone visiting Beitou!
評論2:
[KaiYin Yang] (5分, 11 months ago): The most delicious burger I’ve ever had in here ,
Service is top notch, highly recommended
評論3: [Weizhong Pan] (5分, 2 years ago): Definitely a hidden gem. I did not expect this level of Italian in Beitou. The breads are outstanding. Excellent risotto as well. You can taste/feel the Chef’s high standard from salad all the way to desert!
評論4: [Hsiao-Wen Tsao] (5分, 10 months ago): Tracy is super helpful and friendly. All the other staff are also cheerful.
評論5:
[賴融（Ron）] (4分, a month ago): Afternoon tea is served from 2:30 PM to 5:00 PM.

Today's reservation was for a two-person afternoon tea set. The menu is fixed; you only need to choose your drinks.

The menu is roughly as follows, along with some unprofessional comments:

Kaya Toast: Tastes a bit like a pineapple bun.

Coconut Snowflake Cake: A layer of white chocolate paired with blueberries; the overall flavor is balanced and not too sweet.

Crispy Balls with Rojak Salad: The salad itself is slightly spicy and contains tamarind; avoid if you don't like that.

Nyonya Shrimp Chili Glutinous Rice Cake: Very fragrant, a bit spicier than the salad, but compared to the salad, it's probably close to mildly spicy; surprisingly delicious.

Indonesian Beef Noodle Soup: Unlike Hakka Beef Noodle Soup, it's not sweet. It has a fragrance, but apart from the glutinous rice, the other flavors are somewhat lacking.

Sunny Macarons: The macarons were too sweet for me, but the mango filling wasn't too sweet, which balanced the flavor somewhat.

Pineapple Golden Sand Dacquoise: Not sweet, the filling is similar to pineapple cake.

Purple Taro Dance: Looks a bit like chocolate, but it's actually purple taro. It's not very sweet and the texture is a bit like a brownie.

Nanyang Pandan Pig: Definitely Nanyang style, but a bit overcooked; some parts were a little dry.

P.S. We happened to encounter a photography crew while dining. We all felt that if they were going to photograph passersby, they should have informed them first, rather than using them as a background. Not everyone wants to be on TV… Otherwise, for an afternoon tea experience, the atmosphere would have been 5 stars.

評論1:
[Rio Hikaru] (1分, 3 months ago): This is the worst ramen shop I have visited in Taiwan. Please don’t tell me the owner is from Japan—this quality would only embarrass us Japanese people! The food here is absolutely not worth the price, especially for tourists unfamiliar with the area. I am one of those tourists, and it’s obvious this place is taking advantage of visitors.
The ramen has no flavor at all, the soup is extremely bland, and the portion size is shockingly small. The side dishes are overpriced—for example, two small oysters cost NT$150, while other places offer at least three for the same price. The so-called “onsen tamago” doesn’t resemble a proper Japanese-style egg at all; it’s basically a regular hard-boiled egg with a slightly soft yolk, yet they charge NT$40 for one. I really cannot understand what is special about it.

On top of that, the restaurant charges an extra 10% service fee despite the high prices. Customers should also be careful, as this place even overcharges by one or two dollars. It may seem small, but when they do this to every customer, it becomes an unreasonable profit.

Most diners I saw were also tourists like us, and after this experience, I cannot recommend this restaurant to anyone.

餐廳名稱: 雍翠庭 Chinese Cuisine - 北投麗禧溫泉酒店 Grand View Resort Beitou
Google Map地址: 112台灣臺北市北投區幽雅路30號
評論1:
[Michelle] (5分, 3 months ago): When you taste it, you know how they got the michelin. Very filling as well.
I preferred the Taiwanese one over Western breakfast.
10/10
評論2: [Nathan McCain] (5分, a year ago): We sprung for the “exquisite” set menu at this notable spot and were very pleased with the results. After some cute amuse-style appetizers (one of which was referenced later in the meal), the meal slams on the accelerator for the next three dishes. The Buddha Jumping Over The Wall is nuanced and rich, the lobster ball is sweet and spicy, and the Wagyu beef cheek is peerless, tender enough for a butter knife with thick veins of connective tissue that are a true delight on the palate. This is the set’s high point, not that the successive dishes aren’t great as well. A fun and delicious offering that is worth the expense.
評論3:
[Andrew Ding] (5分, 2 years ago): We dined here on New Year’s Day 2024 as we were staying in the Grand View Hotel and didn’t want to travel far for dinner. So glad we did.

The dress code of the restaurant is relaxed, but the setting is quite refined, so we made an effort. We did notice that you’re more than welcome to dine wearing the resort lounge wear provided upon checking in to your room.

As we were just a party of three, we ordered only a modest amount of dishes. It was a shame because I would have loved to go full Chinese banquet style here.

We each ordered soups to start:
- Signature Sauna Beef Ribs Drizzled with Beef Bone Soup (U.S. Beef)
- Buddha Jumping Over the Wall with Fish Maw (Taiwan Pork)
- Double-Boiled Chicken Soup with Japanese Yam and Job's Tear

All were masterfully executed, especially the Buddha Jumping Over the Wall, which is an extremely labor-intensive dish to pull off. It was remarkably well balanced with deep, complex nuanced flavors. The same goes for the Double-Boiled Chicken Soup; the English translations of these names rarely do justice to the dish. It was also deep and richly flavored, very soothing.

For mains, we ordered:
- Deep-Fried Gentian Grouper with Salted Egg Yolk Sauce
- Crispy Pork Ribs with Garlic and Red Rice Yeast (Taiwan Pork)

These were both incredible, light and crispy, and perfectly seasoned. I was a little apprehensive about ordering two fried meats, thinking it might be too heavy, but that was not the case at all. The crust on each was delicate and delicious.

For dessert, the menu only had one option: Sweet Potato and Taro Ball with Ginger Soup - highly recommended as a great way to cleanse your palate at the end of the meal.

All in all, a refined and masterful meal.
評論4:
[Kara Mok] (5分, 2 years ago): Came here after our soak in the private Onsen, and the food is so good. The staff are knowledgeable about the food and will advise on what to order. They also try their best on special requests.

[Ken Ip] (4分, 9 months ago): This small stir-fry restaurant is located in the old established Huaxi Street Night Market.

I think the lady boss was the cook herself and there was another lady who handled the orders and cash.

Ordered a clam sauteed with sponge gourd丝瓜 (this is very delicious!) and shrimp omelette - All the ingredients were very fresh. The place was "mildly" air-conditioned. They also serve beer & hot pots. I enjoyed the meal very much and recommended.

評論1:
[Tin B.B.] (2分, 2 months ago): For a pricey NT$4,000 omakase, the meal was a total letdown with dishes served in utter disarray: cold starters, then hot appetisers, followed by sashimi and then steamed egg. The jarring shifts in temperature and flavors felt chaotic and off-putting, nowhere near the seamless experience a proper omakase should deliver.

The atmosphere was downright stressful, the boss lady yelling, interrupting diners to chat, and barking at her staff right in front of everyone. She even scolded us for “eating and drinking too slowly,” making the whole evening tense and uncomfortable from start to finish.

To top it off, I ended up with gastroenteritis the next day. I wouldn’t recommend this restaurant.
評論2: [Cina and Angelica Shahabi] (5分, a year ago): Amazing tasting and aesthetically pleasing Japanese inspired dishes! The service was great and the owner is very funny and accommodating. I’ll definitely be coming again 🙏
評論3: [H &H] (5分, 3 months ago): One of the best restaurants in Taipei. Great food, great service, great atmosphere.  Add it to your list of must visits….
評論4:
[C. H. N.] (3分, 3 years ago): Interior with japanese vibe with interesting boss and dishes. Tummy well fed with fresh ingredients.

Edit:
Not sure if it's due to other food I ate (can't think of any due to lack of appetite after the lunch), faced massive diarrhoea the next morning at 5+am.
Distasteful experience after posting the earlier review due to the change in final price charged to us, despite having a reservation made with the set budget. Though the boss told us that the price will be higher due to a recent spike in ingredient cost, it was mentioned only during the midst of the meal.
評論5: [bella W. Singleterry] (5分, 3 months ago): Wow what a wonderful place.  Best hospitality and food. Highly recommended

Google Map地址: 108台灣臺北市萬華區洛陽街67號
評論1:
[Mr Wong] (4分, 3 months ago): Arrived around 7:00PM and got in right away. We all ordered individual beef noodle soup and dumplings to share. I ordered a large and i couldn't finish all the noodles. The noodles had a nice al dente chewiness to them. The beef was nice and tender. I've had richer more complex broths but it was still good.

The plump dumplings were freshly boiled and arrived hot.

The noodles and dumplings are a great  value.
評論2: [Ruby P] (5分, a month ago): This beef noodle shop offers great value. The beef is served in large, tender chunks that are easy to eat, and the noodles have a nice chewy texture. The broth is flavorful and aromatic without being overpowering. A big plus is the efficient seating system—there’s usually no queue, and the food comes out fast. It’s a casual local spot with quick service. They also provide chili butter and pickled greens for self-service. Great choice for a quick, tasty, and affordable meal.
評論3: [G Whizz] (5分, 3 months ago): Fuhong Beef Noodle is the definition of a true local gem in Taipei. The place is small, very run-down and clearly not designed for tourists — everyone inside was local, which is usually the best sign. I had their Taiwanese beef noodle soup and it was outstanding: deep, rich broth, perfectly cooked noodles and incredibly flavorful beef. I was told this place was something special, and that turned out to be absolutely true. No frills, no pretension — just seriously good food.
評論4:
[Ron Yang] (5分, 2 months ago): Well cooked beef noodles. The beef pieces were lean and tender - very juicy and flavourful. The noodles were cooked just right. The soup stock was also great. This combination made me give this shop 5 stars.

They also sell century eggs and tofu on the side. It complements the beef noodles. You can ask for more soup stock.

The toilet is behind the shop and is clean.

Highly recommended. Thumbs up! I will be back for more!
評論5: [Kitsada Saleumxay] (4分, 4 months ago): Fuhong Beef Noodles — I really love their thick flat noodles, perfectly chewy and satisfying! The beef is super tender, flavorful, and melts in your mouth. The soup is rich and comforting, great balance of taste without being too oily. Portion is generous and worth the price. Busy during meal times but service is quick. A must-try spot for beef noodle lovers in Taipei!
