評論1:
[An-Nung Chen] (5分, 3 months ago): Great beef noodles in this lovely corner. The owner wants to replicate her fathers’ taste and this store is the result. The soup was cooked with beef bones for 9 hours; it’s favorable and surprisingly the soup gets even tastier when it’s less hot.

The side dishes are tasty and delicate. Another must try is the shrimp dumplings with sesame flavored hand-made sauce. The owner is hospitable. The place is warm and welcoming. It’s one of a kind dining experience in WanHua. Highly recommended.
評論2: [Andrew Mcilroy] (5分, 3 months ago): An excellent beef noodle - tender beef and superb soup, not thickened, just an edge of vinegar and a deep umami base…. The owner makes it all based on her dad’s recipe…. And she likes to chat! Great value and also very elegant, with a Japanese vibe - my top noodle shop!
評論3:
[Tom Tuan Nguyen] (5分, 2 months ago): Small restaurant, maybe it's hard to find but it's worth if you visit the temple and walk 10min over.

You have to order at the vendor outside, and bring the receipt inside.

The owner really nice, the beef noodle soup is excellent, it's the soy base soup and very well balanced.

Recommend.
評論4:
[Jacob C] (5分, 3 months ago): Delicious food and a lovely owner.
The beef noodle soup is exquisite, with beef that is incredibly tender and melts in your mouth. The broth is rich, deep, and full of flavour.

The owner is exceptionally friendly and welcoming. During my short trip to Taipei, I visited this restaurant twice, and on both occasions I was greeted with a warm smile and even offered complimentary items to accompany my meal.

I highly recommend this restaurant to anyone visiting Taipei.
評論5: [Carolyn Weng] (5分, 4 months ago): The beef noodle soup here is excellent! The beef is incredibly tender, and the broth is light yet full of flavor — not oily or heavy at all. I really like that after finishing the whole bowl, you don’t feel greasy or thirsty like with some other places. Definitely one of the best, most balanced beef noodle soups I’ve had!

Google Map地址: 108台灣臺北市萬華區漢中街32巷1號
評論1:
[Sandy Y. (CameraGourmet)] (5分, a month ago): We stopped by 天天利美食坊 in the heart of Ximending, and it was already buzzing before opening time.  We noticed that they have over 8K reviews, which definitely raised our expectations, and judging by the early lineup, we were not the only ones curious.

We arrived before they opened, but there were already people patiently waiting outside.  That alone tells you how popular this spot is, especially with visitors exploring the area.

We ordered their signature dishes, including the classic 滷肉飯 lu rou fan, 蘿蔔糕 radish cake, and 蚵仔煎 oyster omelette.  Everything came out hot and freshly cooked, which we really appreciated.  Overall, the food was good and satisfying, though the crowd leaned more toward tourists than local Taiwanese diners.

That said, the flavours were solid and comforting, especially when everything is made to order.  You can tell the kitchen moves fast.

If you are exploring Ximending and want to check off a well-known spot, 天天利美食坊 is the place for you.

Food:  🍚🍚🍚🍚🍚 Must try.
Service: 👩‍🍳👩‍🍳👩‍🍳👩‍🍳
Kids: 👶👶👶👶
Presentation: 📷
Transit: 🚌🚌🚌🚌
Tips:
📝 Place and pay for your order first before you get assigned a seat.
📝 For take-out, ask the server directly since no line-up is needed.
📝 Every dish is cooked to order, so expect piping hot food.
📝 The space is small, and the wait for a seat can be long, so go with patience.
Would we return: Yes 👱‍♀️
評論2:
[chu kok wee] (4分, 2 months ago): A great spot for quick and authentic Taiwanese street food right in the heart of busy Ximending. Their signature Lu Rou Fan with egg, fried oyster omelette（蚵仔煎）, and other classic dishes are well worth trying.

Despite the long queues, service is impressively fast and efficient. Prices are reasonable, portions are satisfying, and the flavors feel honest and comforting. However, the ambiance are a bit small and packed. Overall, a place one can visit if you’re in the area and want a true local food experience.
評論3:
[Jolie Soesanto] (5分, 2 months ago): It's good, I eat at around 5pm and not really take a long time to go inside! There's so much option of lu rou fan, my friend said this was the best and her fav lu rou fan, but me personally there's a place even more taste delicious. Fishbowl also okeyish but not really the best fishball I ever eat.But good to try it!
The fishball is 40nt if I'm not wrong and luroufan is 70nt
評論4:
[Acsahh C] (5分, 3 months ago): I finally visited 天天利美食坊 in Ximending and it absolutely lived up to the hype. The food was delicious, comforting, and full of authentic Taiwanese flavors. I ordered their signature dishes including the radish cake, 滷肉飯, 荷包蛋, and a few soups. Everything was freshly made, perfectly seasoned, and served piping hot.

Despite the crowd, the service was fast and efficient. The staff handled the long queue very well and the turnover was smooth. The atmosphere felt lively and local, exactly what you would expect from a beloved Taiwanese eatery.

This is definitely a must-visit spot in Ximending if you want tasty, affordable, no-frills Taiwanese comfort food. I would happily come back again for more.
評論5: [Bon Cam TV] (5分, 4 months ago): We tried the braised pork rice, oyster omelette, and squid soup, and more — and everything was superb! The flavors truly stand out and taste different in the best way possible. Each dish was rich, well-seasoned, and definitely not bland. It’s unlike any other store we’ve been to — the taste is simply exceptional! Highly recommended! Approved!

評論3: [E C] (4分, 7 years ago): There are a wide variety of food here, such as sashimi, seafood plates, deep fried seafood and veggies, sushi, dim sum, salad, steak, etc. Diners are able to cook their own noodles and vegetables, which is quite interesting. Perhaps, it is still new to public, there were not many people for dinners, probably less than half full of the restaurant. However, the price is reasonable though.
評論4: [He Andy] (4分, 2 years ago): The vibe is good. The beef soup you do it by yourself. The beef is so fresh. The sashimi is not fresh enough. There is a beer machine. You can use beer to drink and that is great. The caramel pineapple is very special. You can try it. I think that buffet is good.
評論5: [Chaichana Tosuwancharoen] (5分, a year ago): Excellent buffet and tasty foods. I love the fresh seafoods. Plus you can also order the pastries from the Starbucks in front of .

評論1: [Jon H] (4分, 5 months ago): Recommended this restaurant by my dad as this place has been around since 1946. The sashimi and sushi here are very generous and and the prices are super affordable. Personally, the taste of the cuisine is different from what you'd get in Japan and more of a Taiwanese spin with some sweeter notes. I love the interior as it does have nostalgic vibes and the servers are nice as well.
評論2: [Eric Yu] (5分, 4 months ago): During our visit in Taiwan. We visit them twice in our two weeks holiday. We tried their Sashimi rice bowl, their oyster dish. The sashimi was very fresh and portions were good. The cooked oyster dish was delicious and fresh as well. Very reasonable price and free miso soup with every order. Highly recommended.
評論3:
[C Arroyo] (4分, 3 months ago): I don’t like to give a negative review for a place I do love to eat at. Let it be said I am fair in revisiting and seeing changes to restaurants over time. I have eaten here now four times over the last two years. The restaurant is busy attesting to their popularity in the XiMen neighborhood very near the metro. The food is tasty, and for the most part affordable, while they serve many more expensive dishes. I have found however that the sashimi I ordered, costing $NT700 ( my second time ever having it): the Toro Blue Fin Tuna Sashimi 7 piece includes thick pieces of sashimi, but two of the assortment is the fatty belly most desired sashimi and accounts for the price. It was however truly ONE PIECE SLICED TO HALF THE THICKNESS to provide you the two noted pieces of the seven in the assortment. Perhaps they are looking to higher profits or reduction of costs. It is notable that I was likewise served half a leaf is shiso with my sashimi— a dish costing over $US20 served with one leaf sliced in half and hidden under my half-cut Toro Maguro sashimi. The parallel presentation evoked a need to provide this FULL description. All other elements of my meal and experience was good and comfortable. I also had the baby abalone (under stir fry menu), conger eel nigiri was a generous whole butterfly cut of eel seven inches long! The uni nigiri sea urchin is fresh and comes with a chawan mushi steamed egg cup with clam and shrimp in it. My recommendation of the snowflake lily greens does not disappoint at $NT170 either— if they could just serve it WITH my meal vs. at the end when I literally was done with my meal and had to ask about it!
For value and variety their menu is incredible— but be warned you may be getting half-measures here. I recommend you eat here and just be aware.

In total I spent $US65 for one! I enjoy the food, but I cannot see a good place go bad… I hope this review encourages management to address changes that are noticeably reducing esteem.
評論4: [Santi Lee] (5分, a month ago): Amazing food, Otoro so good, super fast, and 10/10 service! The staff went above and beyond to help us out even with the Chinese language gap. Great experience overall
評論5:
[Nel GTS] (5分, 6 months ago): Very traditional Japanese restaurant since 1946.
The portions here are huge and affordable.
Big eaters will definitely be satisfied with the portions here.
Very fresh sashimi and sushi.
Well cooked dishes.
Lots of japanese food to choose from the menu.
Loved the miso soup here.

Google Map地址: 108台灣臺北市萬華區華西街17-2號
評論1:
[Mano Recto] (5分, a month ago): An outstanding example of Taiwanese street food done right. The gua bao here is beautifully balanced — soft, fluffy buns paired with richly braised pork that is flavorful without being greasy. The acidity and freshness from the garnishes lift the dish perfectly.

What makes this place special is restraint. Nothing is overworked, nothing is exaggerated. It’s confident cooking rooted in tradition. Simple, direct, and incredibly satisfying. Easily one of the best gua bao experiences I’ve had, and absolutely worth a return visit.
評論2: [John Zuluaga] (4分, 2 weeks ago): Pretty nice gua bao recommended by the Michelin guide. There seems to always be a queue, so be prepared to wait. The pork was very tender and juicy, but in my opinion, it a was little bit too sweet. Nice flavors overall and decent price!
評論3: [Hogi B.] (4分, 3 months ago): Michelin awarded pork buns. The pork is so tender, the Bao has a generous filling and a fair price. The taste is very much on the sweet side, I personally would have preferred a bit more balance, just a touch more salt and also acidity from the pickles. Sadly there is no way to adjust the taste, because the only condiment available is chili. Still an excellent quality Bao for a fair price - especially if you like it sweet.
評論4: [Felicia Png] (5分, a month ago): 10/10 the best Gua Bao ever. Well balanced flavours of the pork belly in a warm fluffy bun. This place has set the bar way too high for a Gua Bao. Drooling while typing this.. it is that good.
評論5: [WanderWithSpoon] (5分, 4 months ago): Yuan Fang Gua Bao is delicious and surprisingly affordable. The pork is tender, juicy, and perfectly seasoned, paired with soft steamed buns that soak up all the flavor. It’s that perfect street-food bite — simple but satisfying. Definitely worth a stop if you’re craving authentic Taiwanese gua bao without breaking the wallet.

餐廳名稱: 潮麵堂 花椒牛肉麵
Google Map地址: 108台灣臺北市萬華區梧州街6號1樓
評論1: [Angiee Ng] (5分, 5 months ago): Highly recommended!! Good service and food👍
評論2: [S L] (5分, 9 months ago): Absolutely delicious beef noodles. A little hint of szechuan peppercorns in the broth makes it wonderful! They have an english menu also
評論3:
[鎖螺絲] (5分, 4 months ago): The braised dishes are basically Sichuan peppercorn flavored. I personally prefer mild spicy, but even the mild version here is quite flavorful.

The meat seems to have been pan-fried, giving it a slightly charred appearance, which I thought was nice; the radish and beef tendon were also braised until very tender and thoroughly cooked.

Aside from the location, I think everything else is excellent and I highly recommend it.
評論4:
[張瑞業] (5分, 4 months ago): The price is reasonable.

I highly recommend the Sichuan peppercorn beef noodles. Just one sip of the broth is satisfying, and the next few bites of noodles with a sip of broth are a perfect match. It's not overly salty at all. The radish inside is also stewed until very tender and delicious. 😋
評論5: [郭紋卉] (5分, 4 months ago): There are 3 kinds of soup, I recommend it if you like the aroma of Sichuan pepper, and the beef is quite good.

Google Map地址: 108台灣臺北市萬華區西寧南路70號B1之15室
評論1:
[Pintuwadee Suebsumran] (5分, 2 weeks ago): The restaurant is located in the basement of
Wan Nian Commercial Building (萬年商業大樓) in Ximending.

It’s a bit hidden — you have to go down to the basement level. The area feels like an old-school food court, but each stall is clearly separated.

This was one of my favorite meals in Taiwan

Rich, flavorful broth, tender beef, and freshly handmade noodles. The dumplings were amazing too — especially with chili oil.

Very local, cash only, and totally worth finding.

I’m already craving it again just thinking about it.
評論2: [Michelin Eater] (5分, 3 weeks ago): A go to spot among locals for a quick, satisfying bowl of beef tendon noodles and dumplings. While they offer a variety of items, this is clearly what they’re known for; and for good reason. The sauce bar is a standout, with sour cabbage, chilli oil, fresh green onions, and soy sauce for dipping; so you can not only customize each bowl to your liking but also mix the perfect dip for your dumplings. That freedom to season everything exactly how you like it really elevates the experience. The hand-pulled noodles and dumplings are some of the most authentic and flavourful I’ve had in Taiwan. Some might find the dumplings a bit salty, but I think it perfectly complements the dumpling skin, making every bite a true flavour explosion. Easily a 5 star experience and a place I wish I could go to every day.
評論3:
[Mano Recto] (5分, a month ago): Fantastic handmade noodles with excellent texture and depth of flavor. The dishes here are straightforward, but that simplicity highlights the skill behind them. The noodles are clearly the focus, and they are executed with confidence and consistency.

The atmosphere is very local and no-nonsense, and service is efficient. This is the kind of place you come back to for comfort and reliability. Honest food, done very well.
評論4:
[Culinary Traveler] (5分, 2 months ago): 老山東牛肉麵is a fantastic spot for anyone who loves authentic flavors. Keep in mind that is a cash only restaurant.

The dishes are full of rich, delicious taste, and there’s a great variety on the menu to suit different preferences.

The staff is very friendly and welcoming.

The atmosphere is casual and comfortable, perfect for a satisfying meal.

Highly recommended for anyone visiting the area and craving tasty homemade noodles! 🍜✨
評論5:
[elin chow] (4分, a month ago): Decided to try out the beef noodles here because a lot of people recommended this place. The place was a little hard to find as it is located at the basement of Wan Nian building. I ordered a bowl of beef noodles with only beef chunks. As soon as my order came, I took a sip of the soup and found that it didn’t have the “wow” factor. I wished it would be more robust and flavourful.
But the beef, which came in a large chunks, was delightfully tender and fall apart easily. The handmade noodles were wide and chewy, absorbing the broth very well. Portion was huge and very filling. Overall, it was a decent bowl of beef noodles, but nothing spectacular.

Service was not the best as the restaurant was very busy. I didn’t know that there is a condiment counter where I can add some preserved vegetables to my noodles because I wasn’t informed about it.

Google Map地址: 108台灣臺北市萬華區華西街17之4號攤位153號
評論1:
[Mano Recto] (5分, a month ago): Wang’s Broth delivers a deeply satisfying and honest bowl of beef noodle soup. The broth is the star — clear, clean, and layered with flavor, showing patience and proper technique rather than excess seasoning. The beef was tender and well-portioned, and the noodles had good structure and bite.

There is nothing flashy here, and that’s precisely the appeal. The space is simple and functional, service is efficient, and the focus remains squarely on the food. This is comfort cooking done with confidence and consistency. I would happily return and recommend this to anyone wanting to understand why Taipei takes its beef noodles seriously.
評論2:
[Andrés Felipe Pacheco Girón] (5分, 2 weeks ago): Food is absolutely fantastic. A bit on the saltier side, but delicious nonetheless.

The pork rice is fantastic, very flavorful and has a generous portion of mushrooms and pork. The tofu was also fantastic, herbal and aromatic flavor.

Would absolutely recommend to all the foreigners out there who want to try Taiwanese food! The place was filled with locals (and my local friends say it’s very good).
評論3:
[May Ng] (5分, 3 weeks ago): The signature "Black Gold" Minced Pork Rice is amazing—rich, savory, and the glaze has a great texture.

I also highly recommend the 清湯瓜仔肉; the broth is surprisingly clear and naturally sweet, which balances the richness of the rice perfectly. Don’t skip the Braised Duck Egg—it’s much more fragrant than a regular egg and a must-order side.

Fast service and a clean spot in Wanhua. Definitely worth a visit.
評論4: [Ai Fong Lee] (5分, a month ago): Visited here on 5th Jan during my Taiwan trip. The braised pork rice and stewed pork rice are highly recommended. Very scrumptious and flavourful. There is no queue when we were there. But I heard this shop is very famous among the local and tourist. Their staffs are very friendly and service was extremely fast.
評論5:
[Jim1003] (4分, a month ago): Wang’s Broth (小王煮瓜) – Taipei’s Legendary Braised Pork Belly Heritage
If you're visiting Taipei and don't stop by Huaxi Street Night Market to try Wang’s Broth, you're seriously missing out on a Michelin Bib Gourmand gem! This shop is world-famous for its “Black Gold”—braised pork with a signature deep, dark glaze and an incredibly intense flavor profile.
Must-Order Dishes:
• Stewed Pork Rice with Mushroom (香菇魯肉飯): The braised pork here is glossy and dark. The flavor is perfectly balanced—savory with a hint of sweetness, enriched by the earthy aroma of shiitake mushrooms. The pork practically melts in your mouth when eaten with their hot steamed rice.
• Stewed Pork Rice (控肉飯): For those who love thick cuts, you must order the braised pork belly. The slabs are thick, the skin is delightfully chewy, and the meat is tender without being greasy at all.

餐廳名稱: 艋舺熱海 海鮮餐廳
Google Map地址: 108台灣臺北市萬華區和平西路三段162號
評論1:
[Ben Lin] (4分, a year ago): Had family reunion lunch in this restaurant, 12pax for 14,500 NTD, not too expensive while the portion of each dish is big enough, mainly seafood.
Parking is a big challenge in this area as we are not familiar with its location but not too far, there is a parking tower built by Caesar hotel within walking distance
Deco of this restaurant is quite unique, feel like eating in our own living room at home.
Recommended !!
評論2:
[Louis Ching] (5分, 2 years ago): The Best dinner I have for the 7 days in Taipei, they are quite unique, they don't really let you pick the menu, they just ask for you budget per head (ranging from NT900, NT1,200, NT1,500 or NT1,800).
We took the NT1,500 package for 7 people, and every dish is awesome, fresh, tasty, beautifully plated, variety. The ambience is comfy, the staffs are helpful and attentive.
Surely will be here again if I visit Taipei. This is my new "MUST GO" place.
評論3: [Trang Nguyen] (5分, 5 years ago): There’s no fix menu and the ingredients are so fresh. The restaurant is so loved by the locals that they are fully book for CNY 2022 new year eve’s day!!! That’s crazy. The place is small and cosy.
評論4: [Jaeyun Joung] (5分, 7 months ago): Very good cooking! First time tasted Aloe as desert. Review by KTinVAN
評論5:
[Tetsu莊明哲] (4分, 2 years ago): Old fashion feeling restaurant in the small alley.
The food is good and the facility is most likely as a home feeling.

Google Map地址: 108台灣臺北市萬華區環河南路二段308號
評論1: [Doreen Leong] (4分, 11 months ago): Tasty and good value. Worth a visit for local Taiwanese food.
評論2: [Tim Hetherington] (5分, a year ago): Wonderful cold chicken, sweet $ sour fish and fresh vegetables. Delicious tea made by grandpa! They called someone to help as we speak no Chinese
評論3: [Andrew Wang] (4分, 8 years ago): The food served here are pretty good and authentic, although there still room for improvement in terms of dining environment and service.
評論4: [William Yao] (4分, a month ago): 4.2 stars 🌟 A long-established local restaurant. Although there aren't many competitors nearby, the food here is better than I expected! The shrimp and vermicelli casserole was particularly impressive. The shrimp were large, and the wide, flat, translucent vermicelli was cooked to perfection. The slightly spicy kick and smooth texture were addictive!
評論5:
[翁儀庭] (5分, 3 months ago): Every dish was delicious!

Starting with the refreshing appetizer, the "Japanese-style Dragon King Feast," the large, plump pieces of steamed lobster meat were perfectly complemented by a Japanese-style salad of purple onions, okra, and yam, garnished with slices of zucchini, tomato, and orange—a truly satisfying appetizer.

The Peking duck had fragrant, crispy skin. A slice of roasted duck meat, glistening, crispy skin, and a scallion were wrapped in a thin pancake. One bite, and the skin burst with juice, filling your mouth with the rich flavor of duck—simply delicious! 🤤

The soft-shell crab with crab roe was another highlight! The dipping sauce was unique, slightly spicy, and the soft ham bread paired perfectly with the freshly fried soft-shell crab—a truly satisfying bite!

The most memorable dish for me was the apple-shaped lava bun! The filling was a lightly apple-scented custard lava cake, and the exterior looked exactly like a real apple—it was truly a "steamed" apple! 😆

Finally, of course, we finished off with delicious fruit! Taiwanese mini watermelon, thin-skinned and plump oranges, and perfectly washed purple grapes.

The waitress was incredibly friendly and fun! 🤣

We were very lucky that there was a temple fair parade outside during our gathering that day—a truly lively and wonderful day. We chose a great date! 😊

餐廳名稱: 兩喜號 Liang Xi Hao - 西園店
Google Map地址: 108台灣臺北市萬華區西園路一段194號
評論1:
[Christopher Lee] (4分, 2 weeks ago): Liang Xi Hao literally means “a house of double happiness.”🇹

After the two women made their wishes at Longshan Temple,
this was the very first place we stepped into among the nearby eateries.

'Squid Thick Soup' wasn’t exactly to my taste, to be honest.

But 'Braised Milkfish Belly' gave me, a devoted lover of fish, a quiet kind of delight. 😍
Its flavor was delicate yet rich, with a subtle oiliness that made it unexpectedly appealing.

We dined on the second floor. The space was calm and tidy, pleasantly removed from the noise of the busy street below. That slight distance from the crowd made it even more enjoyable.

And the prices were kind. Truly excellent value for money.

So naturally, it earns a high rating from me. 😄
評論2: [Hartono] (4分, a month ago): This restaurant is small and doesn’t provide dedicated car parking. The noodles are soft, with a clear yet rich and thick broth. The fried pork and cuttlefish are tasty, tender, and full of flavor. The fried noodles, in my opinion, are okay but nothing special. We also ordered fish crackers—it’s better to ask for them to be served without being soaked in the soup. Overall, the service is good and fast.
評論3: [Gencode Ladplakao] (5分, 3 months ago): I like the thick soup with rice. The stir fried noodle is interesting. The veggie stir fry is good and nice. Waitress is very nice to us as she explained how each sauce supposed to be used. Food is hot and clean. Nice price.
評論4: [Stuart Gibson] (5分, 3 months ago): Loved this place from our recent trip. I liked the rice and braised pork (which I believe is a more traditional Taiwanese meal). The servings were a good size and never left the place feeling hungry. Super priced as well with very rapid service
評論5:
[Emily Chua] (4分, 4 months ago): Food portion is small.

The beehoon soup taste like taiwan orh ah mee sua plus singapore lormee. They have chili which makes the whole soup a more umami taste.

The place is very small but the turnover is very fast

餐廳名稱: 燒杯咖啡藝廚 Beaker ChefCafe /寵物友善/寵物店長/融合料理 / 貝果/沙拉-萬華人氣咖啡廳|必吃簡餐|網美下午茶 外帶輕食l融合料理|網美咖啡廳/手作/咖啡燒杯/餐廳/午餐/晚餐
Google Map地址: 108台灣臺北市萬華區西園路一段240號
評論1: [Stanford Chung] (3分, 2 months ago): This café had some fun Americano twists, so curiosity won. Tried the Plum Sparkling Americano and the Ice Cream Americano. The plum version was interesting—the fruit completely takes the lead, tasting more like a refreshing plum juice with just a hint of coffee. Not bad at all, just not the pick if you’re craving a strong coffee-forward drink.
評論2: [Helena Åström] (4分, 4 months ago): Nice selection of drinks, i recommend the Sicillian coffee! Also had Dutch mini pancakes which were nice.
評論3: [JustLinahere] (3分, 8 years ago): Their space is nicely decorated with a mix of tables and lounge type chairs. I had black coffee and butter toast. They are quite good. Not a quiet atmosphere though, everybody's talking loudly. I think this place is good for parties, good for get together. Restroom is inside. The service was okay. I didn't see many staffs there, so you have to wait a bit for your order.
評論4: [Gourment Delligatti] (4分, 3 years ago): If you want a place while raining, this is the place to drink better coffee than 7-11 ☕️
評論5: [Erik Cerenius] (5分, 2 months ago): Very good coffee, a bit pricey but really worth it!

Google Map地址: 108台灣臺北市萬華區西寧南路36號10樓
評論1: [Vannessa Adrienne] (3分, 3 weeks ago): The food was decent, but quite overpriced for the overall experience. Service was slow and not very professional, which made the visit less enjoyable. The ordering system was also confusing — we had to queue ourselves just to get the 蘿蔔糕 and 腸粉, which felt inconvenient and poorly organized. The restaurant feels old, and cleanliness needs improvement; the floor was sticky and the overall hygiene was below expectations. With better management, improved service, and attention to cleanliness, this place could offer a much better dining experience.
評論2:
[Kate Shao] (4分, 6 months ago): Golden Lion was a classic dim sum spot in Ximending with an old-school 80s vibe and even a live fish tank at the entrance. The pushcart service made the experience fun and nostalgic.

1. The roast suckling pig and duck were tasty but salty.
2. The five-flavor squid and shrimp rice rolls were surprisingly fresh and delicious.
3. The BBQ pork buns were a letdown—too fatty and already cold.
4. The sesame balls were my absolute favorite—crispy on the outside and soft and chewy inside. I could have easily finished a whole plate by myself!

A bit pricey at over NT$2,000 for two. No prices on the bill, so we ordered too much without realizing. Still, overall, I enjoyed the food, service, and atmosphere, and I would come back again.
評論3: [Chris SG] (5分, a year ago): very authentic experience - the order amidst the chaos! Definitely an experience to behold as you watch with expectant eyes while the dim sum trolleys come rolling around. Food wise it was ok, not the finest but definitely the atmosphere made up for it. It may seem a little dinghy and unkempt, but I think that's what makes the experience such a mix of nostalgia and amazement that the establishment has been long running since forever. 🤗
評論4: [EATOUT Place to Eat! Place to Go!] (4分, 4 months ago): Food is great but not many choice  friendly staff old school vibe
評論5: [Karen Montina] (2分, 2 months ago): The place was crowded, which is a good sign, the servers were also plenty nice and helpful. There were just too many dishes that didn't look appetizing. Out of ten dishes, one was appealing, the others were room temperature when they should have been hot or cold, or they were not seasoned, or the texture was off. It could have been the lighting, or the impression left by the old building, or I'm spoiled by the exceptional dim sum place I go to back home.

餐廳名稱: 職人丼深夜食堂 萬華店
Google Map地址: 108台灣臺北市萬華區東園街35巷12號
評論1:
[阿明] (4分, 3 months ago): The food and service were average, and the space was rather small. The restaurant seems to primarily focus on delivery orders. There's no restroom inside, which is something that could be improved.

The chicken and pork chops were decent, nothing special. I wouldn't recommend the crispy chicken! It's mostly skin; there's very little meat inside – a disappointment!

I wouldn't recommend the shrimp patties either; they were too soft and didn't seem very fresh. The golden kimchi was good! A small plate costs 40 yuan, so the price is subjective.

Overall, I think if you live nearby, you can check it out, but don't bother making a special trip to the restaurant!

Ordering two main dishes, a small shrimp patty, two hash browns, and a small plate of kimchi totaled 400 yuan, which is acceptable.
評論2: [柏諺] (5分, 3 months ago): Affordable, generous portions, and simple, unpretentious flavors. The menu offers a wide variety of dishes, such as tonkatsu (fried pork cutlet) bowls, beef bowls, and yakiniku (grilled meat), all of a decent standard. Most importantly, it's filling, making it perfect for a quick meal after work or a late-night snack. The ambiance is clean, though not fancy, and the food is served relatively quickly. It's a great place for those who want a simple, satisfying meal without breaking the bank.
評論3: [Citynomadey] (5分, 3 months ago): The donburi (rice bowl) was delicious, and the side dishes were also quite good. It's a small shop with a relaxed atmosphere and the music isn't too loud. However, it's not open until very late; it closes around 10 PM. I recommend it though. There are parking spaces on the street or in the parking lot of the nearby school.
評論4:
[nejmcid Lue] (4分, 7 months ago): We ordered takeout for lunch at work. When I went to pick it up, my colleagues had already finished theirs, and I only managed to take a picture of my own fried horse mackerel and scad donburi.

I think if the donburi I ate was a 70, the one served on-site would definitely be an 85! The seaweed was soaked with steam; however, thankfully the batter for the fried fish and horse mackerel was still quite crispy, the fish was very fresh, and the fried horse mackerel had a moderate, not unpleasant, fishy smell! The egg broth, similar to oyako-don, soaked into the rice, and even after sitting for a while, it was still very appetizing. It would probably be even better eaten on-site. The portion of rice was also quite generous!

Unanimously agreed to add it to our preferred order list!
評論5:
[JL] (2分, 6 months ago): My impressions after eating there twice: The fried food was a bit too salty, and it got greasy after a while.

The cauliflower was delicious.

The curry sauce was just so-so.

The donburi rice was too dry…

餐廳名稱: 悅勝 丼飯、生魚片、壽司專賣店-萬華店
Google Map地址: 108台灣臺北市萬華區西藏路182號
評論1: [Elaine Chua] (5分, 3 years ago): Absolutely food from the heart - their omakase 无菜单料理 is a great experience - generous with the finest ingredients, and well-thought out combination of seasoning.
評論2: [Sophie Smith] (5分, a month ago): My fav place to eat when I come home to visit my parents. The place is cozy and food is delicious. I keep coming back to this place.
評論3: [Joy Ni] (5分, 2 years ago): Doesn't have high-end decor but the sushi exceeded my expectations
評論4:
[林美玲] (3分, 5 months ago): Fortunately, it wasn't too expensive.

There was free hot tea and miso soup (trimmed salmon and tofu), and I had chawanmushi, hand rolls, grilled mackerel rice bowl, and salmon sushi. For some reason, my blood sugar skyrocketed after returning, and I couldn't handle any other Japanese food.
評論5: [林詩涵] (5分, a month ago): A Japanese restaurant I stumbled upon! I was really craving sashimi, and the sashimi was so fresh! The vinegared rice was also delicious. I'll definitely come back next time! 😋😋😋

Google Map地址: 108台灣臺北市萬華區東園街68-1號
評論1:
[Salim Fang] (4分, 3 years ago): A small Taiwanese style izakaya, maybe make a reservation cause it was pretty full when we dined there.

Like most Japanese places in Taiwan, the food there is a bit of a fusion of Taiwanese style as well, but that’s not a bad thing. Maybe choose another place if you want something very authentically Japanese tho.

The food was generally good, but the service was a bit slow.
評論2: [1977Futre] (4分, 5 years ago): Great little place. Good beer and food. Got a dodgy pint, but they replaced in seconds. Staff are friendly and it is good value compared to civic boulevard. I will come here after work a lot, now i know its decent.
評論3:
[Pedro Martins] (5分, 5 years ago): A true to it's name izakaya ! Provides a nice , clean , very informal environment.
The mood inside the space is great, and the food quality and beer / sake ( especially sake ) options follow suit.

Especially appropriate if you're a late eater , go just go in , get a few skewers , a large beer , and relax !
評論4: [Kern Chang] (5分, a year ago): Excellent sake selections, solid draft beer from Naha, very efficient operations and delicious izakaya fares.
評論5: [E C] (4分, 5 years ago): Great food. Great vibe.

餐廳名稱: 家吶子台式居酒屋 Ka-la̍h-á
Google Map地址: 108台灣臺北市萬華區東園街68-4號
評論1: [TaiwanWalker] (5分, 2 years ago): Taiwanese Izakaya with lively vibes and tasty Taiwan style barbecue and fried dishes. Most of the wine are made in Taiwan, have to try their crispy pork, tamagoyaki with cheese and peanut butter. Just ordered the grilled food you’d like to try, they’re all delicious.
評論2: [Chloe Chen] (5分, 3 years ago): Love this Taiwanese izakaya place! So cozy! Food was great. Staff was friendly! They also have extensive selection of plum wine and rice wine! Highly recommend this place!
評論3: [Yaora Huang] (4分, 3 years ago): Really smooth drinks. The food was great as well!
評論4: [Amy Yang] (4分, 4 years ago): Tasty food, but one star deducted for missing 2 dishes (we had to remind them.) It also took awhile for everything to arrive. Recommendation: come on a weekday.
評論5: [B Tee] (5分, 6 years ago): Small restaurant but the food is delicious

餐廳名稱: 四方阿九魯肉飯 萬華店
Google Map地址: 108台灣臺北市萬華區廣州街225號
評論1:
[TAN KC] (5分, a month ago): Trying another braised pork rice for supper.

Portion big. Especially for pork belly. So much bigger than the rest.  Taste is good and flavourful. Give a lot of side dishes and portion is packed. So very worth it and tasty. Hardly see a lot of shop giving so much stuffs.

Service fast and good.
評論2:
[vind] (4分, 11 months ago): Before we take a walk to Lungshan temple, we tried the local foods around. Takes about 3 minute walk from the station.

We tried some of their foods:
• Stew minced pork : it was a good combination to mix warm rice and fats from minced pork, taste more to sweet, pour it with wonton soup made it slurpy was best.
• Stew pork belly : portion big and price was friendly to pocket, texture was incredibly tender, despite the whole plate mostly fats, but still worth it.
• Wonton soup : broth was fresh and light, solid filling with pork meats.

They have outdoor & indoor seats, quick serving time, but the staff was too cold.
評論3: [Keane Leow] (5分, 12 months ago): Very nice pork belly and pork chop rice and noodle. The rice is better than the noodle. Both are very heavily flavored 😍
評論4: [Nathan Ng] (4分, 8 years ago): Really delicious 滷肉飯. Can't get enough of it. Every time we come to Longshan Temple we will stop by this humble place. The braised meat is fatty yet still tasty.
評論5:
[KKT] (3分, a year ago): Generally ok to eat.
Pork belly fatty part not soft, lean part abit hard.

餐廳名稱: 兩喜號 Liang Xi Hao - 總店
Google Map地址: 108台灣臺北市萬華區廣州街245號
評論1: [Phakkanan P.] (5分, a week ago): It was very delicious. I went to eat there two years ago and only just got around to reviewing it. I even went two days in a row! This year I’m planning to go to Taiwan, and this restaurant is one of the places I will definitely go back to eat at! 😋
評論2:
[WK C] (4分, 3 months ago): We recently dined at Liang Xi Hao, and it was a truly standout experience. The food was absolutely great, providing a delicious and memorable meal.

For anyone visiting, I have three must try recommendations: the Squid Soup, the Fried Vermicelli, and the Deep Fried Burdock. The combination of fresh ingredients and expertly balanced flavors made these dishes simply marvelous. If you're looking for truly delicious food, Liang Xi Hao is a superb choice.
評論3:
[Brandon Ho] (5分, 3 months ago): 兩喜號 Liang Xi Hao – 總店
This place is an awesome spot for traditional Taiwanese snacks with a modern twist! The flavors are rich, the ingredients taste fresh, and everything feels homemade with care. The staff are warm and welcoming, and the shop has a cozy, nostalgic vibe. It’s a great stop if you want a quick, tasty bite that really captures the local Taiwanese flavor. Perfect for both tourists and locals looking for something satisfying and classic!
評論4:
[vera] (3分, 4 months ago): Soup tasted like mee sua soup but starchy. It was pretty good! Crispy fish was great, the other ingredients were okay. Beehoon was tasty – glass noodle gave it japchae vibes.

[Oct 2025]
評論5: [Albert Yau] (5分, 2 years ago): Delicious starch soup base with traditional Taiwanese squid, fish, meat, and shrimp. They also serve braise pork and some deep-fried dishes. Seating has 2 floors.

餐廳名稱: 鐵 F.f Teppanyaki 鐵板燒（萬大店）
Google Map地址: 108台灣臺北市萬華區萬大路486巷16號
評論1: [Arthur Pang] (5分, 11 months ago): Fantastic dining experience, we felt taken care of. With only 9 seats, it was intimate watching the chef perform their craft. My spouse doesn't eat beef or pork, and they were able to substitute with lovely alternatives. Highly recommended!
評論2:
[C J] (5分, 2 years ago): Great food, excellent service and value.
1,100 is after service charge. (10%)

Not a spacious restaurant. A bit difficult to move around and restroom could use a bit more work.
評論3: [Saar Rachamim] (5分, 6 years ago): Intimate teppanyaki, 8 seats. High quality of the dishes. Wagyu steak and a mushroom soup with truffel are only a small part of the menu. A cray fish can be added in an extra charge. The chef cooks in front of you, as expected. It is not cheap compared to street food, but it is a real value for money.
評論4: [Wan Ting Liu] (5分, 6 years ago): This place is a hidden gem. The food is very fresh and yummy with nice deco. The chef would innovate 1-2 dishes every week so every month the menu is different. Highly recommend this place to all!
評論5: [Meek Soon] (4分, a year ago): Food freshness and tasty and service were good. One important note is chef need to put gloves while in contact with especially raw food. :)

餐廳名稱: 艋舺螺螄粉 桂林米粉
Google Map地址: 108台灣臺北市萬華區廣州街175號
評論1:
[お小賴] (3分, 3 months ago): I haven't been to China Liuchow where the noodle dish is originated from, so could not judge on the authenticity.

But the taste was good, sour and spicy soup with smooth but very chewy noodle, something you could not find easily in Taiwan.
評論2: [Chomticha S.] (4分, 6 months ago): I found this restaurant while walking to Lungshan Temple. It’s a very local-style noodle shop, and the only English sign I saw was “Cash Only.” I solved the language barrier by pointing at pictures to order my food. They offer many kinds of Taiwanese noodles—some in spicy mala broth, others in peanut-based, and some loaded with beef and wontons in huge portions! Super comforting and totally worth a try.
評論3: [Harshvardhan Sekhsaria] (4分, a month ago): Very local place, the food was good. But i think a bit heavy on the meat for me. But very tasty and local. Enjoyed the food.
評論4: [bear babe] (5分, 3 months ago): Not too spicy, no stinky flavor, taste good
評論5: [Steven] (3分, 8 months ago): Good soup. No bathroom. No drinks. No English. But for $10, we got 2 huge bowls of soup that were pretty good… albeit not great.

Google Map地址: 115台灣臺北市南港區南港路一段229號1f
評論1: [Hsin-Hung Lin] (5分, a month ago): Good~
評論2:
[Rong Jason] (5分, a week ago): 1️⃣ The rice balls (rice balls) have a great texture.

Many reviews mention:

• The rice grains are distinct, warm but not sticky.

• The seaweed aroma is pronounced.

• The overall texture is like a combination of "Japanese rice ball + hamburger."

Common review types:

"The rice has a great texture, not too soft, and it's very fragrant with seaweed."

"It's better than I expected, like a rice ball version of a hamburger."

⸻

2️⃣ The toppings are generous and layered.

As you can see in the photo, many people mention:

• The tamagoyaki (Japanese rolled omelet) is thick.

• The meat or fried food portion is generous.

• It has a layered flavor.

Common reviews:

"The tamagoyaki is very thick and tender."

"There are a lot of toppings; one is actually quite filling."

⸻

The mentaiko flavor you ordered is one of the most popular flavors.

Netizens often say:

• Mentaiko (spicy and savory) – just the right amount of salty and savory.

• A slightly seared flavor.

• Not too greasy.

Common comments:

"The seared mentaiko is very fragrant and goes well with rice."

"The mentaiko isn't too salty; the proportions are just right."

⸻

4️⃣ Japanese Fried Chicken – Delicious!

The other dish you ordered is also popular.

Comments often mention:

• Crispy skin

• Juicy chicken

• Not too oily

Common comments:

"The fried chicken is delicious, crispy on the outside and tender on the inside."

"Fried chicken goes well with rice balls."

⸻

5️⃣ Set Meals – Good Value for Money

Many people order the set meals (drink + fries).

評論4: [Ching Yung Huang] (5分, a month ago): It looks delicious overall~ and tastes amazing too 😎 The garlic shrimp rice has a really strong garlic flavor 😍

評論1: [A Chang] (3分, a month ago): My recent visit left me with mixed feelings compared to my previous experience, and overall it was not as good as the 101 store location, which I usually enjoy more. The atmosphere was still pleasant and the restaurant looked clean and well maintained, but the dining experience did not quite meet the expectations I had based on my first visit. The biggest issue was the waiting time for the food, which took a bit too long considering the restaurant was not overly busy at the time. When the dishes finally arrived, some of them were semi cold, which really took away from the overall enjoyment and made it feel as though the timing in the kitchen was not well coordinated. That said, the staff service was good though, and I want to give credit where it is due. The employees were polite, attentive, and made an effort to check in on us, which helped soften the disappointment slightly. Unfortunately, good service alone could not fully make up for the quality and temperature of the food on this visit. It was a bit disappointing as I had higher standard the first time I went, and I was hoping for the same level of consistency this time around. I still believe the place has potential, especially with such friendly staff, but there clearly needs to be better control over preparation time and food temperature. With some improvements in these areas, I think it could return to the level I experienced before and be much closer to what I expect from a restaurant of this type.

[Stephanie Lee] (5分, 8 months ago): This Hawaiian-style restaurant has quickly become one of my go-to spots. The food is not only delicious but also incredibly authentic — you can really taste the island vibes in every bite. I usually order the combo for two, and it never disappoints. From the fresh salad to the flavorful main courses and the satisfying desserts, everything is consistently excellent.

The desserts are definitely worth saving room for — sweet, tropical, and the perfect way to end the meal.

What really sets this place apart, though, is the atmosphere. The playful, colorful decor instantly puts you in a good mood and truly makes you feel like you’re dining in Hawaii. It’s a fun and relaxing spot that brings a little vacation energy to every visit.

Highly recommend if you’re craving something tropical and tasty!

Pros: The broth was delicious, and the fried dough sticks were great after soaking up the soup. It would be even better if the contents included slices of meat.

Cons: The water cups and bowls in the self-service area all smelled like dishcloths, which was awful…we had to replace them several times…we suggest using clean cloths to wipe them down. The tofu in the soup was burnt.
評論5:
[方Fang] (5分, 3 months ago): The stir-fried pork balls were spicy and flavorful, and a serving of 10 was quite generous.
The Sichuan Mapo Tofu Rice was fragrant, spicy, and smooth, especially with the addition of fresh minced garlic, which elevated the flavor to another level. The minced pork had a slight porky taste, but the tofu was absolutely delicious. Perhaps a vegetarian version could be made in the future?

The stir-fried shredded potatoes with vinegar were simply delicious! 😆 The slow-cooked vegetable and chicken soup, made with sous-vide chicken, had a refreshing and delicious taste. The fact that you can substitute vegetables for the hot pot ingredients is fantastic.

And another thing, which is actually my absolute favorite, is the use of ceramic pots, ceramic or iron spoons, and glass cups. It shows the restaurant's attention to detail.

One slight pity is the slight fermented oil smell in the restaurant; it would be perfect without it.

評論2:
[Foodie] (3分, a year ago): Mixed Bag at This Spot
The vibe here’s sleek and modern. But the experience leaning toward “miss” for the price.
The Good Stuff
That set meal with free-flow buns and toast are d best..Winner. Soft, warm, and not overly sweet spread. The chowder soup was quite creamy.

The Letdowns
Most everything else felt like starch overload with zero protein. The bitter gourd noodles? Bacon bits only. Cauliflower rice? Three sad slices of meat. It’s like they forgot the main event.

Service Snafus
We waited 50 minutes just to get in, which was a bummer. But the real kicker? our dessert: mocha and boba pudding took forever. We left after finished everything, forgot about those desserts, and then we got a call 40 minutes later. No staff checked in while we were there—awkward.
What’re u guys doing there?

So, Those toast, bun and soup saved it from a total fail, but the rest felt like a fancy tease.
(P.S. Fix the wait times and protein portions, and maybe we’ll come back.)

評論3:
[YC H] (1分, 3 months ago): December 5th Update: I ate the beef noodles I took out yesterday today, only to find there was only half a bowl. This restaurant not only threw away a customer's food without apologizing but also only gave the customer half back. They showed no sincerity and I will not return. This is for other potential customers' reference.

The food was good, but the staff training needs improvement.

Service: Today I ate alone. The staff had already seated me halfway through when a foreigner approached, and they panicked, telling me I should wait at the counter while they finished. The beef noodles were 420 NTD a bowl, quite expensive, but I only got about half the value. I wanted to take the other half home, but the staff threw it into the waste bin. A waiter came out to tell me the food had been thrown away, so what? My beef noodles? Finally, perhaps noticing my upset expression, they went back to negotiate with the kitchen to give me a replacement bowl. The experience was very poor. Please improve staff training. The price is higher than Din Tai Fung, but the service is less than half as good.

Food: I ordered the braised beef noodles. The noodles were thick and chewy, somewhat like knife-cut noodles. There were four pieces of beef, one of which was tendon. The beef was stewed until tender and melted in my mouth, which was commendable. The broth was decent, typical of good braised beef noodles. The other side dishes were ordinary, even a bit cheap, including tomato and tofu skin, dried tofu strips (which tasted like non-spicy konjac), and pickled mustard greens. The tea jelly seemed to be made from powder that you could buy at PX Mart, and the fruit was neither sweet nor flavorful—not good at all. I suggest focusing on beef noodles, lowering the price, or improving the quality of the other side dishes.

評論1: [Jackie Yu] (5分, 2 weeks ago): Inside the beautiful Hi Lai hotel, The Orient restaurant was exquisite. Very refined and flavorful dishes blew me away.  The service the ambiance there wasn't a single dish, I didn't care for. My only regret is.I was too full to have more.

評論3: [Sandra McKowen] (5分, 11 months ago): I had the most incredible experience at The Orient inside the Hi Lai Hotel, Taipei. I thought it a vibrant & modern restaurant. I had their signature Peking Duck, it was hearty & satisfying, you must try! The fish was rich and full of flavor, perfectly balanced spicy & savory flavors. The lobster pocketbook pastries were not only tasteful, but a beautiful work of art! This is the place to go when looking for a memorable dining experience. I highly recommend!
評論4: [Kimberly An] (5分, a year ago): My family and I had the BEST meal in Taiwan here at The Orient (located in Grand Hilai Hotel Taipei). The presentation is exquisite and all the dishes tasted divine. You can tell the chef uses high quality ingredients and is a master at cooking. The atmosphere was very relaxing and the cushioned seats were a bonus. We will definitely be back the next time we're in Taipei to try the dim sum lunch.
評論5:
[Andrew Goh] (4分, a year ago): This restaurant offers high-end Cantonese cuisine, using premium ingredients to create a delicious meal. However, the fried Chiron was a bit of a letdown, as it seemed over-fried, resulting in dry meat and bland flavor. My favorite dish was the coconut steamed chicken soup with shark fin—it had great flavor, though I believe they could have substituted the shark fin with other ingredients. The clear soup base was different from the more commonly slow-cooked shark fin broths. The suckling pig, served with only the skin, was decent, though the Peking suckling pig didn’t impress me. The shredded duck meat used in the fried vermicelli was too dry for my taste. The steamed claypot rice was just average, with nothing particularly special about it.

On the other hand, I thoroughly enjoyed the braised pig intestine, smelly tofu, and blood jelly—the flavors were spot on, although a spicier sauce would have made it perfect. The traditional dim sum, like steamed rice noodle rolls with prawn and fried dough, was also enjoyable. Another highlight of the meal was the signature barbecue pork, which stood out as a memorable dish. While the meal was expensive, it’s worth trying. Be aware, though, that service was a bit slow during lunch, so be prepared to wait.

Google Map地址: 115台灣臺北市南港區經貿一路168號2樓
評論1:
[Albert] (1分, 5 months ago): I’m an overseas visitor who has dined at buffet restaurants around the world. I’d planned to visit ISLAND BUFFET since last year and finally made it on 25 September 2025, coming all the way to Nangang specifically for this restaurant. They were fully booked—which I completely understand given the hype.

What I don’t understand is the attitude. As I was flying out the next day, I politely asked the receptionist if I could take a quick look at the buffet for future reference. The answer was a flat refusal—no effort to accommodate, no alternative, just a rigid “no.” It felt needlessly strict and, frankly, arrogant.

At comparable or even pricier places like A Joy at Taipei's 101, staff consistently show hospitality first. A two-minute peek costs nothing and builds goodwill; here, the message was clear: if you’re not dining today, you don’t matter. That arrogance overshadowed any reputation they might have.

Note: The photos I posted are from A Joy, for comparison—not ISLAND BUFFET. A Joy regularly features high-end items such as: Wagyu A5, O-toro sashimi, Giant scallops, Lobsters, King crab. This place might have more variety, but I don’t think they offer better quality than A Joy. Given the arrogance I experienced at the door, I have no reason to find out.

I won’t be returning. I’ll stick with A Joy, where guests are treated with respect from the moment they arrive. Plus, the view from 86th floor is amazing !
評論2: [JungJung Chang] (5分, 11 months ago): I had the best breakfast buffet ever—so many different dishes, all incredibly delicious and satisfying. It was such an enjoyable experience that I didn’t even need lunch afterward. They had signature beef soup, siu mai, goose noodle soup—you could eat to your heart’s content. Everything was so refined and high-quality. They even had alcohol tucked away in a corner, something only the insiders know about. I absolutely loved it and definitely want to stay there again next time. Highly recommended!
評論3:
[Evan] (5分, a year ago): This buffet restaurant, located in a 5-star hotel, boasts an elegant interior and efficient service from the staff.

They offer lunch and dinner on weekdays, while on weekends, they also serve afternoon tea. Prices range from 1,500 to 2,000 NTD plus a 10% service charge. The menu is extensive and well-organized into sections, each featuring wine pairings to complement the dishes.

I made my reservation online three days in advance, and since it's highly sought after, spots tend to sell out quickly. However, checking for last-minute cancellations is a good option. The overall experience was excellent, with fresh ingredients and diverse flavors, and I would definitely return.
評論4:
[Yue Huan Lee] (3分, 6 months ago): Heard this is one of the "must try" buffet avenue in Taipei so I booked. Its very popular and paying a deposit is necessary to secure a table.

On arrival just before the restaurant opening at 11:30am, I was shocked at the massive crowd queing outside the restaurant, numbering at least several hundreds. There were also tour groups that organize eating trips to have their meals here. I had to register and assigned a table.

The buffet spread is absolutely massive and has everything from Japanese to Chinese to western. It's like 2 to 3X the size of typical buffets I had in Singapore and far more diners. Long queues were formed at popular counters like the seafood selection. While the food was mostly tasty and good, and all freshly prepared in the "live" kitchen counters, some of the more premium ( ie expensive ) food like lobsters and grilled foie gras were missing. The seafood counter did had a reasonable selection of fresh prawns, mud crab, clams and snow crab legs though. Ebi Tempura, pan fried scallops and fresh oysters were also on the menu. Drinks like various fruit juices and even wines were included but the wines served were cheap and of inferior quality.

Service cant be great as there are so many diners so forget about any personalized service. Also the chefs that manned the counters can be rather impatient and they will not entertain any special requests. The atmosphere as I mentioned is chaotic due to the sheer number of diners. In fact, one has to be careful while holding plates of food so as not to accidentally "bang" into someone.

Overall, for around NT$3100 per person, its quite value for money considering just food only and only if one can eat a lot to make it worth. My problem is with the ambience, it's simply too crowded and too noisy, like eating in a massive canteen with tables closely packed together. Its simply not condusive to have a relaxing meal. So I don't think I will be back.
評論5: [An Le] (4分, 8 months ago): The breakfast buffet at Island Buffett in the Hilai Hotel Nankang offers a fantastic spread. There's a wide array of options covering Western, Japanese, and Chinese cuisines. We particularly enjoyed the fresh omelettes and French toast, which were quite decent. The Chinese food selections, including the congee, fried rice, and pork, were of very good quality. As expected in Asia, the service was also great, making for a very pleasant experience.

餐廳名稱: 磚窯古早料理南港創始店

評論1:
[Chris Lim] (4分, a year ago): Very old nostalgic place as if it has been untouched by time in the last 50 years. Worth coming to experience this vs the current hustling minimalist dining spaces trending nowadays.

Food wise was ok. Our objective was to experience the Taiwanese food that has been enjoyed by their people in the past and this place was spot on.

After dinner, you can take a slow walk toward the Raohe Night Market which is in close proximity.
評論2:
[J J] (4分, a year ago): This was one of the few restaurants in the area open past 8 pm, so we chose to have our dinner here.

It was a bit tough communicating with the owner lady as she either didn’t really speak Mandarin or didn’t want to (likely she was mostly uttering Taiwanese), so we kind of just winged our order. I also asked for warm water to drink and she just told me no water. But she did give my Indian friend a free whiskey shot 😅

By Taiwanese standards, the food was quite expensive at 1360 NTD for 4 dishes, and the portions weren’t that big. We liked everything—Hakka Stir Fry, XO Scallop and Shrimp, shredded pork noodles—except for Grandma’s Cooking pork dish. The soup was a bit grainy and thick, consisting of pork bits. The spare pork ribs were rubbery and not super appetizing either.

Lastly, the bathroom only had squat toilets.
評論3:

評論1: [謝惠芳] (5分, a year ago): Good
評論2: [Patty Chen] (5分, a month ago): It's very close to Nangang Station. The food is different from typical Loya Loya restaurants; in addition to regular meals, there's also Japanese cuisine. The seating is spacious, clean, and comfortable, and there's a self-service beverage bar.
評論3:
[黃毓婷] (4分, 5 months ago): The Le Ya Le branch in Nangang Biotech Park is located near the Nangang MRT and Nangang Bus Stations, a convenient 3-minute walk.

On the last day of the long holiday, there were very few people. Entering the Biotech Park, I went up to the second floor and found Le Ya Le. The restaurant is spacious, clean, and beautiful, with a very comfortable design.

We've previously had steaks at other branches, which were a bit pricey but not amazing. This time, we all opted for the safer teriyaki chicken, pasta, and salmon steaks—all excellent!

For women, the set meals are generous; if you want more, you can get refills of rice and miso soup.

We scanned the QR code to order, and the staff at the counter brought us two pieces of bread (dividable into four). We half-jokingly asked how we could divide four pieces for five people. The staff didn't respond. There were a variety of drinks at the buffet, but before we had time for a second drink, the staff at the counter reminded us that it was time to eat and rushed us to check out. We ended up going to MOS Burger on the first floor instead.

The other delivery person was relatively friendly. Seeing we were sitting by the window, he even reminded us to draw the curtains to protect us from the sun. That was very thoughtful.

You can pay in cash or other payment methods. Membership offers occasional discounts.
評論4: [HengJay] (3分, 2 months ago): The chicken thigh set meal didn't have many side dishes 🥲 I don't know if I ordered the wrong thing 😓
評論5: [Peggy Chu] (5分, 2 months ago): The space is bright and clean, with spacious seating. The set meals are delicious and great value, and I especially liked that they all come with vegetables. It's perfect for daytime gatherings.

餐廳名稱: 青丹扎西_南港展覽館店

評論1:
[Foodie] (3分, a year ago): I recently visited this restaurant that left me quite disappointed.
The owner had boasted about their generous portion sizes, claiming that most diners find them overwhelming.

However, when I ordered a dish priced at 880, which was supposedly meant for two people, I was surprised to find the portion to be quite small. In fact, I was able to finish it all by myself, which is unusual for a meal advertised for two.

The flavors of the food were also underwhelming. While I expected a strong herbal taste typical of Tibetan cuisine, the dish turned out to be rather plain and lacked the depth of flavor I was hoping for. It felt like a missed opportunity to showcase the richness of traditional Tibetan flavors.

Additionally, I found the pricing of the tea to be excessive. Paying 130 for such a small cup seemed unreasonable and added to my overall dissatisfaction with the meal.

In summary, despite the restaurant’s claims about generous portions and flavorful dishes, my experience indicated that both the quantity and taste fell short of expectations, making it hard to justify the prices.
評論2:
[Frey Chong] (5分, 2 years ago): My favorites of the meal: Milk tea (both savory and sweet), the lamb soup, and the roast lamb.

The soup tasted and smelled herbal / medicinal but in a good way. It leaves the palate fresh and light.

The roast lamb was not gamy at all and was surprisingly soft. Was expecting it to be tough and sinewy because the lamb BBQ was that.

I didn’t feel thirsty after the meal. I guess they didn’t put MSG or was it because of the soup? Which is good!

Food were on the spicy side and numbing….

評論1:
[格格（Lily Nien）] (5分, 3 weeks ago): 260221 Located on the 5th floor of LaLaport in Nangang, the space is spacious, bright, and clean. The seating is comfortable, and the tablet ordering process is smooth and intuitive. Although it gets very crowded on weekends, and diners may have to wait for a table, the food is served quickly, so the wait isn't too long!

✅ Stir-fried Pork Slices with Double Vegetables Set Meal: Highly recommended! It's rare to find such a generous amount of fresh and sweet vegetables among those who eat out. The stir-fry over high heat is incredibly fragrant, and the pork slices are savory and perfect with rice. The rice is especially delicious; I kept getting refills!

✅ Double Shrimp Nanban Fried Chicken: The portion is very generous. The chicken thigh meat is tender and juicy, and the sauce is excellent. The pickled radish and miso soup are both refillable, which is refreshing and helps to cut through the richness. Although my soup bowl was cracked (needs improvement), the food is both plentiful and delicious, making it still worth recommending!
評論2:
[Jay Lin] (4分, 2 months ago): The overall environment is clean and bright, with a quiet and organized atmosphere. The seating isn't too crowded, making it suitable for gatherings with friends or dining with elders.

The eel is moist and not dry, and paired with white rice, miso soup, side dishes, and pickles, it's a perfectly balanced meal.

Unlimited hot tea refills are available, and desserts or drinks can be ordered after the meal. The overall flavor is healthier and lighter, with a Japanese home-style taste, suitable for those who don't like strong flavors.

Ordering is done via a tablet system, which is simple to use and prevents missed orders. Food is served relatively quickly. The clean environment and smooth flow make for a comfortable and pleasant dining experience.

An average set meal costs between $300 and $400. While not super cheap, it's reasonable value for money considering the dining experience within a department store. It's especially suitable for those who want a clean, simple, and nutritionally balanced Japanese set meal.
評論3:
[Rita Wang] (5分, a month ago): As soon as we sat down, we were drawn to the dessert menu and carefully selected three set meals:

Three-way eel, Deluxe Simmered Beef Set Meal, Fresh Salmon Tamagoyaki, and Sukiyaki Tofu Beef Set Meal.

The three-way eel was delicious – first enjoyed plain, then with wasabi, and finally with ochazuke (rice with tea). The layers of flavor were wonderfully layered.

The Deluxe Simmered Beef with onions was so good, I wanted to keep eating it.

The Fresh Salmon Tamagoyaki was very generous with the portion size.

For dessert, we chose a coffee affogato and a matcha affogato, but because the coffee was so bitter, we combined both desserts, which surprisingly turned out to be a delightful combination.

I really loved the food at this restaurant.

The staff were also very friendly.
評論4:
[神隱少女] (3分, a month ago): On a weekday lunchtime, the restaurant was quite busy with diners.

Yayoiken has many branches, and this was my first time dining there. I ordered fresh salmon tamagoyaki, eel tamagoyaki, and a set meal with gold.

The fresh salmon tamagoyaki was honestly very bland; the only way to enhance the flavor was by adding the accompanying seaweed strips. The eel tamagoyaki was a bit better, but the restaurant's tamagoyaki wasn't actually made like tamagoyaki; it was more like a risotto—a pile of white rice topped with a layer of scrambled eggs, but it was really tasteless risotto.

The gold set meal was also very ordinary.

I can only say that you're not really looking for value for money at this kind of restaurant, but it lacked the expected quality and the flavors were rather empty.
評論5: [Momo] (5分, 3 months ago): Members receive a 15% discount on their birthday each year; coincidentally, during the October anniversary sale, spending over $800 entered a lucky draw for a second set meal at half price. My favorite is their three-way eel rice, and I always choose the extra eel option, which makes the eel portion about four times the size of what you get at the food court downstairs; soup, side dishes, and rice are all unlimited refills; I redeemed points for chawanmushi (steamed egg custard), and received a free chawanmushi voucher with my purchase, but it's only valid for ten days!

餐廳名稱: 樂子the Diner 南港店（台北南港區必吃特色早午餐 / 台北好吃聚餐餐廳推薦 / 南港展覽館lalaport附近美食／南港寵物友善）
Google Map地址: 115台灣臺北市南港區經貿二路166號A棟2樓
評論1:
[Bryan] (3分, 10 months ago): April 27 2025

This is probably my 30th visit to The Diner. Based on the recent menu and flavor changes, I can sadly say that ever since the restaurant was sold to TTFB a few years ago, it has completely lost the essence that once defined it — the authentic American taste.

They’ve stripped away the finer, more carefully prepared details from the menu, choosing to prioritize management efficiency over the soul of the food. This isn’t just about a decline in food or service quality anymore; the original spirit and purpose of the restaurant have disappeared entirely.

As someone who has eaten here for over a decade, it is heartbreaking to witness this change. What once felt like a true taste of America has been reduced to just another generic, American-style restaurant.
The Diner I once loved no longer exists.
---

From the beginning, the NanKang branch of The Diner has always had a slight disparity in food quality and standards compared to the original RuiAn store. After being partially acquired by TTFB Thai Town, I have noticed a significant change in the food.

This decline in quality is something I have never experienced in my 15 years of dining at Whalen's RuiAn. The thickness of the pancakes varies, the coleslaw is now coarser with altered ingredient proportions, and the steak has become tougher and harder to chew.
While corporatization may have positive effects on the restaurant's sustainability, it is truly regrettable if it loses the essence that originally attracted people: delicious and authentic American food.

Note: To this day, I still consider TheDiner RuiAn to be the best American brunch restaurant in Taipei, especially for their pancakes and American-Mexican cuisine. Hope Nangang could get better to the same quality.

---

December 08 2024

Returned to the Nangang branch after several months, as the Ruian branch was fully booked. This time, the quality of the waffles improved slightly compared to before, but overall, the gap in quality between the two locations still feels significant.

The hash browns in the breakfast platter were undercooked and fell apart. Additionally, the large screen that usually displays sports matches was oddly playing a Buddhist TV channel, which felt out of place.

I recommend having the manager from the Ruian branch oversee the Nangang branch for a while to help bring the food and service quality of the two locations to a more consistent standard.
評論2: [Calibre Pelligrino] (1分, 8 months ago): I came here with high expectations! It’s called “The Diner” so I was attracted and it’s my first time coming here. I ordered fajitas! but they served it luke warm, not sizzling hot! the soft tortilla is guwwy. No cilantro no pica dillo no jalapenos no Mexican Salsa. My God! Why is this served like this? Really lack of flavor. They gave me Tabasco Sauce(thats a no no for Fajita) If you can’t serve the food the way it should be , then ERASE! this off the menu! It’s like ordering stinky tofu without tofu! Seriously I was disappointed. The manager tried his best to offer me sauce but gave me chili con carne (spoonful) 😂 Then when I asked for cilantro he hid and never showed up. The waiter ignored me, I almost walked out of there without paying. I’ve seen the other food there looks good but can be deceiving, and no wonder why there are a lot of empty seats in this restaurant!

Event the food choice is limited compared to the some popular buffet places around Taipei, but high quality of seafood platter, including Lobsters, Oysters, Scallops, Crabs, Prawns, Clams & Sashimi Bar. Of course, Roast Prime Rib & Pork are simple dishes, these were taste and fresh enough.

Overall, I do enjoy coming to this restaurant, worth it, good value for money. In fact, this is one of my favorite places for weekday lunch. i plan to return often, Definitely be back soon!

評論1:
[Ree] (4分, 4 months ago): Michelin Guide Restaurant in Taipei’s Nangang District

We felt really lucky to visit this Michelin Guide–listed restaurant while in Taipei on a business trip with locals. Our local colleagues organised a huge spread of dishes for us — I’m not sure if it was a set menu, but we were served an impressive variety of food.

Personally, I found it a little harder to judge as some dishes weren’t quite to my usual taste, but the ones I did enjoy were lovely. The service was fast and efficient, though English was limited, which led to a small mix-up over vegetarian options — not the staff’s fault, just a language barrier.

The restaurant itself had a calm, refined atmosphere, and overall, it was a great experience. While I’m not sure it was the best meal we had in Taipei, it was still a memorable opportunity to try a Michelin-recommended local spot.
評論2: [Andy Sun] (4分, 4 months ago): The food is very good especially the beef fried rice. We walked in at 8:20pm w/o reservation and got a table right away.
評論3:
[Mao Heng Sun] (4分, 3 years ago): I flew back to Taiwan to have a proper Taiwanese meal with my family before Lunar New Year ended.
I would highly recommend making an early reservation as it’s always full house due to the office buildings and exhibition centres being located within 5-10 mins walking distance.
As I didn’t make any reservation and walked in during lunchtime, we waited nearly 30 mins to be seated.
Three of us ordered a two-pax lunch set meal and an extra vegan soup and dessert. It’s way too much for us, and we need to take it away.
All the dishes were relatively excellent and appealing, but the pork was served too slowly, and we almost forgot about it. Identical to the dessert, I have to go over to the waitress to inform them that we have finished the meal and please serve the dessert. The green tea is nicely brewed, but the best is still the vegan soup.
The handicapped toilet is on maintenance, and most of the tools are stored in the lady’s room; a waiter came into the lady’s room without knocking, and I was deeply disturbed.
All the all, if you are just talking about the food and people who stand at the counters, they are high quality, trained and well cooked. But the rest of the staff seems not experienced.
It’s a good try.
評論4: [Andrew Goh] (4分, 2 years ago): I finally had the opportunity to enjoy my favorite bamboo-smoked chicken in Taiwan. The chicken was incredibly tender and bursting with flavor. Another standout was the pan-fried pig liver, which was so delicious that I couldn't resist having more. Achieving the perfect doneness was essential to preserve the juiciness of the liver. On the other hand, the claypot-cooked oyster with tofu was a bit lacking in umami flavor and tasted somewhat bland. The stewed white cabbage was excellent but could have used a touch more salt. The steamed fish was decent but lacked seasoning, and a spicier sauce could have elevated it to perfection. We wrapped up the meal with a traditional and authentic serving of fried vermicelli, or bee hoon, which was incredibly satisfying. The key to this dish was the perfect flavor and springiness of the vermicelli. It was a delightful lunch with my business associates, finally indulging in properly cooked Taiwanese cuisine!
評論5: [Johnny Shen] (5分, 2 months ago): Fantastic meal in a private dining room.  Very decorative and delicious dishes.  I see why they earned their Michelin star.  Our server was attentive and quick to respond. Will definitely come back.

Only consideration for future customers would be to take a small group at a time , the place is purposely small , and the team fits the size .

評論1: [Wing Seng Chan] (4分, 2 weeks ago): The food here is great. Still quite crowded even at 3pm. Tried a variety of food including the xiao long bao and crispy red bean pancake. Very well made. The noodle soup is simple but delicious. Overall good food with simple service.
評論2: [金正一] (3分, 10 months ago): After queuing for 20 minutes, I was able to get in quite quickly. The ordering is done via a paper menu, and the sauces, ginger strips, etc., are self-served at a condiment bar, allowing you to decide on the portion sizes, which is quite flexible and nice. As for the food, the dumplings were served piping hot. They were very juicy, and while the flavor wasn't extraordinarily delicious, the price was definitely a winning factor. Since I was dining alone, I couldn't try too many dishes, but I found the hot and sour soup delicious. It was packed with ingredients, though it's worth noting that the pepper flavor was quite strong, giving it a bit of a spicy kick, which was quite tasty. I've attached the April 2025 menu for your reference.
評論3:
[Chen E.] (4分, a year ago): Many friends recommended this place to me, and it's definitely worth visiting. If you're looking for decent dumplings at a reasonable price, this is the place to go.

However, I was a bit disappointed with the shrimp dumplings, as they didn’t match my taste, and the cabbage was a bit too sweet for my liking.

Overall, I would recommend the original soup dumplings, the sweet and sour soup, and other dishes.
評論4:
[Paul Yang] (5分, a year ago): 5/5 — xiao long bao.  Good with pork in it.
5/5 — red bean pancake.  Sweet and salty, very good flavor.  Has sesame.
5/5 — Rib noodles.  Meat is a bit sweet which is good
4/5 — green onion biscuit.  Good, fluffy
評論5:
[Chen Dow] (4分, a year ago): A very popular place specializing in steamed pork dumpling (小籠包 or xiao-long-bao XLB).  Foods are good to very good and very reasonably priced.  Service is lout and energetic.  The decor is plain, with most people needing to share tables.

It's very full at 6 pm on a Wednesday evening, but the two of us did not have to wait to get seated.  You are seated and given an order form first, pay at the register, then are served the food at your table.  You can take appetizers from the display case next to the register and mark on the form (all are NT 45 or $1.5 US).  You also get condiments, including the must-have ginger slices and vinegar if you have XLB, from a board next to the kitchen.

XLB we got had lots of juice, thin wrapper, and decent port filling.  It's not Ding Tai Fung (鼎泰豐), but not bad, especially at 7 for NT 90 ($3 US).  That's less than half the price of DTF (6 for NT 200).

評論1:
[Glenn Wong] (4分, 3 months ago): Nice little hideaway on the top of the mountain with a nice view of Taipei 101 in the distant. The decor is well presented and the piped music of Christmas classics made you feel like you are not in Taipei but a Western country. The air is fresh but unfortunately the let down is that there is quite a fair amount of houseflies around on the table and food area, which dampens the dining experience.

The good is pretty decent, including the fried rice, fried tofu, fried mushrooms and french fries. The service could be better as there is little communication.
評論2: [Qiu Ju Yeo] (5分, 4 months ago): Second time here. Ordered 茶香豆腐and 铁观音茶鸡汤. One tip is to turn the soup stove to low heat and slowly let it simmer for 20-30min till the soup turn dark brown. That’s when the tea flavour is already infused. No high heat! No need to top up the soup as all the flavours would already be infused in the first round. This is a family restaurant, and the service personnel tried to be as helpful as much as they can. Not sure what kind of services people are expecting as mentioned in their reviews. 茶叶炒饭 also have some subtle tea flavour! Finished up the meal with their tie guan yin tea! 👍 Also ordered the local tea flavoured craft beers to try! If you know what to pick, all the dishes actually complement each other!
評論3: [Zai] (4分, 4 months ago): Tea infused fried rice is very tasty. Tea infused mee sua is a little heavy on the tea side. Rest of it was alright. Service is almost non existent and they only take cash.

評論5:
[Jay Li] (5分, a year ago): An Unforgettable Culinary Gem in 貓空
Prepare to be dazzled! 龍門客棧 is a culinary masterpiece nestled amidst the breathtaking beauty of 貓空. From the moment you step inside, you'll be captivated by the warm ambiance and impeccable service.

We recently feasted on a twelve-course extravaganza, each dish meticulously crafted to perfection. Our guests from Singapore, renowned for their discerning taste buds, were utterly blown away. The highlight was the exceptional hospitality of Eddie Pong, who not only served us with grace but also shared his passion for Taiwanese cuisine.

貓空龍門客棧is more than just a restaurant; it's an experience that will linger in your memory long after your visit. Whether you're seeking a romantic evening for two or a delightful gathering with friends and family, this hidden gem is sure to exceed your expectations.

Here's why we wholeheartedly recommend 龍門客棧:

Unparalleled Culinary Excellence: Every dish is a masterpiece, showcasing the finest ingredients and culinary techniques.

Warm and Inviting Atmosphere: The restaurant's cozy setting and friendly staff create a welcoming and unforgettable dining experience.

Exceptional Service: The attentive and knowledgeable staff go above and beyond to ensure your every need is met.

Breathtaking Views: Enjoy panoramic vistas of Taipei City while savoring your delectable meal.

Don't miss the opportunity to indulge in a culinary journey like no other. Visit 龍門客棧 and discover why it's the most celebrated Taiwanese restaurant in 貓空.

評論1:
[Felix H] (5分, a year ago): We had the mushroom appetiser , the pork chop and the clam fettuccine . The appetiser was ok but the pork chop and fettuccine were outstanding ! The pork chop had a nice smokey flavour and was done perfectly . The clam fettuccine was full of fresh clams and this dish is totally garlic flavoured ; Excellent ! I had the guava and pineapple juice - very good 👍
The creme brûlée is a must order .
Overall a great find ….. good food , good prices and good service !
評論2: [Shaun Liu] (5分, 7 years ago): Hands down the best Italian Restaurant in Taipei. Must try their seafood pizza and their signature 黑米 （Squid Ink Risotto). When I came in for dinner it was full of people but I got seated relatively quickly. The atmosphere makes you feel at home, and upscale at the same time. The wait staff is very professional with a natural ambiance, the chefs are also incredibly talented and have an incredibly well thought out menu prepared for any occasion. The Risotto and Veggie Pasta are phenomenal. Not overly seasoned and full of complimentary flavors. Portions were very reasonable for the price, and the selection of spicy to veggie options added a considerate touch as well. All in all I'm impressed that Taiwan can bring a creative and unique touch to Italian food at an affordable price. It's a bit hidden, but completely worth the experience! A must try!
評論3:
[Sheryl Liu] (3分, a year ago): The service wasn’t very friendly, the waiter gave us the menu and just stood next to us without saying anything (such as making recommendations) waiting to get the order, it was a very close distance and that felt very uncomfortable. Not sure what that was about. The waiter taking the check at the end was much nicer.
The restaurant seating arrangement was curious and inconvenient, there were two tables set at the busy entrance with not enough space to move around, there were pillows and a cat bed on the floor, it was simply too compact.
The two pasta dishes we ordered were delicious though, and the cat in the restaurant was adorable.
評論4:
[Chen E.] (5分, a year ago): I went there with some friends and sat outside on a cool day, which made the whole experience even better.

The food was pretty decent—I had the pasta with a generous amount of clams, and the set came with a tasty pumpkin cream soup. The hot coffee wasn’t bad either. Overall, I really enjoyed my time there.

For the value, I’d say the price is definitely worth it.
評論5:
[Fabian V] (4分, 3 years ago): It's a nice place, especially appreciate the opening hours that are somewhat unusual for Taiwan. The food is good, but don't expect overly authentic Italian cuisine, as it's heavily adjusted to local taste preferences.

I found it a bit funny that the linguine contained exactly the same vegetables as a European restaurant would use if they wanted to make it "Asian": snap peas, baby corn, some kind of Asian mushroom (see photo). The pasta was cooked nicely al dente.

The ciabatta is good but it would be nice if it came with more olive oil and some salt and pepper grinders to spice things up.

The rocket salad is probably my favorite but for some reason the kind of rocket they use consists mostly of stems and not so many leaves. Also, typical for Taiwan, knives do not form part of the cutlery so you'll somehow have to eat it with a fork and a spoon and have half a rocket awkwardly hanging out of your mouth.

The zucchini is equally cut in pieces that are too big to put in your mouth, so without a knife you'll have to eat it like a skewer, basically taking bites while it's on your fork. The pieces were also quite salty, as were the fries.

Service is swift and there's many waiters, but it can be hard to raise their attention. Prices are elevated and so it's surprising that card payments are not possible.

Might come back for the nice atmosphere (outside seating without cigarette smoke! Cute cat!)  and the overall good taste.
